legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, local customary law, aristocratic arbitration, and Greek-style legal practice in towns. Major cases were judged by the crown or senior officials, while land, marriage, and debt disputes were often handled according to local tradition.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自土地税、王室领地租赋、牧群税、关税、市场税与矿产收益。税收多以实物、牲畜和金属缴纳，货币税在城镇与贸易节点更常见。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from land taxes, rents from royal estates, herd taxes, customs duties, market tolls, and mineral income. Taxes were often paid in kind, livestock, and metal, with cash payments more common in towns and trade nodes.

religion.zh: 宗教以安纳托利亚本土信仰、伊朗宗教传统与希腊化神祇崇拜并存。地方神庙与火、太阳、山地及丰饶相关的崇拜重要，王室也吸纳希腊式宗教表现以服务外交与王权合法性。
religion.en: Religion combined native Anatolian cults, Iranian religious traditions, and Hellenistic worship of Greek deities. Local sanctuaries and cults tied to fire, the sun, mountains, and fertility were important, while the monarchy also adopted Greek religious forms for diplomacy and royal legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 宫廷思想并无统一哲学正统，而是以王权实用主义为主，吸收波斯式王道观、希腊化教育与地方贵族伦理。受希腊文化影响的上层阶级熟悉修辞、王政观念与一定程度的哲学讨论。
philosophy.en: Court thought had no single orthodox philosophy, but was guided by practical monarchy, drawing on Persian concepts of kingship, Hellenistic education, and local aristocratic ethics. Greek-influenced elites were familiar with rhetoric, ideas of kingship, and some philosophical discourse.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与外交中广泛使用希腊字母书写的希腊语；同时地方社会可能继续使用阿拉姆语书写传统或口头行政习惯。官方铭文与对外往来以希腊文最具声望。
writingSystem.en: Greek written in the Greek alphabet was widely used for administration and diplomacy; at the same time, local society likely continued some Aramaic scribal traditions or oral administrative practice. Greek held the greatest prestige in official inscriptions and external relations.

culturalAchievements.zh: 卡帕多细亚在221 BCE并非地中海主要文化中心，但其成就在于维持了内陆安纳托利亚王国的连续性，将伊朗化贵族传统与希腊化宫廷文化结合，并在马扎卡等城市推动了宫廷礼仪、铸币与宗教赞助。
culturalAchievements.en: Cappadocia was not a leading Mediterranean cultural center in 221 BCE, but its achievement lay in maintaining a durable inland Anatolian kingdom that blended Iranianized aristocratic traditions with Hellenistic court culture, while fostering court ceremonial, coinage, and religious patronage in cities such as Mazaca.

languageFamily.zh: 上层与行政语言主要属印欧语系希腊语；地方居民还使用安纳托利亚和伊朗相关语言成分，形成多语言环境。
languageFamily.en: Elite and administrative language was primarily Indo-European Greek; local populations also used Anatolian and Iranian-related speech elements, producing a multilingual environment.

unit.zh: 卡帕多细亚德拉克马年产值
卡帕多细亚德拉克马/人/年

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业和畜牧业为主，城镇手工业和区域转运贸易为辅。人均产出在希腊化世界属中等偏低，但王国通过控制高原通道、马匹和牧产资源维持稳定收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by urban craft production and regional transit trade. Per capita output was lower-middle by Hellenistic standards, but the kingdom maintained stable income through control of plateau routes, horses, and pastoral resources.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与园圃农业、绵羊与山羊牧养、马匹繁育、毛织品、皮革、陶器、金属加工与地方采矿
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, vineyards and garden agriculture, sheep and goat herding, horse breeding, wool textiles, leatherworking, pottery, metalworking, and local mining

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、羊毛、皮革、谷物、盐、金属制品、陶器、葡萄酒，以及从叙利亚和小亚沿海转运而来的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Horses, wool, leather, grain, salt, metal goods, pottery, wine, and luxury goods transited from Syria and the Anatolian coasts

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、农具和家族关系网络，而非大量货币积累。贵族财富则集中于大片地产、马群、依附人口与金银器物。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in land access, livestock, grain stores, tools, and kinship networks rather than large cash holdings. Noble wealth was concentrated in large estates, horse herds, dependent populations, and precious metal objects.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室领地、贵族庄园和村社生产并存的混合经济。国家并不全面控制生产，但通过税收、道路、市场与铸币影响资源流动。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining royal domains, aristocratic estates, and village production. The state did not comprehensively direct production, but influenced resource flows through taxation, roads, markets, and coinage.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模对一个内陆中等王国而言较为稳健，足以应付常规边防、宫廷运作和短期外交支出，但若遭遇长期大战或继承危机，储备很快会承压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was fairly solid for a medium-sized inland kingdom, sufficient for routine border defense, court operations, and short-term diplomatic spending, but prolonged war or a succession crisis would quickly strain reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守稳健，重视维持贵族忠诚、边防供给与适度储备。王室避免对乡村进行过度货币化征税，以免削弱农业和牧业基础。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, prioritizing noble loyalty, frontier provisioning, and moderate reserves. The crown avoided excessively monetizing rural taxation so as not to weaken the agrarian and pastoral base.

description.zh: 由王室卫队、城防部队和受薪步兵组成，负责守卫马扎卡、王室领地和主要交通线。
description.en: Composed of the royal guard, fortress troops, and salaried infantry, responsible for defending Mazaca, royal estates, and major routes.

description.zh: 由地方贵族及其随从提供，是王国最具机动性和政治象征意义的兵种，适于巡边、突击与追击。
description.en: Provided by regional nobles and their retainers, this was the kingdom's most mobile and politically important arm, suited to patrol, shock action, and pursuit.

description.zh: 由乡村社群和地方领主在战时征发，装备较不统一，适合防御、围城辅助和补充兵力。
description.en: Raised in wartime from rural communities and local lords, less uniformly equipped, suitable for defense, siege support, and reinforcement.

description.zh: 多来自牧区和边地，负责侦察、传令、掩护和骚扰敌军。
description.en: Drawn largely from pastoral and frontier zones, they handled scouting, dispatch, screening, and harassment.

commanderInChief.zh: 阿里亚拉塞斯五世国王

technology.zh: 军备属典型希腊化安纳托利亚水平，使用长矛、剑、盾、复合弓、投枪、皮甲与部分金属甲。攻城技术有限，重在高原机动作战、堡垒防御与骑兵行动。
technology.en: Military equipment was at a typical Hellenistic Anatolian level, using spears, swords, shields, composite bows, javelins, leather armor, and some metal armor. Siege technology was limited; emphasis lay on plateau maneuver, fortress defense, and cavalry operations.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自塞琉古、本都和亚美尼亚等更强邻国的干预，以及王国内部贵族在外部支持下引发的继承或派系危机。
threats.en: The main threats came from interference by stronger neighbors such as the Seleucids, Pontus, and Armenia, as well as succession or factional crises within the kingdom backed by outside powers.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以农村居民为主，分布在高原农业区、牧区和若干城市节点。族群与文化构成多样，包括本地安纳托利亚居民、伊朗化贵族成分以及部分希腊化城镇人口。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly rural, spread across plateau farming zones, pastoral districts, and a few urban nodes. Its ethnic and cultural composition was mixed, including local Anatolian inhabitants, Iranianized noble elements, and some Hellenized urban populations.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室与地方贵族；其下是祭司、军官、宫廷文书和城镇富户；多数人口为农民、牧民和小生产者；另有家内奴仆与依附劳动者，但并非一切生产都依靠大规模奴隶制。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the royal family and regional nobility; beneath them were priests, officers, court scribes, and urban notables; most people were peasants, pastoralists, and small producers; there were also household slaves and dependent laborers, though not all production rested on large-scale slavery.

allies.zh: 无牢固的大型盟友体系；王国通常通过婚姻、使节往来和均势外交，在塞琉古、本都与其他安纳托利亚诸国之间维持生存空间。
allies.en: There was no firm large-scale alliance system; the kingdom usually relied on marriage ties, embassies, and balance-of-power diplomacy to preserve room for maneuver between the Seleucids, Pontus, and other Anatolian states.

enemies.zh: 没有持续全面战争中的固定死敌，但塞琉古帝国的压力、本都的扩张倾向以及亚美尼亚方向的变动都可能迅速转化为敌对关系。
enemies.en: It had no fixed mortal enemy in continuous total war, but pressure from the Seleucid Empire, the expansionist tendency of Pontus, and shifts from Armenia could quickly turn into hostile relations.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以谨慎中立、权衡强邻和维护王朝独立为核心。卡帕多细亚倾向避免单方面依附任何一方，同时通过联姻、朝贡式姿态或有限军事合作换取自治空间。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on cautious neutrality, balancing stronger neighbors, and preserving dynastic independence. Cappadocia tended to avoid one-sided dependence on any power, instead using marriage alliances, tribute-like gestures, or limited military cooperation to secure autonomy.

era.zh: 希腊化时代早中期内陆安纳托利亚技术阶段
era.en: Early-to-middle Hellenistic inland Anatolian technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 关键并非原创发明，而是对希腊化铸币、筑城、骑兵装备、灌溉农业和长距离行政通信方式的吸收与地方化应用。
keyInnovations.en: Its key advances lay not in original inventions but in the adoption and local adaptation of Hellenistic coinage, fortification methods, cavalry equipment, irrigated agriculture, and long-distance administrative communications.

strengths.zh: 王朝延续性较强，地处高原内陆易守难攻；贵族骑兵和牧业资源提供可靠军事基础；在强邻之间保持灵活外交是其重要优势。
strengths.en: The kingdom benefited from relatively strong dynastic continuity, a defensible inland plateau position, noble cavalry and pastoral resources as a reliable military base, and flexible diplomacy between stronger neighbors.

weaknesses.zh: 国家官僚化程度有限，对地方贵族依赖很深；城市和财政基础不如沿海希腊化强国；一旦外部大国扶持宫廷派系，内部稳定容易被打破。
weaknesses.en: The state was only lightly bureaucratized and deeply dependent on regional nobility; its urban and fiscal base was weaker than that of coastal Hellenistic powers; if a great power backed court factions, internal stability could be undermined quickly.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE时卡帕多细亚总体稳定，短期内能维持独立王国地位，但长期前景取决于其在塞琉古、本都与其他安纳托利亚强权之间的平衡能力，以及王位继承是否顺利。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE Cappadocia was broadly stable and could preserve its status as an independent kingdom in the short term, but its long-term outlook depended on its ability to balance the Seleucids, Pontus, and other Anatolian powers, and on smooth dynastic succession.

description.zh: 内陆安纳托利亚的卡帕多细亚在塞琉古与本都之间维持王权。
description.en: Inland Cappadocia maintained its monarchy between Seleucid and Pontic influence.

governmentForm.zh: 希腊化君主制，王权集中并依托宫廷官僚、城邦机构与附属城市网络共同治理
governmentForm.en: A Hellenistic monarchy with centralized royal authority supported by court bureaucracy, civic institutions, and a network of subordinate cities

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室与宫廷精英为核心，上层包括马其顿—希腊贵族、军政官员、祭司与富裕地主；中层为城市公民、商人、工匠与驻军；下层为小农、佃户、雇工、奴隶及乡村依附民。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on the royal house and court elite, with an upper stratum of Macedonian-Greek aristocrats, military and administrative officials, priests, and wealthy landowners; a middle stratum of urban citizens, merchants, artisans, and garrison personnel; and lower strata of small farmers, tenants, laborers, slaves, and dependent rural populations.

rulingClass.zh: 阿塔罗斯王室、宫廷近臣、将领、受王室扶植的希腊城邦显贵以及掌握大片地产与税收承包的精英家族
rulingClass.en: The Attalid royal family, court companions, generals, royal protégés among the Greek civic elites, and elite families controlling large estates and tax-farming interests

succession.zh: 王位以王朝内部的世袭继承为原则，通常由最具政治与军事支持的男性宗室成员承继，并由宫廷与军队承认
succession.en: The throne followed dynastic hereditary succession, usually passing to the male member of the royal house with the strongest political and military backing, confirmed by court and army

structure.zh: 国家由国王直接统辖。中央以宫廷为核心，设财政、军务、外交、司法、王室地产与神庙事务等部门；地方通过王室任命的总督、军镇长官和各希腊城邦的本地议会共同运作，形成王权与城邦自治并存的体系。
structure.en: The state was ruled directly by the king. The central government revolved around the court, with departments for finance, military affairs, diplomacy, justice, royal estates, and temple affairs; locally it operated through royal governors, garrison commanders, and the civic councils of Greek poleis, creating a mixed system of royal authority alongside civic self-government.

function.zh: 征收地租、关税、矿山收入、港口税与附属城市贡赋，管理铸币、仓储与王室支出
function.en: Collected rents, customs, mining income, harbor dues, and tribute from dependent cities, and managed minting, storage, and royal expenditures

function.zh: 统筹驻军、雇佣军、要塞守备、军粮运输、武器制造与边境防务
function.en: Supervised garrisons, mercenaries, fortress defense, military supply transport, arms production, and frontier security

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存档案、处理外交文书、王令传达与地方报告
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept archives, handled diplomatic correspondence, transmitted royal orders, and processed provincial reports

name.en: Department of Provincial and Civic Affairs

function.zh: 监督附属城市、调停城邦争端、确认地方特权并协调征税与兵役义务
function.en: Oversaw dependent cities, mediated civic disputes, confirmed local privileges, and coordinated taxation and military obligations

function.zh: 处理王室管辖案件、审查法令、仲裁土地和税务纠纷，并监督监狱与处罚执行
function.en: Handled cases under royal jurisdiction, reviewed decrees, arbitrated land and tax disputes, and supervised prisons and penalties

function.zh: 管理王室赞助的神庙、祭祀经费、祭司任命与王朝崇拜活动
function.en: Managed royally sponsored temples, cult expenditure, priestly appointments, and dynastic cult activities

function.zh: 经营王室土地、牧场、林地与粮仓，监督租佃、灌溉与农业供给
function.en: Managed royal lands, pastures, forests, and granaries, and supervised tenancy, irrigation, and agricultural supply

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由米西亚、埃奥利斯及沿海属地的王室官员、驻防军官和城邦自治机关共同组成。希腊城市通常保有议会、公民大会与地方法官，但在税收、驻军、外交与战略要塞方面受国王直接控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined royal officials, garrison commanders, and civic institutions across Mysia, Aeolis, and the coastal possessions. Greek cities often retained councils, assemblies, and local magistrates, but taxation, garrisons, diplomacy, and strategic fortresses remained under direct royal control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为希腊城邦法、王室敕令与地方习惯法的混合。城市内部民事案件多依城邦法律处理，涉及王室地产、财政、军务和重大治安案件时则由王室司法体系裁决。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was a hybrid of Greek civic law, royal decrees, and local customary law. Civil disputes within cities were generally handled under polis law, while matters involving royal estates, finance, military concerns, and major public order cases fell under royal jurisdiction.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地租税、港口关税、市场税、矿山与林地产出、王室垄断收益及附属城市贡赋为主，部分税项由承包人征收。王国对海贸与内陆转运课税尤为重视。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system relied chiefly on land dues, harbor customs, market taxes, revenues from mines and forests, royal monopolies, and tribute from dependent cities, with some taxes farmed out to contractors. The kingdom placed particular emphasis on taxing maritime trade and inland transit.

religion.zh: 宗教以希腊多神信仰为主，崇拜雅典娜、宙斯、狄俄尼索斯、得墨忒耳和阿斯克勒庇俄斯等神祇，并融合安纳托利亚地方崇拜。王室积极推动王朝祭祀，以强化合法性与城市忠诚。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Greek polytheistic, centered on deities such as Athena, Zeus, Dionysus, Demeter, and Asclepius, while also incorporating Anatolian local cults. The royal house actively promoted dynastic cults to reinforce legitimacy and civic loyalty.

philosophy.zh: 佩加蒙为希腊化学术中心之一，受柏拉图学园、逍遥学派、斯多亚学派与修辞传统影响明显。宫廷赞助学者、图书收藏与教育活动，强调希腊教养与政治修辞。
philosophy.en: Pergamon was one of the intellectual centers of the Hellenistic world, influenced by Platonic, Peripatetic, Stoic, and rhetorical traditions. The court sponsored scholars, book collecting, and education, emphasizing Greek paideia and political rhetoric.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用希腊字母书写通用希腊语，王室法令、铭文、商业文书和文学作品皆以希腊文为主；部分地方社群仍保留安纳托利亚语言的口语传统。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom primarily used the Greek alphabet to write Koine Greek. Royal decrees, inscriptions, commercial records, and literary works were mainly in Greek, though some local communities retained Anatolian languages in spoken use.

culturalAchievements.zh: 佩加蒙以王宫、卫城建筑群、神庙建设、雕塑艺术、图书馆传统和城市规划闻名。阿塔罗斯王朝以文化赞助塑造王国形象，使其成为爱琴海东部重要的艺术与学术中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Pergamon was renowned for its palace complex, acropolis architecture, temple construction, sculpture, library tradition, and urban planning. The Attalid dynasty used cultural patronage to shape the kingdom’s image, making it a major artistic and intellectual center of the eastern Aegean.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与高文化语言属于印欧语系希腊语支，地方亦有安纳托利亚诸语言残存
languageFamily.en: The official and high-cultural language belonged to the Hellenic branch of the Indo-European family, alongside surviving local Anatolian languages

gdpDescription.zh: 佩加蒙经济在希腊化诸国中属富庶而多元，得益于肥沃腹地农业、爱琴海海运、手工业、税收网络与政治稳定。其财富虽不及托勒密埃及等超大王国，但以人口规模而言相当繁荣。
gdpDescription.en: Pergamon’s economy was wealthy and diversified by Hellenistic standards, benefiting from fertile hinterland agriculture, Aegean maritime trade, artisanal production, tax networks, and political stability. Its wealth did not match that of huge states such as Ptolemaic Egypt, but it was notably prosperous relative to its size.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒、橄榄油、畜牧、陶器、金属加工、纺织、木材、采石、铸币、港口贸易与奢侈品再分销
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine, olive oil, pastoralism, pottery, metalworking, textiles, timber, quarrying, minting, harbor trade, and redistribution of luxury goods

tradeGoods.zh: 出口葡萄酒、橄榄油、陶器、纺织品、金属制品、木材与农产品；进口埃及粮食补充、香料、精细织物、奴隶、部分金属原料、纸草与东地中海奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included wine, olive oil, pottery, textiles, metal goods, timber, and agricultural produce; imports included supplementary Egyptian grain, spices, fine textiles, slaves, some metal raw materials, papyrus, and eastern Mediterranean luxury goods

householdWealth.zh: 城市精英家庭拥有地产、仓储、船只、奴隶和货币财富，生活水平高；普通农户多以土地、牲畜、农具和少量银币为主要财产；城市贫民与奴隶财富极低。
householdWealth.en: Urban elite households held estates, storehouses, ships, slaves, and cash wealth, enjoying high living standards; ordinary farming households mainly possessed land, livestock, tools, and limited silver coin; urban poor and slaves had very little wealth.

economicSystem.zh: 经济为王室主导下的市场型希腊化经济。王室控制关键地产、税收与部分垄断行业，城市市场和私人商人则在海贸、手工业与金融借贷中发挥重要作用。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a market-oriented Hellenistic system under royal direction. The crown controlled key estates, taxes, and some monopolized sectors, while urban markets and private merchants played major roles in maritime trade, artisanal production, and credit.

source.en: Customs, harbor dues, and market taxes

source.zh: 附属城市贡赋与盟邦缴纳
source.en: Tribute from dependent cities and allied contributions

category.zh: 外交、馈赠与雇佣军补助

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库储备丰厚，部分来自长期积累、战利品、贡赋和贸易税。阿塔罗斯王朝以稳健财政和文化—军事双重支出著称，能够在危机中动员现银、粮仓和贵金属储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was substantial, built from long-term accumulation, war spoils, tribute, and trade taxes. The Attalid dynasty was known for prudent finances and balanced cultural-military spending, enabling rapid mobilization of cash, grain reserves, and precious metals in crises.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守务实，注重维持常年盈余、积累贵金属储备、以公共建筑和宗教赞助提升合法性，并在战争时用丰厚财力维持雇佣军与同盟关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic, emphasizing regular surpluses, accumulation of precious-metal reserves, use of public building and religious patronage to enhance legitimacy, and maintenance of mercenaries and alliances through strong financial capacity in wartime.

description.zh: 核心重步兵，装备长矛、盾牌与盔甲，用于会战与守卫要地
description.en: Core heavy infantry equipped with sarissas, shields, and armor, used in pitched battle and defense of key positions

description.zh: 包含投枪兵、弓箭手、标枪兵与地方辅助部队，适合山地与机动战
description.en: Included javelin-men, archers, peltasts, and local auxiliaries suited for mountain warfare and maneuver combat

description.zh: 数量不大但机动性强，承担侦察、侧翼掩护、追击和通信任务
description.en: Not numerous but mobile, used for reconnaissance, flank protection, pursuit, and communications

description.zh: 来自希腊世界与安纳托利亚各地，包括凯尔特人、色雷斯人和希腊雇佣兵，是王国军力的重要补充
description.en: Drawn from across the Greek world and Anatolia, including Galatians, Thracians, and Greek mercenaries, forming an important supplement to royal forces

description.zh: 维持有限舰队、港口守备和海岸巡逻，保护沿岸交通与贸易据点
description.en: Maintained a limited fleet, harbor garrisons, and coastal patrols to protect seaborne communications and trade stations

technology.zh: 军队采用希腊化时代标准装备与攻城技术，包括长枪方阵、复合弓、投石机、攻城塔、城防工事和成熟的军粮运输体系，但整体规模逊于塞琉古或托勒密等大国。
technology.en: The army used standard Hellenistic equipment and siege techniques, including sarissa phalanxes, composite bows, catapults, siege towers, fortifications, and developed logistical systems, though on a smaller scale than the Seleucid or Ptolemaic great powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自小亚细亚的权力竞争，尤其是塞琉古势力、比提尼亚、本都及加拉太人的军事活动；同时需警惕爱琴海局势变化与马其顿、罗马等更大势力介入希腊事务。
threats.en: The main threats came from power struggles in Asia Minor, especially Seleucid influence, Bithynia, Pontus, and Galatian military activity; the kingdom also had to watch shifts in the Aegean balance and the growing involvement of larger powers such as Macedon and Rome in Greek affairs.

populationDescription.zh: 王国人口约九十万，主要集中于西小亚细亚的城市、沿海平原和肥沃内陆河谷。居民构成复杂，包括希腊—马其顿移民后裔、安纳托利亚土著、商人社群、驻军、奴隶及附属乡村人口。
populationDescription.en: The kingdom’s population of about 900,000 was concentrated in western Asia Minor’s cities, coastal plains, and fertile inland valleys. Its population was mixed, including descendants of Greek-Macedonian settlers, Anatolian natives, merchant communities, soldiers, slaves, and dependent rural populations.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与贵族、城市公民精英、商人与手工业者、自由农民、佃农、雇工、外来居民与奴隶。希腊城市公民通常享有较高法律地位，而乡村土著与奴隶在政治权利上处于边缘。
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included the royal house and nobility, urban citizen elites, merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, laborers, resident foreigners, and slaves. Greek urban citizens generally enjoyed higher legal standing, while rural natives and slaves were politically marginal.

allies.zh: 佩加蒙在221年前后与部分希腊城邦、若干爱琴海盟友及反塞琉古力量保持合作，也倾向与罗马建立友好关系以制衡马其顿与亚洲诸敌，但正式稳固同盟仍在发展中。
allies.en: Around 221 BCE Pergamon cooperated with certain Greek cities, some Aegean partners, and anti-Seleucid forces, and was inclined toward friendship with Rome as a counterweight to Macedon and regional rivals, though firm formal alliances were still developing.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是加拉太人残余势力与在小亚细亚争夺影响的塞琉古王国，此外与比提尼亚、本都及部分地方势力存在竞争与边境紧张。
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were remnant Galatian forces and the Seleucid kingdom competing for influence in Asia Minor; it also faced rivalry and border tensions with Bithynia, Pontus, and various local powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持小亚细亚西部均势、保障沿海贸易、扶植亲佩加蒙城邦并通过馈赠、婚姻、文化赞助和军事干预扩大声望为主。阿塔罗斯一世尤其重视利用反加拉太人的胜利塑造国际合法性。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining the balance of power in western Asia Minor, securing coastal trade, supporting pro-Pergamene cities, and expanding prestige through gifts, marriage ties, cultural patronage, and selective military intervention. Attalus I especially used his victories against the Galatians to strengthen international legitimacy.

era.zh: 希腊化时代晚期
era.en: Late Hellenistic Age

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的石造防御工事、攻城器械、标准化铸币、城市供水与梯田式卫城建筑、精细雕塑工艺、图书收藏与档案管理技术
keyInnovations.en: Advanced stone fortifications, siege machinery, standardized coinage, urban water systems and terraced acropolis architecture, refined sculptural techniques, and developed methods of book collecting and archival management

strengths.zh: 佩加蒙的优势在于财政充裕、城市经济活跃、王权稳定、文化声望高，并拥有较强的外交灵活性。其地理位置使之能够控制爱琴海与小亚细亚西部之间的重要贸易和军事通道。
strengths.en: Pergamon’s strengths lay in its ample finances, vibrant urban economy, stable monarchy, high cultural prestige, and considerable diplomatic flexibility. Its location allowed it to control important trade and military routes between the Aegean and western Asia Minor.

weaknesses.zh: 王国领土与人口规模有限，军力不足以单独抗衡希腊化大帝国；对雇佣军和外交平衡依赖较深，若遭遇多线战争或贸易受阻，财政与安全都将承压。
weaknesses.en: The kingdom’s territory and population were limited, and its military was insufficient to confront the great Hellenistic empires alone. It depended heavily on mercenaries and diplomatic balancing; in a multi-front war or trade disruption, both finances and security would come under strain.

outlook.zh: 221年时佩加蒙前景总体乐观。若能继续维持财富积累、利用文化与外交资本、谨慎处理与塞琉古、马其顿及新兴罗马势力的关系，王国有望进一步扩大在爱琴海东部与小亚细亚的影响。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE Pergamon’s outlook was generally favorable. If it could continue accumulating wealth, leveraging its cultural and diplomatic capital, and carefully managing relations with the Seleucids, Macedon, and the rising Roman power, the kingdom was well positioned to expand its influence in the eastern Aegean and Asia Minor.

description.zh: 爱琴海东岸的佩加蒙是富庶而活跃的希腊化君主国。
description.en: Pergamon on the Aegean fringe was a wealthy and dynamic Hellenistic monarchy.

capital.zh: 前尼科美狄亚时期的王室中心体系（以齐乌斯、阿斯塔科斯与内陆宫廷据点为核心）
capital.en: Pre-Nicomedia royal center system (centered on Cius, Astacus, and inland royal residences)

governmentForm.zh: 希腊化君主制下的地方贵族联合王权
governmentForm.en: Hellenistic monarchy supported by regional aristocratic power

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、马其顿化与希腊化贵族、地方比提尼亚地主、城市公民、乡村自由农、工匠商人以及奴隶构成；沿海希腊城邦传统与内陆部族贵族结构并存。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house, Macedonianized and Hellenized nobles, native Bithynian landholding elites, urban citizens, rural free peasants, artisans and merchants, and slaves; coastal Greek polis traditions coexisted with inland tribal-aristocratic structures.

rulingClass.zh: 国王及其家族、宫廷近臣、军功贵族、沿海希腊城邦精英与内陆大地主
rulingClass.en: The king and royal family, court companions, military nobility, coastal Greek civic elites, and inland great landholders

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，通常由王室男性成员继承，但常受宫廷支持、贵族认可与武力控制影响
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy, usually passing within the male royal line, though in practice shaped by court backing, aristocratic acceptance, and military control

structure.zh: 国家围绕国王的宫廷运作，中央直接控制王室领地、税收、军队与对外事务；沿海希腊城市保有一定地方自治，内陆则通过总督、驻军长官和依附贵族管理。
structure.en: The state operated around the king's court, with the center directly controlling royal domains, taxation, the army, and foreign affairs; coastal Greek cities retained limited local autonomy, while inland regions were governed through governors, garrison commanders, and dependent nobles.

function.zh: 起草诏令、保存盟约、管理王室文书与外交通信
function.en: Drafted decrees, preserved treaties, and managed royal records and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 征收土地税、关税、港口税、矿产与王室地产收入
function.en: Collected land taxes, customs dues, harbor tolls, mining revenues, and income from royal estates

function.zh: 组织近卫军、驻军、征召、军粮与边防事务
function.en: Organized the guard, garrisons, levies, provisions, and frontier defense

function.zh: 监管黑海与海峡贸易、航运税、仓储与市场秩序
function.en: Supervised Black Sea and straits trade, shipping dues, storage, and market order

function.zh: 协调各地城镇、王室庄园、附庸贵族与司法申诉
function.en: Coordinated towns, royal estates, subordinate nobles, and judicial appeals

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分为沿海城市与内陆领地两类：沿海希腊城市由公民机构和亲王室寡头管理，向国王纳税并提供港口与舰船支持；内陆由王室任命的地方长官、驻军指挥官和与王室结盟的土著贵族共同治理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration fell into two broad types: coastal Greek cities managed by civic bodies and royalist oligarchs, paying taxes and supporting harbors and shipping; and inland territories governed jointly by royal appointees, garrison commanders, and native nobles allied to the crown.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具希腊城邦法、王室敕令和地方习惯法。商业与港口事务多依希腊法处理，土地、贡赋、军役和叛乱问题则更多受王权裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Greek civic law, royal decrees, and local customary law. Commercial and harbor matters were often handled under Greek legal practice, while land, tribute, military obligations, and rebellion were more directly subject to royal judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自土地税、王室地产收益、港口与海关税、市场税、矿产和林木资源、附属地区贡赋以及战时非常征收。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from land tax, income from royal estates, harbor and customs duties, market taxes, minerals and timber resources, tribute from dependent districts, and extraordinary wartime levies.

religion.zh: 宗教以希腊诸神崇拜、地方安纳托利亚神祇、祖先与王室祭祀并存。宙斯、阿波罗、阿耳忒弥斯与地方母神受到敬奉，城市神庙和王室祭礼均具政治意义。
religion.en: Religion combined worship of the Greek gods, local Anatolian deities, ancestral cults, and royal ritual. Zeus, Apollo, Artemis, and local mother-goddess traditions were honored, and both civic temples and royal ceremonies had political significance.

philosophy.zh: 思想上受希腊化世界影响，以实用政治伦理、宫廷修辞和城邦教育传统为主；斯多亚、犬儒与学院派思想通过希腊城市精英间接传播，但尚未形成国家意识形态。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by the Hellenistic world, emphasizing practical political ethics, court rhetoric, and civic educational traditions; Stoic, Cynic, and Academic ideas circulated indirectly among Greek urban elites, but no single philosophy formed a state ideology.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为希腊字母，用于行政、铭文、商业与外交；地方口语可能继续存在，但官方与城市文书以希腊文为主。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Greek alphabet, used for administration, inscriptions, commerce, and diplomacy; local spoken languages likely persisted, but official and urban documentation was predominantly in Greek.

culturalAchievements.zh: 比提尼亚的文化成就在于将黑海沿岸希腊城市文化、安纳托利亚地方传统与王权宫廷风格结合。港口城市建筑、碑铭、祭祀空间、贵族墓葬和军事道路建设体现了这种混合文化面貌。
culturalAchievements.en: Bithynia's cultural achievement lay in blending Black Sea Greek urban culture, Anatolian local traditions, and royal court style. Harbor-city architecture, inscriptions, sacred spaces, elite tombs, and military road building reflected this mixed cultural profile.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与高文化语言属印欧语系希腊语；地方居民可能使用色雷斯—比提尼亚相关语言及安纳托利亚诸方言
languageFamily.en: The official and high-cultural language was Greek, an Indo-European language; local populations may also have spoken Thraco-Bithynian related languages and Anatolian dialects

gdpDescription.zh: 比提尼亚经济规模在安纳托利亚诸中等王国中属中上，依赖农业、畜牧、林木、渔业、港口贸易和黑海航线转运。由于地处海峡与黑海门户附近，其商业价值高于其人口规模所显示的水平。
gdpDescription.en: Bithynia's economy ranked in the upper-middle range among the medium-sized kingdoms of Anatolia, relying on agriculture, herding, timber, fishing, port trade, and Black Sea transit routes. Its position near the straits and the Black Sea gateway gave it a commercial importance greater than its population alone would suggest.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄、牧畜、木材采伐、造船材料供应、渔盐业、陶器、金属加工、港口仓储与转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, vines and olives, animal husbandry, timber extraction, naval materials supply, fisheries and salt, pottery, metalworking, harbor storage, and re-export trade

tradeGoods.zh: 输出木材、船桅材、谷物、牲畜、皮革、鱼制品、树脂、陶器与地方金属制品；输入葡萄酒、精制陶器、奢侈纺织品、银币、军械、橄榄油及希腊化世界工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included timber, mast-wood, grain, livestock, leather, fish products, resin, pottery, and local metal goods; imports included wine, fine ceramics, luxury textiles, silver coinage, arms, olive oil, and Hellenistic craft goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距明显。沿海商人、船主、城市地主和王室贵族积累了较多银币、奴隶和土地；多数乡村家庭拥有小块土地、牲畜和有限储粮，抗灾能力较弱。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Coastal merchants, shipowners, urban landholders, and royal nobles accumulated notable amounts of coin, slaves, and land; most rural households held small plots, livestock, and limited grain reserves, leaving them vulnerable to bad harvests.

economicSystem.zh: 王室控制与市场交换并行的希腊化混合经济。国王直接掌握重要土地、林木、港口和战略贸易税源，而城市市场和私人商人主导日常商业活动。
economicSystem.en: A Hellenistic mixed economy combining royal control and market exchange. The king directly controlled key lands, timber resources, ports, and strategic tax streams, while city markets and private merchants dominated everyday commerce.

source.en: Harbor dues, customs, and market taxes

source.zh: 王室地产、林木与矿产收入
source.en: Royal estates, timber, and mineral income

source.zh: 附属地区贡赋与盟城贡献
source.en: Tribute from dependent districts and contributions from allied cities
Confiscations, judicial income, and miscellaneous receipts

category.en: Court, royal household, and diplomatic gifts
Religious rites, urban subsidies, and miscellaneous spending

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库保持稳健但并不奢侈，主要以银币、贵金属器物、储粮、战备用牲畜与可动员的港口税源构成。其财政足以应对短期战争或外交贿赠，但难以支撑长期大规模会战。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was sound but not lavish, consisting mainly of silver coin, precious metal vessels, stored grain, draft animals for war, and readily mobilizable harbor revenues. It was sufficient for short wars or diplomatic payments, but not for sustaining prolonged large-scale campaigns.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，重视维持军队、笼络城市精英和积蓄应急储备。王权倾向通过贸易税与王室地产扩充收入，而非对乡村人口施加过度直接税负，以避免激起地方不满。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, prioritizing army maintenance, the loyalty of city elites, and emergency reserves. The monarchy preferred to expand revenue through trade duties and royal estates rather than imposing excessive direct burdens on the rural population, which might provoke local unrest.

description.zh: 以长矛步兵、盾兵和部分希腊化重装步兵为核心，负责会战与守城。
description.en: Centered on spearmen, shield-bearing infantry, and some Hellenistic heavy infantry, serving in battle lines and fortress defense.

description.zh: 由贵族骑兵、王室近卫骑兵和地方骑乘战士组成，用于侦察、侧翼与追击。
description.en: Composed of noble cavalry, royal guard horsemen, and local mounted warriors, used for reconnaissance, flanking, and pursuit.

description.zh: 驻守港口、山口和战略城镇，维持秩序并压制叛乱。
description.en: Stationed in ports, passes, and strategic towns to maintain order and suppress revolt.

description.zh: 包括投枪兵、弓箭手、山地兵和地方附庸部队，适合复杂地形作战。
description.en: Included javelin-men, archers, hill fighters, and local auxiliary forces suited to rough terrain.

technology.zh: 军备属于典型希腊化安纳托利亚水平，使用长矛、短剑、盾牌、复合或简单弓、投枪、攻城器械和防御工事。海军规模有限，主要依靠征用商船与港口资源，而非大规模常备舰队。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a typical Hellenistic Anatolian level, using spears, short swords, shields, composite or simple bows, javelins, siege engines, and fortifications. Naval capacity was limited and depended more on requisitioned merchant craft and port resources than on a large permanent fleet.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自本都、帕加马、卡帕多西亚及塞琉古势力在小亚细亚西北部的影响竞争，也包括黑海沿岸城市忠诚不稳、内陆贵族叛离和海峡贸易路线受扰。与更强大的希腊化大国相比，比提尼亚必须依靠地形、外交和机动作战求存。
threats.en: Major threats came from rivalry with Pontus, Pergamon, Cappadocia, and the wider influence of Seleucid power in northwestern Asia Minor, as well as uncertain loyalty in Black Sea coastal cities, inland noble defection, and disruption of straits trade routes. Against stronger Hellenistic powers, Bithynia had to rely on terrain, diplomacy, and mobile warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约六十万，分布于黑海与普罗πον提斯沿岸城市、河谷农业区、丘陵村落与内陆贵族领地。人口构成混合，包含希腊城市居民、比提尼亚土著、安纳托利亚人群及少量移民与奴隶。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 600,000, distributed across Black Sea and Propontis coastal towns, river-valley farming districts, hill villages, and inland noble estates. It was ethnically mixed, including Greek urban residents, native Bithynians, other Anatolian groups, and smaller numbers of migrants and slaves.

name.zh: 卡尔刻冬对岸诸王室港镇

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、军事贵族、城市富商与大地主；中层为城镇公民、船主、手工业者与较富裕农民；下层为佃农、小农、雇工、港口劳工和奴隶。不同地区的身份标准不一，沿海更偏城邦公民制，内陆更偏领主依附关系。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, military nobles, urban merchants, and great landholders; the middle strata included townsmen, shipowners, craftsmen, and better-off farmers; the lower strata comprised tenants, smallholders, laborers, dockworkers, and slaves. Standards of status differed by region, with the coast leaning toward polis citizenship and the interior toward lord-dependent relations.

allies.zh: 无固定长期盟友，外交上通常与部分黑海希腊城市、反本都或反帕加马势力保持临时合作
allies.en: No fixed long-term allies; diplomatically it often maintained temporary cooperation with some Black Sea Greek cities and powers opposed to Pontus or Pergamon

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手主要为本都、帕加马及在安纳托利亚角逐影响力的其他希腊化王国；必要时也警惕塞琉古干预
enemies.en: Its principal potential enemies were Pontus, Pergamon, and other Hellenistic kingdoms competing for influence in Anatolia; Seleucid intervention was also a concern when relevant

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以平衡周边列强、控制沿海城市、确保海峡与黑海贸易安全为核心。比提尼亚倾向于在更大强权之间灵活结盟，通过婚姻、馈赠、边境冲突与有限战争维护自主地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on balancing neighboring powers, controlling coastal cities, and securing trade through the straits and Black Sea. Bithynia tended to maneuver flexibly among larger states, using marriage ties, gifts, border conflict, and limited war to preserve its autonomy.

era.zh: 希腊化时代前中期，处于秦汉与地中海希腊化诸国并存时期
era.en: Middle Hellenistic era, contemporary with the Qin-Han transition and the Hellenistic Mediterranean states

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术并非原创突破，而是对希腊化军事、筑城、造币、港口经营和行政书写技术的吸收运用；山地要塞、道路节点和港口仓储管理是其最实用的技术优势。
keyInnovations.en: Its key advances were not original inventions but the adoption of Hellenistic military methods, fortification, coinage, harbor management, and administrative writing; hill forts, road nodes, and port storage management were its most practical technological strengths.

strengths.zh: 比提尼亚的优势在于地理位置优越，靠近海峡与黑海贸易线；王权较稳固，能在沿海城市与内陆贵族之间维持平衡；经济虽不庞大，但港口税与木材资源赋予其相当的战略价值。
strengths.en: Bithynia's strengths lay in its favorable geography near the straits and Black Sea trade routes; a relatively stable monarchy able to balance coastal cities and inland nobles; and an economy that, while not huge, derived strategic weight from harbor dues and timber resources.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于人口与财政规模有限，难与大型希腊化帝国长期抗衡；国家结构带有拼合性，沿海城市、地方贵族与王室之间始终存在利益张力；军事上偏重防御和局部作战，远征能力不足。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were limited population and finances, making prolonged competition with major Hellenistic empires difficult; a composite state structure with persistent tensions among coastal cities, local nobles, and the crown; and a military better suited to defense and local warfare than sustained expeditionary campaigns.

outlook.zh: 221年前后的比提尼亚总体稳定，若能继续利用列强对立并巩固王室对港口与内陆的控制，短期内可保持独立并扩大地区影响；但从长远看，其命运仍将取决于本都、帕加马、塞琉古以及后来更西方强权的竞争格局。
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE Bithynia was broadly stable, and if it continued exploiting rivalries among larger powers while strengthening royal control over ports and the interior, it could preserve independence and expand regional influence in the short term; in the long run, however, its fate would depend on the competition among Pontus, Pergamon, the Seleucids, and eventually stronger western powers.

description.zh: 西北安纳托利亚的比提尼亚是黑海与海峡地带的重要王国。
description.en: Bithynia in northwestern Anatolia was an important kingdom near the Black Sea and Straits.

ruler.zh: 托勒密四世·菲洛帕托尔

governmentForm.zh: 希腊化君主制下的法老国家，由马其顿希腊王室统治埃及本土与海外属地
governmentForm.en: A Hellenistic monarchy organized as a pharaonic state, ruled by a Macedonian Greek royal house over Egypt and overseas possessions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷贵族、希腊马其顿军政精英、埃及祭司集团、城市公民、驻军殖民者、村社农民、工匠、商人及奴隶构成；族群与法律身份差异明显
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal household and court nobility, Greek and Macedonian military-administrative elites, Egyptian priesthoods, urban citizens, military settlers, village peasants, artisans, merchants, and slaves; ethnic and legal distinctions remained pronounced

rulingClass.zh: 以托勒密王室、宫廷官僚、希腊马其顿将领及获得王室支持的高等祭司为核心
rulingClass.en: The ruling class centered on the Ptolemaic royal family, court officials, Greek-Macedonian commanders, and senior priesthoods supported by the crown

succession.zh: 王朝世袭，通常由托勒密家族内部继承，王位合法性依赖宫廷承认、军队支持与埃及宗教加冕
succession.en: Hereditary dynastic succession within the Ptolemaic house, with legitimacy dependent on court recognition, military backing, and Egyptian religious coronation

structure.zh: 中央集权程度很高，国王通过宫廷与书吏系统直接控制财政、土地、税收、军队、港口和王室垄断产业；地方由州区与村社官员执行政令
structure.en: Highly centralized, with the king governing through the court and a scribal bureaucracy that directly controlled finance, land, taxation, the army, ports, and royal monopolies; local directives were executed by regional and village officials

function.zh: 负责土地登记、税收征解、国库收入、王室垄断与预算调配
function.en: Managed land registration, tax assessment and collection, treasury income, royal monopolies, and budget allocation

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存希腊文与埃及文文书、统计人口与地产、监督地方呈报
function.en: Drafted decrees, maintained Greek and Egyptian records, compiled population and property data, and supervised local reporting

function.zh: 管理驻军、军饷、军屯、边防、舰队建造与海军基地
function.en: Administered garrisons, military pay, cleruchic settlements, frontier defense, fleet construction, and naval bases

function.zh: 处理王室法庭事务、城市治安、监禁、诉讼登记与混合法律案件
function.en: Handled royal court business, urban order, imprisonment, lawsuit registration, and cases involving mixed legal jurisdictions

function.zh: 监督尼罗河泛滥测量、堤坝运河维护、粮仓、种子配给与王田经营
function.en: Supervised Nile flood measurement, maintenance of dikes and canals, granaries, seed distribution, and crown estates

localAdmin.zh: 全国分为若干州区，州长、财政官、书记官与村长共同治理；法尤姆与三角洲等高产区管理尤其严密，王室通过希腊裔官员与本地神庙网络并行控制乡村
localAdmin.en: The kingdom was divided into regional districts governed by strategoi, finance officers, scribes, and village headmen; productive zones such as the Fayum and Delta were especially tightly managed, with the crown ruling the countryside through both Greek officials and local temple networks

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系并存希腊法、埃及习惯法与王室敕令；不同族群可在不同法庭诉讼，但涉及财政、王产与治安时王室司法具有优先权
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Greek law, Egyptian customary law, and royal decrees; different communities could litigate in different courts, but royal jurisdiction prevailed in matters involving taxation, crown property, and public order

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地税、灌溉相关征收、实物粮税、人头与职业税、关税、港口费和王室专卖收入为主，征税细密且依赖书面登记与包税并行
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on land tax, irrigation-related levies, grain in kind, poll and occupational taxes, customs duties, port charges, and revenue from royal monopolies, with highly detailed assessments based on written registration and some tax farming

religion.zh: 国家宗教融合传统埃及多神信仰与希腊宗教，崇拜伊西斯、欧西里斯、荷鲁斯、阿蒙，同时发展塞拉比斯与王室崇拜
religion.en: State religion fused traditional Egyptian polytheism with Greek religion, venerating Isis, Osiris, Horus, and Amun while promoting Serapis and the royal cult

philosophy.zh: 宫廷与城市文化以希腊哲学传统为主，包括柏拉图学派、逍遥学派与斯多亚思想；埃及祭司传统则延续宇宙秩序、神庙学问与祭礼解释
philosophy.en: Court and urban culture was rooted in Greek philosophical traditions, including Platonic, Peripatetic, and Stoic currents; Egyptian priestly learning preserved concepts of cosmic order, temple scholarship, and ritual interpretation

writingSystem.zh: 行政与高等文化主要使用希腊字母书写的通用希腊语；宗教与本地文书继续使用世俗体埃及文和圣书体
writingSystem.en: Administration and high culture mainly used Koine Greek written in the Greek alphabet; religious and local records continued in Demotic Egyptian and hieroglyphic scripts

culturalAchievements.zh: 亚历山大里亚仍是地中海最重要的学术与文学中心之一，继承图书馆、缪斯神庙、医学、数学、地理学和文献编校传统；埃及神庙建筑、双语诏令与宗教融合艺术高度发展
culturalAchievements.en: Alexandria remained one of the Mediterranean's foremost centers of scholarship and literature, sustaining the traditions of the Library, the Mouseion, medicine, mathematics, geography, and textual scholarship; Egyptian temple architecture, bilingual decrees, and syncretic religious art were highly developed

languageFamily.zh: 官方与城市上层语言属印欧语系希腊语支，本地多数人口使用亚非语系埃及语晚期形式
languageFamily.en: The official and urban elite language belonged to the Indo-European Greek branch, while most of the native population spoke late forms of Egyptian within the Afroasiatic family

gdpDescription.zh: 托勒密埃及是当时最富庶的国家之一，经济依赖尼罗河农业剩余、亚历山大里亚港口贸易、纸草与油类专卖、纺织、玻璃、金属加工及地中海航运，国家对经济活动干预极强
gdpDescription.en: Ptolemaic Egypt was among the wealthiest states of its age, drawing strength from Nile agricultural surplus, the port trade of Alexandria, monopolies in papyrus and oils, textiles, glass, metalworking, and Mediterranean shipping, with exceptionally strong state intervention in economic life

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、亚麻与纺织、纸草制造、葡萄酒与油类加工、玻璃、陶器、造船、金属加工、香料与奢侈品转口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, flax and textiles, papyrus production, wine and oil processing, glassmaking, pottery, shipbuilding, metalworking, and entrepôt trade in spices and luxury goods

tradeGoods.zh: 出口谷物、纸草、亚麻布、玻璃器、埃及麻布、酒、油和部分金属制品；进口木材、战马、银、香料、乳香、没药、象牙、珍贵石料与爱琴海和近东手工业品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, papyrus, linen, glassware, Egyptian cloth, wine, oils, and some metal goods; imports included timber, warhorses, silver, spices, frankincense, myrrh, ivory, precious stones, and manufactured goods from the Aegean and Near East

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大；希腊裔城市精英、军屯地主、王室承包人和神庙机构拥有显著财富，而多数农户仅能维持温饱并承担重税与劳役
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal; Greek urban elites, military settlers, royal contractors, and temple institutions held substantial assets, while most peasant households lived near subsistence under heavy taxation and labor obligations

economicSystem.zh: 王室主导的混合经济，土地、税收、矿产、盐、油、纸草及部分制造业受国家直接控制或严密许可，市场交换在城市和港口高度活跃
economicSystem.en: A crown-directed mixed economy in which land, taxation, minerals, salt, oil, papyrus, and parts of manufacturing were directly controlled or tightly licensed by the state, while market exchange remained highly active in cities and ports

source.zh: 关税、港口税与贸易收费
source.en: Customs, harbor dues, and trade charges
Poll taxes, occupational taxes, and miscellaneous levies
Tributary income, temple contributions, and confiscations

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库充实，得益于长期积累的农业税收、贸易收益与较成熟的征收体系；但宫廷奢侈、雇佣军成本和大战准备会迅速消耗储备
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was substantial thanks to long-accumulated agricultural taxes, commercial income, and a mature revenue apparatus; however, court luxury, mercenary costs, and major war preparations could rapidly drain reserves

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王室垄断、高强度征税、储粮和货币征收并行为核心，在稳定年份追求结余以支持舰队、驻军和外交支出
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on preserving royal monopolies, maintaining intensive taxation, and combining grain and monetary collection, aiming in stable years to generate surpluses that supported the fleet, garrisons, and diplomacy

name.zh: 马其顿式步兵与方阵部队

description.zh: 核心重装步兵，装备长枪与大盾，兼有希腊裔定居军与受训埃及士兵，是会战主力
description.en: Core heavy infantry equipped with sarissas and large shields, including Greek settler troops and trained Egyptian soldiers, forming the main battle line

description.zh: 由投石手、标枪兵、弓箭手和地方守备队组成，用于侦察、骚扰、守城和河谷警备
description.en: Consisted of slingers, javelin men, archers, and local garrison units used for scouting, skirmishing, siege defense, and Nile valley security

description.zh: 包括希腊与外籍骑兵，承担侧翼掩护、追击和边境机动任务；战车已属象征性或有限使用
description.en: Included Greek and foreign cavalry for flanking, pursuit, and frontier mobility; chariots survived only in symbolic or very limited use

description.zh: 维持东地中海重要海军力量，负责亚历山大里亚、塞浦路斯及近海航道防卫，并可投送陆战部队
description.en: Maintained a major eastern Mediterranean naval force responsible for Alexandria, Cyprus, and nearby sea lanes, and capable of transporting troops for amphibious operations

description.zh: 驻守叙利亚方向、利比亚边境、上埃及与红海通道，在战时可迅速扩编
description.en: Stationed toward Syria, the Libyan frontier, Upper Egypt, and Red Sea routes, and capable of rapid wartime expansion

commanderInChief.zh: 托勒密四世·菲洛帕托尔

technology.zh: 军制属成熟希腊化水平，使用长枪方阵、复合弓、投石器、攻城器械、多列桨战舰与坚固港防设施；海军工程和围城技术尤为先进
technology.en: Military technology was at a mature Hellenistic level, employing sarissa phalanxes, composite bows, artillery, siege engines, multi-banked warships, and strong harbor defenses; naval engineering and siegecraft were especially advanced

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自塞琉古帝国在叙利亚与腓尼基方向的竞争、爱琴海与小亚细亚势力变化、南方努比亚边境压力，以及国内对重税和宫廷政治的不满
threats.en: Primary threats came from Seleucid rivalry in Syria and Phoenicia, shifting power balances in the Aegean and Anatolia, pressure along the southern Nubian frontier, and domestic discontent over taxation and court politics

populationDescription.zh: 人口高度集中于尼罗河河谷与三角洲，绝大多数为埃及农村居民；亚历山大里亚与其他城市聚居大量希腊人、犹太人及来自东地中海各地的商人和工匠
populationDescription.en: Population was heavily concentrated in the Nile Valley and Delta, with the great majority being Egyptian rural inhabitants; Alexandria and other cities contained large Greek and Jewish communities as well as merchants and artisans from across the eastern Mediterranean

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、宫廷贵族、希腊裔官员、军官与大祭司；中层包括城市公民、商人、工匠、神庙管理者和退役军屯地主；下层为多数农民、佃户、劳工、河运工与奴隶
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal family, court nobility, Greek officials, officers, and high priests; the middle strata included urban citizens, merchants, artisans, temple administrators, and veteran military settlers; the lower strata comprised most peasants, tenants, laborers, rivermen, and slaves

allies.zh: 无牢固长期盟友，但与部分爱琴海城邦、岛屿势力及反塞琉古利益集团维持合作关系，并通过赠礼、婚姻传统和补贴影响希腊世界
allies.en: There were no fully secure long-term allies, but the kingdom maintained cooperative ties with certain Aegean cities, island powers, and anti-Seleucid interests, influencing the Greek world through gifts, old marriage diplomacy, and subsidies

enemies.zh: 主要对手是塞琉古帝国；在南部与库施关系时紧时缓，在东地中海也需防备马其顿、部分小亚细亚王国及海上竞争者的政策变化
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the Seleucid Empire; relations with Kush in the south fluctuated, and in the eastern Mediterranean it also had to watch shifts in the policies of Macedon, some Anatolian kingdoms, and maritime competitors

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维护科埃勒叙利亚利益、保障塞浦路斯与海上航线、保持亚历山大里亚商业优势、阻止塞琉古势力南下为核心，偏好以舰队威慑、金钱外交和有限干预结合
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on defending interests in Coele-Syria, securing Cyprus and sea lanes, preserving Alexandria's commercial supremacy, and preventing further Seleucid expansion southward, combining naval deterrence, financial diplomacy, and limited intervention

era.zh: 成熟希腊化时代
era.en: Mature Hellenistic Age

keyInnovations.zh: 高水平港口与灯塔工程、纸草与书籍生产、医学研究、天文学与地理测量、复杂税务档案系统、灌溉与水利管理、重型战舰和攻城机械
keyInnovations.en: Advanced harbor and lighthouse engineering, papyrus and book production, medical research, astronomy and geographic measurement, complex fiscal record-keeping, irrigation and water management, and heavy warships and siege machinery

strengths.zh: 尼罗河农业极其富庶，税基稳定；亚历山大里亚是世界级港口与学术中心；官僚体系发达；舰队强大，能够影响东地中海战略格局；国库与粮仓储备优于多数同时代国家
strengths.en: The Nile agricultural base was exceptionally rich and stable; Alexandria was a world-class port and intellectual center; bureaucracy was highly developed; the fleet was powerful enough to shape the eastern Mediterranean balance; and treasury and grain reserves surpassed those of most contemporary states

weaknesses.zh: 王朝政治常受宫廷斗争、君主个人能力与近臣专权影响；社会分层和族群差异深刻；财政压榨容易激起地方不满；陆军质量与忠诚度在长期和平和奢靡统治下可能下降
weaknesses.en: Dynastic politics were often undermined by court intrigue, the personal quality of the monarch, and powerful favorites; social stratification and ethnic divides were deep; fiscal pressure could provoke local resentment; and army quality and loyalty could decline under prolonged peace and luxurious rule

outlook.zh: 221年前后王国总体稳定且仍属地中海顶级强国，但其长期前景取决于能否维持财政纪律、整合本土埃及人口并在即将到来的对塞琉古冲突中守住叙利亚方向
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE the kingdom was broadly stable and still ranked among the Mediterranean's great powers, but its longer-term prospects depended on preserving fiscal discipline, integrating the native Egyptian population, and holding the Syrian front in the coming struggle with the Seleucids

description.zh: 托勒密埃及控制尼罗河流域，并维持东地中海的强大海上影响力。
description.en: Ptolemaic Egypt controlled the Nile valley and maintained major naval influence in the eastern Mediterranean.

leader.zh: 托勒密四世·菲洛帕托尔

ideology.zh: 希腊化君主专制，王室崇拜与法老神权并行，中央财政垄断，地中海海权与领土扩张
ideology.en: Hellenistic royal absolutism, combining royal cult with pharaonic divine kingship, centralized fiscal monopoly, Mediterranean naval power and territorial expansion

relationship.zh: 托勒密王朝是埃及的绝对统治者，以马其顿希腊王室身份兼任法老，掌控国家全部军事、财政与外交权力。托勒密四世继位时王朝仍然富庶强盛，但他本人沉迷享乐、疏于政务，将大权委托给宠臣索西比乌斯和阿加托克勒斯，开启了王朝衰落的序幕。尽管如此，王室仍通过庞大的官僚体系和军屯制度维持对尼罗河流域的有效控制。
relationship.en: The Ptolemaic dynasty was the absolute ruler of Egypt, holding the dual identity of Macedonian Greek royalty and pharaoh, commanding all military, fiscal, and diplomatic power. When Ptolemy IV succeeded to the throne, the dynasty remained wealthy and powerful, but his personal indulgence and negligence of statecraft led him to delegate authority to favorites Sosibios and Agathocles, inaugurating the dynasty's decline. Nevertheless, the royal house maintained effective control of the Nile valley through its massive bureaucracy and cleruchic military settlement system.

description.zh: 托勒密王朝由亚历山大大帝的将领托勒密一世·索特尔于前305年正式建立，至前221年已传四代。王朝融合了马其顿军事传统与埃及法老体制，建立了古代世界最精密的财政管理系统——从土地登记、谷物征收到纸草和油类专卖，国家对经济生活的干预无所不至。亚历山大里亚是地中海世界最宏伟的城市之一，拥有举世闻名的大图书馆和缪斯神庙（Mouseion），是希腊化文明的最高学术殿堂。托勒密三世时代是王朝的军事巅峰，一度攻入塞琉古帝国腹地。但托勒密四世即位后宫廷内斗加剧，他杀害了母亲和弟弟以巩固权位，却将实际统治交给索西比乌斯等宫廷权臣。拉菲亚战役（前217年）虽然击退了安条克三世的入侵，但战役中大规模使用埃及本地士兵的做法唤醒了本土埃及人的政治意识，为后来的上埃及叛乱埋下种子。
description.en: The Ptolemaic dynasty was formally established by Ptolemy I Soter, one of Alexander the Great's generals, in 305 BCE and had passed through four generations by 221 BCE. The dynasty fused Macedonian military traditions with the Egyptian pharaonic system, building the most sophisticated fiscal administration in the ancient world — from land registration and grain collection to papyrus and oil monopolies, the state intervened in every aspect of economic life. Alexandria was one of the Mediterranean's grandest cities, home to the renowned Great Library and Mouseion, the supreme academic institution of Hellenistic civilization. The reign of Ptolemy III marked the dynasty's military zenith, briefly invading deep into the Seleucid heartland. But after Ptolemy IV's accession, court intrigue intensified — he killed his mother and brother to secure his position, yet delegated actual governance to courtiers like Sosibios. The Battle of Raphia (217 BCE) repelled Antiochus III's invasion, but the mass recruitment of native Egyptian soldiers for that battle awakened indigenous political consciousness, planting the seeds for later Upper Egyptian revolts.

role.zh: 在位君主，沉迷文艺与享乐，实际权力委托宠臣，拉菲亚战役的名义统帅
role.en: Reigning pharaoh, devoted to literature and pleasure, delegating real power to favorites, nominal commander at the Battle of Raphia

role.zh: 托勒密四世最有权势的宠臣，拉菲亚战役的实际策划者，宫廷阴谋的操纵者
role.en: The most powerful favorite of Ptolemy IV, the actual planner of the Battle of Raphia, and manipulator of court intrigues

ideology.zh: 维护传统埃及宗教信仰与祭祀制度，保存法老神权的埃及本土合法性，扩大神庙地产与免税特权
ideology.en: Preserving traditional Egyptian religious beliefs and ritual systems, maintaining the indigenous legitimacy of pharaonic divine kingship, expanding temple estates and tax exemptions

headquarters.zh: 孟菲斯 / 底比斯（卡纳克）

relationship.zh: 埃及祭司集团是托勒密王朝在本土社会中最重要的合作伙伴。王朝需要祭司为其提供法老身份的宗教合法性——加冕仪式、神庙铭文和王室崇拜均由祭司主持或参与。作为交换，王室向神庙慷慨捐赠土地、黄金和免税特权。这种互利关系在托勒密四世时期通过一系列诏令和法令得到进一步巩固，但双方之间始终存在文化和权力的微妙张力。
relationship.en: The Egyptian priestly establishment was the Ptolemaic dynasty's most important partner in indigenous society. The dynasty needed the priests to provide religious legitimacy for the pharaonic identity — coronation rites, temple inscriptions, and royal cult ceremonies were all conducted or assisted by priests. In exchange, the crown made generous donations of land, gold, and tax exemptions to the temples. This mutually beneficial relationship was further cemented under Ptolemy IV through a series of decrees and ordinances, though subtle tensions of culture and power always persisted between the two sides.

description.zh: 埃及祭司阶层是延续数千年的本土精英群体，在托勒密统治下仍保持着巨大的社会影响力和经济实力。孟菲斯、底比斯、埃德富和丹德拉等地的大神庙拥有广大的地产、粮仓和手工作坊，构成了独立于希腊化行政体系之外的本土权力网络。祭司不仅是宗教仪式的执行者，也是天文历法、医药知识和文字传承的守护者。托勒密王朝通过颁布双语诏令（如后来著名的罗塞塔石碑即属同类）来巩固与祭司阶层的联盟——国王以法老身份出现在神庙浮雕中，向众神献祭、修建神殿。然而祭司集团也有自己的议程：他们利用王室需要本土合法性的弱点，持续争取更多的免税特权和自治空间。在上埃及（底比斯地区），祭司的影响力尤其强大，这一地区后来成为本土叛乱的温床。祭司集团是连接希腊化王室与数百万埃及农民之间的关键桥梁，同时也是本土文化认同的核心守护者。
description.en: The Egyptian priestly class was an indigenous elite group with millennia of continuity, retaining immense social influence and economic power under Ptolemaic rule. Great temples at Memphis, Thebes, Edfu, and Dendera possessed vast estates, granaries, and workshops, constituting an indigenous power network independent of the Hellenistic administrative system. Priests were not only ritual performers but also guardians of astronomical, medical, and scribal knowledge. The Ptolemaic dynasty consolidated its alliance with the priesthood by issuing bilingual decrees (of the same type as the later famous Rosetta Stone) — the king appeared in temple reliefs as pharaoh, making offerings to the gods and constructing sanctuaries. Yet the priestly establishment had its own agenda: leveraging the crown's need for indigenous legitimacy, they continuously negotiated for greater tax exemptions and autonomous space. In Upper Egypt (the Theban region), priestly influence was especially potent, and this area later became a hotbed of indigenous revolt. The priesthood served as the critical bridge between the Hellenistic royal house and millions of Egyptian peasants, while also standing as the core guardian of native cultural identity.

role.zh: 埃及本土宗教权威的最高代表，主持法老加冕仪式，是王室与祭司联盟的关键人物
role.en: Supreme representative of Egyptian indigenous religious authority, presiding over pharaonic coronation, the key figure in the royal-priestly alliance

role.zh: 上埃及最具影响力的祭司集团，控制卡纳克神庙群，是南方本土势力的精神核心
role.en: The most influential priestly body in Upper Egypt, controlling the Karnak temple complex, the spiritual core of southern indigenous power

name.zh: 亚历山大里亚希腊精英

leader.zh: 亚历山大里亚市政显贵

ideology.zh: 希腊文化至上主义——维护希腊语文化圈的主导地位，亚历山大里亚作为希腊化世界学术与商业中心的地位，抵制本土埃及文化的政治抬头
ideology.en: Greek cultural supremacism — preserving the dominance of the Greek-speaking cultural sphere, Alexandria's status as the scholarly and commercial center of the Hellenistic world, and resisting the political rise of indigenous Egyptian culture

headquarters.zh: 亚历山大里亚（缪斯神庙 / 大图书馆）

relationship.zh: 亚历山大里亚的希腊精英与托勒密王室之间既有合作也有张力。他们是王国文化声望的创造者和商业繁荣的推动者，但也是最具独立精神和批判意识的社会群体。城市中的希腊人自视为文明的承载者，对王室向埃及本土祭司和文化做出的妥协深感不满。亚历山大里亚城市暴动在托勒密历史上并非罕见，希腊市民在遗产纠纷、粮食短缺或政治不满时常常诉诸街头暴力。
relationship.en: The Alexandrian Greek elite had a relationship with the Ptolemaic crown that combined cooperation and tension. They were the creators of the kingdom's cultural prestige and drivers of commercial prosperity, but also the most independently minded and critically aware social group. Greeks in the city regarded themselves as bearers of civilization and deeply resented royal concessions to native Egyptian priesthoods and culture. Urban riots in Alexandria were not uncommon in Ptolemaic history, with Greek citizens frequently resorting to street violence over inheritance disputes, grain shortages, or political grievances.

description.zh: 亚历山大里亚是托勒密埃及的首都和地中海世界最大的城市之一，拥有约五十万至一百万居民。城市中的希腊马其顿裔居民构成了一个特权阶层，他们在法律上享有希腊公民身份，与本土埃及人适用不同的法庭和法律体系。缪斯神庙和大图书馆是希腊化世界的学术圣殿，汇聚了欧几里得（几何学）、埃拉托斯特尼（地理学）、阿里斯塔克斯（天文学）等划时代学者的智慧遗产。亚历山大里亚的希腊商人控制着地中海贸易网络的东端枢纽，从红海-印度洋航线到尼罗河谷的粮食出口，这座城市是全球性贸易网络的关键节点。然而，希腊精英对埃及本土人口的文化优越感也造成了深刻的社会裂隙。随着托勒密四世在拉菲亚战役中大量动员埃及本地士兵，本土埃及人的政治自信上升，希腊精英感受到了前所未有的文化和政治威胁——这种族群张力将在此后数十年中持续加剧，最终成为王国内部不稳定的重要来源。
description.en: Alexandria was the capital of Ptolemaic Egypt and one of the Mediterranean's largest cities, with an estimated 500,000 to one million inhabitants. The Greek-Macedonian residents formed a privileged class, enjoying Greek civic legal status with separate courts and legal systems from native Egyptians. The Mouseion and Great Library were the academic sanctuaries of the Hellenistic world, housing the intellectual legacies of epoch-making scholars such as Euclid (geometry), Eratosthenes (geography), and Aristarchus (astronomy). Alexandrian Greek merchants controlled the eastern hub of Mediterranean trade networks, from the Red Sea-Indian Ocean route to Nile Valley grain exports, making the city a critical node in the global trade system. However, the Greek elite's cultural superiority complex toward the native Egyptian population also created a deep social fissure. As Ptolemy IV's mass mobilization of native Egyptian soldiers at Raphia boosted indigenous political confidence, the Greek elite felt an unprecedented cultural and political threat — this ethnic tension would intensify over the following decades and ultimately become a major source of internal instability for the kingdom.

title.zh: 亚历山大里亚图书馆馆长

role.zh: 希腊化时代最伟大的博学家之一，精确计算地球周长，代表亚历山大里亚学术巅峰
role.en: One of the greatest polymaths of the Hellenistic age, who accurately calculated Earth's circumference, representing the pinnacle of Alexandrian scholarship

name.zh: 亚历山大里亚商人行会

role.zh: 控制地中海东部与红海贸易网络的关键经济力量，是城市繁荣的基石
role.en: The key economic force controlling eastern Mediterranean and Red Sea trade networks, the foundation of the city's prosperity

governmentForm.zh: 以大祭司为核心、由祭司贵族与王朝家族主导的神权君主制
governmentForm.en: A theocratic monarchy centered on the High Priest, dominated by priestly aristocrats and the Hasmonean ruling house

socialStructure.zh: 社会以圣殿祭司集团、王朝军事精英、地方长老、自由农民、城市手工业者与商人为主，下有佃农、雇工与少量奴仆；犹地亚本土社群、撒马利亚人、以土买人和新并入地区居民共同构成国家人口。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the Temple priesthood, dynastic military elites, local elders, free peasants, urban artisans, and merchants, with tenant farmers, laborers, and a limited number of household slaves beneath them; the population included core Judeans, Samaritans, Idumaeans, and inhabitants of recently incorporated territories.

rulingClass.zh: 哈斯蒙尼家族、耶路撒冷祭司贵族、圣殿官员以及忠于统治者的军政贵族
rulingClass.en: The Hasmonean family, Jerusalem priestly aristocracy, Temple officials, and military-administrative notables loyal to the ruler

succession.zh: 名义上依王朝家族内部继承并与大祭司职位结合，实际由统治家族、祭司集团与军事实力共同决定
succession.en: Nominally hereditary within the ruling dynasty and linked to the high priesthood, but in practice shaped by family politics, priestly support, and military power

structure.zh: 中央政权以耶路撒冷圣殿与大祭司府为核心，统治者通过亲族、祭司长官、军队指挥官和地方总督治理全国；地方事务常由城镇长老与宗教法传统配合执行。
structure.en: The central state was organized around the Jerusalem Temple and the High Priest's court, with the ruler governing through relatives, priestly officers, military commanders, and local governors; local affairs were often administered in cooperation with town elders and religious legal tradition.

function.zh: 管理祭祀、圣殿财库、献祭秩序、节期与宗教人员编制
function.en: Managed sacrifice, the Temple treasury, ritual order, festivals, and religious personnel

function.zh: 负责诏令、档案、税册、外交文书与土地记录
function.en: Handled decrees, archives, tax registers, diplomatic correspondence, and land records

function.zh: 征收地税、关税、贡赋及管理国库收入
function.en: Collected land tax, customs duties, tribute, and managed state revenues

function.zh: 监督各地总督、要塞、城镇长老与新并入地区行政整合
function.en: Oversaw regional governors, fortresses, town elders, and the integration of newly annexed territories

function.zh: 负责征募、军粮、边防要塞、武器储备与行军调度
function.en: Managed recruitment, supplies, frontier forts, arms stores, and campaign logistics

function.zh: 协调宗教律法裁判、民事争端、地方审判与对异族地区的行政法处置
function.en: Coordinated religious law judgments, civil disputes, local courts, and administrative legal measures in non-Judean regions

localAdmin.zh: 地方以耶路撒冷直接控制的核心地区、若干军政总督辖区和附属城镇共同构成；耶利哥、撒马利亚、以土买等地由官员、驻军与地方精英协同管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration combined directly controlled core territories, several military-governor districts, and dependent towns; areas such as Jericho, Samaria, and Idumaea were managed through officials, garrisons, and cooperation with local elites.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以摩西律法与圣殿权威为根本，辅以王朝政令、地方习惯和针对新征服地区的行政措施；宗教、民事与部分刑事事务常交由祭司与长老裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was grounded in the Torah and Temple authority, supplemented by dynastic decrees, local custom, and administrative measures for newly conquered lands; religious, civil, and some criminal matters were often judged by priests and elders.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要来自农业税、什一奉献、贸易关税、市场征费、征服地贡赋以及圣殿相关收入；核心犹太地区的宗教性征收与国家财政紧密相连。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came mainly from agricultural taxes, tithes, trade customs, market dues, tribute from conquered lands, and Temple-related income; in the Judean heartland, religious levies were closely intertwined with public finance.

religion.zh: 以第二圣殿时期的犹太教为国教，耶路撒冷圣殿是祭祀、历法和民族身份的中心；国家扩张伴随对犹太教礼仪与律法秩序的强化。
religion.en: Second Temple Judaism served as the state religion, with the Jerusalem Temple as the center of sacrifice, calendar, and collective identity; state expansion was accompanied by stronger enforcement of Jewish ritual and legal norms.

philosophy.zh: 思想核心是律法、圣约、祖先传统与圣殿敬拜，同时受希腊化世界影响，社会内部出现重视祭司权威、律法解释与政治现实的不同倾向。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core lay in law, covenant, ancestral tradition, and Temple worship, while contact with the Hellenistic world fostered differing tendencies regarding priestly authority, legal interpretation, and political pragmatism.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用方形阿拉姆字母书写希伯来语与阿拉姆语，行政与商业场合中阿拉姆语尤为常见；部分外交和对外交流也会使用希腊语。
writingSystem.en: The main script was the square Aramaic script used for Hebrew and Aramaic, with Aramaic especially common in administration and commerce; Greek was also employed in some diplomatic and external contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 圣殿礼仪体系、经卷抄写传统、宗教法学发展、坚固城堡与宫殿建设、礼仪纯洁规范以及在希腊化环境中保持犹太身份认同，构成这一时期的主要文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements included the Temple ritual system, scribal transmission of sacred texts, development of religious legal learning, construction of strong fortresses and palatial sites, purity regulations, and the preservation of Jewish identity within a Hellenistic environment.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系为主，核心语言为希伯来语与阿拉姆语；希腊语在外交和部分城市上层中具有影响
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Semitic languages, especially Hebrew and Aramaic; Greek had influence in diplomacy and among segments of the urban elite

gdpDescription.zh: 犹太经济以集约农业、圣殿带动的再分配、地区贸易和征服地贡赋为支柱。以其人口规模而言，国家财政动员能力较强，耶路撒冷的宗教中心地位使财富和物资持续汇聚。
gdpDescription.en: Judea's economy rested on intensive agriculture, Temple-centered redistribution, regional trade, and tribute from conquered territories. For its population size, the state had strong fiscal mobilization, and Jerusalem's role as a religious center continually drew in wealth and goods.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、枣椰与香膏栽培、牧业、陶器、纺织、石工、金属加工、圣殿相关供给与区域商贸
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine and olive oil production, date and balsam cultivation, herding, pottery, textiles, stonework, metalworking, Temple supply industries, and regional commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 橄榄油、葡萄酒、谷物、盐、死海沥青、枣椰、香膏、羊毛织物、陶器、金属器，以及来自阿拉伯和地中海世界的香料、奢侈品与铸币银
tradeGoods.en: Olive oil, wine, grain, salt, Dead Sea bitumen, dates, balsam, wool textiles, pottery, metal goods, and imported spices, luxuries, and coined silver from Arabia and the Mediterranean world

name.zh: 银德拉克马与泰尔舍客勒并行
name.en: Silver drachmae and Tyrian shekels in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在土地、葡萄园、橄榄树、牲畜、储粮与家用器具。城市富裕阶层和祭司贵族拥有更高比例的金银器、地产与贸易资本。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in land, vineyards, olive groves, livestock, stored grain, and domestic goods. Urban wealthy groups and priestly aristocrats held larger shares of silverware, real estate, and trading capital.

economicSystem.zh: 这是一个以农业为基础、由圣殿与王朝共同汲取资源、兼具市场交换与贡赋再分配的混合经济体系。
economicSystem.en: This was a mixed economy based on agriculture, with resources extracted jointly by the Temple and dynasty, combining market exchange with tributary redistribution.

source.zh: 关税、市场税与商路收费
source.en: Customs, market taxes, and route tolls

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库与圣殿财库并非完全等同，但彼此联系紧密。耶路撒冷集中储存金银、谷物、献礼与战争掠获，使国家在危机时具有较强筹资能力。
treasuryDescription.en: The state treasury and Temple treasury were not entirely identical, but they were closely linked. Jerusalem concentrated silver, grain, votive wealth, and war booty, giving the state significant capacity to raise resources in emergencies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向谨慎积储与为扩张战争服务的动员体制，优先保障圣殿运转、军费和新征服地区的整合。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored careful accumulation and a mobilization system suited to expansion, prioritizing Temple operations, military expenditure, and the integration of newly conquered territories.

description.zh: 由核心犹太战士、地方征召兵和驻防部队构成，装备矛、盾、剑与投射兵器，是军队主力。
description.en: Composed of core Judean fighters, locally levied troops, and garrison soldiers, equipped with spears, shields, swords, and missile weapons; this was the main arm of the army.

description.zh: 包括投石手、弓兵和山地作战分队，适合丘陵、荒漠边缘和围城支援。
description.en: Included slingers, archers, and hill-country detachments suited for rough terrain, desert margins, and siege support.

description.zh: 数量较少，多用于侦察、追击、传令和边境巡逻，部分来自附属地区或同盟力量。
description.en: Relatively limited in number, cavalry served for reconnaissance, pursuit, communications, and frontier patrol, with some drawn from subordinate territories or allied contingents.

technology.zh: 军制融合本地犹太传统与希腊化战争方式，使用铁制兵器、攻城器械、城堡体系和地中海东部常见的战术编成，但重骑兵与大型专业攻城军仍逊于塞琉古或帕提亚强国。
technology.en: Military practice combined local Judean traditions with Hellenistic methods, using iron weapons, siege engines, fortress systems, and eastern Mediterranean tactical formations, though it remained weaker than Seleucid or Parthian great powers in heavy cavalry and large professional siege forces.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自塞琉古王国残余势力、与纳巴泰的边境摩擦、撒马利亚和以土买等新并入地区的不稳定，以及更大希腊化国家对黎凡特局势的干预。
threats.en: Main threats came from the remaining Seleucid power, border friction with Nabataea, instability in newly incorporated areas such as Samaria and Idumaea, and intervention by larger Hellenistic states in Levantine affairs.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于耶路撒冷及其周边高地、犹地亚乡村、耶利哥绿洲和若干新纳入地区。节期朝圣使首都人口具有明显季节性波动。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in Jerusalem and the surrounding hill country, the Judean countryside, the Jericho oasis, and several newly incorporated regions. Pilgrimage festivals caused major seasonal fluctuations in the capital's population.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为哈斯蒙尼家族、祭司贵族与大地主；中层包括地方长老、军官、书记、商人和手工业者；下层是自由农民、佃农、雇工和债务负担较重者。奴隶存在但并非社会主体。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of the Hasmonean dynasty, priestly aristocrats, and major landholders; the middle included local elders, officers, scribes, merchants, and artisans; the lower consisted of free peasants, tenants, laborers, and the debt-burdened. Slavery existed but was not the dominant social condition.

allies.zh: 犹太在221 BCE与罗马并无约翰·许尔卡努斯一世时期的正式成熟同盟体系；其外交更现实地依赖与部分地方势力的临时安排，并在大国之间维持自主。
allies.en: In 221 BCE Judea did not possess the later formal diplomatic profile associated with John Hyrcanus I; its diplomacy relied more on temporary arrangements with local powers and on preserving autonomy amid larger states.

enemies.zh: 主要对手是塞琉古王国及其地方支持者，同时与纳巴泰和周边城邦存在竞争与冲突。
enemies.en: Its principal adversary was the Seleucid Kingdom and its local supporters, while rivalry and conflict also existed with Nabataea and neighboring cities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以扩张安全纵深、控制要塞与商路、削弱塞琉古影响并整合周边居民为核心，兼顾在希腊化诸国夹缝中的生存与自主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on expanding strategic depth, controlling forts and trade routes, reducing Seleucid influence, and integrating surrounding populations, while preserving autonomy within the Hellenistic interstate system.

era.zh: 晚期铁器时代至希腊化时代黎凡特
era.en: Late Iron Age to Hellenistic Levant

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的石造防御工事、蓄水池与山地聚落工程、文书抄写体系、圣殿礼仪组织、葡萄酒与橄榄油压榨技术，以及采用希腊化攻城与铸币实践。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included advanced stone fortifications, cistern and hill-settlement engineering, scribal manuscript systems, Temple ritual organization, wine and olive pressing techniques, and adoption of Hellenistic siegecraft and minting practices.

strengths.zh: 国家拥有强烈的宗教凝聚力、以耶路撒冷为中心的财政和象征权威、适应山地作战的军队，以及在塞琉古衰弱背景下迅速扩张的机会。
strengths.en: The state possessed strong religious cohesion, fiscal and symbolic authority centered on Jerusalem, forces well suited to hill-country warfare, and opportunities for rapid expansion amid Seleucid weakness.

weaknesses.zh: 统治合法性高度依赖祭司权威与王朝声望，内部可能出现宗教与政治派系分化；国家人口和资源总量仍有限，对外长期承压于更强大的希腊化邻国。
weaknesses.en: Its legitimacy depended heavily on priestly authority and dynastic prestige, leaving room for internal religious and political factionalism; overall population and resources remained limited compared with stronger Hellenistic neighbors.

outlook.zh: 若能持续利用塞琉古衰退并稳固对撒马利亚、以土买和约旦河谷的控制，犹太有望继续扩张并成为南黎凡特的重要区域强权；但过度扩张与内部权力斗争也可能削弱其长期稳定。
outlook.en: If it could continue exploiting Seleucid decline and consolidate control over Samaria, Idumaea, and the Jordan Valley, Judea could develop into a major regional power in the southern Levant; however, overexpansion and internal struggles could undermine long-term stability.

description.zh: 哈斯蒙尼政权以耶路撒冷为中心扩张，是黎凡特重要的犹太国家。
description.en: Centered on Jerusalem, the Hasmonean state was expanding as a major Jewish polity in the Levant.

governmentForm.zh: 商路导向的部族君主制，王权与城市精英及部族领袖协商统治
governmentForm.en: A trade-oriented tribal monarchy in which royal authority governed in consultation with urban elites and tribal leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族与部族首领、商人阶层、定居农民、牧民、工匠、自由民与少量奴隶构成；绿洲居民与沙漠游牧群体之间联系紧密。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, nobles and tribal chiefs, merchant strata, settled farmers, pastoral nomads, artisans, free commoners, and a smaller enslaved population; oasis dwellers and desert pastoral groups were closely linked.

rulingClass.zh: 纳巴泰王室、佩特拉商人贵族、主要部族酋长与控制商道节点的地方首领
rulingClass.en: The Nabataean royal house, Petra’s merchant aristocracy, major tribal chieftains, and local leaders controlling caravan nodes

succession.zh: 大体为世袭君主制，通常在王族内部继承，但实际继位需获得王室成员、部族精英与商贸网络支持。
succession.en: Succession was broadly hereditary within the royal family, but effective accession depended on support from royal kin, tribal elites, and the commercial network.

structure.zh: 王国政体仍处于早期整合阶段。中央以佩特拉宫廷为核心，直接掌握税收、商路保护、外交与重要蓄水工程；外围地区通过附属部族、绿洲据点和商站间接统治。
structure.en: The kingdom was still in an early stage of political consolidation. The center was the court at Petra, which directly controlled taxation, caravan protection, diplomacy, and major water installations; outer regions were ruled indirectly through allied tribes, oasis posts, and trade stations.

function.zh: 负责王令发布、契约记录、外交文书、贸易许可与印玺管理
function.en: Handled royal decrees, contract records, diplomatic correspondence, trade permits, and seal administration

function.zh: 征收过境税、保护驼队、管理商路驿站与边境关卡
function.en: Collected transit duties, protected caravans, and managed route stations and frontier checkpoints

function.zh: 维护蓄水池、渠道、堤坝、泉井与城市供水系统
function.en: Maintained cisterns, channels, dams, wells, and urban water supply systems

function.zh: 管理贡赋、仓储、贵重物资、军费和宫廷支出
function.en: Managed revenues, storage, valuables, military payments, and court expenditures

function.zh: 协调部族义务、调停争端、安排护送与征召向导骑手
function.en: Coordinated tribal obligations, mediated disputes, arranged escorts, and mobilized guide-riders

function.zh: 管理要塞、小型驻军、巡逻队与沙漠警戒
function.en: Managed forts, small garrisons, patrol units, and desert surveillance

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以佩特拉、赫格拉及若干绿洲据点为核心，由王室任命的代理人、地方首领和商站管理者共同执行；许多沙漠地区更依赖盟属部族而非密集官僚统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on Petra, Hegra, and several oasis stations, implemented jointly by royal agents, local chiefs, and caravan-post managers; many desert areas relied more on allied tribes than on dense bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于阿拉伯部族习惯、商贸契约惯例与王室裁断，并受阿拉姆文书传统影响；涉及债务、货物损失、通行权与水源使用的争端尤为重要。
legalSystem.en: Law rested mainly on Arab tribal custom, commercial contract practice, and royal adjudication, with influence from Aramaic scribal traditions; disputes over debt, cargo loss, passage rights, and water usage were especially important.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以商队过境税、市场税、绿洲农业贡赋、牲畜税、井泉与水利使用费及附属部族贡献为主；相较纯农业国家，贸易税地位更高。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system relied on caravan transit duties, market taxes, oasis agricultural dues, livestock taxes, fees for wells and water installations, and contributions from subordinate tribes; compared with agrarian states, trade taxation was more important.

religion.zh: 纳巴泰宗教为多神信仰，崇拜杜沙拉、阿尔乌扎等神祇，并吸收阿拉伯北部、叙利亚和更广泛近东宗教元素；圣石、露天圣所和高地祭坛常见。
religion.en: Nabataean religion was polytheistic, venerating deities such as Dushara and al-'Uzza while absorbing elements from northern Arabian, Syrian, and wider Near Eastern cults; betyls, open-air sanctuaries, and high places were common.

philosophy.zh: 王国并无如希腊化世界那样成体系的哲学学派，思想生活更多体现为部族荣誉观、契约诚信、神圣庇护、实用商贸伦理以及对水与沙漠生存的深刻经验。
philosophy.en: The kingdom had no formal philosophical schools like those of the Hellenistic world; intellectual life was expressed more through tribal honor, contractual trust, sacred protection, practical mercantile ethics, and deep experience of water and desert survival.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉姆字母书写的早期纳巴泰文与通用阿拉姆文文书；口语属于古阿拉伯语支环境。
writingSystem.en: Writing primarily used early Nabataean script derived from the Aramaic alphabet alongside Imperial Aramaic documentary practice; spoken language belonged to an Old Arabic linguistic environment.

culturalAchievements.zh: 纳巴泰人的突出成就在于沙漠水利、岩石营建、商路组织、跨文化中介能力以及将绿洲、山谷与驼队网络整合为高效贸易体系。
culturalAchievements.en: Nabataean achievements lay especially in desert hydraulic engineering, rock-based construction, caravan organization, cross-cultural brokerage, and integrating oases, valleys, and camel networks into an efficient trade system.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系，口语偏古阿拉伯语，书面行政多用阿拉姆语传统
languageFamily.en: Semitic; spoken forms were Old Arabic-like, while written administration largely followed Aramaic tradition

gdpDescription.zh: 以人口规模而言，纳巴泰并非大型农业帝国，但因控制阿拉伯北部香料、乳香、没药、沥青、盐与奢侈品转运，其人均财富和商业活力高于许多同人口规模的内陆政体。
gdpDescription.en: By population, Nabataea was not a large agrarian empire, but by controlling northern Arabian transit in aromatics, frankincense, myrrh, bitumen, salt, and luxury goods, it likely enjoyed higher per-capita wealth and commercial dynamism than many inland polities of similar size.

mainIndustries.zh: 商队贸易、转口贸易、驼畜运输、绿洲农业、葡萄与果树种植、陶器、石工、蓄水工程、沥青与盐类贸易
mainIndustries.en: Caravan trade, entrepôt exchange, camel transport, oasis agriculture, viticulture and orchard cultivation, pottery, stoneworking, water engineering, and trade in bitumen and salts

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、香膏、香料、阿拉伯胶、沥青、盐、铜器、纺织品、酒、橄榄油、玻璃器、染料、皮革、牲畜及来自地中海与南阿拉伯的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, perfumes, spices, gum resins, bitumen, salt, copper goods, textiles, wine, olive oil, glassware, dyes, leather, livestock, and luxury items from the Mediterranean and South Arabia

unitName.zh: 德拉克马与地方银重当量

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富差异很大。绿洲农户拥有井水权、果园、牲畜和储粮；商人家庭常持有骆驼、仓货与债权；游牧家庭则以畜群与人际保护网络为主要财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Oasis farmers held water rights, orchards, livestock, and grain stores; merchant families often owned camels, warehoused goods, and credit claims; nomadic households relied chiefly on herds and patronage networks.

economicSystem.zh: 王室监管下的商路经济，兼具部族护运、市场交换、绿洲农业与区域转口贸易特征；国家不完全垄断商业，但通过关税、护商和节点控制取得巨大利益。
economicSystem.en: A route-regulated commercial economy combining tribal convoy protection, market exchange, oasis agriculture, and regional entrepôt trade; the state did not fully monopolize commerce but gained substantial profit through customs, protection, and control of key nodes.

source.zh: 王室地产、罚金与外交馈赠
source.en: Royal estates, fines, and diplomatic gifts

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室积蓄主要以银重、贵重货物、香料存货、牲畜资产和可迅速征调的商队资源形式存在，而非单纯大量铸币库存。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves existed mainly as silver by weight, valuable merchandise, aromatics inventories, livestock assets, and caravan resources that could be quickly mobilized, rather than purely as massive coin stocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守务实：优先维护商路安全和水利命脉，在繁荣时期积储金银与货物，同时通过减免、馈赠和契约保护来维持商人与部族合作。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic: priority went to maintaining route security and hydraulic lifelines, building reserves in prosperous years, and sustaining cooperation with merchants and tribes through remissions, gifts, and contract protection.

description.zh: 适合沙漠远程巡逻、护送商队、侦察水源与追击小股袭扰者，是纳巴泰最具地方特色的机动力量。
description.en: Suited for long-range desert patrols, caravan escort, reconnaissance of water sources, and pursuit of raiders; this was Nabataea’s most distinctive mobile arm.

description.zh: 由城镇居民、绿洲守军和部族战士组成，装备矛、短剑、盾与弓，用于守卫关卡、山道与仓储点。
description.en: Drawn from townsmen, oasis garrisons, and tribal fighters, equipped with spears, short swords, shields, and bows for guarding passes, checkpoints, and depots.

description.zh: 依托高地、峡谷和设防据点作战，擅长伏击与防御，适合佩特拉周边复杂地形。
description.en: Fought from heights, gorges, and fortified posts, excelling in ambush and defense in the rugged terrain around Petra.

description.zh: 战时由盟属部族提供，熟悉沙漠道路与季节迁徙路线，但忠诚度更依赖盟约与赏赐。
description.en: Provided by allied tribes in wartime; they knew desert tracks and seasonal routes well, but loyalty depended more on alliance and reward.

commanderInChief.zh: 国王或其指定的王族统帅
commanderInChief.en: The king or a designated royal commander

technology.zh: 军事技术总体属希腊化时代水平，但纳巴泰优势不在重型会战装备，而在骆驼运输、沙漠导航、设伏、山地据守和后勤耐力。
technology.en: Military technology was broadly at a Hellenistic-era level, but Nabataean strengths lay not in heavy battlefield equipment so much as in camel transport, desert navigation, ambush, hill defense, and logistical endurance.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括阿拉伯沙漠中的竞争部族与劫掠者、南北商路上的对手势力、犹地亚和科勒叙利亚方向的政治变化，以及塞琉古与托勒密争霸对边境贸易的间接冲击。
threats.en: Main threats included rival tribes and raiders in the Arabian desert, competing powers along the north-south trade routes, political changes in Judea and Coele-Syria, and the indirect disruption of frontier trade by Seleucid-Ptolemaic rivalry.

populationDescription.zh: 人口由佩特拉等城市居民、绿洲农业人口、半定居商旅社区和广泛的游牧及半游牧部族共同构成；人口分布受水源和商路节点强烈制约。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted of urban residents in Petra and other centers, oasis agricultural communities, semi-settled merchant groups, and extensive nomadic and semi-nomadic tribes; settlement patterns were strongly constrained by water sources and caravan nodes.

name.zh: 加沙商站网络中的纳巴泰聚居点
