name.en: Nabataean communities in the Gaza trade network

name.zh: 阿瓦拉与北部商路据点

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、贵族商人和部族酋长；中层为富裕商队经营者、工匠、果园主和地方管理者；下层为普通农民、牧民、脚夫、仆役与债役者。奴隶存在，但不是社会经济的主体劳动力。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata were the royal family, merchant nobles, and tribal chiefs; the middle strata included prosperous caravan operators, artisans, orchard owners, and local administrators; the lower strata comprised ordinary farmers, herders, porters, servants, and debt dependents. Slavery existed but was not the main basis of labor.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的正式大国同盟，更多依靠与周边阿拉伯部族、南阿拉伯商人网络及部分黎凡特城市势力的务实合作。
allies.en: There were no formal great-power alliances in a strict sense; Nabataea relied more on pragmatic cooperation with neighboring Arab tribes, South Arabian merchant networks, and some Levantine urban interests.

enemies.zh: 并无持续全面战争中的单一宿敌，但与竞争商路的部族集团、可能限制其北向贸易的塞琉古地方势力，以及与其争夺香料贸易收益的其他阿拉伯政体存在摩擦。
enemies.en: It had no single permanent archenemy in full-scale war, but faced friction with tribal groups competing over caravan routes, Seleucid local authorities that might restrict its northern trade, and other Arabian polities seeking shares of the aromatics trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以贸易优先、避免与大型希腊化国家正面冲突、稳固沙漠中继站和水源控制、通过馈赠与婚盟维系部族忠诚为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized trade, avoided direct confrontation with major Hellenistic states, sought to secure desert relay stations and water control, and maintained tribal loyalty through gifts and marriage alliances.

era.zh: 希腊化时代晚期的阿拉伯沙漠商路文明
era.en: A Late Hellenistic Arabian desert caravan civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 精密蓄水池与引水系统、山谷堤坝、岩壁开凿与储藏设施、骆驼商队后勤组织、长距离沙漠导航与商站网络管理
keyInnovations.en: Sophisticated cistern and water-channel systems, wadi dams, rock-cut and storage installations, camel-caravan logistics, long-distance desert navigation, and management of relay trading stations

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于掌握沙漠交通知识、控制乳香没药贸易中继、善于在强邻之间保持灵活外交，并能把稀缺水源转化为政治与经济力量。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were mastery of desert transport knowledge, control of the frankincense and myrrh transit trade, flexible diplomacy between stronger neighbors, and the ability to turn scarce water resources into political and economic power.

weaknesses.zh: 王权整合仍有限，行政体系不如农业帝国深入；财富高度依赖商路畅通与地区和平，一旦遭遇大国封锁、路线转移或连续旱灾，财政与人口承载都会受压。
weaknesses.en: Royal integration remained limited, and administration was less deep than that of agrarian empires; wealth depended heavily on open trade routes and regional peace, so great-power blockade, route diversion, or repeated drought could strain both finances and carrying capacity.

outlook.zh: 在前221年前后，纳巴泰处于上升阶段。只要能继续巩固佩特拉、维持与南北商队及部族的合作，并避开被塞琉古或埃及直接吞并，其未来仍有望进一步扩展为更成熟的区域贸易王国。
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE, Nabataea was in a rising phase. If it could continue to consolidate Petra, preserve cooperation with north-south caravans and tribal groups, and avoid direct absorption by the Seleucids or Egypt, it had good prospects of developing into a more mature regional trade kingdom.

description.zh: 纳巴泰人依托佩特拉与商道控制阿拉伯北部沙漠贸易。
description.en: The Nabataeans used Petra and caravan routes to dominate desert trade in northern Arabia.

name.zh: 萨巴、盖特班、哈德拉毛与希木叶尔诸政体
name.en: Saba, Qataban, Hadramawt, and Himyarite Polities

capital.zh: 马里卜及其他区域中心

governmentForm.zh: 分立王国体制，以灌溉城邦、部族联盟与商路控制为基础的君主政体
governmentForm.en: Fragmented monarchies based on irrigated urban centers, tribal alliances, and control of trade routes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族氏族、神庙阶层、商人、灌溉农业社群、自由部族民与奴仆构成；各王国之间既竞争又通过婚盟、贸易和宗教传统相互联系。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of royal houses, noble clans, temple elites, merchants, irrigated farming communities, free tribespeople, and dependents or slaves; the kingdoms competed with one another while remaining linked by marriage alliances, trade, and shared religious traditions.

rulingClass.zh: 各王国王室、部族贵族、灌溉工程控制者与大香料商人
rulingClass.en: Royal families, tribal aristocrats, controllers of irrigation works, and major incense merchants in each kingdom

succession.zh: 通常在王族内部世袭，但常需得到主要氏族、部落首领与地方精英承认
succession.en: Usually hereditary within royal houses, though effective succession often required recognition by major clans, tribal leaders, and local elites

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央国家，而是由萨巴、盖特班、哈德拉毛及早期希木叶尔势力分别治理。各国以王为核心，依靠宫廷、书记官、神庙管理者、地方城镇长官及部族首领维持统治，并通过对水利、城墙、商道驿站和关税点的控制取得权威。
structure.en: There was no single centralized state; rather, Saba, Qataban, Hadramawt, and early Himyarite power centers ruled separately. Each kingdom was centered on a king and relied on court officials, scribes, temple administrators, local urban authorities, and tribal chiefs, deriving authority from control of irrigation, fortifications, caravan stations, and customs points.

function.zh: 管理王室仪礼、王令传达、使节接待与宫廷档案
function.en: Managed royal ceremony, proclamation of decrees, reception of envoys, and court records

function.zh: 维护堤坝、水渠、蓄水设施与耕地分配，尤其在马里卜等灌溉核心区至关重要
function.en: Maintained dams, canals, water storage systems, and arable allocation, especially crucial in irrigation centers such as Marib

function.zh: 监管香料商队、征收过境税、保护驿站并协调与外商往来
function.en: Supervised incense caravans, collected transit dues, protected caravan stations, and coordinated dealings with foreign merchants

function.zh: 管理祭祀、神庙地产、奉献物与宗教节庆
function.en: Administered cult activity, temple lands, offerings, and religious festivals

name.zh: 地方城镇与部族联络署
name.en: Local Town and Tribal Liaison Office

function.zh: 协调城镇议事机构、地方长官与部族盟约，处理边缘地区忠诚与纠纷
function.en: Coordinated town councils, local governors, and tribal agreements, handling loyalty and disputes in peripheral areas

function.zh: 记录契约、裁决诉讼、起草碑铭与管理公私法律文本
function.en: Recorded contracts, adjudicated disputes, drafted inscriptions, and managed legal texts for public and private use

function.zh: 征收谷物、畜产、香料及工艺品，维持王室仓储与再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, aromatics, and manufactured goods, maintaining royal storage and redistribution

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托城镇中心、绿洲聚落和部族领地。王权在核心灌溉区较强，在边远高地和沙漠边缘地区则更多依靠盟约、贡赋与商道节点控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on towns, oasis settlements, and tribal territories. Royal power was strongest in core irrigated zones, while in uplands and desert margins it relied more on alliance, tribute, and control of caravan nodes.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断、部族协定和神庙见证契约为基础。财产、水权、商队责任、债务与婚姻关系是常见法律事务，铭文常用于固定捐献、建筑与司法决定。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, royal judgment, tribal agreements, and temple-witnessed contracts. Property, water rights, caravan liability, debt, and marriage relations were common legal matters, and inscriptions were often used to formalize donations, construction acts, and judicial decisions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源包括农产征收、香料与没药贸易关税、商队过路费、神庙奉献、附属部族贡赋及战利品。税赋通常以实物、香料、牲畜和金属支付，而非完全统一的货币税制。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from agricultural levies, customs on frankincense and myrrh, caravan tolls, temple offerings, tributary payments from subordinate tribes, and spoils. Taxes were commonly paid in kind, aromatics, livestock, and metal rather than through a fully uniform monetary tax system.

religion.zh: 南阿拉伯多神信仰占主导，各国崇拜月神、太阳神、阿萨塔尔等地方神灵，神庙与祭祀在王权合法性和公共工程中发挥重要作用。
religion.en: South Arabian polytheism predominated, with kingdoms venerating lunar deities, solar deities, Athtar, and other local gods; temples and cult practice played major roles in royal legitimacy and public works.

philosophy.zh: 没有形成如希腊或中国那样系统化的哲学学派，但政治伦理强调王权、氏族荣誉、契约、敬神和对水利与商路秩序的维护。
philosophy.en: No fully systematized philosophical schools comparable to those of Greece or China emerged, but political ethics emphasized kingship, clan honor, contractual obligation, piety, and maintenance of irrigation and trade order.

writingSystem.zh: 使用古南阿拉伯字母书写萨巴语、盖特班语、哈德拉毛语及相关方言，常见于石刻、献辞、建筑铭文和官方记录。
writingSystem.en: Ancient South Arabian script was used to write Sabaic, Qatabanic, Hadramitic, and related dialects, commonly appearing in stone inscriptions, dedications, building texts, and official records.

culturalAchievements.zh: 重要成就包括马里卜大坝等高水平灌溉工程、石刻铭文传统、香料贸易组织能力、精致石建筑与对阿拉伯南端商路网络的长期经营。
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included advanced irrigation engineering such as the Marib Dam, a rich inscriptional tradition, sophisticated organization of the incense trade, refined stone architecture, and long-term management of the trade networks of southern Arabia.

languageFamily.zh: 闪米特语系南闪米特分支
languageFamily.en: Semitic, South Semitic branch

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济建立在灌溉农业、畜牧、区域手工业以及连接印度洋、阿拉伯内陆与地中海东方市场的香料贸易之上。其财富密度在阿拉伯半岛属较高水平，但资源和收入在不同王国与商路节点间分布极不均衡。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on irrigated agriculture, pastoralism, regional crafts, and the incense trade linking the Indian Ocean, inland Arabia, and eastern Mediterranean markets. Its wealth density was high by Arabian standards, but resources and income were distributed very unevenly among kingdoms and trade nodes.

mainIndustries.zh: 乳香与没药贸易、灌溉农业、葡萄与椰枣种植、畜牧、石工建筑、陶器、金属加工与商队服务
mainIndustries.en: Frankincense and myrrh trade, irrigated agriculture, grape and date cultivation, pastoralism, stone construction, pottery, metalworking, and caravan services

tradeGoods.zh: 乳香、没药、芳香树脂、香膏、葡萄酒、椰枣、皮革、牲畜、盐、石材制品，以及来自更远地区转运的印度洋货物
tradeGoods.en: Frankincense, myrrh, aromatic resins, perfumes, wine, dates, leather, livestock, salt, stone products, and transshipped Indian Ocean goods from more distant regions

name.zh: 称量银与外来钱币并用

householdWealth.zh: 富裕集中于王室、神庙、商队经营者和灌溉核心区地主。普通家庭财富主要表现为耕地用益权、水权、牲畜、储粮与氏族网络，而非大量铸币储蓄。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was concentrated in royal houses, temples, caravan operators, and landholders in irrigated cores. Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, water rights, livestock, stored grain, and clan ties rather than large stores of coin.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室—部族—商人合作为特征的贸易导向型再分配经济，兼具灌溉农业基础、关税收入和地方交换网络
economicSystem.en: A trade-oriented redistributive economy characterized by cooperation among royal houses, tribes, and merchants, combining an irrigated agricultural base with customs revenue and local exchange networks

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Royal estates, workshops, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备分散于数个王国的宫廷仓库、神庙宝藏、香料储备和贵金属称量储藏中，不是单一国库。财富同时以树脂、粮食、牲畜和盟友义务等非金属形式保存。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were dispersed across several kingdoms in court storehouses, temple treasuries, aromatic stockpiles, and weighed precious metal holdings rather than a single national treasury. Wealth was also preserved in resin stocks, grain, livestock, and obligations from allies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持水利系统、保障商路安全并通过关税与贡赋扩大王室资源；在商路受阻时，国家更依赖农业征收与部族忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining irrigation systems, securing trade routes, and expanding royal resources through customs and tribute; when trade was disrupted, governments relied more heavily on agricultural levies and tribal loyalty.

description.zh: 由王室亲兵、城防守军和受雇步兵组成，负责守卫王都、仓储、神庙和灌溉设施
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, garrison troops, and hired infantry, tasked with guarding capitals, storehouses, temples, and irrigation works

description.zh: 战时由各部族提供，以矛、盾、弓和短刃为主，适合护送商队与进行地方冲突
description.en: Provided by tribes in wartime, mainly armed with spears, shields, bows, and short blades, suitable for caravan protection and local conflict

description.zh: 以骆驼和部分马匹实现快速机动，用于侦察、商路巡逻、追击与沙漠联络
description.en: Used camels and some horses for rapid movement, serving reconnaissance, trade-route patrol, pursuit, and desert communications

description.zh: 驻守关口、谷地入口和税卡，维持对交通与山地通道的控制
description.en: Stationed at passes, wadi entrances, and customs posts to maintain control over movement and upland routes

commanderInChief.en: Each king served as commander-in-chief of his own realm

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁制兵器、弓矛、城墙防御、塔楼、骆驼运输与旱区后勤为核心，不具备地中海列国那样的大型攻城体系，但在山地与沙漠环境中适应性较强。
technology.en: Military technology centered on iron weapons, bows and spears, wall defenses, towers, camel transport, and arid-zone logistics. It lacked the large-scale siege systems of major Mediterranean powers but was well adapted to mountain and desert environments.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自王国间竞争、部族叛离、商路争夺、沙漠游动势力袭扰，以及北方阿拉伯中介贸易者和海上航线变动造成的经济压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from rivalry among the kingdoms, tribal defections, competition over trade routes, raiding by mobile desert groups, and economic pressure from northern Arabian intermediaries and changing maritime routes.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口分布于也门高地、灌溉谷地、沿海地带和内陆商道节点，人口集中在可稳定供水的农业区与商贸城镇。诸王国总人口合计约一百二十万属合理估计，但各国间差异明显。
populationDescription.en: The total population was distributed across the Yemeni highlands, irrigated valleys, coastal zones, and inland trade nodes, with concentration in reliably watered agricultural zones and commercial towns. A combined population of about 1.2 million for these kingdoms is plausible, though unevenly divided among them.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室和氏族贵族；其次为神庙祭司、书记、富商和灌溉区地主；中层为手工业者、小商人、自由农民和牧民；下层包括债役者、战俘奴仆与依附劳工。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata consisted of royal and clan aristocracies; below them stood temple priests, scribes, wealthy merchants, and landholders in irrigated districts; middle ranks included artisans, small traders, free farmers, and pastoralists; lower strata included debt dependents, war captives, and servile laborers.

allies.zh: 与阿拉伯南部及西南部若干部族、红海对岸的非洲沿岸社群、印度洋商人团体保持务实合作；各王国之间亦常通过婚盟与临时协约协调贸易。
allies.en: They maintained pragmatic cooperation with various southern and southwestern Arabian tribes, coastal communities across the Red Sea in Africa, and Indian Ocean merchant groups; the kingdoms also frequently coordinated trade through marriages and temporary agreements among themselves.

enemies.zh: 彼此之间常互为竞争者；同时与争夺乳香商路的北阿拉伯势力、部分沙漠部族和试图绕开陆路中介的外部商贸力量存在利益冲突。
enemies.en: They were often rivals to one another; they also had conflicting interests with northern Arabian powers competing for incense routes, certain desert tribes, and external trading forces seeking to bypass overland intermediaries.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制香料产地、保障内陆商路、维持红海与阿拉伯海接触、平衡周边部族和吸引外来商人为核心，偏重商业现实主义而非领土帝国扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling aromatic production zones, securing inland caravan routes, maintaining Red Sea and Arabian Sea connections, balancing surrounding tribes, and attracting foreign merchants, favoring commercial realism over territorial imperial expansion.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期南阿拉伯商贸文明
era.en: Late Iron Age South Arabian mercantile civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 大型堤坝与分水系统、旱区农业管理、石刻行政记录、长距离驼队物流、香料储运与山地—沙漠复合商路组织
keyInnovations.en: Large dams and water-distribution systems, arid-zone agricultural management, inscription-based administration, long-distance camel caravan logistics, aromatic storage and transport, and integrated mountain-desert trade route organization

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于控制世界级香料贸易节点、拥有成熟灌溉农业和高度适应地方环境的政治—部族网络。其地理位置使其能够在印度洋、红海和阿拉伯内陆贸易之间充当关键中介。
strengths.en: Their greatest strengths lay in controlling world-class incense trade nodes, possessing mature irrigated agriculture, and maintaining political-tribal networks highly adapted to local conditions. Their geography allowed them to serve as crucial intermediaries between the Indian Ocean, Red Sea, and inland Arabian trade.

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是政治分裂、诸国相互竞争、财政对商路波动敏感，以及王权对部族与地方精英的依赖度高。一旦水利失修或贸易改道，核心财富基础就会受到显著冲击。
weaknesses.en: Their main weaknesses were political fragmentation, rivalry among the kingdoms, fiscal sensitivity to trade disruption, and the heavy dependence of kings on tribes and local elites. If irrigation faltered or trade routes shifted, the core wealth base could be seriously damaged.

outlook.zh: 在公元前221年前后，这些政体总体稳定且富于活力，但长期前景取决于能否维持灌溉工程、协调彼此竞争并适应海上贸易逐渐增强的趋势。短期内仍将是阿拉伯南部最重要的商贸文明群。
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE these polities were broadly stable and economically dynamic, but their long-term outlook depended on maintaining irrigation works, managing mutual rivalry, and adapting to the gradual growth of maritime trade. In the near term, they would remain the leading commercial civilization cluster of southern Arabia.

description.zh: 也门地区由数个依赖乳香与商道的南阿拉伯王国共同构成。
description.en: Yemen was divided among several South Arabian kingdoms sustained by incense and caravan trade.

ruler.zh: 执政官普布利乌斯·科尔内利乌斯·西庇阿与马尔库斯·米努基乌斯·鲁福斯
ruler.en: Consuls Publius Cornelius Scipio and Marcus Minucius Rufus

governmentForm.zh: 贵族主导的混合共和政体，由民众大会、元老院与年度选举的高级政务官共同构成
governmentForm.en: An aristocratically dominated mixed republic composed of popular assemblies, the Senate, and annually elected magistrates

socialStructure.zh: 社会由元老贵族、骑士阶层、普通公民、意大利同盟者、自由民与奴隶构成；公民身份与家族门第决定政治机会与法律地位
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of senatorial aristocrats, equestrians, common citizens, Italian allies, freedmen, and slaves; citizenship and family standing shaped political opportunity and legal status

rulingClass.zh: 元老院贵族家族与担任过高级政务官的显贵阶层
rulingClass.en: Senatorial noble families and the elite who had held high magistracies

succession.zh: 无世袭君主；最高政务官由公民大会每年选举，元老院通过威望、资历与顾问权维持政策连续性
succession.en: No hereditary monarch; supreme magistrates were elected annually by citizen assemblies, while the Senate maintained policy continuity through prestige, seniority, and advisory authority

structure.zh: 国家权力形式上分散于百人团大会、部族大会、平民会议、元老院及各级政务官之间；实际决策高度受元老院与少数贵族家族影响。执政官掌军政大权，法务官负责司法，财务官管理国库，监察官负责人口与财产登记及元老院名册，保民官保护平民权益并可否决部分措施。战时可依法任命独裁官，但221 BCE并非常态使用。
structure.en: State authority was formally distributed among the Centuriate Assembly, Tribal Assembly, Plebeian Council, Senate, and the magistrates; in practice decision-making was heavily influenced by the Senate and a small number of noble families. Consuls held supreme civil and military authority, praetors oversaw justice, quaestors managed the treasury, censors supervised census and senatorial rolls, and tribunes defended plebeian interests with veto power. In wartime a dictator could legally be appointed, though this was not the normal arrangement in 221 BCE.

function.zh: 负责财政监督、外交建议、行省事务、战争与和约的政策指导，并通过元老院决议影响所有高级官员
function.en: Oversaw finance, advised on diplomacy, provincial affairs, war, and treaties, and influenced all senior magistrates through senatorial decrees

function.zh: 两名执政官主持国家行政、召集元老院与大会，并统帅主要野战军
function.en: The two consuls directed state administration, convened the Senate and assemblies, and commanded the principal field armies

function.zh: 管理民事与部分刑事司法，并在需要时担任军事与地方指挥
function.en: Managed civil and some criminal jurisdiction and could serve in military or regional command when needed

function.zh: 协助执政官与行省长官，管理国库、军费拨付、账目与部分税收记录
function.en: Assisted consuls and provincial commanders and managed the treasury, military disbursements, accounts, and parts of tax record-keeping

function.zh: 代表平民利益，可提出法案并对若干官员行为行使否决权
function.en: Represented plebeian interests, could propose legislation, and exercised veto power over certain official actions

function.zh: 定期进行人口财产登记，监督公共道德，承包公共工程与税务合同，并整编元老院名册
function.en: Conducted the census periodically, supervised public morals, contracted public works and tax leases, and revised the senatorial roll

localAdmin.zh: 意大利本土由罗马直接统治的公民社区、殖民地、市镇与盟邦网络组成；各地保留一定自治，但须服兵役并服从罗马外交与军事领导。海外地区尚未形成后世成熟的行省体系，西西里、撒丁-科西嘉等地已有被监督的征税与驻军安排。
localAdmin.en: Italy consisted of Roman citizen communities, colonies, municipia, and allied polities under Roman leadership; localities retained some self-government but owed military service and obedience in foreign and military affairs. Overseas territories had not yet developed the full later provincial system, though Sicily and Sardinia-Corsica already had supervised taxation and garrison arrangements.

legalSystem.zh: 以《十二铜表法》传统、习惯法、政务官告示与元老院意见为基础；公民法与盟邦、外邦人的处理有所区分，司法程序高度形式化但仍受贵族人脉与公民身份影响。
legalSystem.en: Based on the tradition of the Twelve Tables, customary law, magistrates' edicts, and senatorial opinion; Roman citizen law differed from the handling of allies and foreigners, and judicial procedure was highly formalized but still influenced by aristocratic networks and civic status.

taxationSystem.zh: 罗马公民在意大利本土通常不承担经常性直接税，国家主要依靠战争赔款、战利品、公共土地收入、关税、矿业与海外贡赋；在部分海外地区通过包税人征收什一税、地租与港口税。
taxationSystem.en: Roman citizens in Italy generally did not bear regular direct taxation; the state relied chiefly on indemnities, war booty, public land revenues, customs, mining, and overseas tribute; in some overseas territories tithes, rents, and harbor dues were collected through tax farmers.

religion.zh: 罗马传统多神教为主，崇拜朱庇特、朱诺、密涅瓦、玛尔斯、维斯塔等神祇，并通过祭司团、占卜、誓约与国家祭礼维系政教秩序；同时不断吸收希腊与意大利各地神灵崇拜。
religion.en: Traditional Roman polytheism predominated, centered on deities such as Jupiter, Juno, Minerva, Mars, and Vesta, with priestly colleges, divination, oaths, and state ritual sustaining public order; Greek and Italian cults were continually being absorbed as well.

philosophy.zh: 公共伦理以祖先之道、节制、勇武、责任与公民义务为核心；希腊哲学尤其斯多亚与学院派思想开始影响罗马上层，但尚未完全主导公共文化。
philosophy.en: Public ethics were centered on mos maiorum, discipline, courage, duty, and civic obligation; Greek philosophy, especially Stoic and Academic thought, was beginning to influence the Roman elite but had not yet fully dominated public culture.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写拉丁语，行政与法律文书中亦常见希腊语影响；铭文、法令、宗教奉献与公共纪念文本已较普遍。
writingSystem.en: Latin was written in the Latin alphabet, with Greek influence also visible in administration and law; inscriptions, decrees, religious dedications, and public commemorative texts were already widespread

culturalAchievements.zh: 在法律制度、道路与桥梁建设、军营规划、公共宗教仪式、拉丁文学早期发展以及对希腊文化的吸收改造方面成就显著；喜剧、史诗翻译与政治演说传统正在成熟。
culturalAchievements.en: The republic had notable achievements in legal institutions, roads and bridge building, military camp planning, public religious ceremony, the early development of Latin literature, and the adaptation of Greek culture; comedy, translated epic, and political oratory were maturing traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 意大利语族中的拉丁-法利斯坎支
languageFamily.en: Italic language family, specifically the Latino-Faliscan branch

gdpDescription.zh: 以古代标准衡量，罗马共和国经济规模庞大且高度扩张，核心在于意大利农业、战争收益、奴隶劳动力、矿产与西地中海贸易。人均产出不高，但凭借人口、军事组织与对同盟网络的动员能力，总量已居地中海强国前列。
gdpDescription.en: By ancient standards the Roman Republic had a large and highly expansionary economy, centered on Italian agriculture, war gains, slave labor, mineral extraction, and western Mediterranean trade. Per capita output was modest, but total scale ranked among the leading Mediterranean powers due to population, military organization, and the ability to mobilize an allied network.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与葡萄酒生产、橄榄种植、畜牧、陶器、金属加工、建筑、采矿、盐业、造船与军需制造
mainIndustries.en: Grain and wine production, olive cultivation, animal husbandry, pottery, metalworking, construction, mining, salt production, shipbuilding, and military supply manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 粮食、葡萄酒、橄榄油、陶器、金属器、奴隶、盐、木材、皮革，以及自希腊与东方输入的奢侈品、精美器皿和艺术品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, pottery, metal goods, slaves, salt, timber, leather, and imported luxuries, fine wares, and artworks from the Greek world and the East

name.zh: 阿斯与早期银德纳里体系

householdWealth.zh: 财富分布极不均衡。元老与骑士家族拥有广阔土地、战利品收益与承包收入；普通小农拥有有限土地并承担兵役；城市平民依靠手工业、日工与施惠网络；奴隶虽无财产权，却构成许多富裕家庭与庄园生产的核心劳力。
householdWealth.en: Wealth was distributed very unevenly. Senatorial and equestrian families controlled broad estates, war profits, and contracting income; ordinary smallholders held limited land while bearing military duties; urban plebeians depended on crafts, day labor, and patronage; slaves lacked property rights yet formed the labor core of many wealthy households and estates.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭农业和地主庄园为基础的前工业混合经济，由国家征发、战争收益、私人贸易、包税制度与奴隶劳工共同驱动
economicSystem.en: A pre-industrial mixed economy based on household farming and landholder estates, driven jointly by state requisitioning, war gains, private trade, tax farming, and slave labor

source.zh: 西西里等地农业税与地租
source.en: Agricultural taxes and rents from Sicily and other territories

source.zh: 关税、港口税与商业收费
source.en: Customs, harbor dues, and commercial charges

source.zh: 公共土地、矿业与承包收入
source.en: Public land, mining, and contract income

category.en: Army and navy pay and supply

category.zh: 地方治理、使节与行政支出
宗教祭祀、节庆与非常支出
category.en: Religious rites, festivals, and extraordinary expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要存放于土星神庙的国库体系，并受财务官管理。财政健康高度依赖胜利战争、同盟供给与对海外资源的持续获取，因此虽然账面充盈，结构上仍带有强烈军事扩张色彩。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was chiefly held in the aerarium associated with the Temple of Saturn and managed by quaestors. Fiscal health depended heavily on victorious wars, allied contributions, and continued access to overseas resources, so despite substantial reserves it remained structurally tied to military expansion.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 总体上采取保守而机会主义的财政政策：平时依靠间接收入与海外征收，尽量减轻意大利公民直接税负；战时则通过征发、同盟供给、借贷与战利品弥补开支。
fiscalPolicy.en: Overall fiscal policy was conservative yet opportunistic: in normal times it relied on indirect income and overseas levies while minimizing direct taxation on Italian citizens; in wartime it met costs through requisitions, allied support, borrowing, and booty.

description.zh: 由罗马公民重装步兵构成，按操典、年龄与财产资格征集，使用投枪、短剑与大盾，是共和国野战军的核心。
description.en: Composed of Roman citizen heavy infantry raised by levy according to established practice, age, and property qualification, equipped with pila, short swords, and large shields; they formed the core of the republican field army.

description.zh: 来自拉丁殖民地与意大利盟邦，人数通常与罗马军团相匹配，承担主力战斗与驻防任务。
description.en: Drawn from Latin colonies and Italian allies, usually fielded in numbers comparable to Roman legions, and used for major combat and garrison service.

description.zh: 包括罗马骑士、拉丁与意大利盟邦骑兵，以及少量雇佣和附庸骑兵，用于侦察、追击与侧翼掩护。
description.en: Included Roman equites, Latin and Italian allied cavalry, and small numbers of mercenary or client horse for scouting, pursuit, and flank protection.

description.zh: 由桨手、水手、船上士兵与港口守备组成，主要确保第勒尼安海、西西里周边与西地中海航路安全，并防范迦太基或海盗威胁。
description.en: Consisted of rowers, sailors, shipborne soldiers, and port garrisons, mainly securing the Tyrrhenian Sea, waters around Sicily, and western Mediterranean routes while guarding against Carthaginian or pirate threats.

commanderInChief.zh: 两位执政官为最高军政统帅，元老院负责战略指导
commanderInChief.en: The two consuls served as supreme military magistrates, with the Senate providing strategic direction

technology.zh: 军制已发展出较成熟的操典化军团体系，重视筑营、道路行军、攻城器械、标准化武备与严厉军纪；海军仍保持从布匿战争中获得的战术与造船经验。
technology.en: The military had developed a fairly mature doctrinal legionary system emphasizing fortified camps, road marching, siege equipment, standardized equipment, and strict discipline; the navy still retained tactical and shipbuilding experience gained from the Punic Wars.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西地中海的迦太基竞争、阿尔卑斯与波河流域高卢部族、伊利里亚与海盗势力，以及巴尔干与希腊世界中可能卷入的马其顿和其他希腊国家；内部上则需持续防范意大利盟邦不稳与征兵负担引发的不满。
threats.en: Primary threats came from rivalry with Carthage in the western Mediterranean, Gallic tribes in the Alpine and Po Valley regions, Illyrian and pirate forces, and possible entanglement with Macedon and other Greek states in the Balkans and Greek world; internally, Rome also had to guard against instability among Italian allies and resentment caused by heavy military levies.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约七百万，包含罗马公民、拉丁殖民者、意大利盟邦居民、奴隶与若干海外属地人口。人口基础主要位于意大利半岛，农业社区占绝大多数，但罗马城本身已成为地中海最大的城市之一。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about seven million, including Roman citizens, Latin colonists, Italian allied populations, slaves, and people in a few overseas possessions. The demographic base lay primarily in the Italian peninsula, where rural communities predominated, but the city of Rome itself had already become one of the largest urban centers in the Mediterranean.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为元老贵族与骑士，其次为普通公民农民、城市平民与工匠，再下为盟邦居民、自由民和大批奴隶。阶层差异不仅体现在财富，也体现在法律权利、服役义务与政治参与程度上。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood senatorial nobles and equestrians, followed by ordinary citizen farmers, urban plebeians, and artisans, then allied communities, freedmen, and a large slave population. Social differences were expressed not only in wealth but also in legal rights, military obligations, and degree of political participation.

allies.zh: 主要盟友为拉丁殖民地、意大利同盟城邦与若干受保护的地中海邦国；在具体事务上也会与希腊城邦、努米底亚势力或地方君主建立临时合作。
allies.en: Its principal allies were the Latin colonies, Italian allied communities, and certain protected Mediterranean states; in specific circumstances Rome could also form temporary cooperation with Greek cities, Numidian powers, or local monarchs.

enemies.zh: 迦太基是最重要的战略对手；此外，高卢部族、伊利里亚海盗及未来可能对抗的马其顿王国也是主要敌手来源。
enemies.en: Carthage was the most important strategic rival; in addition, Gallic tribes, Illyrian pirates, and the kingdom of Macedon as a likely future opponent were major sources of hostility.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策兼具防御与扩张特征：以确保意大利安全和海上交通为名，通过联盟、干预、驻军、和约与分而治之逐步扩大影响力，并在胜利后建立更牢固的财政与军事控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined defensive and expansionary elements: in the name of securing Italy and maritime communications, Rome expanded its influence through alliances, interventions, garrisons, treaties, and divide-and-rule practices, then imposed firmer fiscal and military control after victory.

era.zh: 铁器时代后期的地中海古典时代
era.en: Late Iron Age Mediterranean classical era

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟军团编制、标准化营地建设、道路与里程体系、拱券和混合建筑技术的早期发展、改良攻城器械、铸币流通扩大，以及将希腊数学、医学与工艺知识吸收进拉丁世界。
keyInnovations.en: A mature legionary organization, standardized camp construction, road and milestone systems, early development of arch-based and mixed building techniques, improved siege engines, wider monetization through coinage, and the absorption of Greek mathematical, medical, and technical knowledge into the Latin world.

strengths.zh: 罗马最大的优势在于庞大的人口动员能力、以同盟体系为核心的兵源网络、灵活而韧性的共和政治、成熟的军团战术、不断扩展的财政汲取能力，以及在失败后仍能迅速重建军力的制度性耐久性。
strengths.en: Rome’s greatest strengths lay in its vast capacity for demographic mobilization, its alliance-based manpower network, a flexible and resilient republican political order, mature legionary tactics, expanding fiscal extraction, and an institutional ability to rebuild military power even after setbacks.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括贵族派系竞争激烈、贫富分化扩大、对盟邦忠诚与持续征兵的依赖、海外治理经验尚不完全成熟，以及财政收入过度依靠战争、贡赋和扩张成果。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included intense aristocratic factional competition, widening inequality, dependence on allied loyalty and sustained military levies, still-incomplete experience in overseas governance, and fiscal revenues overly dependent on war, tribute, and the proceeds of expansion.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE的罗马正处于上升阶段，若继续维持意大利同盟体系并在西地中海击败主要竞争者，极可能成为整个地中海世界的决定性强权；但随着疆域扩大，内部社会矛盾、军事负担与行省治理问题也将日益突出。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE Rome was in an ascending phase; if it maintained the Italian alliance system and defeated its major rivals in the western Mediterranean, it was very likely to become the decisive great power of the Mediterranean world. Yet as its sphere expanded, internal social tensions, military burdens, and the challenges of provincial governance would become increasingly serious.

description.zh: 第二次布匿战争结束后，罗马已成为西地中海主导力量并继续东向扩张。
description.en: After the Second Punic War, Rome had become the dominant power in the western Mediterranean and continued expanding eastward.

ideology.zh: 贵族共和保守主义——维护元老院对国家政策的主导权，坚持传统的意大利同盟管理体系，强调渐进稳健的扩张策略与祖制权威
ideology.en: Aristocratic republican conservatism — upholding senatorial dominance over state policy, maintaining the traditional Italian alliance management system, emphasizing gradual and steady expansion and ancestral authority

relationship.zh: 元老院体制派是罗马共和国的权力核心，以费边家族和西庇阿家族等世袭贵族为代表。他们通过庇护网络、宗教权威与政务官选举掌控国家大政，但内部在对外战略上存在费边的稳健路线与西庇阿的积极扩张路线之间的分歧。
relationship.en: The senatorial establishment was the power core of the Roman Republic, represented by hereditary aristocratic families like the Fabii and Scipiones. They controlled state affairs through patronage networks, religious authority, and magistrate elections, though internal divisions existed between the cautious Fabian strategy and the aggressive expansionist Scipionic approach to foreign policy.

description.zh: 在公元前221年前后，罗马元老院正处于权力的巅峰期。第一次布匿战争的胜利使罗马获得了西西里、撒丁和科西嘉，元老院对地中海事务的影响力急剧扩大。费边·马克西穆斯代表了元老院中谨慎务实的一派——他在后来的第二次布匿战争中因采取消耗战略而获得“延误者”（Cunctator）的称号。元老院通过年度政务官选举、行省分配和军事指挥权的授予维持对国家的控制。西庇阿家族则代表了更积极进取的扩张路线。元老院贵族之间的竞争虽然激烈，但仍在共和制度框架内运作——凯旋式的荣耀、显赫的姓氏和对国家的贡献是政治晋升的基本资本。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, the Roman Senate was at the height of its power. Victory in the First Punic War had won Rome Sicily, Sardinia, and Corsica, dramatically expanding senatorial influence over Mediterranean affairs. Fabius Maximus represented the cautious and pragmatic faction within the Senate — he would later earn the sobriquet 'Cunctator' (Delayer) for his attrition strategy during the Second Punic War. The Senate maintained control of the state through annual magistrate elections, provincial assignments, and the granting of military command. The Scipio family represented a more aggressively expansionist approach. Competition among senatorial aristocrats was fierce but still operated within the republican institutional framework — triumphal glory, illustrious family names, and contributions to the state were the fundamental currency of political advancement.

role.zh: 保守战略派领袖，消耗战理论的代表人物
role.en: Leader of the conservative strategic faction, champion of attrition warfare

name.zh: 普布利乌斯·科尔涅利乌斯·西庇阿

role.zh: 西庇阿家族当代代表，其子将成为击败汉尼拔的“阿非利加的征服者”
role.en: Contemporary head of the Scipio family, whose son would become Scipio Africanus the conqueror of Hannibal

leader.zh: 盖乌斯·弗拉米尼乌斯式改革者

leaderTitle.zh: 保民官与改革派执政官

ideology.zh: 平民权利扩张——推动新征服土地的分配、扩大公民权范围、挑战元老院贵族对国家资源的垄断
ideology.en: Plebeian rights expansion — promoting distribution of newly conquered lands, expanding citizenship, and challenging senatorial aristocratic monopoly over state resources

relationship.zh: 民众派通过保民官制度与公民大会挑战元老院精英的政策垄断，特别是在土地分配、公共工程和公民权扩展等议题上。弗拉米尼乌斯在前232年推动了将高卢土地分配给平民的法案，被视为后来格拉古兄弟改革的先驱。
relationship.en: The popular faction challenged the senatorial elite's policy monopoly through the tribunate and citizen assemblies, particularly on issues of land distribution, public works, and citizenship expansion. Flaminius promoted the distribution of Gallic land to plebeians in 232 BCE, seen as a precursor to the later Gracchan reforms.

description.zh: 盖乌斯·弗拉米尼乌斯是这一时期民众派政治家的典型代表。他在前232年以保民官身份强行通过了将亚得里亚海沿岸高卢塞诺尼人土地分配给罗马贫民的法案，遭到元老院激烈反对。弗拉米尼乌斯的举措反映了一个日益严峻的社会问题：随着罗马扩张带来大量公有土地，这些土地究竟应由贵族大族占有还是分配给无地平民？这一矛盾贯穿了整个共和国中后期历史。民众派政治家通常通过保民官职位发起立法攻势，利用部族大会的投票机制绕过元老院的阻挠。他们的支持基础包括城市平民、退伍军人和意大利盟邦中渴望获得罗马公民权的群体。虽然距格拉古兄弟的全面土地改革还有近一个世纪，但土地、公民权与政治平等的核心议题已经清晰浮现。
description.en: Gaius Flaminius was the archetypal popular politician of this period. As tribune in 232 BCE, he forced through a law distributing conquered Gallic Senonian land along the Adriatic to Rome's poor, provoking fierce senatorial opposition. Flaminius's action reflected an increasingly acute social problem: as Roman expansion brought vast public lands, should these be held by aristocratic families or distributed to landless plebeians? This tension ran through the entire later republican period. Popular politicians typically launched legislative offensives through the tribunate, using the tribal assembly's voting mechanism to bypass senatorial obstruction. Their support base included urban plebeians, veterans, and Italian allies seeking Roman citizenship. Though the comprehensive land reforms of the Gracchi lay nearly a century away, the core issues of land, citizenship, and political equality had already emerged clearly.

role.zh: 土地分配改革先驱，后于特拉西梅诺湖战役中阵亡
role.en: Pioneer of land distribution reform, later killed at the Battle of Lake Trasimene

name.zh: 盖乌斯·特伦提乌斯·瓦罗

role.zh: 出身卑微的民众派政治家，代表反贵族政治倾向
role.en: A popular politician of humble origins, representing anti-aristocratic political tendencies

name.zh: 布匿战争军事统帅集团

ideology.zh: 军事职业化与个人统帅权——通过长期远征战争的实践推动军队向个人效忠转化，军事英雄在政治中获得超越制度的影响力
ideology.en: Military professionalization and personal command authority — through the practice of extended campaigns, shifting army loyalty toward individual commanders, with military heroes gaining political influence that transcended institutional norms

relationship.zh: 布匿战争期间和之后的军事统帅集团名义上服从元老院，但长期远征赋予统帅们对军队和被征服领土的实际控制权。西庇阿·阿非利加努斯因击败汉尼拔而获得近乎超人的声望，其个人影响力已超越共和制度的常规约束。
relationship.en: The military command group during and after the Punic Wars nominally obeyed the Senate, but extended campaigns gave commanders effective control over their armies and conquered territories. Scipio Africanus achieved near-superhuman prestige for defeating Hannibal, with personal influence already exceeding the normal constraints of republican institutions.

description.zh: 布匿战争催生了一种新型罗马军事政治力量。当汉尼拔于前218年翻越阿尔卑斯山入侵意大利时，传统的年度轮换指挥体制暴露出严重不足——坎尼之战的惨败正是指挥权分散与经验不足的恶果。此后罗马不得不授予个别将领更长期的指挥权，西庇阿·阿非利加努斯在西班牙和北非的辉煌战绩就建立在这种非常规的权力授予之上。年仅二十五岁就获得军事指挥权的西庇阿打破了传统的政务官晋升序列（cursus honorum），其军事天才和个人魅力使士兵更多效忠于他本人而非罗马国家。这种模式——伟大将领的个人权威凌驾于共和制度之上——在西庇阿时代尚属萌芽，但它预示了一个世纪后苏拉、庞培和凯撒将完成的共和政体解构。军队的长期服役化、士兵对统帅个人恩赐的依附以及远方战争造成的信息不对称，共同为军事独裁埋下了制度性种子。
description.en: The Punic Wars gave birth to a new type of Roman military-political power. When Hannibal crossed the Alps and invaded Italy in 218 BCE, the traditional annual command rotation system revealed serious inadequacies — the catastrophic defeat at Cannae was precisely the result of dispersed command and inexperience. Rome was subsequently forced to grant individual generals longer command tenures, and Scipio Africanus's brilliant campaigns in Spain and North Africa were built upon such extraordinary grants of power. Scipio, who received military command at the unprecedented age of twenty-five, broke the traditional cursus honorum, and his military genius and personal charisma made soldiers loyal more to him personally than to the Roman state. This pattern — a great general's personal authority overriding republican institutions — was still nascent in Scipio's era, but it foreshadowed the republican deconstruction that Sulla, Pompey, and Caesar would complete a century later. The professionalization of long-service armies, soldiers' dependence on their commanders' personal patronage, and the information asymmetry created by distant wars together planted the institutional seeds of military dictatorship.

name.zh: 普布利乌斯·科尔涅利乌斯·西庇阿·阿非利加努斯

role.zh: 在扎马战役中击败汉尼拔，成为罗马最伟大的军事英雄，其个人声望重塑了共和政治
role.en: Defeated Hannibal at the Battle of Zama, becoming Rome's greatest military hero whose personal prestige reshaped republican politics

name.zh: 马库斯·克劳狄乌斯·马尔凯卢斯

role.zh: 赢得最高军事荣誉“丰盈战利品”（spolia opima）的传奇将领
role.en: Legendary general who won the supreme military honor of spolia opima

ruler.zh: 迦太基元老院与苏费特

dynasty.zh: 共和政体，处于巴卡家族主导时代之后
dynasty.en: Republic, in the period after the era of Barcid dominance

governmentForm.zh: 寡头共和制，由元老院、民众机构与年度选举的苏费特共同运作
governmentForm.en: An oligarchic republic governed through the senate, popular bodies, and annually elected suffetes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由富裕商人贵族、大土地持有者、城市手工业者与商人、利比亚与腓尼基裔平民、雇佣军群体、附属社群及奴隶构成；公民身份主要集中于迦太基本城，海外与内陆属地多承担贡赋与兵役义务。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of wealthy mercantile aristocrats, large landholders, urban artisans and traders, Libyan and Phoenician commoners, mercenary communities, subject peoples, and slaves; full citizenship was concentrated in the city of Carthage, while overseas and inland dependencies chiefly bore tribute and service obligations.

rulingClass.zh: 以商贸贵族、航海家族与大地产阶层为核心的元老院寡头
rulingClass.en: Senatorial oligarchs drawn from mercantile elites, maritime families, and major landholding interests

succession.zh: 非世袭君主制；苏费特由年度选举产生，重要政策由元老院主导，军事统帅可由贵族家族通过任命与声望取得
succession.en: Not a hereditary monarchy; suffetes were elected annually, major policy was dominated by the senate, and military commands could be secured by aristocratic families through appointment and prestige

structure.zh: 国家中枢由元老院、两名年度苏费特、若干公民会议及贵族司法监督机构构成。元老院负责外交、财政、战争与殖民事务；苏费特主持政务与司法；高级将领在海外省区和战区拥有较大自主权，但在第二次布匿战争前夕已受到罗马条约与国内派系平衡的强烈限制。
structure.en: The central state was organized around the senate, two annual suffetes, several citizen assemblies, and aristocratic judicial oversight bodies. The senate directed diplomacy, finance, war, and colonial affairs; the suffetes presided over administration and justice; senior generals held substantial autonomy in overseas provinces and theaters, though on the eve of the Second Punic War this autonomy was strongly constrained by Roman treaty obligations and domestic factional balance.

function.zh: 处理法令、外交往来、盟约、议案记录与跨海属地指令
function.en: Handled decrees, diplomacy, treaties, agenda records, and directives to overseas possessions

function.zh: 征收关税、港口税、土地收入、属地贡赋，并管理国库与公共支出
function.en: Collected customs, harbor dues, land revenues, and subject tribute while managing the treasury and public expenditure

function.zh: 监管商港、军港、船坞、航运登记、仓储与海运安全
function.en: Supervised the commercial harbor, military harbor, docks, shipping registration, warehousing, and maritime security

name.zh: 司法与百人法院事务厅

function.zh: 管理商业纠纷、契约、财产争议及对高级官员和将领的审查
function.en: Managed commercial disputes, contracts, property cases, and scrutiny of senior officials and generals

function.zh: 监督北非腹地附属社群、剩余海外据点与承包征税人
function.en: Oversaw subordinate communities in the North African hinterland, remaining overseas stations, and tax-farm contractors

function.zh: 登记公民兵、利比亚部队、骑兵盟友、外籍雇佣军契约与军需供给
function.en: Registered citizen levies, Libyan troops, allied cavalry, foreign mercenary contracts, and logistical supply

localAdmin.zh: 本城由公民机构、区段官员和港务管理者治理；北非近郊与农业腹地通过附属城镇、利比亚社群首领及税务代理人间接统治；在伊比利亚的旧有势力范围于221 BCE主要受巴卡家族军政体系控制，但其合法性仍与迦太基本土政治相连。
localAdmin.en: The capital was governed by civic institutions, district officials, and harbor administrators; the nearby North African countryside and agricultural hinterland were ruled indirectly through subordinate towns, Libyan community leaders, and tax agents; in Iberia, the former sphere of influence in 221 BCE was largely controlled by the Barcid military-administrative system, though its legitimacy remained connected to Carthaginian metropolitan politics.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以腓尼基—迦太基习惯法、商业契约传统和贵族司法审查为基础，重视财产、航运、债务和税赋。高级官员任满后可能受到追责，体现共和体制下对将领和执政者的事后监督。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Phoenician-Carthaginian customary law, commercial contract traditions, and aristocratic judicial oversight, with strong attention to property, shipping, debt, and taxation. Senior officials could face review after leaving office, reflecting republican post-term accountability for generals and magistrates.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠港口关税、贸易手续费、属地贡赋、北非农田收入、矿业收益分成及战时非常征敛。对附属社群和非公民地区的征收重于对核心公民体的直接税。
taxationSystem.en: State income relied chiefly on harbor customs, commercial fees, subject tribute, revenues from North African farmland, shares of mining income, and extraordinary wartime levies. Exaction fell more heavily on dependent communities and non-citizen territories than on the core citizen body.

religion.zh: 以腓尼基多神信仰为主，崇拜巴力·哈蒙、塔尼特、梅尔卡特与埃什蒙等神祇，并吸收北非与地中海各地宗教元素。祭祀、誓约与公共献祭在国家政治和海上商业生活中均占重要地位。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Phoenician polytheism centered on deities such as Baal Hammon, Tanit, Melqart, and Eshmun, while also absorbing North African and wider Mediterranean elements. Ritual, oath-taking, and public sacrifice were important in both state politics and maritime-commercial life.

philosophy.zh: 迦太基并无如希腊城邦般系统化哲学学派，其思想更偏重务实政治、航海经验、商业伦理、家族荣誉与宗教传统；同时受希腊化世界的知识与艺术风尚影响。
philosophy.en: Carthage did not cultivate systematized philosophical schools in the Greek manner; its outlook was more strongly shaped by pragmatic politics, seafaring experience, commercial ethics, family honor, and religious tradition, though it was influenced by the intellectual and artistic fashions of the Hellenistic world.

writingSystem.zh: 使用布匿文，为腓尼基字母系统的一支，主要用于铭文、行政记录、宗教奉献、商业标记与墓志。
writingSystem.en: The Punic script, a branch of the Phoenician alphabetic system, was used for inscriptions, administration, religious dedications, commercial markings, and funerary texts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 迦太基在港口工程、海军设施、航海技术、农业经营、染料与金属工艺、跨区域商业网络组织方面成就显著。其环形军港与商港布局为古代城市海事工程典范，农业传统后来以马戈的农业著作闻名于地中海世界。
culturalAchievements.en: Carthage achieved distinction in harbor engineering, naval infrastructure, seafaring techniques, agricultural management, dyeing and metalworking, and the organization of long-distance commercial networks. Its circular military harbor and commercial port were exemplary works of ancient urban maritime engineering, and its agrarian tradition later became famous throughout the Mediterranean through the agricultural writings associated with Mago.

languageFamily.zh: 布匿语属闪米特语系西北支；同时广泛接触利比亚语、希腊语与其他地中海语言
languageFamily.en: Punic belonged to the Northwest Semitic branch; it existed alongside broad contact with Libyan languages, Greek, and other Mediterranean tongues

gdpDescription.zh: 尽管在第一次布匿战争后失去西西里并承受赔款压力，迦太基在221 BCE仍是西地中海最活跃的商业与转运中心之一。其经济基础由港口贸易、北非高产农业、手工业、航运服务和伊比利亚联系网络共同支撑，但整体国力较战争前高峰明显下降，并受到罗马势力扩张及条约限制压制。
gdpDescription.en: Although Carthage had lost Sicily and borne indemnity burdens after the First Punic War, in 221 BCE it remained one of the most active commercial and transshipment centers in the western Mediterranean. Its economy rested on harbor trade, productive North African agriculture, manufacturing, shipping services, and Iberian connections, yet overall power was clearly below its prewar height and constrained by Roman expansion and treaty limitations.

mainIndustries.zh: 海运贸易、港口仓储、造船与修船、金属加工、陶器、纺织与染色、橄榄油与谷物生产、葡萄酒、渔业制品、象牙与奢侈品转运
mainIndustries.en: Maritime trade, port warehousing, shipbuilding and repair, metalworking, pottery, textiles and dyeing, olive oil and grain production, wine, fish products, and the transshipment of ivory and luxury goods

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、橄榄油、葡萄酒、盐渍鱼、布匹、紫染织物、金属器、银、铜、铅、木材、奴隶、象牙、香料转口品、精致陶器与珠宝
tradeGoods.en: Grain, olive oil, wine, salted fish, cloth, purple-dyed textiles, metal goods, silver, copper, lead, timber, slaves, ivory, re-exported aromatics, fine pottery, and jewelry

householdWealth.zh: 城市贵族和大型商人家庭拥有高额土地、船队股份、海外债权与仓储资本；普通市民和工匠多依赖工资、作坊与小规模贸易；附属农民与被统治社群负担较重，财富差距显著。
householdWealth.en: Urban aristocratic and major merchant households held extensive land, shipping shares, overseas credit, and warehousing capital; ordinary citizens and artisans depended more on wages, workshops, and small trade; subject farmers and dominated communities bore heavier burdens, and wealth inequality was marked.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换、跨海商业、贵族资本投资、属地贡赋和国家监管港口为核心的商业寡头经济
economicSystem.en: A commercial oligarchic economy centered on market exchange, overseas trade, aristocratic capital investment, subject tribute, and state-regulated ports

source.en: North African agricultural and estate revenues

source.zh: 属地贡赋与附属社群征收
source.en: Tribute and exactions from dependent communities

category.zh: 港口、船坞与公共工程维护
category.en: Maintenance of harbors, docks, and public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍保持可观流动性，依托港口税收、农业剩余与伊比利亚相关收益，但与大战前的扩张时代相比已更谨慎，且必须预备应对罗马压力、雇佣军成本与突发海上危机。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury retained substantial liquidity based on port taxation, agricultural surplus, and Iberian-linked income, but compared with the great expansionary period before the war it was managed more cautiously and had to be prepared for Roman pressure, mercenary costs, and sudden maritime crises.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守务实，优先维持港口运行、偿付义务、保障商路与维持可迅速动员的军力；避免正面挑战罗马的同时，尽量通过海外商业和伊比利亚资源恢复国力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and pragmatic, prioritizing port operations, debt obligations, trade-route security, and a force that could be mobilized quickly; while avoiding direct confrontation with Rome, Carthage sought to restore strength through overseas commerce and Iberian resources.

description.zh: 负责都城守备、要塞防御与紧急动员，核心由本城公民和受国家供养的守备部队构成。
description.en: Responsible for capital defense, fortifications, and emergency mobilization, formed from city citizens and state-maintained garrison troops.

name.zh: 利比亚与非洲附属步兵

description.zh: 来自北非腹地的重装或中装部队，是迦太基陆军的重要基础之一，但忠诚度受税负与待遇影响。
description.en: Drawn from the North African hinterland, these heavy or medium infantry formed an important basis of Carthaginian land forces, though loyalty was influenced by tax burden and treatment.

description.zh: 轻骑机动性强，擅长侦察、袭扰与追击，是迦太基最具价值的战场兵种之一。
description.en: Highly mobile light cavalry skilled in scouting, harassment, and pursuit, among the most valuable battlefield arms available to Carthage.

description.zh: 负责商路护航、港口巡防、舰船操持与有限两栖任务；规模不复第一次布匿战争时期盛况，但传统技术仍存。
description.en: Handled trade-route escort, harbor patrol, ship operation, and limited amphibious duties; the force no longer matched the scale of the First Punic War, but retained traditional expertise.

commanderInChief.zh: 元老院授权的将军与苏费特共同统辖
commanderInChief.en: Senate-authorized generals in conjunction with the suffetes

technology.zh: 军备以地中海晚期铁器时代水平为主，使用长矛、盾牌、剑、投射武器、攻城器与桨帆战船。迦太基尤其擅长造船、港务工程和雇佣多族兵种协同，但在长期公民化陆军组织上弱于罗马。
technology.en: Military technology reflected the later Iron Age Mediterranean, using spears, shields, swords, missile weapons, siege engines, and oared warships. Carthage excelled particularly in shipbuilding, harbor engineering, and multi-ethnic force coordination, but was weaker than Rome in the organization of a durable citizen land army.

threats.zh: 最主要威胁来自罗马的战略压制与条约框架；其次是努米底亚势力的离合、附属社群不稳、海上竞争及伊比利亚局势变化。若罗马认定巴卡家族扩张违反均势，战争风险将迅速上升。
threats.en: The greatest threat came from Roman strategic pressure and the treaty framework; secondary dangers included shifting Numidian alignments, instability among dependent communities, maritime competition, and changes in Iberia. If Rome judged Barcid expansion to violate the balance, the risk of war would rise rapidly.

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括迦太基本城居民、近郊农业人口、港口流动商人与工匠、外籍商人、奴隶及受其控制的北非附属社群。城市核心人口极为稠密，呈现强烈的海港都会特征。
populationDescription.en: The population included residents of the city of Carthage, nearby agricultural communities, mobile port merchants and artisans, foreign traders, slaves, and North African dependent populations under its control. The urban core was very dense and had the marked character of a major harbor metropolis.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为元老贵族、富商与大地主；中层为船主、商人、作坊主、祭司与专业文书；下层为工匠、码头工人、小农、附属民与雇佣兵家属；最底层为奴隶和战俘。族群上则混合腓尼基裔、利比亚裔、希腊裔及其他地中海人群。
socialClasses.en: The upper ranks consisted of senatorial nobles, rich merchants, and great landowners; the middle ranks included shipowners, traders, workshop masters, priests, and professional scribes; the lower ranks comprised artisans, dock workers, small farmers, subject peoples, and mercenary families; the bottom tier consisted of slaves and captives. Ethnically the society mixed Phoenician, Libyan, Greek, and other Mediterranean populations.

allies.zh: 正式盟友有限，更多依赖贸易伙伴、北非附属社群、部分努米底亚首领以及伊比利亚的巴卡家族网络
allies.en: Formal allies were limited; Carthage relied more on trade partners, North African subordinate communities, some Numidian chiefs, and the Barcid network in Iberia

enemies.zh: 罗马为首要敌对强权；与部分努米底亚势力关系紧张，并对西地中海中倾向罗马的城邦和势力保持戒备
enemies.en: Rome was the primary hostile great power; relations with some Numidian forces were tense, and Carthage remained wary of western Mediterranean cities and powers inclined toward Rome

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交方针以生存、恢复财富与避免过早总决战为主，利用商业、使节、婚盟、补贴与地区代理人维持影响力，同时通过伊比利亚资源重建战略纵深。但在罗马猜疑加剧的背景下，这种平衡极其脆弱。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival, economic recovery, and avoiding a premature decisive war, using commerce, embassies, marriage alliances, subsidies, and regional proxies to preserve influence while rebuilding strategic depth through Iberian resources. Yet under mounting Roman suspicion this balance was extremely fragile.

era.zh: 地中海晚期铁器时代与希腊化时代
era.en: Late Iron Age Mediterranean and Hellenistic era

keyInnovations.zh: 先进港口设计、圆形军港、熟练远洋与沿岸航海、标准化商船与战船制造、农业园艺管理、跨区域仓储与金融性商业操作
keyInnovations.en: Advanced harbor design, the circular military harbor, skilled blue-water and coastal navigation, standardized merchant and warship construction, agricultural estate management, and transregional warehousing and finance-oriented commerce

strengths.zh: 迦太基的优势在于卓越的港口与航运体系、深厚商业资本、成熟的税收与贸易网络、高度发达的北非农业，以及在多民族军队组织和外交交易上的灵活性。
strengths.en: Carthage's strengths lay in its exceptional harbor and shipping system, deep commercial capital, mature taxation and trade networks, highly developed North African agriculture, and flexibility in organizing multi-ethnic forces and diplomatic bargains.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括对雇佣军与附属社群的依赖、核心公民陆军薄弱、政治上受寡头派系与巴卡家族张力影响、战后国力下滑，以及在战略上长期受罗马压制。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included dependence on mercenaries and subject communities, a relatively weak core citizen land army, political tension between oligarchic factions and the Barcid interest, postwar decline in power, and long-term strategic subordination to Rome.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE的迦太基仍富有、仍重要、仍具恢复潜力，但已不是不受约束的海上霸权。若能谨慎整合伊比利亚资源并维持国内合作，尚可短期复兴；然而罗马与迦太基之间的结构性竞争几乎不可避免，前景高度危险。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE Carthage remained wealthy, important, and capable of recovery, but it was no longer an unconstrained maritime hegemon. If it could carefully integrate Iberian resources and preserve domestic cooperation, a short-term revival was possible; yet the structural rivalry between Rome and Carthage was nearly unavoidable, making the outlook highly dangerous.

description.zh: 战败后的迦太基仍保有城市与商贸活力，但已受罗马严格限制。
description.en: Defeated Carthage retained urban and commercial vitality but was now tightly constrained by Rome.

dynasty.zh: 馬西利與馬薩埃西利王族支系

capital.zh: 基爾塔及其他王權中心

governmentForm.zh: 部族君主制，帶有聯盟性質的王權結構
governmentForm.en: Tribal monarchy with a confederative royal structure

socialStructure.zh: 社會以部族、氏族與酋邦網絡構成，上層為王族與貴族騎士，中間為自由牧民與農民，下層包括依附者、戰俘與奴隸。沿海與內陸商路上的城鎮居民逐漸增加，但政治核心仍在部族首領與王室關係網中。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized through tribes, clans, and chiefdom networks. At the top stood royal lineages and noble horse-owning elites, followed by free pastoralists and cultivators, with dependents, war captives, and slaves below. Town populations along inland and coastal trade routes were growing, but political power still rested primarily in tribal chiefs and royal kinship networks.

rulingClass.zh: 王族、部族酋長、騎兵貴族與掌握牧群和土地的地方首領
rulingClass.en: Royal lineages, tribal chiefs, cavalry nobility, and local leaders controlling herds and land

succession.zh: 王位繼承多依王族血統與部族共識決定，常受軍事實力、貴族支持與聯姻影響，並非嚴格長子繼承。
succession.en: Succession generally followed royal bloodlines and tribal consensus, often shaped by military strength, noble support, and marriage alliances rather than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 221年前後的努米底亞尚未形成完全統一的中央官僚國家，而是由馬西利與馬薩埃西利等主要部族王權構成。國王依賴親族、酋長與軍事隨從治理，並通過使者、集會、封賞與人質制度維繫統治。
structure.en: Around 221 BCE, Numidia had not yet developed into a fully unified centralized bureaucratic state, but consisted of major royal tribal powers such as the Massylii and Masaesyli. Kings ruled through kin networks, chiefs, and military retainers, maintaining authority by envoys, assemblies, patronage, and hostage arrangements.

function.zh: 管理王室財產、牧群、使節接待、王庭供給與親兵事務
function.en: Managed royal property, herds, diplomatic reception, court provisioning, and royal retainers

function.zh: 協調各部族酋長、徵召兵力、處理婚盟、人質與朝貢關係
function.en: Coordinated tribal chiefs, mobilized manpower, and handled marriage alliances, hostages, and tribute relations

function.zh: 統計牧群、監督王室牧場、徵收穀物與牲畜貢賦
function.en: Counted herds, supervised royal pastures, and collected grain and livestock tribute

function.zh: 組織王家騎兵、戰時動員與邊境巡防
function.en: Organized royal cavalry, wartime mobilization, and frontier patrols

function.zh: 管理內陸商道、集市、通行稅與對外交易監督
function.en: Managed inland trade routes, markets, transit dues, and oversight of external exchange

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要依靠部族酋長、氏族長老與王室任命或承認的地方代表。城市與定居點的管理較鬆散，多以集市、駐軍與王室代理人為核心。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied chiefly on tribal chiefs, clan elders, and local representatives appointed or recognized by the king. Governance in towns and settlements was relatively loose, centered on markets, garrisons, and royal agents.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以習慣法、部族仲裁、王命與盟約義務為主。財產、婚姻、放牧權與血仇賠償多由長老和首領裁斷，重大政治與軍事案件則由國王或其近臣處理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on customary practice, tribal arbitration, royal command, and alliance obligations. Property, marriage, grazing rights, and blood-compensation cases were usually decided by elders and chiefs, while major political and military matters were handled by the king or his close retainers.

taxationSystem.zh: 課徵方式不如地中海官僚國家規整，主要包括牲畜與穀物貢納、戰利品分配、商路通行稅、附屬部族貢賦，以及在戰時對人力與物資的徵發。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was less regularized than in Mediterranean bureaucratic states, consisting mainly of livestock and grain tribute, shares of war booty, tolls on trade routes, dues from subordinate tribes, and wartime requisitions of manpower and supplies.

religion.zh: 努米底亞宗教以柏柏爾本土信仰為主，崇敬祖先、自然神祇、聖石與地方保護神，同時受布匿—迦太基宗教影響，部分地區也接觸希臘化宗教元素。
religion.en: Numidian religion was primarily based on indigenous Berber beliefs, including reverence for ancestors, natural divinities, sacred stones, and local protective deities, while also showing Punic-Carthaginian influence and some contact with Hellenistic religious elements.

philosophy.zh: 並無成體系的文字哲學傳統，社會觀念主要來自部族榮譽、勇武、忠誠、盟約、家族義務與對土地牧場的實際經驗。沿海接觸區則逐漸吸收布匿與希臘化政治觀念。
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophical tradition; social ideas were rooted mainly in tribal honor, valor, loyalty, alliance, family obligation, and practical knowledge of land and pasture. In contact zones near the coast, Punic and Hellenistic political ideas were gradually absorbed.

writingSystem.zh: 行政與外交場合常借用布匿文，口語則以柏柏爾語支為主；本地書寫傳統有限，記錄多依賴外來文字文化影響。
writingSystem.en: Punic script was often used in diplomatic and administrative contexts, while spoken life was dominated by Berber languages; indigenous writing traditions were limited, and record-keeping relied heavily on external literate influences.

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅長輕騎兵作戰、馬匹馴養、半乾旱地帶牧農結合生活方式，以及在內陸與海岸之間建立靈活商路。葬俗、巨石遺跡與地方王室儀式顯示出獨特的北非文化連續性。
culturalAchievements.en: Notable achievements included mastery of light cavalry warfare, horse breeding, mixed pastoral-agricultural lifeways in semi-arid lands, and the maintenance of flexible routes between the interior and the coast. Funerary customs, megalithic remains, and royal ritual practices reflected a distinctive North African cultural continuity.

languageFamily.zh: 阿非羅-亞細亞語系柏柏爾語支，並與布匿語有密切接觸
languageFamily.en: Afroasiatic, mainly Berber branches, with close contact with Punic

unit.zh: 努米底亞銀德拉克馬等值
努米底亞銀德拉克馬等值

gdpDescription.zh: 經濟以牧業為主，輔以穀物種植、林地資源、馬匹繁育與區域轉口貿易。其財富總量不及迦太基或托勒密埃及等高度城市化國家，但在北非內陸屬於相當強健的區域經濟。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on pastoralism, supported by grain cultivation, woodland resources, horse breeding, and regional transit trade. Its total wealth was far below that of highly urbanized states such as Carthage or Ptolemaic Egypt, but it was a comparatively strong regional economy within inland North Africa.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧、馬匹飼養、穀物種植、皮革加工、鹽與地方礦產交換、狩獵採集與商旅護送
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, horse breeding, grain cultivation, leather working, exchange in salt and local minerals, hunting and gathering, and caravan escort services

tradeGoods.zh: 馬匹、牛羊、皮革、獸皮、穀物、蜂蜜、蠟、木材、象牙轉運品、鹽與奴隸
tradeGoods.en: Horses, cattle, sheep, leather, hides, grain, honey, wax, timber, transit ivory, salt, and slaves

name.zh: 布匿與希臘式銀幣並用
name.en: Mixed Punic and Greek-style silver coinage

householdWealth.zh: 家庭財富多以牲畜、馬匹、穀倉儲備、金屬器物與家族依附人口衡量，而非單純貨幣持有。貴族之家可憑大量馬群與隨從展現地位。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured largely in livestock, horses, grain stores, metal goods, and dependent persons rather than in cash alone. Noble households displayed status through large horse herds and numerous retainers.

unit.zh: 努米底亞銀德拉克馬等值
努米底亞銀德拉克馬等值

economicSystem.zh: 以部族控制下的混合牧農經濟為主，帶有貢納、再分配與市場交換並存的特徵。對外經濟與迦太基沿海市場密切相連，也受到戰爭與結盟變化的強烈影響。
economicSystem.en: The economy was a tribal-controlled mixed pastoral-agrarian system combining tribute, redistribution, and market exchange. External economic life was closely tied to coastal markets linked to Carthage and was strongly affected by war and shifting alliances.

unit.zh: 努米底亞銀德拉克馬等值

source.zh: 商路通行稅與集市徵收
