description.en: Responsible for defending forts, storehouses, and settlement approaches; they could be expanded in wartime and in peacetime also served labor and security roles.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅；战时由最强部族首领或盟军推举统帅
commanderInChief.en: No permanent supreme commander; in war, command was taken by the strongest tribal chief or an elected coalition leader

technology.zh: 高卢擅长铁制武器、锁子甲、长盾、骑兵装备与木土复合工事。其冶铁与金工水平较高，但围城技术、后勤组织与持续统一指挥不及地中海强国。
technology.en: The Gauls were skilled in iron weapons, mail armor, long shields, cavalry equipment, and composite timber-earth fortifications. Their ironworking and metalcraft were strong, but siegecraft, logistics, and sustained unified command were weaker than those of major Mediterranean powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部族间竞争、日益扩张的罗马势力、阿尔卑斯与莱茵边缘的跨区域冲突，以及与伊比利亚、高卢南部贸易通道相关的争夺。
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal rivalry, the growing power of Rome, cross-regional conflict along the Alpine and Rhine frontiers, and competition over routes linked to Iberia and southern Gaul.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于今日法国大部、比利时部分地区、莱茵河西岸及邻近区域，以农村聚落、小型村社和若干大型奥皮杜姆为主；人口密度在河谷、平原与贸易节点较高。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across most of present-day France, parts of Belgium, the west bank of the Rhine, and adjacent regions, with settlement focused in rural communities, small villages, and a number of major oppida; density was highest in river valleys, plains, and trade nodes.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为部族贵族、骑士与德鲁伊；中层为自由农民、工匠和商贩；下层为依附民、债务附庸、战俘与奴隶。社会流动有限，但战争功绩、婚姻与酋长恩宠可改变地位。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of tribal nobles, horsemen, and druids; the middle included free farmers, artisans, and traders; the lower included dependents, debt clients, captives, and slaves. Social mobility was limited, but war service, marriage, and chiefly favor could alter status.

allies.zh: 无统一对外同盟体系；各部族常与邻近高卢部落、部分不列颠凯尔特人或伊比利亚凯尔特群体建立临时联盟。
allies.en: There was no single foreign alliance system; individual tribes often formed temporary alliances with neighboring Gaulish groups, some Brittonic Celts, or Celtic groups in Iberia.

enemies.zh: 无统一共同敌人，但许多高卢部族警惕罗马、马赛及其地中海盟友，同时也彼此竞争并与部分日耳曼群体发生边境冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single common enemy, but many Gaulish tribes were wary of Rome, Massalia and its Mediterranean partners, while also competing with one another and clashing with some Germanic groups on the frontier.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以婚盟、人质交换、贸易、佣兵服务、边界协定和临时战争同盟为核心；高卢政治高度分裂，因此对外政策因部族而异，重在维护声望、商路与地区均势。
foreignPolicy.en: Diplomacy centered on marriage alliances, hostage exchange, trade, mercenary service, border agreements, and temporary wartime coalitions; because Gaulish politics were highly fragmented, foreign policy varied by tribe and focused on prestige, trade routes, and regional balance.

era.zh: 晚期拉坦文化时代
era.en: Late La Tène period

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟铁器冶炼、锁子甲、改良长剑与盾牌、骑兵马具、铸币、木土城防、轮制陶与专业化金工装饰艺术
keyInnovations.en: Mature iron smelting, mail armor, improved swords and shields, cavalry horse gear, coinage, timber-earth fortifications, wheel-made pottery, and specialized decorative metalwork

strengths.zh: 人口充足，农业基础稳固，贵族骑兵与铁器制造强，地方社会韧性高，奥皮杜姆促进了手工业与贸易发展。
strengths.en: It possessed a large population, a solid agrarian base, strong noble cavalry and iron production, resilient local societies, and oppida that fostered craft production and trade.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，缺乏持久统一指挥和标准化财政，部族竞争削弱整体实力，难以长期对抗组织严密的国家型强权。
weaknesses.en: It was deeply politically fragmented, lacked durable unified command and standardized finance, and intertribal rivalry weakened overall strength, making sustained resistance to tightly organized state powers difficult.

outlook.zh: 221年前后的高卢总体稳定，但这种稳定建立在地区均势与部族分立之上。若外部强权持续介入，尤其是罗马向北推进，高卢诸部将面临被逐步征服、结盟重组或短暂统一反抗的前景。
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE Gaul was broadly stable, but that stability rested on regional balance and tribal division. If outside great powers intervened more persistently, especially with Roman expansion northward, the Gauls would face gradual conquest, alliance realignment, or brief episodes of unified resistance.

description.zh: 今日法国及邻近地区由多个高卢部族与城塞中心主导。
description.en: What is now France and adjacent regions was dominated by many Gaulish tribes and fortified centers.

name.zh: 伊比利亚诸城邦与部族

dynasty.zh: 地方王族与部落政权世系
dynasty.en: Local royal lines and tribal polities

capital.zh: 无单一都城，重心位于萨贡图姆以南的多处中心聚落
capital.en: No single capital; multiple centers south of Saguntum

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落君主制与贵族寡头制混合体
governmentForm.en: Decentralized mixture of tribal monarchy and aristocratic oligarchy

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、地方贵族、战士集团、自由民农牧者、手工业者与依附人口构成；沿海和河谷聚落较分层，内陆堡寨则更具部落与亲族色彩。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of clans, local aristocrats, warrior groups, free peasant-pastoralists, artisans, and dependent populations; coastal and riverine settlements were more stratified, while inland hillforts retained stronger tribal and kin-based structures.

rulingClass.zh: 地方王公家族、骑士贵族、城寨长老与掌握土地和武装的显贵
rulingClass.en: Local princely families, mounted aristocrats, fortified-town elders, and notable landholding warrior elites

succession.zh: 以家族世袭为主，但常受贵族拥立、婚姻联盟与武力竞争影响
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within leading families, though often shaped by noble acclamation, marriage alliances, and armed rivalry

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构；各城寨、山城和部族领地由地方王公、议事贵族与战士随从共同治理。较大的中心对周边村落和小聚落征收贡赋、组织防务并主持祭祀与外交。
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; individual oppida, hillforts, and tribal territories were governed by local princes, aristocratic councils, and warrior retainers. Larger centers collected tribute from surrounding villages and smaller settlements, organized defense, and managed ritual and diplomacy.

function.zh: 管理首领亲兵、宴飨、使者接待、贡物分配与宫廷工匠
function.en: Managed the ruler's retainers, feasting, reception of envoys, distribution of tribute, and court artisans

function.zh: 商议战争、盟约、继承、争端调解与对外关系
function.en: Deliberated on war, alliances, succession, dispute settlement, and external relations

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、金属与劳役，管理仓窖和再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, metals, and labor dues, managed storehouses, and oversaw redistribution

function.zh: 维护城墙、守门、警戒、集结民兵并监督武器储备
function.en: Maintained walls, guarded gates, kept watch, mustered militias, and supervised weapon stores

function.zh: 管理地方神祇祭祀、祖先崇拜、奉献物与神圣空间
function.en: Managed cults of local deities, ancestor observances, votive offerings, and sacred spaces

function.zh: 在沿海和主要市场监督度量、交换、外来商人往来及部分城邦式铸币活动
function.en: Oversaw measures, exchange, visiting merchants, and limited city-based coin production at coastal and major market centers

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理依赖城寨首领、村社长老与附属聚落头人。控制力强弱不一，通常通过人身依附、贡赋网络和军事保护来维系，而非成文官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on hillfort chiefs, village elders, and heads of subordinate settlements. Control varied greatly and was usually maintained through personal dependence, tribute networks, and military protection rather than a formal written bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、部落仲裁、血亲赔偿、宣誓与首领裁断为核心；重视财产、婚姻、侮辱与杀伤的补偿。与希腊人、腓尼基—布匿传统接触较深地区，契约和城邑规范更发达。
legalSystem.en: Law was centered on customary practice, tribal arbitration, kin-compensation, oaths, and judgments by chiefs; compensation for property, marriage matters, insult, and injury was important. Areas with stronger Greek and Phoenician-Punic contact showed more developed contractual and urban norms.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，主要通过谷物、葡萄酒、油料、牲畜、金属、战利品分成、港市收费及劳役征收贡赋。战时附庸聚落需提供兵员和运输。
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform tax system; revenue came mainly through tribute in grain, wine, oil products, livestock, metals, shares of booty, market or port dues, and labor obligations. In wartime, subordinate communities also provided troops and transport.

religion.zh: 以地方多神崇拜、祖先祭祀、圣山圣林与泉源崇拜为主，并受腓尼基—布匿及希腊宗教观念影响。贵族常以武器、雕像和金属器奉献于圣所。
religion.en: Religion centered on local polytheistic cults, ancestor observance, and reverence for sacred hills, groves, and springs, with influence from Phoenician-Punic and Greek religious ideas. Aristocrats often dedicated weapons, statues, and metal objects at sanctuaries.

philosophy.zh: 无成体系哲学学派；价值观强调勇武、家族荣誉、馈赠关系、祖先传统与地方神圣秩序。沿海地区因与希腊和布匿世界接触而吸收部分外来观念。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical school; values emphasized martial prowess, family honor, gift exchange, ancestral tradition, and local sacred order. Coastal regions absorbed some outside ideas through contact with Greek and Punic worlds.

writingSystem.zh: 使用多种古伊比利亚文字，尤以东北伊比利亚字母音节混合系统影响较广；东南地区亦见南方伊比利亚书写传统。文字主要用于铭文、陶器标记、铅板记录、墓葬与货币。
writingSystem.en: Several Paleohispanic scripts were in use, especially the Northeastern Iberian semi-syllabary, while Southeastern Iberian writing traditions also appeared in the region. Writing was mainly used for inscriptions, pottery marks, lead tablets, funerary contexts, and coin legends.

culturalAchievements.zh: 石雕与墓葬艺术发达，延续了早期伊比利亚贵族雕塑传统；堡寨规划、城墙建筑、彩绘陶器、金属工艺、骑战文化和地方铸币均具特色。沿海地区在贸易与文化上深受地中海网络影响。
culturalAchievements.en: Stone sculpture and funerary art were notable, continuing earlier Iberian aristocratic sculptural traditions; fortified settlement planning, wall construction, painted pottery, metalworking, mounted warrior culture, and local coinage were distinctive. Coastal areas were deeply shaped by Mediterranean trade and cultural networks.

languageFamily.zh: 伊比利亚语，属前罗马伊比利亚半岛本地语言群，未被可靠归入印欧语系
languageFamily.en: Iberian language, part of the pre-Roman indigenous languages of the peninsula and not securely classified as Indo-European

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、矿产开采、地方手工业和区域交换为基础。东南沿海与内陆金属产区联系紧密，部分城寨通过地中海贸易获益，但总体仍是分散的部落型经济，财富集中于贵族与控制通道的中心聚落。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, herding, mining, local craft production, and regional exchange. The southeastern coast was closely linked to inland metal zones, and some centers profited from Mediterranean trade, but the overall system remained a decentralized tribal economy with wealth concentrated among aristocrats and settlements controlling routes.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、葡萄与橄榄栽培、牧羊与养马、铁铜银铅开采与冶炼、陶器制作、纺织、盐业、武器与饰物制造
mainIndustries.en: Cereal farming, viticulture and olive cultivation, sheep herding and horse breeding, extraction and smelting of iron, copper, silver, and lead, pottery production, textiles, salt working, and manufacture of weapons and ornaments

tradeGoods.zh: 输出金属、盐、皮革、羊毛、染色织物、蜂蜜、蜡、奴隶、陶器及部分农产；输入精制陶器、酒、奢侈金属器、首饰、外来武器样式与地中海商品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included metals, salt, hides, wool, dyed textiles, honey, wax, slaves, pottery, and some agricultural produce; imports included fine ceramics, wine, luxury metalware, jewelry, foreign weapon styles, and Mediterranean goods.

name.zh: 地方铸币与称量银并用

unitName.zh: 德拉克马、阿斯及地方币

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓、陶器、工具与亲族网络；贵族则拥有马匹、武器、金银饰物、奴仆和外来奢侈品。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth consisted of land-use rights, livestock, stored grain, pottery, tools, and kin connections; aristocrats possessed horses, weapons, precious ornaments, servants, and imported luxury goods.

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、部落贡赋、贵族再分配和市场交换并存的混合经济。沿海聚落更趋商业化，内陆地区则仍以自给和附庸关系为主。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combined household production, tribal tribute, aristocratic redistribution, and market exchange. Coastal settlements were more commercialized, while inland areas remained more self-sufficient and dependent on patronal ties.

category.zh: 仓储、运输与粮食再分配
外交、雇佣兵与使节往来

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，财富分散储存在各王公居所、圣所、金属库存、谷仓和贵族私藏中。战时财政高度依赖临时征集、掠获和贵族个人资源。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified treasury; wealth was dispersed among princely residences, sanctuaries, metal stocks, granaries, and aristocratic hoards. Wartime finance depended heavily on emergency levies, plunder, and the personal resources of elites.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持武装追随者、修筑防御、巩固附庸关系和保障宴飨再分配为主。221 BCE时因第二次布匿战争波及伊比利亚东部与南部，许多政权财政趋于紧张并更依赖非常征发。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining armed retainers, building defenses, reinforcing subordinate ties, and supporting feasting and redistribution. In 221 BCE, as the Second Punic War affected eastern and southern Iberia, many polities faced fiscal strain and relied more on extraordinary exactions.

description.zh: 由地方贵族及其随从组成，是伊比利亚军事声望的核心，擅长突击、追击和展示性作战。
description.en: Composed of local aristocrats and their retainers, this arm formed the prestige core of Iberian warfare and excelled in shock action, pursuit, and status display.

description.zh: 装备长矛、大盾、剑与头盔，多来自较富裕自由民和战士集团，负责守城和近战主力。
description.en: Armed with spears, large shields, swords, and helmets, these men came largely from wealthier freemen and warrior groups and served as the main force in defense and close combat.

description.zh: 数量最多，机动性高，适合山地、伏击和袭扰，是部落战争与反入侵防御中的关键兵种。
description.en: The most numerous arm, highly mobile and well suited to rugged terrain, ambushes, and skirmishing; they were central in tribal warfare and anti-invasion defense.

description.zh: 由各城寨驻守者和临时征召居民构成，负责守门、巡逻、输运和应急防御。
description.en: Made up of settlement garrisons and temporarily mobilized inhabitants, they guarded gates, patrolled, transported supplies, and provided emergency defense.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各部落王公与战首分区指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; leadership was divided among tribal princes and war leaders

technology.zh: 武器包括伊比利亚直剑与弯刀、长矛、标枪、圆盾或大型盾、锁子甲与青铜或铁盔在精英中有限使用。擅长依托山地堡寨、石墙和复杂地形作战，受凯尔特、希腊与布匿军事影响。
technology.en: Weapons included Iberian straight swords and falcatas, spears, javelins, round or larger shields, with mail and bronze or iron helmets in limited elite use. Warfare made strong use of hillforts, stone walls, and broken terrain, with influences from Celtic, Greek, and Punic military practice.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自迦太基势力扩张、与罗马相关的战争外溢、邻近凯尔特伊比利亚部族冲突、沿海控制权争夺及各伊比利亚政权之间的长期竞争。
threats.en: Main threats came from Carthaginian expansion, the spillover of war involving Rome, conflict with neighboring Celtiberian peoples, struggles over coastal control, and chronic rivalry among Iberian polities themselves.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于东南伊比利亚沿海平原、河谷、丘陵山地与设防城寨网络之中。多数居民生活在农村村落、农庄和小型堡寨，少数集中于较大的奥庇杜姆式中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across the southeastern Iberian coast, river valleys, uplands, and networks of fortified settlements. Most people lived in rural villages, farmsteads, and small hillforts, with a minority concentrated in larger oppidum-like centers.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为地方王公、骑士贵族与祭祀权威；中层为自由战士、富农、牧民和工匠；下层为普通农户、佃附人口、债务依附者与奴隶。社会流动有限，但军事功绩、婚姻联盟和财富积累可提升地位。
socialClasses.en: Upper strata consisted of local princes, mounted aristocrats, and ritual authorities; the middle included free warriors, prosperous farmers, herders, and artisans; the lower included ordinary cultivators, dependent populations, debt-bound persons, and slaves. Social mobility was limited, but military success, marriage alliance, and wealth accumulation could raise status.

allies.zh: 无持久统一联盟；部分政权与迦太基结盟或依附，另一些与希腊化沿海商人、邻近伊比利亚部落或反迦太基力量保持务实合作。
allies.en: There was no lasting unified alliance system; some polities were allied with or subordinate to Carthage, while others maintained pragmatic ties with Hellenized coastal traders, neighboring Iberian groups, or anti-Carthaginian forces.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象因部族而异，包括迦太基扩张势力、竞争性的伊比利亚邻邦、凯尔特伊比利亚部族，以及未来可能介入的罗马势力。
enemies.en: Enemies varied by polity and included expanding Carthaginian forces, rival Iberian neighbors, Celtiberian groups, and the potentially intervening Roman power.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以生存和地方利益为中心，偏好短期联盟、婚姻纽带、人质交换、贡纳安排与雇佣兵服务。221 BCE时在大国竞争阴影下，多数首领采取摇摆与观望政策，以保全城寨和贸易通道。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on survival and local advantage, favoring short-term alliances, marriage ties, hostage exchange, tributary arrangements, and mercenary service. In 221 BCE, under the shadow of great-power competition, most leaders pursued hedging and watchful pragmatism to preserve their strongholds and trade routes.

era.zh: 伊比利亚铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late Iberian Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 石砌堡寨与城墙、铁器冶炼、地方铸币、彩绘陶器、铅板书写、改良骑战装备、地中海式酒油储运器与区域贸易度量习惯。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included stone-built hillforts and walls, iron metallurgy, local coinage, painted ceramics, writing on lead tablets, improved mounted warfare equipment, Mediterranean-style storage and transport containers for wine and oil, and regional trade measures.

strengths.zh: 地形复杂、堡寨密布、战士传统强、骑兵素质高，并拥有矿产和沿海贸易资源。地方政权灵活，能迅速结盟、转向或采取游击与袭扰战术。
strengths.en: Strengths included difficult terrain, dense fortified settlements, a strong warrior tradition, high-quality cavalry, and access to minerals and coastal trade. Local polities were flexible and could quickly realign, shift loyalties, or use guerrilla and raiding tactics.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权和持久统帅体系，内部竞争频繁，财政和军队难以长期集中；面对迦太基或罗马这类组织化强国时，容易被分化、收买或逐个击破。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included the absence of unified statehood and enduring central command, frequent internal rivalry, and difficulty sustaining concentrated finance and armies over long periods; against highly organized powers such as Carthage or Rome, they were vulnerable to division, bribery, and piecemeal defeat.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE的伊比利亚东南诸政权正处在关键转折点。短期内仍能凭借地形、防御与机动战保持自主，但随着第二次布匿战争扩大，它们将越来越被卷入迦太基与罗马的争霸之中，最终面临附庸化、征服或被重组为罗马化地方秩序的一部分。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE the southeastern Iberian polities stood at a critical turning point. In the short term they could still preserve autonomy through terrain, fortification, and mobile warfare, but as the Second Punic War widened they would be drawn ever more deeply into the struggle between Carthage and Rome, ultimately facing subordination, conquest, or incorporation into an emerging Romanized provincial order.

description.zh: 东南伊比利亚沿海和内陆存在众多伊比利亚王公政体与设防聚落。
description.en: Southeastern Iberia contained many Iberian princely polities and fortified settlements.

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部落联盟与酋邦体制，各部落由酋长、贵族家族与自由民大会共同治理
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal confederations and chiefdoms, with tribes governed by chiefs, noble families, and assemblies of free men

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、氏族与部落为核心，自由民构成主体，另有贵族战士、随从、依附民与奴隶
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on kin groups, clans, and tribes, with free commoners forming the majority alongside noble warriors, retainers, dependents, and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 部落贵族、战士首领与宗族长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal nobles, war leaders, and clan elders

succession.zh: 多为家族世袭并经部众认可，战时统帅常凭威望、武功与支持而产生
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families but subject to recognition by the people; wartime leaders often emerged through prestige, military success, and support

structure.zh: 无统一国家机构；治理层级通常为家族、村社、氏族、部落与临时联盟。重大事务由自由民集会讨论，酋长负责仲裁、祭祀主持、馈赠分配与军事动员
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; authority typically operated through household, village, clan, tribe, and temporary confederation. Major matters were debated in assemblies of free men, while chiefs handled arbitration, ritual leadership, gift distribution, and military mobilization

function.zh: 协助酋长处理部落协调、对外谈判、馈赠分配与召集战士
function.en: Assisted the chief in tribal coordination, external negotiation, gift distribution, and calling up warriors

name.en: Council of Elders and Judicial Assembly

function.zh: 主持纠纷调解、血亲赔偿、誓约见证与习惯法裁断
function.en: Oversaw dispute mediation, kin-compensation settlements, oath witnessing, and judgments under customary law

function.zh: 主持祭礼、占卜、圣林仪式与公共神圣传统
function.en: Conducted sacrifices, divination, sacred grove rites, and public religious traditions

function.zh: 组织粮食、牲畜、手工品与劳役的征发，多为临时性与地方性
function.en: Organized collection of grain, livestock, craft goods, and labor services, usually on a temporary and local basis

function.zh: 负责战时传令、召集盟部与编组战士
function.en: Handled wartime communication, summoned allied groups, and organized warriors

localAdmin.zh: 基层管理依托村落、家族与氏族长；并无常设文官体系，日常事务靠惯例、声望与面对面协商运作
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on villages, households, and clan heads; there was no permanent bureaucracy, and daily affairs operated through custom, prestige, and face-to-face negotiation

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为核心，强调誓言、血亲责任、赔偿金与集会裁断；重罪或叛逆可能遭放逐、复仇或处死
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, emphasizing oaths, kin responsibility, compensation payments, and assembly judgments; grave crimes or treachery could lead to exile, feud, or execution

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一定额税制，资源主要通过酋长征收贡物、战利品分配、宴飨馈赠网络与劳役征发获得
taxationSystem.en: There was no standardized tax system; resources were obtained mainly through chiefly tribute, redistribution of war booty, feast-gift networks, and labor levies

religion.zh: 多神信仰与祖先崇拜并存，崇敬天空、雷电、战争、生育与自然灵力，圣林、沼泽与祭坛为重要宗教场所
religion.en: Polytheistic belief and ancestor veneration coexisted, honoring powers of sky, thunder, war, fertility, and nature; sacred groves, bogs, and open-air sanctuaries were important religious sites

philosophy.zh: 尚无成体系书面哲学，价值观强调勇武、忠诚、亲族义务、名誉、复仇与馈赠互惠
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophy, but core values emphasized courage, loyalty, kin obligation, honor, vengeance, and reciprocal gift exchange

writingSystem.zh: 尚无普遍本土文字，信息主要依靠口述传统、记忆、符号装饰与外来接触中的少量借用记号
writingSystem.en: There was no widespread native writing system; information was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, memory, symbolic decoration, and limited borrowed marks through foreign contact

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长口头史诗、战歌、金属饰物、木工、织造、琥珀贸易与长屋聚落传统；在北欧青铜与早期铁器传统基础上形成独特部落文化
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in oral epic, war songs, metal ornaments, woodworking, weaving, amber exchange, and longhouse settlement traditions; on the foundations of Nordic Bronze Age and early Iron Age traditions, they developed a distinct tribal culture

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族早期方言群
languageFamily.en: Early dialects of the Germanic branch of the Indo-European language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、林地采集、铁器手工业与区域性互市为主，货币化程度极低。总体产出不低，但分散、季节性强，难以形成国家财政规模
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence farming, herding, forest gathering, ironworking, and regional barter exchange, with very low monetization. Total output was substantial, but highly dispersed and seasonal, making state-scale finance difficult

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、养牛养猪、畜牧、铁冶与锻造、琥珀采集与转运、纺织、木器制作、制革
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, cattle and pig raising, pastoralism, iron smelting and smithing, amber collection and transit, textiles, woodworking, and leatherworking

tradeGoods.zh: 琥珀、兽皮、奴隶、蜂蜜、蜡、牲畜、毛织品、铁器、盐、木材，以及从凯尔特与地中海世界获得的酒器、金属器与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Amber, hides, slaves, honey, wax, livestock, woolens, iron goods, salt, and timber, along with vessels, metalware, and luxury goods obtained from the Celtic and Mediterranean worlds

name.zh: 以物易物与称量金属并用

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜数量、武器、首饰、储粮与可动员亲族随从，而非成文产权或大量铸币
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed chiefly in land use, livestock numbers, weapons, ornaments, stored grain, and mobilizable kin and retainers rather than formal property records or large coin holdings

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、部落再分配、馈赠经济与有限互市相结合的低货币化经济
economicSystem.en: A low-monetized economy combining household production, tribal redistribution, gift exchange, and limited market interaction

source.zh: 部落贡物与酋长馈赠集中
source.en: Tribal tribute and concentration of chiefly gifts
Exactions from trade routes and markets

category.zh: 酋长亲随与联盟维系馈赠
category.en: Chiefly retinues and gifts to maintain alliances

category.zh: 防御工事、储粮与集会场所
category.en: Defensive works, grain storage, and assembly sites

category.zh: 使节往来与贸易交换支出
category.en: Envoys and exchange expenditures in trade

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一中央国库；财富分散储存在各部酋长的牲畜群、粮仓、金属器、首饰与战利品中，少量贵金属作为威望物而非标准财政储备
treasuryDescription.en: There was no unified central treasury; wealth was stored in dispersed form among chiefs as herds, granaries, metal goods, ornaments, and booty, with small amounts of precious metal held as prestige goods rather than standardized fiscal reserves

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作重在维系追随者与联盟，而非积累官僚税收；稳定时期以贡物和馈赠平衡权力，战时则依靠征发与掠夺补充资源
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining followers and alliances rather than building bureaucratic taxation; in stable times tribute and gift exchange balanced power, while in war resources were supplemented by levies and raiding

description.zh: 以自由民战士为核心，装备长矛、盾牌、短剑或斧，作战依赖密集冲锋、亲族协同与地形熟悉
description.en: Centered on free warriors armed with spears, shields, short swords, or axes; combat relied on close charges, kin cohesion, and familiarity with terrain

description.zh: 由酋长身边的精锐随从构成，装备较好，是首领威望与战斗力的核心
description.en: Composed of elite retainers around chiefs, better equipped than ordinary warriors and central to chiefly prestige and combat power

description.zh: 承担侦察、伏击、边境袭扰与森林作战，机动性高但缺乏统一训练
description.en: Handled scouting, ambush, frontier raiding, and woodland fighting; highly mobile but lacking standardized training

description.zh: 数量有限，主要用于传令、追击、侦察与彰显身份，尚未形成大规模重骑兵传统
description.en: Limited in number, used mainly for messaging, pursuit, reconnaissance, and status display; there was not yet a large-scale heavy cavalry tradition

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各部战时首领分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; wartime leaders commanded separately by tribe

technology.zh: 处于欧洲早期铁器时代后期，广泛使用铁制矛头、剑、斧与木盾；防具有限，缺少攻城器械、标准化后勤与大规模永久工事体系
technology.en: They were in the later European Early Iron Age, widely using iron spearheads, swords, axes, and wooden shields; armor was limited, and they lacked siege engines, standardized logistics, and large permanent fortification systems

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自部落间冲突、与凯尔特群体的边境竞争、资源压力以及南方更复杂国家体系扩张带来的长期风险
threats.en: Main threats came from intertribal conflict, frontier competition with Celtic groups, resource pressures, and the longer-term risk posed by expansion of more complex southern state systems

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散居住于中欧与北欧广大森林、河谷、湿地与沿海地区，多为小型村落、农庄与部落聚居地，人口密度较低
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across broad areas of central and northern Europe in forests, river valleys, wetlands, and coastal zones, mostly in small villages, farmsteads, and tribal settlements with low population density

name.zh: 无真正城市，较大聚居中心如日德兰与易北河流域部落集会地
name.en: No true cities; larger settlement centers included tribal meeting places in Jutland and the Elbe region

name.zh: 波罗的海南岸琥珀集散地

socialClasses.zh: 上层为贵族家族、酋长与精锐战士；中层为自由农民与牧民；下层为依附民、债役者与奴隶。社会地位与武功、血统、牲畜财富和追随者数量密切相关
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of noble families, chiefs, and elite warriors; the middle consisted of free farmers and herders; the lower included dependents, debt bondsmen, and slaves. Status was closely tied to martial success, lineage, livestock wealth, and the number of followers

allies.zh: 无统一同盟体系；常见的是部落间临时结盟，也与部分邻近凯尔特群体保持贸易或互不侵犯关系
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; temporary alliances among tribes were common, and some neighboring Celtic groups maintained trade ties or non-aggression arrangements

enemies.zh: 无统一国家级敌人，但与若干部落、部分凯尔特群体及争夺土地与贸易通道的邻近社群存在长期冲突
enemies.en: There was no single state-level enemy, but long-running conflicts existed with rival tribes, some Celtic groups, and neighboring communities competing for land and trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存、防御、迁徙调适、互市与机会性袭掠并行；尚无统一长期大战略，各部依地理位置与资源条件分别行事
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy combined survival, defense, migration adjustment, barter exchange, and opportunistic raiding; there was no unified long-term grand strategy, and tribes acted according to geography and resource conditions

era.zh: 欧洲早期铁器时代晚期
era.en: Late European Early Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器锻造普及、木制长屋、简易防御土木工事、改良农具、纺织技术、琥珀与金属远距离交换网络
keyInnovations.en: Widespread ironworking, timber longhouses, simple earth-and-timber defenses, improved agricultural tools, textile production, and long-distance amber and metal exchange networks

strengths.zh: 社会韧性强，亲族纽带稳固，适应森林与寒冷环境的能力突出；战士传统旺盛，机动灵活，对地形熟悉，并拥有琥珀等区域贸易资源
strengths.en: They possessed strong social resilience, solid kin bonds, and notable adaptation to forested and cold environments; warrior traditions were vigorous, mobility was high, terrain knowledge was strong, and they controlled regional trade resources such as amber

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，缺乏统一征税、常备军、文字行政和持久联盟机制；农业剩余有限，城镇化低，对复杂国家的组织化战争能力不足
weaknesses.en: They were politically highly fragmented and lacked unified taxation, a standing army, written administration, and durable alliance mechanisms; agricultural surplus was limited, urbanization was low, and they were less capable of organized warfare against complex states

outlook.zh: 221年前后的日耳曼诸部总体稳定，但仍处于松散部落网络阶段。未来随着人口流动、对外贸易加深、与凯尔特和罗马世界接触增强，部分部落有可能形成更大的联盟并逐渐进入地中海史家的视野
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE the Germanic peoples were broadly stable but remained in the stage of loose tribal networks. In the future, with population movements, deeper trade, and intensified contact with Celtic and Roman worlds, some tribes could form larger confederations and increasingly enter the view of Mediterranean historians

description.zh: 中北欧广大地区由分散的日耳曼部族网络占据。
description.en: A wide zone of central and northern Europe was occupied by dispersed Germanic tribal networks.

ruler.zh: 阿尔卡马尼（约同时代统治者）

governmentForm.zh: 以神王观念为核心的世袭君主制，统治尼罗河中上游诸地区
governmentForm.en: A hereditary monarchy centered on sacral kingship, ruling the middle and upper Nile regions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷贵族、祭司集团、地方总督与军政官员、工匠与商人、农民牧民以及依附劳动力构成；王权与神庙体系相互支撑，地方社会保留较强区域性。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the royal house and court nobles, priestly groups, provincial governors and military-administrative officials, artisans and merchants, peasants and pastoralists, and dependent laborers; royal power and temple institutions reinforced each other, while local society retained strong regional character.

rulingClass.zh: 麦罗埃王室、宫廷贵族、神庙祭司及高级军政官员
rulingClass.en: The Meroitic royal family, court nobility, temple priests, and senior military-administrative officials

succession.zh: 王位通常在王室内部世袭，继承原则受王族血统、宫廷支持与宗教合法性共同影响，王太后与王族女性在继承政治中具有重要作用。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal house, with succession shaped by dynastic bloodline, court support, and religious legitimacy; queen mothers and royal women played an important role in succession politics.

structure.zh: 中央政权设于麦罗埃，由国王统领宫廷、神庙和军政体系；地方以尼罗河谷重要城镇和区域中心为节点，由总督、要塞长官和神庙管理者共同治理。
structure.en: The central government was based at Meroe, where the king directed the court, temple, and military-administrative system; the provinces were organized around major Nile settlements and regional centers, governed jointly by provincial officials, fortress commanders, and temple authorities.

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、礼仪、使节接待及王命传达
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, ceremonial functions, reception of envoys, and transmission of royal orders

function.zh: 监督主要神庙、祭祀活动、神庙地产与宗教合法性事务
function.en: Oversaw major temples, ritual activity, temple estates, and matters of religious legitimacy

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、手工业品与贸易关税，管理仓储和再分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, craft goods, and trade duties, and managed storage and redistribution

function.zh: 管理地方官员、边境站点、河谷交通线及沙漠通道
function.en: Managed provincial officials, frontier posts, Nile transport routes, and desert corridors

function.zh: 统筹驻军、征召、武器储备、战象与边境防务
function.en: Coordinated garrisons, levies, weapons stores, war elephants, and frontier defense

function.zh: 监督铁器生产、王家工坊、采矿、窑业与大型工程物资
function.en: Supervised iron production, royal workshops, mining, kilns, and supplies for major works

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托尼罗河沿岸城镇、神庙中心与军事据点展开。下努比亚、第四至第六瀑布间地区及东部通向红海的通道均设有王室代表、驻军和税务征收点。地方首领在接受王权承认后保留部分自治权。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on Nile towns, temple centers, and military posts. Lower Nubia, the regions between the Fourth and Sixth Cataracts, and eastern corridors toward the Red Sea all contained royal representatives, garrisons, and tax collection points. Local chiefs retained limited autonomy once recognized by the crown.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、习惯法、神庙规范和行政裁断为基础。土地、赋役、贸易、婚姻与继承事务多由地方官、祭司和王室法官共同处理，重大案件上诉至宫廷。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on royal decrees, customary law, temple norms, and administrative judgment. Matters of land, taxation, labor obligations, trade, marriage, and inheritance were often handled by local officials, priests, and royal judges, with major cases appealable to the court.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以实物为主，包括谷物、牲畜、黄金、铁器、手工业制品及劳役；尼罗河航运与沙漠商道征收关税，王室和神庙地产直接向中央提供收入。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was mainly in kind, including grain, livestock, gold, iron goods, craft products, and labor service; customs duties were levied on Nile shipping and desert trade routes, while royal and temple estates contributed revenue directly to the center.

religion.zh: 库施宗教融合本地努比亚传统与埃及宗教元素，崇拜阿蒙、阿佩德马克、伊西斯、奥西里斯等神祇；国王被视为神圣秩序的维护者，神庙祭祀与王权合法性密切相连。
religion.en: Kushite religion blended local Nubian traditions with Egyptian religious elements, venerating deities such as Amun, Apedemak, Isis, and Osiris; the king was seen as a guardian of sacred order, and temple ritual was closely tied to royal legitimacy.

philosophy.zh: 并无如希腊或中国般成体系的哲学学派，思想核心体现为王权神授、祖先崇敬、神庙宇宙秩序观，以及对土地、水源与丰产循环的宗教理解。
philosophy.en: There was no formal philosophical tradition comparable to Greece or China; the intellectual core lay in divinely sanctioned kingship, ancestor veneration, temple cosmology, and religious understandings of land, water, and cycles of fertility.

writingSystem.zh: 使用麦罗埃文字，并在部分宗教和纪念性场合延续使用埃及圣书体或其传统。麦罗埃文字包括表音符号与元音记写，是国家行政与王室铭文的重要工具。
writingSystem.en: The kingdom used the Meroitic script, while Egyptian hieroglyphic tradition persisted in some religious and monumental contexts. Meroitic, with phonetic signs and vowel notation, served as an important medium for state administration and royal inscriptions.

culturalAchievements.zh: 麦罗埃时期在金字塔建筑、神庙装饰、石刻艺术、铁器冶炼、陶器生产和王室葬俗方面成就显著；其文化形成了独立于埃及但深受其影响的尼罗河上游文明风格。
culturalAchievements.en: The Meroitic period saw notable achievements in pyramid construction, temple decoration, stone carving, ironworking, pottery production, and royal funerary customs; it produced an upper Nile civilization with a distinct style that, while influenced by Egypt, remained clearly independent.

languageFamily.zh: 麦罗埃语尚未被现代完全释读，通常被视为东北非语言环境中的独特语言，可能与东苏丹语支有关
languageFamily.en: The Meroitic language is not fully deciphered in modern scholarship; it is generally treated as a distinct language of the Northeast African linguistic sphere, possibly related to Eastern Sudanic

gdpDescription.zh: 库施经济以尼罗河农业、畜牧、黄金与铁器生产、以及区域转运贸易为基础。按前现代农业王国标准，整体经济稳健但仍高度依赖河谷产出和王室再分配体系。
gdpDescription.en: The Kushite economy was based on Nile agriculture, pastoralism, gold and iron production, and regional transit trade. By the standards of a premodern agrarian kingdom, it was solid but still highly dependent on riverine output and royal redistribution.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉与洪泛农业、牧畜业、金矿开采、铁器冶炼、陶器烧制、石材加工、王家建筑与河运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Floodplain and irrigated agriculture, animal husbandry, gold mining, iron smelting, pottery firing, stone working, royal construction, and riverine trade

tradeGoods.zh: 黄金、铁器、象牙、乌木、牲畜、皮革、谷物、陶器、奴隶、香料转口品以及来自非洲内陆和红海方向的奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Gold, iron goods, ivory, ebony, livestock, hides, grain, pottery, slaves, transshipped aromatics, and luxury items from inner Africa and Red Sea routes

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、工具和亲族网络；富裕家庭与神庙、地方贵族和商队经营有关，持有更多牲畜、贵金属与劳动力资源。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed in land-use access, livestock, grain stores, tools, and kinship networks; wealthy households linked to temples, local elites, or caravan trade held larger herds, precious metals, and labor resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业基础上的王室—神庙再分配经济为核心，并结合地方交换、商队贸易和金属价值计量；国家对战略资源、长途贸易节点与部分工坊保持较强控制。
economicSystem.en: The economic system centered on a royal-temple redistributive order built on agriculture, combined with local exchange, caravan trade, and metal-value accounting; the state maintained strong control over strategic resources, long-distance trade nodes, and some workshops.

source.en: Income from royal and temple estates

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库由贵金属、谷物储备、牲畜、工坊制成品和神庙积储共同构成，并非全部以铸币或单一金属形式存在。稳定时期能够支持祭祀、驻军和大型建筑开支。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of precious metals, grain reserves, livestock, workshop products, and temple stores rather than coin alone or a single metal form. In stable years it could support rituals, garrisons, and major building expenditures.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，强调维持粮仓、边防驻军和宗教工程的长期供给。国家通过控制战略资源与关税来弥补农业波动，避免对地方社群征收过重现金税负。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively conservative, emphasizing sustained support for granaries, frontier garrisons, and religious works. The state balanced agricultural fluctuations through control of strategic resources and customs duties, avoiding excessive cash burdens on local communities.

description.zh: 步兵是军队主体，装备长矛、弓、盾和部分铁制兵器，用于守卫尼罗河谷城镇、边境据点和随军远征。
description.en: Infantry formed the core of the army, equipped with spears, bows, shields, and some iron weapons, and was used to guard Nile settlements, frontier posts, and accompany expeditions.

description.zh: 努比亚弓箭传统久负盛名，弓箭兵在河谷作战、边境袭扰和防守据点中极具价值。
description.en: Nubian archery was long renowned, and archers were highly valuable in Nile warfare, frontier raiding, and defense of strongpoints.

description.zh: 包括有限的骑乘兵与礼仪性或战术用途尚存的战车单位，主要用于侦察、传令和机动支援。
description.en: These included limited mounted troops and chariot units still retained for ceremonial or tactical purposes, mainly for scouting, communications, and mobile support.

description.zh: 王室维持小规模精锐部队和少量战象，用于彰显威望、护卫君主并在特定战场发挥冲击作用。
description.en: The crown maintained a small elite corps and a limited number of war elephants, used to project prestige, guard the monarch, and provide shock value in selected engagements.

technology.zh: 军队使用铁制武器、复合或强化弓、长矛、皮革与木制盾牌，并依托尼罗河运输和边境要塞体系。与地中海强国相比，其攻城技术和重装体系较弱，但在河谷与沙漠边缘作战经验丰富。
technology.en: The army used iron weapons, composite or reinforced bows, spears, leather and wooden shields, and relied on Nile transport and frontier fortifications. Compared with major Mediterranean states, its siege methods and heavy equipment were weaker, but it was experienced in warfare along the river valley and desert margins.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方托勒密埃及的边境竞争、下努比亚局势波动、沙漠商道上的袭扰，以及南方和东部边缘地区部族的周期性不稳。
threats.en: The main threats came from frontier rivalry with Ptolemaic Egypt to the north, instability in Lower Nubia, raiding along desert trade routes, and periodic unrest among southern and eastern frontier groups.

populationDescription.zh: 全国人口约一百万，主要分布于尼罗河中上游可耕河谷、麦罗埃周边农业区、若干地方城镇以及牧业和采矿地带。人口密度沿河显著高于周边草原和沙漠地区。
populationDescription.en: The kingdom's population was about one million, concentrated in the cultivable middle and upper Nile valley, agricultural zones around Meroe, several regional towns, and pastoral and mining districts. Population density was much higher along the river than in the surrounding steppe and desert.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族位居顶层，其次为祭司、军政官员和地方首领；中层包括工匠、商人、熟练冶铁者和神庙管理人员；底层为农民、牧民、矿工、劳役者与依附人口。社会流动有限，但军功、神庙服务和地方权势可带来上升机会。
socialClasses.en: The royal family and nobility stood at the top, followed by priests, military-administrative officials, and local chiefs; the middle strata included artisans, merchants, skilled ironworkers, and temple personnel; the lower strata consisted of peasants, herders, miners, laborers, and dependent populations. Social mobility was limited, though military service, temple employment, and local influence could bring advancement.

allies.zh: 无稳定的正式大国盟友，但与部分南方、东部和内陆地方社群保持朝贡、婚姻或贸易性的友好关系。
allies.en: It had no stable formal great-power ally, but maintained friendly tributary, marital, or commercial relations with some southern, eastern, and inner-African local communities.

enemies.zh: 主要潜在对手为北方的托勒密王国；此外对下努比亚边境势力和商道袭扰集团保持警惕。
enemies.en: Its principal potential adversary was the Ptolemaic Kingdom to the north; it also remained wary of frontier groups in Lower Nubia and raiders along trade corridors.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制尼罗河中上游、保障边境安全、维持对黄金产地和商路节点的掌握为核心；对北方采取谨慎防御与有限交涉并存的方针，对南方和东部则以吸纳地方首领进入库施体系为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on controlling the middle and upper Nile, securing frontiers, and maintaining command over gold-producing regions and trade nodes; toward the north it combined cautious defense with limited diplomacy, while toward the south and east it focused on incorporating local chiefs into the Kushite order.

era.zh: 前铁器时代后期的东北非王国文明阶段
era.en: Late Iron Age Northeast African kingdom civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 麦罗埃以高水平铁器冶炼闻名，并发展了适应本地行政和纪念用途的麦罗埃文字；在王室建筑、石工、陶窑、河运组织和干旱环境下的资源整合方面具有明显优势。
keyInnovations.en: Meroe was noted for advanced ironworking and for the development of the Meroitic script suited to local administration and monumentality; it also showed clear strengths in royal construction, stoneworking, ceramic kilns, river transport organization, and resource integration in arid environments.

strengths.zh: 国家依托尼罗河、黄金、铁器和商路形成稳定的区域强国地位；王权、神庙与地方精英之间的合作较为成熟，首都麦罗埃具备较强的手工业和政治整合能力。
strengths.en: The state drew strength from the Nile, gold, iron production, and trade routes, giving it a stable position as a regional power; cooperation among the monarchy, temples, and local elites was fairly well developed, and Meroe possessed substantial craft and political integrative capacity.

weaknesses.zh: 经济对河谷农业和王室再分配依赖较高，边境控制成本大，地方性强；面对希腊化国家时，在攻城、重装部队和大型财政动员方面处于劣势。
weaknesses.en: The economy depended heavily on riverine agriculture and royal redistribution, frontier control was costly, and regionalism remained strong; against Hellenistic states, Kush was at a disadvantage in siegecraft, heavy troop systems, and large-scale fiscal mobilization.

outlook.zh: 221年时库施王国总体稳定，仍能维持对努比亚核心区域的有效统治。若继续掌握贸易与矿产资源，并控制北方边境压力，麦罗埃王国在未来数代内仍有望保持繁荣与独立；但长期而言，外部贸易格局变化与内部生态压力可能削弱其基础。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE the Kingdom of Kush was broadly stable and still capable of effective rule over the Nubian core. If it continued to control trade and mineral resources while containing pressure on the northern frontier, the Meroitic kingdom could remain prosperous and independent for several more generations; in the longer term, however, changes in external trade patterns and internal ecological strain could weaken its foundations.

description.zh: 以麦罗埃为中心的库施王国统治尼罗河中上游的努比亚地区。
description.en: Centered on Meroe, the kingdom of Kush ruled Nubia along the middle and upper Nile.

name.zh: 厄立特里亚-提格雷高地诸政体
name.en: Highland Polities of Eritrea and Tigray

name.zh: 原阿克苏姆文化诸政体

governmentForm.zh: 以高地定居中心为核心、由多个酋邦组成的分散型部族—早期国家混合体制
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal-to-early-state chiefdom system centered on highland settled nodes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由地方首领家族、祭祀与神庙相关群体、武装随从、自由农牧民、工匠与商旅群体构成，并包含依附劳作者。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of local ruling lineages, priestly and temple-associated groups, armed retainers, free agro-pastoralists, artisans, merchants, and dependent laborers.

rulingClass.zh: 控制土地、水源、祭祀场所与长途贸易通道的地方贵族与酋长家族
rulingClass.en: Local noble and chiefly lineages controlling land, water, ritual sites, and long-distance trade routes

succession.zh: 以宗族世袭为主，但需得到地方精英、战士随从与祭祀权威承认
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within ruling lineages, though requiring recognition from local elites, warrior retainers, and ritual authorities

structure.zh: 不存在统一帝国式中央官僚体系，而是由叶哈等中心的酋长宫廷、神庙机构与附属聚落首领共同治理；重大事务通过贡赋、婚盟、祭祀与武力维系。
structure.en: There was no unified imperial bureaucracy; governance was exercised through chiefly courts at centers such as Yeha, temple institutions, and subordinate settlement leaders, with tribute, marriage alliances, ritual, and force binding the system together.

function.zh: 管理酋长家族财产、朝贡收集、粮畜储备与宴飨分配
function.en: Managed chiefly household property, tribute collection, grain and livestock reserves, and redistributive feasting

function.zh: 维护圣所、主持祭祀、保管奉献物，并强化统治合法性
function.en: Maintained sanctuaries, oversaw rituals, stored offerings, and reinforced political legitimacy

function.zh: 协调内陆商道、与红海及阿拉伯贸易伙伴往来、征收关卡贡赋
function.en: Coordinated inland caravan routes, contacts with Red Sea and Arabian partners, and collection of tolls and trade dues

function.zh: 组织耕作、放牧地分配、蓄水与季节性劳役
function.en: Organized cultivation, pasture allocation, water storage, and seasonal labor obligations

function.zh: 维持酋长卫队、护送商队、镇压争端并守卫山地通道
function.en: Maintained chiefly guards, escorted caravans, suppressed disputes, and guarded highland passes

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托聚落长老、氏族首领和酋长委任的代理人进行，乡村层面以亲属关系和贡赋义务而非成文行政区划为主。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village elders, clan heads, and agents appointed by chiefs; at the rural level it was based more on kinship and tribute obligations than on formal territorial districts.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、酋长裁决、赔偿原则与宗教誓约为核心；涉及土地、水源、牲畜、婚姻和贸易纠纷时，多由长老与首领共同仲裁。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary practice, chiefly judgment, compensation principles, and religious oath-taking; disputes over land, water, livestock, marriage, and trade were commonly arbitrated by elders and chiefs.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政征收主要表现为实物贡赋、劳役、牲畜进献、贸易通行费与祭祀奉献，而非统一货币税制。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue extraction mainly took the form of tribute in kind, labor service, livestock offerings, trade tolls, and ritual donations rather than a uniform monetary tax system.

religion.zh: 以南阿拉伯相关神祇崇拜、地方祖先祭祀与高地圣所传统并存的多神信仰为主
religion.en: A polytheistic religious mix combining South Arabian-related deity worship, local ancestor cults, and highland sanctuary traditions

philosophy.zh: 尚未形成成体系哲学学派，思想以王权神圣性、祖先传统、祭祀秩序和实用性的族群伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: No formal philosophical schools had emerged; thought centered on sacred rulership, ancestral tradition, ritual order, and practical clan ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 上层与宗教—纪念用途可能使用南阿拉伯字母体系，日常社会仍以口传为主
writingSystem.en: Elite and religious-monumental contexts likely used a South Arabian script tradition, while most everyday transmission remained oral

culturalAchievements.zh: 叶哈等地的石构建筑、神庙传统、阶层化聚落、跨红海文化交流，以及高地农牧结合的社会组织，构成该时期最重要的文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements included stone architecture at sites such as Yeha, temple traditions, socially stratified settlements, trans-Red Sea cultural exchange, and a durable highland agro-pastoral social order.

languageFamily.zh: 以早期埃塞俄比亚闪米特语支与库希特语族成分并存的多语言环境为特征
languageFamily.en: Characterized by a multilingual environment including early Ethio-Semitic and Cushitic elements

unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地谷物种植、牲畜饲养、地方手工业与转口贸易为核心，整体生产能力高于一般游牧社会，但低于同时期的大型官僚帝国。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on highland grain cultivation, livestock herding, local craft production, and intermediary trade; its productive capacity was above that of purely pastoral societies but below that of the great bureaucratic empires of the age.

mainIndustries.zh: 大麦与小麦种植、畜牧业、蜂蜜与乳制品、石工建筑、陶器、金属小规模加工、香料与奢侈品转运贸易
mainIndustries.en: Barley and wheat cultivation, livestock herding, honey and dairy production, stone construction, pottery, small-scale metalworking, and transit trade in aromatics and prestige goods

tradeGoods.zh: 象牙、兽皮、乳香与没药类香料、牲畜、盐、黄金少量、黑曜石、陶器，以及来自阿拉伯和红海世界的珠饰、金属器和织物
tradeGoods.en: Ivory, hides, aromatic resins such as frankincense and myrrh, livestock, salt, small quantities of gold, obsidian, pottery, and beads, metal goods, and textiles arriving from Arabia and the Red Sea world

name.zh: 实物贡赋与金属按重交换
name.en: Tribute in kind and weighed metal exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以耕地、牛羊、谷仓存粮、婚姻网络和可用于交换的奢侈物衡量，货币化程度较低。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in farmland, cattle and sheep, stored grain, marriage alliances, and exchangeable prestige goods, with limited monetization.

unit.zh: 粮食等值千克/劳动者/年
unit.en: kilograms of grain equivalent per worker per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业和畜牧为基础、由酋长再分配和商路交换补充的混合型贡赋经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribute economy based on village agriculture and herding, supplemented by chiefly redistribution and route-based exchange

source.zh: 商路通行费与市场抽取

category.en: Roads, water storage, and building maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 财富主要以谷物、牲畜、香料、象牙、金属器和神庙奉献物形式分散储存于宫廷仓储、神庙与地方首领据点，而非集中成统一金库。
treasuryDescription.en: Wealth was stored mainly as grain, livestock, aromatics, ivory, metal goods, and temple offerings in palace stores, sanctuaries, and local chiefly centers rather than in a single centralized treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在稳定贡赋来源、维持精英再分配和商道安全，常通过宴飨、赏赐与祭祀来巩固忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on securing tribute flows, maintaining elite redistribution, and keeping trade routes safe, often reinforcing loyalty through feasts, gifts, and ritual patronage.

description.zh: 由各地酋长维持的核心战斗力量，负责护卫、突袭和内部秩序维护。
description.en: The core fighting force maintained by local chiefs, responsible for protection, raids, and internal order.

description.zh: 由农牧民在战时征集组成，装备长矛、盾牌、投枪和短兵器，适于山地作战。
description.en: Wartime levies drawn from agro-pastoral communities, armed with spears, shields, javelins, and short weapons, suited to highland warfare.

description.zh: 负责商队护送、边地巡查和通道预警，机动性较高。
description.en: Tasked with caravan escort, frontier patrol, and route warning, with relatively high mobility.

description.zh: 驻守主要聚落、圣所和仓储地点，防止盗袭与邻近冲突。
description.en: Stationed at major settlements, sanctuaries, and storage sites to prevent raids and nearby conflicts.

commanderInChief.zh: 最高权威由各大酋长联合主导，无单一常设总司令
commanderInChief.en: Supreme authority was exercised collectively by major chiefs, without a single permanent commander-in-chief

technology.zh: 军事技术以铁器和青铜器混用为主，装备包括长矛、投枪、盾牌、短剑和简易防护具；缺乏大型攻城器和成熟骑兵体系。
technology.en: Military technology relied on a mix of iron and bronze equipment, including spears, javelins, shields, short swords, and simple protective gear; large siege engines and a developed cavalry arm were absent.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自周边高地与低地群体的资源争夺、商路劫掠、内部酋邦竞争，以及红海贸易联系中断所造成的政治失衡。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition over resources with neighboring highland and lowland groups, raiding along trade routes, rivalry among chiefdoms, and political imbalance caused by disruption of Red Sea commerce.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于厄立特里亚与提格雷高地的农业聚落、山间谷地和若干宗教—贸易中心，整体密度不高但核心高地定居连续性较强。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the agricultural settlements, intermontane valleys, and a few ritual-commercial centers of the Eritrean and Tigrayan highlands; overall density was moderate, but settlement continuity in the core highlands was strong.

name.zh: 阿杜利斯前身沿海节点

socialClasses.zh: 上层为酋长与贵族宗族，其次为祭司、武装随从和富裕商人；主体为自由农牧民，底层包括依附劳作者、战俘和债务性从属者。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of chiefs and noble lineages, followed by priests, armed retainers, and wealthy merchants; the majority were free agro-pastoralists, while the lower stratum included dependent laborers, captives, and debt-bound subordinates.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的正式盟友，但与南阿拉伯诸国尤其是萨巴文化圈保持密切贸易和宗教联系，并与红海沿岸若干港口社群维持合作。
allies.en: There were no formal allies in a strict state sense, but the polities maintained close trade and religious ties with South Arabian states, especially the Sabaean sphere, and cooperated with various Red Sea littoral communities.

enemies.zh: 无持续性的单一宿敌，更多是与邻近低地部族、竞争性高地酋邦及商路掠夺者发生周期性冲突。
enemies.en: There was no single permanent enemy; instead, the polities faced recurring conflict with neighboring lowland tribes, rival highland chiefdoms, and raiders targeting trade routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以确保红海贸易通道、吸纳阿拉伯技术与宗教象征、维持高地内部平衡和扩大贡赋网络为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on securing Red Sea trade routes, absorbing Arabian techniques and religious symbols, maintaining internal highland balance, and extending tribute networks.

era.zh: 早期铁器时代高地—红海交流阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age Highland-Red Sea interaction phase

keyInnovations.zh: 石构纪念建筑、蓄水与梯地农业、南阿拉伯式文字与建筑影响、铁器使用、长途驮运贸易组织
keyInnovations.en: Stone monumental building, water storage and terraced agriculture, South Arabian-influenced script and architecture, use of iron tools, and organization of long-distance caravan trade

strengths.zh: 凭借高地农业基础、对红海与阿拉伯贸易网络的连接、较强的宗教—政治象征能力和山地防御优势，这些政体具有相当稳定性与区域整合潜力。
strengths.en: With a highland agricultural base, links to Red Sea and Arabian trade networks, strong ritual-political symbolism, and defensible terrain, these polities possessed notable stability and regional integrative potential.

weaknesses.zh: 政治权力分散，行政制度不够统一，对商路和降雨条件依赖较大，且内部继承争端和地方割据容易削弱整体力量。
weaknesses.en: Political power was fragmented, administrative structures were not fully unified, dependence on trade routes and rainfall was high, and succession disputes and local particularism could weaken overall cohesion.

outlook.zh: 若能持续控制高地核心区并深化红海贸易联系，这些原阿克苏姆政体未来可能向更集中、更具王权色彩的国家形态演化。
outlook.en: If they continued to control the highland core and deepen Red Sea commercial ties, these Proto-Aksumite polities could eventually evolve into a more centralized and explicitly monarchic state form.

description.zh: 非洲之角高地存在与阿拉伯海贸易相连的早期定居政体。
description.en: The Horn of Africa highlands contained early settled polities linked to Red Sea and Arabian trade.

name.zh: 尼日尔河与萨赫勒诸社会

capital.zh: 杰内杰诺及其他早期中心

governmentForm.zh: 以村社、氏族和区域酋邦为基础的分散部族自治体制
governmentForm.en: Decentralized tribal governance based on villages, clans, and regional chiefdoms

socialStructure.zh: 社会以宗族、年龄等级、农牧家庭和手工业群体构成，地方首领通过亲属关系、祭祀权威和对土地与贸易节点的控制维系权力。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around kin groups, age grades, farming and pastoral households, and craft communities, with local chiefs maintaining authority through lineage ties, ritual prestige, and control of land and trade nodes.

rulingClass.zh: 氏族首领、长老、祭司性权威人物与控制贸易和牲畜资源的地方精英
rulingClass.en: Clan chiefs, elders, ritual authorities, and local elites controlling trade and livestock resources

succession.zh: 多为氏族内部世袭并经长老认可，部分酋邦也结合母系或父系继承传统与协商推举
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within leading lineages and confirmed by elders, with some chiefdoms combining matrilineal or patrilineal customs with negotiated selection

structure.zh: 无统一国家中央机构，各聚落和区域网络由酋长、长老会议与宗教权威共同治理；较大中心对周边村落具有松散的号召与协调能力。
structure.en: There was no unified central state; settlements and regional networks were governed by chiefs, councils of elders, and ritual authorities, while larger centers exercised loose influence and coordination over surrounding villages.

function.zh: 裁决争端、确认继承、协调村社关系并监督习惯法执行
function.en: Adjudicated disputes, confirmed succession, coordinated village relations, and supervised customary law

function.zh: 征集谷物、牲畜和手工制品贡赋，并管理公共储粮与再分配
function.en: Collected tribute in grain, livestock, and craft goods, and managed communal storage and redistribution

function.zh: 主持祖先祭祀、土地与水源仪式，并维护神圣场所和社会秩序
function.en: Conducted ancestor rites, land and water ceremonies, and maintained sacred places and social order

function.zh: 监管地方集市、河渡与商旅往来，征收通行性贡赋
function.en: Oversaw local markets, river crossings, and merchant movement, collecting transit tribute

function.zh: 在冲突或掠夺威胁时动员战士，守卫聚落、牧场和贸易路线
function.en: Mobilized warriors during conflict or raiding threats and defended settlements, pasturelands, and trade routes

localAdmin.zh: 基层治理以村庄、宗族和邻近聚落联盟为单位，较大中心通过定期集市、婚姻联盟和祭祀关系维系周边地区。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through villages, kin groups, and alliances of nearby settlements, while larger centers maintained surrounding areas through periodic markets, marriage alliances, and ritual ties.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，由长老与酋长裁断，重点处理土地使用、婚姻、牲畜赔偿、血亲纠纷和贸易争议。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, judged by elders and chiefs, focusing on land use, marriage, livestock compensation, kin disputes, and trade conflicts.

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一货币税制，主要通过谷物、牲畜、劳动役、手工制品和集市通行贡赋实现资源集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; resources were gathered mainly through tribute in grain, livestock, labor service, craft goods, and market or transit dues.

religion.zh: 以祖先崇拜、自然灵、土地与水域祭祀为核心的传统信仰体系，不同地区存在多样化地方神圣传统。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor veneration, nature spirits, and rites tied to land and waters, with diverse local sacred traditions across regions.

philosophy.zh: 社会观念强调祖先合法性、长老权威、群体互助、土地与季节循环的神圣秩序。
philosophy.en: Social thought emphasized ancestral legitimacy, the authority of elders, communal reciprocity, and the sacred order of land and seasonal cycles.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本地成熟文字体系，知识主要依靠口述传统、记忆、仪式和手工技艺代际传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no developed local writing system; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, memory, ritual, and craft apprenticeship.

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成早期城市化聚落如杰内杰诺，发展陶器、铁器使用、河泛平原农业、区域集市网络与高度成熟的口头叙事传统。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included early urbanized settlements such as Jenne-Jeno, pottery production, iron use, floodplain agriculture, regional market networks, and highly developed oral narrative traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 以尼日尔-刚果语系与尼罗-撒哈拉语系诸语言为主，萨赫勒地带可能并存多种地方语言群。
languageFamily.en: Languages were primarily from the Niger-Congo and Nilo-Saharan families, with multiple local linguistic groups likely coexisting across the Sahel.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、渔业、牧业和地方手工业为基础，并通过尼日尔河及萨赫勒地带的短中程交换网络形成区域性剩余流动。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on subsistence farming, fishing, herding, and local crafts, with regional surpluses moving through short- and medium-distance exchange networks along the Niger River and the Sahel.

mainIndustries.zh: 粟类与稻作农业、渔业、畜牧业、陶器制作、铁器锻造、编织与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Millet and rice agriculture, fishing, pastoralism, pottery production, ironworking, weaving, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、鱼干、牲畜、皮革、盐、陶器、铁器、珠饰、木材与地方奢侈性祭祀用品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, dried fish, livestock, leather, salt, pottery, iron tools, beads, timber, and local prestige or ritual goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为粮仓储备、牲畜数量、耕地使用权、渔具、铁制工具与亲属网络支持。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in stored grain, livestock holdings, access to cultivated land, fishing gear, iron tools, and support from kin networks.

unit.zh: 国际元（1990年盖瑞-卡汉姆）/人/年
unit.en: international dollars (1990 Geary-Khamis) per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户生产、村社再分配、酋长贡赋和区域集市交换并存的混合型部族经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed tribal economy combining household production, village redistribution, chiefly tribute, and regional market exchange

source.en: Labor obligations and public service equivalent

category.zh: 首领再分配与公共储粮
祭祀、庆典与合法性支出

category.en: Maintenance of markets, crossings, and transport

category.zh: 酋长家族、使者与行政供养
category.en: Support for chiefly households, messengers, and administration

treasuryDescription.zh: 财富储备主要不是金属货币，而是粮食、牲畜、手工制品和首领控制下的礼仪性财富，用于饥荒救济、宴飨与结盟。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves consisted chiefly not of coined metal but of grain, livestock, craft goods, and ritual wealth controlled by chiefs, used for famine relief, feasting, and alliance-building.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政倾向稳健，以维持储粮、社会再分配和局部防务为主，避免对家户生产施加过重持续性负担。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, prioritizing grain reserves, social redistribution, and local defense while avoiding excessive sustained burdens on household production.

description.zh: 由地方酋长维持的常备武装核心，负责护卫中心聚落、首领与仓储要地。
description.en: The core standing force maintained by local chiefs, tasked with guarding central settlements, leaders, and storage sites.

description.zh: 由农民、牧民和渔民在需要时临时动员，承担防御、追击掠夺者和保卫耕地牧场的任务。
description.en: Temporarily mobilized farmers, herders, and fishers used for defense, pursuit of raiders, and protection of fields and pasture.

description.zh: 萨赫勒较干燥地区的机动战士，可能使用马匹或快速步战方式执行侦察、袭扰与护送。
description.en: Mobile warriors from drier Sahelian zones, possibly using horses where available or fast-moving infantry tactics for scouting, raiding, and escort duty.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地酋长及其战争首领
commanderInChief.en: Local chiefs and their war leaders

technology.zh: 武器以矛、弓、盾、木棍和铁制刀刃为主，防护装备有限，作战依赖熟悉地形、伏击和快速集结。
technology.en: Weapons were mainly spears, bows, shields, clubs, and iron blades, with limited armor; warfare relied on local terrain knowledge, ambush, and rapid assembly.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近群体间的资源竞争、牲畜掠夺、旱灾引发的人口流动与局部贸易线路争夺，而非外来帝国入侵。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition over resources with neighboring groups, livestock raiding, population movements caused by drought, and contests over local trade routes rather than invasion by distant empires.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于尼日尔河内陆三角洲、萨赫勒南缘与周边稀树草原，以村庄群、河岸聚落和少数区域中心构成。
populationDescription.en: The population was distributed across the Inland Niger Delta, the southern Sahel, and surrounding savanna, organized in village clusters, riverine settlements, and a few regional centers.

name.zh: 尼日尔河内陆三角洲区域聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较温和但已出现差异，包括酋长家族、长老与祭司、自由农民和牧民、工匠、商旅群体以及在战争或债务关系中失去自由者。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was moderate but real, including chiefly families, elders and ritual specialists, free farmers and herders, artisans, merchant groups, and persons who had lost freedom through war or debt.

allies.zh: 以邻近村社联盟、婚姻网络和河流贸易伙伴为主，无跨大陆正式盟国
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring village leagues, marriage networks, and riverine trade partners, with no formal intercontinental allies

enemies.zh: 无固定统一敌国，主要对手是争夺水源、牧场、渡口与贸易节点的邻近部族或酋邦
enemies.en: There was no single fixed enemy state; main rivals were nearby tribes or chiefdoms competing for water, pasture, crossings, and trade nodes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以地方性平衡、互市、联姻和冲突后和解为主，优先维持区域交通与生态资源的稳定使用。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized local balance, exchange, intermarriage, and post-conflict reconciliation, prioritizing stable use of regional routes and ecological resources.

era.zh: 西非早期铁器时代与区域聚落网络发展期
era.en: West African Early Iron Age and Regional Settlement Network Phase

keyInnovations.zh: 铁器工具和武器、耐火陶器、洪泛平原农业技术、储粮设施、渔业装置与区域集市网络组织
keyInnovations.en: Iron tools and weapons, durable pottery, floodplain farming techniques, grain storage facilities, fishing equipment, and organization of regional market networks

strengths.zh: 生态适应能力强，兼具农业、牧业和渔业的多元生计；区域交换网络发达；口述传统和宗族结构有助于维持社会韧性。
strengths.en: The societies were highly adaptable ecologically, combining farming, herding, and fishing; they possessed active regional exchange networks, and oral tradition with kin structures supported social resilience.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低，缺乏统一中央权力和书写行政；易受气候波动、局部冲突和贸易节点竞争影响。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was low, lacking unified central authority and written administration; they were vulnerable to climate fluctuation, local conflict, and competition over trade nodes.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE时期整体稳定，若区域贸易与铁器扩散继续发展，未来可能出现更大的酋邦整合与早期城市扩张；但环境压力仍将限制持续集中化。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE the region was broadly stable; if regional trade and iron diffusion continued, larger chiefdom consolidations and early urban expansion could emerge, though environmental pressures would still limit lasting centralization.

description.zh: 西非萨赫勒与尼日尔河流域存在农业聚落与区域首领政治网络。
description.en: West African Sahel and Niger basin societies included farming settlements and regional chiefdom networks.

capital.zh: 埃尔米拉多尔及其他中心

governmentForm.zh: 分散的神权君主制城邦体系，各城邦由王室、贵族与祭司共同治理
governmentForm.en: A decentralized theocratic monarchical city-state system, with each polity governed by royal houses, nobles, and priesthoods

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为王室与贵族、祭司与书记、工匠与商人、普通农民，以及劳役者与战俘；血缘、宗教职责与对王室的依附决定地位
socialStructure.en: Society was divided into royal families and nobles, priests and scribes, artisans and traders, common farmers, and laborers or captives; status depended on lineage, religious duty, and ties to ruling houses

rulingClass.zh: 世袭王族、地方贵族宗族、祭司集团与掌握历法和书写知识的书记精英
rulingClass.en: Hereditary royal lineages, local noble clans, priestly groups, and scribal elites who controlled calendrical and writing knowledge

succession.zh: 通常为王室世袭，以父系继承为主，但具体安排因城邦而异，常受贵族支持、宗教合法性与婚姻联盟影响
succession.en: Succession was usually hereditary within royal houses, generally patrilineal, but varied by city-state and was shaped by noble backing, religious legitimacy, and marriage alliances

structure.zh: 不存在统一帝国政府，而是由多个大型与中型城邦组成；每个中心围绕王宫、神庙与行政庭院运作，王权通过宗教仪式、贡赋征集、劳役组织和附属聚落控制地方
structure.en: There was no single imperial government; instead, many large and medium city-states existed. Each center operated around palaces, temples, and administrative courts, with royal authority exercised through ritual, tribute collection, labor organization, and control over subordinate settlements

function.zh: 管理宫廷、王室地产、宴飨、使者接待与王室劳役调配
function.en: Managed the court, royal estates, feasting, reception of envoys, and allocation of labor for royal needs

function.zh: 主持祭祀、天象观测、历法制定、吉日选择与统治合法性仪式
function.en: Oversaw rituals, astronomical observations, calendar keeping, auspicious date selection, and ceremonies legitimizing rule

function.zh: 征集玉米、可可、棉布、盐、羽毛、玉石与手工业品，并管理储藏设施
function.en: Collected maize, cacao, cotton cloth, salt, feathers, jade, and craft goods, and managed storage facilities

function.zh: 组织道路、蓄水设施、神庙、金字塔、平台与宫殿的修建和维护
function.en: Organized construction and maintenance of roads, waterworks, temples, pyramids, platforms, and palaces

function.zh: 管理王家卫队、战时征召、边界警戒、战俘看管与附庸镇压
function.en: Managed royal guards, wartime levies, frontier watch, custody of captives, and suppression of dependent communities

function.zh: 负责记事、家谱、贡赋记录、碑铭、图像叙事与王室宣传
function.en: Handled record keeping, genealogies, tribute accounts, inscriptions, visual narratives, and royal propaganda

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托核心都城、次级仪式中心、村落与农田社区；附属聚落由地方贵族或受封首领管理，向中心提供粮食、劳力与仪式支持
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on core capitals, secondary ceremonial centers, villages, and farming communities; dependent settlements were managed by local nobles or subordinate chiefs who supplied food, labor, and ritual support to the center

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王命、宗教禁忌与贵族仲裁为基础；案件多由地方首领、王宫官员或祭司裁决，惩罚包括赔偿、劳役、羞辱、流放或处死
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary practice, royal command, religious taboo, and noble arbitration; cases were judged by local leaders, palace officials, or priests, with penalties including compensation, labor service, humiliation, exile, or execution

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自实物贡赋与强制劳役，而非统一货币税制；常见负担包括玉米、豆类、棉布、盐、可可、羽毛、黑曜石制品以及用于建筑和战争的服役
taxationSystem.en: State finance relied mainly on tribute in kind and compulsory labor rather than a uniform monetary tax system; common obligations included maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, cacao, feathers, obsidian goods, and service for construction and war

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以自然神、祖先崇拜、王权神圣化与宇宙循环观念为核心；祭祀、供品、放血仪式与公共庆典在政治生活中居核心地位
religion.en: Religion was polytheistic, centered on nature deities, ancestor veneration, sacral kingship, and cyclical cosmology; ritual offerings, bloodletting, and public ceremonies were central to political life

philosophy.zh: 思想体系并非抽象哲学传统，而是以历法宇宙观、祖先合法性、神人沟通与时间循环为核心的宗教政治世界观
philosophy.en: The intellectual system was not an abstract philosophical school but a religio-political worldview centered on calendrical cosmology, ancestral legitimacy, communication between gods and humans, and cyclical time

writingSystem.zh: 早期玛雅文字已在部分精英环境中发展，使用徽号与语音成分结合的象形文字，主要见于纪念物、器物与精英记录传统
writingSystem.en: Early Maya writing had developed in some elite contexts, using hieroglyphic signs combining logographic and phonetic elements, chiefly on monuments, objects, and elite record traditions

culturalAchievements.zh: 大规模金字塔与平台建筑、精密城市规划、白色堤道、蓄水工程、历法与天象观察、早期碑铭传统，以及高度发达的仪式艺术与壁画风格
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included large pyramids and platform architecture, sophisticated urban planning, raised white causeways, water management works, calendar and astronomical observation, early monument traditions, and highly developed ceremonial art and mural styles

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以玉米农业为基础，辅以豆类、南瓜、可可、棉花、狩猎、采集和手工业；大型中心通过贡赋和劳役吸纳周边剩余产品。由于缺乏统一货币与跨区域价格体系，估值仅为现代换算意义上的粗略比较。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on maize agriculture, supplemented by beans, squash, cacao, cotton, hunting, gathering, and craft production; major centers drew in surrounding surplus through tribute and labor. Because there was no unified currency or region-wide price system, this estimate is only a rough comparative conversion

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、建筑、石器制造、陶器生产、纺织、玉石与贝饰加工、羽毛工艺、可可与盐的区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, construction, stone tool making, pottery production, textiles, jade and shell ornament working, feather craft, and regional exchange in cacao and salt

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石、玉石、贝壳、盐、可可、棉布、羽毛、陶器、石器、颜料、蜂蜜与干鱼
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian, jade, shell, salt, cacao, cotton cloth, feathers, pottery, stone tools, pigments, honey, and dried fish

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、住房、陶器、衣物、工具、储粮和亲族网络；精英财富则集中于玉器、羽饰、可可、奴役劳力与仪式性建筑控制权
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners consisted mainly of access to fields, dwellings, pottery, clothing, tools, stored food, and kinship ties; elite wealth was concentrated in jade ornaments, feather regalia, cacao, commanded labor, and control over ceremonial architecture

economicSystem.zh: 以村社农业、王室再分配、精英贡赋征收和长距离交换网络并存的宫廷主导型经济
economicSystem.en: A palace-directed economy combining village agriculture, royal redistribution, elite tribute extraction, and long-distance exchange networks

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非统一铸币储备，而是分散于各城邦宫殿仓储中的粮食、纺织品、可可、礼器、玉器和可调度劳力；核心中心拥有相当可观的储备以支持工程和祭典
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a reserve of coin but a dispersed stock of food, textiles, cacao, ritual goods, jade, and mobilizable labor held in palace storehouses across city-states; major centers maintained substantial reserves for building projects and ceremonies

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调通过贡赋和劳役动员资源，优先维持王权威望、祭祀活动、大型建筑与地区再分配，而非追求货币化财政均衡
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized mobilizing resources through tribute and labor, prioritizing royal prestige, ritual activity, monumental construction, and regional redistribution rather than monetary budget balance

description.zh: 由王室直属战士与贵族随从组成，负责宫廷守卫、仪式展示和核心战斗任务
description.en: Composed of royal retainers and noble followers, responsible for palace security, ceremonial display, and core combat duties

description.zh: 战时从附属聚落与农民社群中征发，装备矛、木盾、投射武器和石刃兵器，适用于突袭、围攻与地区冲突
description.en: Raised in wartime from subordinate settlements and farming communities, armed with spears, wooden shields, projectile weapons, and stone-bladed arms for raids, sieges, and regional conflict

description.zh: 由地方贵族与其追随者组成，兼具指挥、侦察、突袭和俘获敌酋的作用
description.en: Consisted of local nobles and their retainers, serving in command, scouting, raiding, and the capture of rival leaders

commanderInChief.zh: 各城邦圣王及其任命的贵族战首
commanderInChief.en: Each city-state sacral king and his appointed noble war leaders

technology.zh: 武器以矛、投矛器、木盾、棍棒、石刃武器和防护棉甲雏形为主；战争重视地形、突袭、俘虏与象征性胜利，而非大规模常备军会战
technology.en: Weaponry centered on spears, atlatls, wooden shields, clubs, stone-bladed weapons, and early padded cotton protection; warfare emphasized terrain, raids, captives, and symbolic victory rather than huge standing-army set battles

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近玛雅城邦之间的竞争、附属聚落叛离、贸易路线争夺与生态压力引发的人口流动，而非跨大陆外敌
threats.en: Main threats came from rival Maya city-states, defection of subordinate settlements, competition over trade routes, and population movements caused by ecological stress rather than transcontinental foreign enemies

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于玛雅低地的核心农业区、季节性湿地边缘、堤道连接的仪式中心与众多村落；大型中心周边人口密度较高
populationDescription.en: The population was widely distributed across core agricultural zones of the Maya lowlands, edges of seasonal wetlands, ceremonial centers linked by causeways, and numerous villages; densities were higher around major centers

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、贵族、祭司和书记；中间层包括工匠、商人、地方首领与专业建造者；下层为农民家庭、劳役者与战俘。社会流动有限，但通过婚姻、军事功绩和宗教服务可获得一定上升机会
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of royals, nobles, priests, and scribes; the middle included artisans, traders, local headmen, and specialist builders; the lower consisted of peasant households, laborers, and captives. Mobility was limited, though marriage, military achievement, and religious service could provide some advancement

allies.zh: 不存在统一对外同盟体系，城邦间多以婚姻、宗教往来、贸易互惠和临时军事协定形成松散联盟
allies.en: There was no unified external alliance system; city-states formed loose alliances through marriage ties, ritual exchange, trade reciprocity, and temporary military agreements

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者是竞争性的邻近玛雅政体，以及争夺黑曜石、盐、可可和政治威望的地方势力
enemies.en: The main enemies were rival neighboring Maya polities and local powers competing over obsidian, salt, cacao, and political prestige

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护贸易通道、扩大仪式影响、争取附庸与展示王权威望为目标；远距离交往更多体现为交换网络而非正式国家外交
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to secure trade routes, expand ritual influence, attract subordinate communities, and display royal prestige; long-distance contacts were expressed more through exchange networks than formal state diplomacy

era.zh: 前古典期晚段玛雅文明
era.en: Late Preclassic Maya civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 大型石灰灰泥建筑、堤道系统、蓄水与地景改造、早期文字与王权图像传统、精细历法计算、天象观察以及高度组织化的劳役工程
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included large lime-plaster architecture, causeway systems, water storage and landscape modification, early writing and royal iconographic traditions, refined calendrical calculation, astronomical observation, and highly organized labor engineering

strengths.zh: 农业基础稳固、仪式中心宏伟、精英组织能力强、区域交换活跃，并已形成复杂的王权合法性与文化表达体系
strengths.en: Strengths included a solid agricultural base, monumental ceremonial centers, strong elite organizational capacity, active regional exchange, and an already complex system of royal legitimacy and cultural expression

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，城邦竞争激烈，对劳役与环境条件依赖很深；在干旱、土壤压力或贸易中断时，宏大中心的维持成本极高
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included high political fragmentation, intense city-state rivalry, and deep dependence on labor extraction and environmental conditions; in times of drought, soil stress, or trade disruption, the cost of sustaining major centers was very high

outlook.zh: 221年前后的玛雅低地总体仍处于兴盛与扩张阶段，若环境与交换网络保持稳定，若干大中心将继续壮大并推动更成熟的王朝政治与文字传统发展
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE, the Maya lowlands were generally still in a phase of prosperity and expansion; if environmental conditions and exchange networks remained stable, several major centers would continue to grow and foster more mature dynastic politics and writing traditions

description.zh: 低地玛雅地区已出现大型礼仪中心和复杂城邦体系。
description.en: The Maya lowlands already featured major ceremonial centers and complex city-state systems.

ruler.zh: 蒙特阿尔班统治精英集团

governmentForm.zh: 以蒙特阿尔班为中心的城邦型礼仪国家，由贵族家族、祭司与军事首领共同执政
governmentForm.en: A city-state ceremonial polity centered on Monte Alban, governed jointly by noble lineages, priests, and military leaders

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈明显等级化结构，上层为王族与贵族家系，其次为祭司、武士和行政人员，中层为工匠、商人及地方社群首领，下层为普通农民、劳役者与附属村社居民
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with royal and noble lineages at the top, followed by priests, warriors, and administrators, then artisans, merchants, and local community heads, and finally common farmers, laborers, and residents of subordinate villages

rulingClass.zh: 蒙特阿尔班贵族、祭司阶层及与其结盟的地方世家
rulingClass.en: The Monte Alban nobility, priestly elite, and allied provincial noble houses

succession.zh: 统治权大体在贵族血缘集团内部世袭，并通过婚姻联盟、宗教合法性与精英协商加以确认
succession.en: Authority was broadly hereditary within elite lineages, confirmed through marriage alliances, religious legitimacy, and negotiation among the elite

structure.zh: 政治核心位于蒙特阿尔班的宫殿—宗教复合中心。中央权力通过贵族议事、祭司解释仪式历法、军事首领执行征调，以及对附属谷地聚落的朝贡管理而运作
structure.en: The political core lay in the palace-temple complex of Monte Alban. Central power operated through noble deliberation, priestly control of ritual and calendrics, military leadership in mobilization, and tribute management over dependent valley settlements

function.zh: 协调统治家族、分配土地与劳役、裁决高层政治事务
function.en: Coordinated ruling lineages, allocated land and labor, and adjudicated high political affairs

function.zh: 主持祭典、维护神庙、记录历法并为战争、播种和继承提供神意解释
function.en: Conducted ceremonies, maintained temples, kept calendrical records, and interpreted divine signs for war, planting, and succession

function.zh: 征收玉米、豆类、棉布、盐和工艺品等贡物，并管理公共仓储
function.en: Collected tribute in maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, and crafted goods, and managed public storage

function.zh: 监督附属村社与区域中心的忠诚、贡赋和劳役履行情况
function.en: Oversaw the loyalty, tribute, and labor obligations of subordinate communities and regional centers

function.zh: 组织武士阶层、征召附属社群兵员并保卫山地中心与交通线
function.en: Organized the warrior class, levied troops from subordinate communities, and defended the highland center and communication routes

function.zh: 管理纪念建筑、平台、墓葬、道路、梯田和排水等公共工程
function.en: Managed monumental building, platforms, tombs, roads, terraces, and drainage works

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托谷地村社、次级中心和附属首领运转。中央派驻或认可地方贵族管理贡赋、劳役与治安，但保留一定地方自治习惯
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated through valley villages, secondary centers, and subordinate chiefs. The center appointed or recognized local nobles to manage tribute, labor, and order, while allowing some customary local autonomy

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要以习惯法、统治者裁决、宗教禁忌与对贡赋义务的强制执行为基础。严重犯罪、叛乱、渎神和拒贡会受到严厉惩罚
legalSystem.en: Law was based chiefly on customary norms, elite judgments, religious taboo, and enforcement of tribute obligations. Serious crimes, rebellion, sacrilege, and refusal of tribute faced severe penalties

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自实物贡赋与劳役，包括玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、盐、黑曜石工艺品以及建筑和运输劳役
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from tribute in kind and labor service, including maize, beans, chili, cotton cloth, salt, obsidian goods, and labor for building and transport

religion.zh: 多神信仰，以祖先崇拜、山岳与雨神崇拜、农业生育祭祀及统治合法性的神圣仪式为核心，并伴随献祭与占卜传统
religion.en: Polytheistic religion centered on ancestor veneration, mountain and rain cults, agricultural fertility rites, and sacred ceremonies legitimizing rule, accompanied by sacrifice and divination

philosophy.zh: 其思想体系并非抽象哲学传统，而是以宇宙秩序、历法循环、祖先权威、神意占兆和社会等级义务为核心的宗教政治世界观
philosophy.en: Its intellectual system was not an abstract philosophical tradition but a religio-political worldview centered on cosmic order, calendrical cycles, ancestral authority, divination, and hierarchical social obligations

writingSystem.zh: 使用早期中美洲文字与历法记号体系，主要见于碑铭、纪念物和墓葬，用于记载人名、地名、征服、仪式日期和王权象征
writingSystem.en: Used an early Mesoamerican writing and calendrical notation system, mainly on inscriptions, monuments, and tombs, to record names, places, conquests, ritual dates, and symbols of rulership

culturalAchievements.zh: 蒙特阿尔班的大型礼仪建筑群、广场与平台布局、石刻碑铭、精致墓葬壁饰、灰陶与玉石工艺，以及在历法、书写和区域政治整合方面的早期成就都极为突出
culturalAchievements.en: Major achievements included Monte Alban’s monumental ceremonial architecture, planned plazas and platforms, carved stone monuments, elaborate tomb decoration, fine grayware and jade craftsmanship, and early advances in calendrics, writing, and regional political integration

languageFamily.zh: 奥托-曼格语系萨波特克语支
languageFamily.en: Zapotec branch of the Oto-Manguean language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地农业、区域朝贡与手工业生产为基础，虽未形成货币化市场经济，但在瓦哈卡谷地内部已具备较高组织水平和剩余征集能力
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on highland agriculture, regional tribute, and craft production. Though not monetized as a market economy, it displayed a relatively high level of organization and capacity to mobilize surplus within the Valley of Oaxaca

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类和南瓜种植、棉布织造、陶器制造、石器与黑曜石加工、建筑营造、盐与区域交换
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cotton weaving, pottery making, stone and obsidian working, construction, salt production, and regional exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、陶器、黑曜石器、贝壳、玉石、羽毛、盐和礼仪用品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili, cotton cloth, pottery, obsidian tools, shell, jade, feathers, salt, and ritual goods

name.zh: 无统一铸币，以实物贡赋与交换物为主
name.en: No unified coinage; in-kind tribute and exchange goods predominated

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、粮食储备、房屋、织物、陶器和亲族网络；贵族则拥有更多土地控制权、贡赋收入、装饰品和墓葬资源
householdWealth.en: Household wealth for commoners consisted mainly of land-use rights, stored grain, houses, textiles, pottery, and kin ties; nobles held greater control over land, tribute income, prestige goods, and funerary resources

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于农业—朝贡型区域再分配模式，市场交换存在但从属，中央通过政治和宗教权威动员剩余并支持大型公共工程
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an agrarian-tributary regional redistribution model, with market exchange present but secondary; the center mobilized surplus through political and religious authority and used it to support major public works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库存量主要不是金属货币，而是储存在仓廪中的谷物、纺织品、盐、工艺品和可供再分配的礼仪物资
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted chiefly not of metal currency but of grain in storage, textiles, salt, craft goods, and ceremonial resources available for redistribution

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持精英统治、资助纪念性工程、保障祭祀权威并通过储备与再分配缓冲歉收风险
