fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining elite rule, funding monumental projects, securing ritual authority, and buffering poor harvests through storage and redistribution

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从组成，是精锐作战力量，负责对外征讨、仪式战争和统治象征
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, this was the elite fighting force used for campaigns, ritual warfare, and symbols of rule

description.zh: 负责蒙特阿尔班及周边要地守卫、道路巡防和仓储保护的常备及半常备部队
description.en: Responsible for guarding Monte Alban and nearby strategic points, patrolling roads, and protecting storehouses as standing and semi-standing troops

description.zh: 战时由附属聚落提供，以步兵为主，适合山地作战、运输和围堵行动
description.en: Raised from subordinate settlements in wartime, these were mainly infantry suited to mountain fighting, transport, and encirclement operations

commanderInChief.zh: 蒙特阿尔班统治精英与军事贵族委员会
commanderInChief.en: The ruling elite and military noble council of Monte Alban

technology.zh: 军事技术以木石兵器、黑曜石刃器、投矛器、棍棒、矛、盾和防御性山地据点为主，缺乏金属武器和大型攻城器械
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden and stone weapons, obsidian blades, atlatls, clubs, spears, shields, and defensive hilltop positions, lacking metal weapons and major siege machines

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自瓦哈卡内部竞争中心、被征服聚落的反抗、贸易路线争夺以及与米斯特克等周边高地群体的冲突
threats.en: Main threats came from rival centers within Oaxaca, resistance from conquered communities, contests over exchange routes, and conflict with neighboring highland groups such as the Mixtec

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于瓦哈卡谷地及其附属高地聚落，形成以蒙特阿尔班为中心的多层级定居网络。人口规模反映其作为区域文明核心的繁荣状态
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Valley of Oaxaca and its dependent highland settlements, forming a multi-tiered settlement network centered on Monte Alban. This population reflects its thriving role as a regional civilizational core

name.zh: 特拉科卢拉谷地聚落群

socialClasses.zh: 王族与高等贵族、祭司、军事贵族、书写与行政人员、工匠与商贩、普通农民、劳役者及附属社群居民构成主要社会阶层
socialClasses.en: The main social classes were royal and high noble lineages, priests, military nobles, scribal and administrative personnel, artisans and traders, common farmers, laborers, and inhabitants of subordinate communities

allies.zh: 以瓦哈卡谷地内受其影响或与其通婚结盟的地方首领和附属中心为主，没有跨洋意义上的正式盟邦
allies.en: Its allies were mainly local chiefs and subordinate centers within Oaxaca tied by influence or marriage alliance; it had no formal overseas allies in the Old World sense

enemies.zh: 主要敌对者是瓦哈卡及邻近地区挑战其霸权的地方政体，尤其是反叛附庸、竞争高地中心与西部和南部边缘群体
enemies.en: Its enemies were primarily local polities in Oaxaca and nearby regions that challenged its hegemony, especially rebellious subordinates, rival highland centers, and western and southern frontier groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以区域霸权、婚姻联盟、朝贡网络、军事威慑和对贸易通道的控制为核心，重点不在远距离征服而在维持瓦哈卡高地秩序
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional hegemony, marriage alliance, tribute networks, military deterrence, and control of exchange routes, focusing less on distant conquest than on maintaining order in the Oaxacan highlands

era.zh: 中美洲前古典晚期
era.en: Late Formative Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度包括山地都市规划、纪念性石建筑、梯田与农业管理、早期文字与历法记录、精细陶器烧制以及区域再分配型政治组织
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations and institutions included hilltop urban planning, monumental stone architecture, terracing and agricultural management, early writing and calendrical record-keeping, fine ceramic firing, and a regionally redistributive political organization

strengths.zh: 其优势在于成熟的区域中心蒙特阿尔班、稳定的农业基础、较强的朝贡动员能力、显著的宗教合法性以及在建筑、书写和礼仪文化上的领先地位
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in the mature regional center of Monte Alban, a stable agricultural base, strong tribute mobilization capacity, marked religious legitimacy, and leadership in architecture, writing, and ceremonial culture

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括政治结构依赖精英共治与附庸忠诚、交通受山地地形限制、军事技术局限明显，以及对地方反叛和环境波动较为敏感
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included a political structure dependent on elite cohesion and subordinate loyalty, transport constrained by mountainous terrain, clear limits in military technology, and sensitivity to local rebellion and environmental fluctuation

outlook.zh: 到公元前221年，萨波特克文明仍处于繁荣期，蒙特阿尔班保持强大区域影响力。短期内有望继续主导瓦哈卡高地，但中长期将面临地方离心、竞争中心兴起和权力分散的压力
outlook.en: By 221 BCE, Zapotec civilization remained in a thriving phase, with Monte Alban retaining strong regional influence. In the short term it was likely to continue dominating the Oaxacan highlands, but over the longer term it would face pressures from local fragmentation, rising rival centers, and dispersal of power

description.zh: 瓦哈卡高地的萨波特克城市文明已形成强大的区域中心。
description.en: Zapotec urban civilization in the Oaxaca highlands had developed a strong regional center.

name.zh: 亚马孙与热带低地诸酋邦

governmentForm.zh: 分散的村落社会与地区性酋邦网络，由亲族、婚姻联盟与仪式威望维系
governmentForm.en: Dispersed village societies and regional chiefdom networks held together by kinship, marriage alliances, and ritual prestige

socialStructure.zh: 以氏族、扩大家庭与村落共同体为基础，部分河谷和肥沃地带形成有威望家系主导的酋邦，其余地区多为较平等的部落组织
socialStructure.en: Based on clans, extended families, and village communities; some river valleys and fertile zones developed chiefdoms dominated by prestigious lineages, while many other areas remained relatively egalitarian tribal communities

rulingClass.zh: 地方首领、战士精英、祭仪主持者与掌握远距交换的人群
rulingClass.en: Local headmen, warrior elites, ritual specialists, and groups controlling long-distance exchange

succession.zh: 通常依亲族与威望继承，常见父系或兄弟支系传承，但须获得长老、战士与姻亲网络认可
succession.en: Usually based on kinship and prestige, often through patrilineal or collateral succession, but requiring recognition from elders, warriors, and affinal networks

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构，各地区由村长、酋长与长老议事会治理；较大的聚落联盟可在战争、祭祀或贸易季节形成临时性的区域领导
structure.en: There was no unified state apparatus; local communities were governed by village headmen, chiefs, and councils of elders, while larger settlement alliances could form temporary regional leadership during war, ritual seasons, or trade gatherings

function.zh: 调解纠纷、决定迁徙、婚姻联盟与土地使用
function.en: Mediated disputes and decided migration, marriage alliances, and land use

function.zh: 组织宴飨、动员劳力、接待外来使者与维持威望交换
function.en: Organized feasts, mobilized labor, hosted outsiders, and maintained prestige exchange

function.zh: 主持仪式、治疗疾病、管理祖灵与自然灵相关知识
function.en: Conducted rituals, treated illness, and managed knowledge concerning ancestors and natural spirits

function.zh: 在冲突时期组织战士、设伏防御与保护河道交通
function.en: Organized warriors in times of conflict, coordinated ambush defense, and protected river routes

function.zh: 维持村社间物资流通、婚盟联络与跨流域信息传递
function.en: Maintained inter-village movement of goods, marriage ties, and cross-basin information flow

localAdmin.zh: 治理单位主要是村落与河段聚落群，行政依靠口头协商、首领威望与季节性集会，而非固定官署
localAdmin.en: The main governing units were villages and river-section settlement clusters, administered through oral negotiation, chiefly prestige, and seasonal gatherings rather than permanent offices

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法为主，重视亲族赔偿、和解、婚姻调节、驱逐与复仇平衡；严重冲突可导致分村迁移或长期敌对
legalSystem.en: Customary law predominated, emphasizing kin compensation, reconciliation, marriage settlement, expulsion, and the balancing of revenge; severe conflicts could lead to fission of villages or prolonged hostility

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一税制，酋长通过贡献食物、劳役、手工品和宴飨回馈来集中资源，带有礼物交换与再分配性质
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; chiefs concentrated resources through contributions of food, labor, and craft goods, then redistributed them through feasting, making the system gift-exchange and redistributive in character

religion.zh: 泛灵信仰、祖灵崇敬与萨满实践并存，森林、河流、动物与天象被视为具有灵性力量
religion.en: Animism, ancestor veneration, and shamanic practice coexisted, with forests, rivers, animals, and celestial phenomena understood as spiritually powerful

philosophy.zh: 宇宙观强调人与森林生态的互惠关系、亲族义务、战功声望与仪式秩序，而非成文哲学传统
philosophy.en: Their worldview emphasized reciprocal relations between people and forest ecologies, kin obligations, martial reputation, and ritual order rather than a written philosophical tradition

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，知识通过口述传统、歌谣、神话、记忆地景与仪式传承
writingSystem.en: No writing system existed; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, song, myth, remembered landscapes, and ritual practice

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长热带森林适应、独木舟航行、园圃农业、陶器制作、羽饰艺术、木器编织、植物药知识与人造土壤管理
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in tropical forest adaptation, canoe travel, horticulture, pottery, feather art, wood and fiber work, medicinal plant knowledge, and the management of anthropogenic soils

languageFamily.zh: 由多种语言群构成，可能包括早期图皮、阿拉瓦克、加勒比及其他未统一归类的地方语言
languageFamily.en: They comprised multiple language groups, likely including early Tupi, Arawakan, Cariban, and other locally distinct languages not unified under a single family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性园圃农业、渔猎采集与村社再分配为主，市场化和货币化程度极低，但在食物、木材、纤维、陶器与珍贵饰品方面具有稳定生产能力
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on subsistence horticulture, fishing, hunting, gathering, and village redistribution, with very low marketization and monetization, but with stable productive capacity in foodstuffs, timber, fiber, pottery, and prestige ornaments

mainIndustries.zh: 木薯与其他园圃作物种植、渔业、狩猎、采集、陶器、编织、独木舟制造、羽毛饰品与石木骨器制作
mainIndustries.en: Cassava and other horticultural crop cultivation, fishing, hunting, gathering, pottery, weaving, canoe building, feather ornament production, and stone-wood-bone tool making

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、鱼干、陶器、羽毛、木材、树脂、药用植物、石斧坯料、贝饰、染料与地区性奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Salt, dried fish, pottery, feathers, timber, resins, medicinal plants, stone axe blanks, shell ornaments, dyes, and regional prestige goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在园地、独木舟、工具、储粮、亲族劳力、婚姻联系与装饰性物品，而非金属货币积累
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was primarily expressed in garden plots, canoes, tools, stored food, kin labor, marriage ties, and ornamental goods rather than accumulated metallic currency

economicSystem.zh: 以村落自给、互惠交换、酋长再分配和有限区域贸易构成的部落经济
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy composed of village subsistence, reciprocal exchange, chiefly redistribution, and limited regional trade

source.en: Labor service and public works mobilization

category.zh: 独木舟、居所与公共劳作

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在统一国库，所谓储备主要是分散于各村社和酋长家户中的干粮、工具、装饰物、独木舟与人情债务网络
treasuryDescription.en: No unified treasury existed; reserves were mainly dispersed among village communities and chiefly households as dried food, tools, ornaments, canoes, and networks of social obligation

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政运作以储粮、礼物、劳役和再分配巩固首领权威，倾向维持社会稳定而非积累可抽象化财富
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice used stored food, gifts, labor, and redistribution to reinforce chiefly authority, prioritizing social stability rather than the accumulation of abstract wealth

description.zh: 由成年男子在冲突时动员，使用弓箭、矛、棍棒与木盾，擅长伏击和近距离袭扰
description.en: Mobilized from adult men during conflict, using bows, spears, clubs, and wooden shields, skilled in ambush and close-range raiding

description.zh: 依托河网快速机动，用于突袭、护送和沿岸防御
description.en: Used the river network for rapid movement, raiding, escort duty, and bank-side defense

description.zh: 由首领亲族与声望战士组成，负责领导作战、护卫仪式中心与维持联盟威慑
description.en: Composed of chiefly kin and prestigious fighters, they led combat, guarded ritual centers, and maintained alliance deterrence

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各地区酋长与战首领分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No unified commander; regional chiefs and war leaders commanded separately

technology.zh: 以木、骨、石与植物纤维武器为主，擅长独木舟、水路导航、森林伏击、毒药与环境利用，但无金属制式武器和攻城能力
technology.en: Weaponry was primarily of wood, bone, stone, and plant fiber; they were adept in canoe use, river navigation, forest ambush, poisons, and environmental warfare, but lacked standardized metal arms and siege capability

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近族群的袭击、复仇循环、资源竞争、迁徙压力与局部酋邦间争夺，而非外部帝国入侵
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring groups' raids, cycles of revenge, competition over resources, migration pressure, and rivalry among local chiefdoms rather than invasion by external empires

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散在广阔河流、支流、洪泛平原与森林边缘，多数聚居于中小型村落，局部地区存在较大的河岸聚落群
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across broad rivers, tributaries, floodplains, and forest margins, mostly in small and medium-sized villages, with some larger riverbank settlement clusters in certain regions

name.zh: 无真正城市，主要为大型河岸聚落
name.en: No true cities, only large riverine settlements

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层总体有限，但可区分酋长家系、战士与祭仪专家、普通村民，以及在战争中取得或吸纳的外来人口
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was generally limited, though chiefly lineages, warriors and ritual specialists, ordinary villagers, and outsiders captured or absorbed through war could be distinguished

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系，通常与邻近村社、同语族群或婚盟对象维持松散合作
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; cooperation was usually maintained with neighboring villages, related language groups, or marriage partners

enemies.zh: 无统一外敌，敌对关系多为地方性世仇、资源争端与河道控制冲突
enemies.en: There was no unified external enemy; hostile relations were mostly local blood feuds, resource disputes, and conflicts over river control

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以婚姻交换、礼物往来、共同祭仪和有限战争并行，重在维持地区平衡与生存空间
foreignPolicy.en: External relations combined marriage exchange, gift circulation, shared ritual, and limited warfare, focusing on maintaining regional balance and living space

era.zh: 前铁器时代的热带森林园圃社会
era.en: Pre-iron tropical forest horticultural societies

keyInnovations.zh: 木薯加工技术、刀耕火种与园圃轮作、独木舟制造、陶器烧制、编织与鱼毒利用、人造黑土与森林资源精细管理
keyInnovations.en: Cassava processing, slash-and-burn horticulture with plot rotation, canoe construction, pottery firing, weaving and fish-poison use, anthropogenic dark earths, and fine-grained forest resource management

strengths.zh: 对热带环境适应极强，食物来源多样，河网交通便利，社会组织灵活，口述知识与生态技术丰富，能够在缺乏国家机构的条件下维持稳定生计
strengths.en: They were highly adapted to tropical environments, had diverse food sources, benefited from riverine mobility, possessed flexible social organization, and maintained rich oral and ecological knowledge, enabling stable livelihoods without state institutions

weaknesses.zh: 政治高度分散，难以长期统一行动；缺乏文字、金属工具和大型公共工程能力；人口密度有限且容易受疾病、洪水、歉收与部族冲突影响
weaknesses.en: Politics were highly fragmented, making sustained unified action difficult; they lacked writing, metal tools, and large-scale public works capacity; population density remained limited and was vulnerable to disease, floods, poor harvests, and tribal conflict

outlook.zh: 221年前后的亚马孙诸族总体处于稳定状态，将继续以地方适应和区域交换维持生存；短期内不可能形成统一国家，但部分河谷酋邦和聚落网络有进一步复杂化的潜力
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE the Amazonian peoples were broadly stable and likely to continue sustaining themselves through local adaptation and regional exchange; a unified state was unlikely in the near term, but some riverine chiefdoms and settlement networks had potential for further complexity

description.zh: 南美热带低地广布分散而多样的村落社会与酋邦网络。
description.en: The tropical lowlands of South America were home to dispersed and diverse village societies and chiefdom networks.

name.zh: 安第斯前期区域诸文化
后查文时代与安第斯地区诸政体

governmentForm.zh: 由多个互不统一的部族酋邦、地方领地与区域祭祀中心构成的分散政治结构
governmentForm.en: A decentralized political landscape composed of multiple ununified tribal chiefdoms, local domains, and regional ceremonial centers

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、村社与地方首领网络为基础；高地与海岸社会通常由贵族首领、祭司、工匠、农民、牧民、渔民与劳役依附者构成，不同地区差异显著
socialStructure.en: Society was based on kinship groups, village communities, and networks of local leaders; highland and coastal societies commonly included noble chiefs, ritual specialists, artisans, farmers, herders, fishers, and dependent laborers, with strong regional variation

rulingClass.zh: 地方贵族家族、祭司集团与控制灌溉、仓储或驼队交换路线的首领阶层
rulingClass.en: Local noble lineages, ritual elites, and chieftain groups controlling irrigation, storage, or llama-caravan exchange routes

succession.zh: 多为家族世袭并辅以地方精英认可，部分地区通过联姻、资历与宗教权威巩固继承
succession.en: Usually hereditary within leading families, reinforced by recognition from local elites; in some regions succession was strengthened by marriage alliances, seniority, and ritual authority

structure.zh: 不存在统一国家机构；各地由村社首领、山谷酋长和祭祀中心管理，较大的区域政体能够动员周边聚落进行灌溉工程、祭祀集会、战争与再分配
structure.en: There was no single state apparatus; local communities were governed by village headmen, valley chiefs, and ceremonial centers, while larger regional polities could mobilize nearby settlements for irrigation works, ritual gatherings, warfare, and redistribution

function.zh: 主持祭祀、历法仪式、祖灵崇拜与公共庆典，维护首领合法性
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, calendrical rites, ancestor worship, and public festivals, sustaining chiefly legitimacy

function.zh: 组织梯田、渠道、集体劳役与耕作分配，尤其在沿海灌溉谷地和高地农业区重要
function.en: Organized terraces, canals, communal labor, and cultivation allocation, especially important in coastal irrigated valleys and highland farming zones

function.zh: 保管粮食、纺织品、工具和贡品，并在祭祀、灾年或战争时再分配
function.en: Stored grain, textiles, tools, and tribute goods and redistributed them during ceremonies, bad harvests, or warfare

function.zh: 管理羊驼运输、山地与海岸间的交换联系以及珍贵物品流通
function.en: Managed llama transport, exchange links between highlands and coast, and the circulation of valued goods

function.zh: 召集战士、护卫首领、组织修筑工事与征调公共劳役
function.en: Called up warriors, protected chiefs, organized fortifications, and mobilized communal labor

localAdmin.zh: 基层以村社和亲族单位为核心，地方事务由长者、首领和祭司共同裁决；较大的山谷可形成多聚落联盟
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on villages and kin groups, with elders, chiefs, and ritual specialists jointly deciding affairs; larger valleys could form multi-settlement alliances

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、亲族仲裁、赔偿与仪式性调解为主，重视土地、水源、婚姻、劳役和血亲义务；严重冲突可能演变为部族战争或复仇循环
legalSystem.en: Customary law, kin arbitration, compensation, and ritual mediation predominated, with strong concern for land, water, marriage, labor, and kin obligations; serious disputes could escalate into inter-group warfare or feud cycles

taxationSystem.zh: 缺乏统一货币税制，贡赋主要以粮食、纺织品、劳役、手工制品、海产与畜群形式征收，由首领用于宴飨、祭祀、工程和再分配
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified monetary tax system; tribute was mainly collected in food, textiles, labor, crafted goods, marine produce, and livestock, then used by chiefs for feasting, ritual, public works, and redistribution

religion.zh: 宗教以山岳、祖灵、自然神力、猫科与鸟蛇等复合神灵象征为核心，延续部分查文传统，同时各地区发展自身祭祀形式、圣地与献祭制度
religion.en: Religion centered on mountains, ancestors, natural forces, and composite sacred imagery such as felines, birds, and serpents, preserving some Chavin-era traditions while each region developed its own ritual forms, shrines, and offering systems

philosophy.zh: 无成文哲学传统，世界观强调人与山川、祖先、农业周期和共同体义务之间的平衡，政治权威与宗教权威高度交织
philosophy.en: There was no written philosophical canon; the worldview emphasized balance among people, mountains, ancestors, agricultural cycles, and communal obligations, with political and ritual authority deeply intertwined

writingSystem.zh: 尚无已知成熟文字系统，信息主要通过口传、图像符号、纪念性建筑、纺织纹样和仪式记忆传承
writingSystem.en: No known mature writing system existed; information was transmitted primarily through oral tradition, visual symbols, monumental architecture, textile patterns, and ritual memory

culturalAchievements.zh: 在陶器、纺织、金属初步加工、石构建筑、土台与礼仪中心营建方面表现突出；高地梯田农业、海岸灌溉与跨生态区交换网络显示出高度适应性
culturalAchievements.en: These societies excelled in ceramics, textiles, early metallurgy, stone construction, platform building, and ceremonial centers; highland terraced farming, coastal irrigation, and cross-ecological exchange networks showed notable adaptability

languageFamily.zh: 语言高度多样，可能包括早期克丘亚相关方言、艾马拉相关方言以及许多今已消失或未明归属的地方语言
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, likely including early Quechuan-related dialects, Aymaran-related dialects, and many local languages now lost or of uncertain affiliation

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和区域再分配为主，市场交换有限但跨生态带互补显著；总量不低，但多以实物、劳役和礼物网络体现，难以按后世货币标准衡量
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on subsistence agriculture and regional redistribution, with limited market exchange but strong complementarity across ecological zones; total output was substantial, though expressed mainly through goods, labor, and gift networks rather than money

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、块根作物与豆类种植，羊驼和美洲驼牧养，沿海渔业与贝类采集，纺织，陶器制作，石工和小规模金银铜加工
mainIndustries.en: Maize, tuber, and bean cultivation; llama and alpaca herding; coastal fishing and shellfish gathering; textile production; ceramics; stoneworking; and small-scale gold, silver, and copper working

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、藜麦、马铃薯类块根、干鱼、盐、纺织品、贝壳、黑曜石、羽毛、金属饰品、药用与染色植物、羊驼及其毛料
tradeGoods.en: Maize, quinoa, potato-like tubers, dried fish, salt, textiles, shells, obsidian, feathers, metal ornaments, medicinal and dye plants, llamas, and wool products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、畜群数量、储粮、纺织品、工具和亲族互助网络，而非金属货币储蓄
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through land-use rights, herd size, stored food, textiles, tools, and kin support networks rather than metallic monetary savings

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、首领再分配和跨生态互补为核心的礼物—贡赋经济，局部存在周期性集市式交换，但尚未形成统一商业货币体系
economicSystem.en: A gift-and-tribute economy centered on village subsistence, chiefly redistribution, and cross-ecological complementarity, with some periodic market-like exchange but no unified commercial currency system

source.zh: 纺织品、陶器与手工业贡品

category.zh: 公共宴飨、祭祀与礼仪中心
category.en: Public feasting, ritual, and ceremonial centers

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非单一中央金库，而是分散在各区域中心的储粮仓、纺织品、器物、牲畜与贵重祭器储备
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a single central vault but a dispersed reserve of storehouses, textiles, goods, livestock, and valued ritual objects held in regional centers

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政逻辑以维持首领威望、确保工程动员、储备粮食和在危机中进行再分配为核心，偏重实物积累而非货币征收
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on sustaining chiefly prestige, ensuring labor mobilization for works, storing food, and enabling redistribution in crisis, emphasizing goods accumulation rather than monetary taxation

description.zh: 由首领亲族、随从和专业性较强的战士组成，负责护卫中心聚落、押运贡品和执行突袭
description.en: Composed of chiefly kin, retainers, and more specialized warriors, responsible for guarding central settlements, escorting tribute, and conducting raids

description.zh: 战时由各村社和山谷联盟征发，装备以木矛、投石器、棍棒、简易盾牌和石铜刃器为主
description.en: Raised from village communities and valley alliances in wartime, equipped mainly with wooden spears, slings, clubs, simple shields, and stone or copper-edged weapons

name.zh: 高地投石手与山地机动队
name.en: Highland Slingers and Mountain Mobile Bands

description.zh: 适应高地地形，擅长投石、伏击和山路作战，也可担任驼队护卫与边地巡逻
description.en: Adapted to highland terrain, skilled in slinging, ambush, and mountain fighting, and also used as caravan escorts and frontier patrols

commanderInChief.zh: 各地大酋长或战时联盟首领
commanderInChief.en: Regional paramount chiefs or wartime alliance leaders

technology.zh: 军备以石、木、骨和早期铜器为主，擅长利用山地、堡寨、梯田边坡和投石技术；缺乏大型攻城机械、骑兵与冶铁武器
technology.en: Military equipment relied on stone, wood, bone, and early copper, with strong use of mountains, hillforts, terrace slopes, and sling warfare; they lacked major siege machinery, cavalry, and iron weaponry

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近山谷与海岸政体的资源竞争、灌溉水源冲突、驼队路线争夺和周期性部族战争，而非外来帝国入侵
threats.en: The main threats came from resource competition with neighboring valley and coastal polities, conflicts over irrigation water, disputes over caravan routes, and periodic tribal warfare rather than invasion by external empires

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于安第斯高地谷地、山麓、沿海绿洲河谷与渔村，区域差异极大；高地农业—牧业复合带和北中部海岸灌溉谷地最为密集
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across Andean highland valleys, foothills, coastal oasis valleys, and fishing settlements, with strong regional variation; the densest zones were mixed farming-herding highlands and the irrigated valleys of the north-central coast

name.zh: 查文·德万塔尔旧宗教中心
name.en: Former Ceremonial Center of Chavin de Huantar

socialClasses.zh: 大体可分为首领与贵族家族、祭司与礼仪专家、工匠、商旅与驼队组织者、普通农民牧民渔民，以及战俘或依附劳役群体
socialClasses.en: Broadly divided into chiefs and noble families, priests and ritual specialists, artisans, traders and caravan organizers, ordinary farmers herders and fishers, and captives or dependent laboring groups

allies.zh: 无统一对外联盟；各政体通过联姻、祭祀往来、交换网络和临时军事同盟维持关系
allies.en: There was no unified external alliance; polities maintained ties through marriage, ritual exchange, trade networks, and temporary military coalitions

enemies.zh: 无固定共同敌国，但相邻山谷、海岸社群和高地牧业集团之间常因土地、水源、海产和贸易路线发生敌对
enemies.en: There was no single common enemy, but neighboring valleys, coastal communities, and highland herding groups often became hostile over land, water, marine resources, and trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以区域平衡、交换互惠、婚姻联盟和在必要时进行报复性战争为主；尚不存在覆盖整个安第斯的统一外交体系
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on regional balance, reciprocal exchange, marriage alliances, and retaliatory warfare when necessary; no diplomatic system yet existed for the Andes as a whole

era.zh: 安第斯形成期后段至早期地区发展时代
era.en: Late Post-Formative Andes to Early Regional Development

keyInnovations.zh: 梯田农业、山地灌溉、驼队运输、精细纺织、礼仪性建筑、早期冶金与适应多生态带资源互补的生产组织
keyInnovations.en: Terrace agriculture, mountain irrigation, llama-caravan transport, fine textile production, ceremonial architecture, early metallurgy, and productive organization adapted to complementary ecological zones

strengths.zh: 生态适应能力强，能在海岸、山地和高原之间建立互补网络；村社凝聚力高，礼仪中心具有整合功能，农业与储备体系具备一定韧性
strengths.en: These societies showed strong ecological adaptability, building complementary networks between coast, mountains, and high plateau; village cohesion was high, ceremonial centers had integrative power, and agricultural storage systems offered some resilience

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，缺乏统一文字、统一法制和常备国家军队；交通受地形限制明显，跨区域权力难以长期稳定扩展
weaknesses.en: They were politically fragmented and lacked a unified script, legal order, and standing state army; transportation was heavily constrained by terrain, making durable expansion of supra-regional power difficult

outlook.zh: 短期内总体稳定，但区域竞争将持续推动更复杂的酋邦和早期国家形成；未来数世纪安第斯可能出现更强大的海岸与高地政体，为后来的大规模文明整合奠定基础
outlook.en: In the short term the region was broadly stable, but continuing competition would drive the rise of more complex chiefdoms and early states; over the coming centuries the Andes would likely produce stronger coastal and highland polities, laying foundations for later large-scale civilizational integration

description.zh: 安第斯地区由多个高地与海岸社会构成，尚未形成统一帝国。
description.en: The Andes consisted of multiple highland and coastal societies and had not yet produced a unified empire.

governmentForm.zh: 由村落联盟与区域酋邦组成的分散部族政治体系，各地首领通过亲族、婚盟、仪式威望与贸易网络维持统治。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal political order composed of village alliances and regional chiefdoms, in which local leaders ruled through kinship, marriage alliances, ritual prestige, and trade networks.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落共同体为基础，上层为酋长家族、祭司与战士精英，中层为自由农民、工匠与商旅，下层包括依附者、俘虏与家内劳作者，但尚未形成高度制度化的国家等级体系。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on village communities, with chiefdom families, ritual specialists, and warrior elites at the top, free farmers, artisans, and traders in the middle, and dependents, captives, and household laborers below, though without a fully institutionalized state hierarchy.

rulingClass.zh: 地方酋长家族、仪式权威者、资深战士首领与控制长途交换的地方精英。
rulingClass.en: Local chiefly lineages, ritual authorities, senior war leaders, and regional elites who controlled long-distance exchange.

succession.zh: 多为家族继承与长支优先的混合方式，通常由酋长家族内部推举最具威望者继位，部分地区兼顾婚盟与战功。
succession.en: Succession was usually a mixed system of hereditary transmission and senior-line preference, with the most prestigious member of a chiefly lineage selected, sometimes influenced by marriage alliances and military reputation.

structure.zh: 无统一中央官僚机构，政治权力分布在若干河谷、湖区与高地酋邦中；酋长依靠家族长老会议、祭仪组织者与战士随从处理司法、分配、战争与对外交往。
structure.en: There was no unified central bureaucracy; political authority was distributed among chiefdoms in valleys, lake basins, and uplands. Chiefs relied on councils of elders, ritual organizers, and warrior retainers to handle justice, redistribution, war, and external relations.

function.zh: 协调村落关系、婚盟、继承与争端调停，维持酋长家族对地方共同体的权威。
function.en: Coordinated village relations, marriage alliances, succession, and dispute mediation, sustaining chiefly authority over local communities.

function.zh: 主持祖先崇拜、公共仪式、季节性祭礼与神圣场所管理，并指导农业时序。
function.en: Oversaw ancestor worship, public ceremonies, seasonal rites, and sacred places, and helped guide the agricultural cycle.

function.zh: 征集玉米、豆类、棉布、盐、陶器与劳役，在节庆、战争和灾荒时进行再分配。
function.en: Collected maize, beans, cotton cloth, salt, ceramics, and labor obligations, redistributing them during festivals, warfare, and scarcity.

function.zh: 召集战士、组织袭掠与防御行动，保护贸易路线、耕地和聚落边界。
function.en: Mobilized warriors, organized raiding and defense, and protected trade routes, farmlands, and settlement boundaries.

function.zh: 管理区域交换节点，维系与中部高原、太平洋海岸及邻近文化区的物资流通。
function.en: Managed regional exchange nodes and maintained the flow of goods with the central highlands, Pacific coast, and adjacent cultural areas.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村社、宗族长老与小型酋邦中心为核心。湖盆、河谷和高地聚落各有首领，依附村落定期输送贡品、劳役与战士。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on village communities, lineage elders, and small chiefly centers. Settlements in lake basins, valleys, and uplands each had their own leaders, while dependent villages regularly provided tribute, labor, and warriors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，由长老、酋长与祭司共同裁断。案件多涉及土地使用、婚姻、血亲义务、赔偿、盗窃与伤害，重在赔偿、和解与仪式性修复而非成文法。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on customary practice, judged by elders, chiefs, and ritual specialists. Cases often involved land use, marriage, kin obligations, compensation, theft, and injury, emphasizing restitution, reconciliation, and ritual repair rather than written codes.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在统一税制，主要通过贡赋、节庆献纳、公共工程劳役和战时征发实现资源动员。征收多为实物，如粮食、布匹、盐、手工艺品与劳动力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no unified tax system; resources were mobilized through tribute, ceremonial offerings, labor for communal works, and wartime levies. Collections were mainly in kind, including food, cloth, salt, craft goods, and labor.

religion.zh: 宗教以祖先崇拜、自然神灵崇拜与地方圣地仪式为核心，常与山岳、泉水、湖泊、农业生育力和冥界观念相联系。祭礼、音乐、舞蹈与供品是维系政治权威和共同体认同的重要手段。
religion.en: Religion centered on ancestor worship, nature spirits, and rituals at local sacred places, often linked to mountains, springs, lakes, agricultural fertility, and underworld beliefs. Ceremony, music, dance, and offerings were crucial means of maintaining political authority and communal identity.

philosophy.zh: 未形成成文哲学传统，世界观主要体现为亲族义务、祖先合法性、人与土地的互惠关系，以及通过仪式维持宇宙秩序与社会平衡的观念。
philosophy.en: There was no formal written philosophical tradition; the worldview was expressed through kinship obligations, ancestral legitimacy, reciprocal relations between people and land, and the idea that ritual maintained cosmic order and social balance.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，信息主要通过口传、仪式记忆、图像符号、器物装饰和代际传承保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; information was preserved mainly through oral tradition, ritual memory, visual symbols, object decoration, and intergenerational transmission.

culturalAchievements.zh: 西墨西哥地区以精致陶器、竖井墓传统、玉石与贝饰工艺、音乐舞蹈、村落仪式建筑和区域交换网络闻名。墓葬随葬品丰富，反映出复杂的祖先崇拜与社会身份表达。
culturalAchievements.en: Western Mexico was notable for refined ceramics, the shaft-tomb tradition, jade and shell ornament working, music and dance, village ceremonial architecture, and regional exchange networks. Rich burial assemblages reflected complex ancestor veneration and expressions of social identity.

languageFamily.zh: 语言高度多样，可能包括乌托-阿兹特克语系早期群体，以及其他地方语言传统；整体上为多语言、多方言环境。
languageFamily.en: Languages were highly diverse, likely including early Uto-Aztecan-speaking groups along with other local linguistic traditions; overall the region was multilingual and dialectally varied.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为主，辅以渔猎、采集、家庭手工业和区域交换。缺乏统一货币和国家市场，财富更多体现为土地使用权、粮食储备、装饰品、劳动力和交换关系。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence agriculture, supplemented by fishing, hunting, gathering, household crafts, and regional exchange. There was no unified currency or state market; wealth was expressed more through access to land, food reserves, ornaments, labor, and exchange ties.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米农业、豆类与南瓜种植、棉纺织、陶器制作、石器加工、贝饰与玉饰制造、盐产、渔猎采集。
mainIndustries.en: Maize agriculture, bean and squash cultivation, cotton weaving, pottery production, stone-tool making, shell and jade ornament manufacture, salt production, and fishing-hunting-gathering.

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、陶器、黑曜石、盐、贝壳、玉石、羽毛、染料、木材与地方性奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, cotton cloth, ceramics, obsidian, salt, shells, jade, feathers, dyes, timber, and local prestige goods.

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为住房、耕地使用权、储粮、陶器、纺织品、工具与家族关系网络；精英家庭则拥有更多装饰品、祭器、俘虏劳力和远方交换品。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth consisted mainly of dwellings, access to farmland, stored food, pottery, textiles, tools, and kinship networks; elite households possessed more ornaments, ritual goods, captive labor, and imported prestige items.

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、村社互助、酋长再分配和区域交换并存的混合经济。市场交换尚不统一，礼物交换与贡赋关系对财富流动具有核心作用。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining household production, village mutual aid, chiefly redistribution, and regional exchange. Market exchange was not standardized, and gift exchange and tribute relations played a central role in the movement of wealth.

source.en: Labor levies and public works obligations

source.zh: 战利品与附属村落进献
source.en: War booty and dependent village payments

category.en: Maintenance of chiefly households and retainers

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金库，而是分散在各酋邦中心的粮仓、纺织品储备、盐、陶器、祭器和可用于馈赠的奢侈物资总和。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not a centralized vault but the combined stores of grain, textiles, salt, ceramics, ritual goods, and giftable prestige items held across chiefly centers.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持精英合法性和社会整合为目标，通过贡赋征集再用于祭礼、宴飨、救荒和战争动员。积累财富的重要方式是再分配而非长期货币储蓄。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed at preserving elite legitimacy and social cohesion, collecting tribute to fund ceremonies, feasting, relief, and war mobilization. Wealth was accumulated chiefly through redistribution rather than long-term monetary saving.

description.zh: 由酋长直接维系的核心武装，负责护卫首领、看守仓储与在冲突中充当前锋，装备较好。
description.en: The core armed force maintained directly by chiefs, tasked with guarding leaders, protecting stores, and serving as the vanguard in conflicts, usually with better equipment.

description.zh: 由各村落在冲突或袭掠时临时征集的成年男子，主要使用矛、棍棒、投射器和木盾。
description.en: Adult men mobilized temporarily from villages during conflict or raiding, mainly armed with spears, clubs, projectiles, and wooden shields.

description.zh: 机动性较强的小股战士，用于侦察、伏击、护送商路与夺取战利品或俘虏。
description.en: More mobile small war bands used for scouting, ambush, route protection, and the seizure of booty or captives.

commanderInChief.en: Regional chiefs and their war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以石制、木制与骨制兵器为主，常见武器包括长矛、投射器、弓箭、木棍和刀片工具。尚无金属武器体系与攻城技术，作战多为小规模冲突、突袭和领地防御。
technology.en: Military technology relied mainly on stone, wood, and bone weaponry, including spears, spear-throwers, bows, clubs, and bladed tools. There was no developed metal weapon system or siegecraft, and warfare was usually small-scale conflict, raiding, and territorial defense.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近酋邦间的资源竞争、俘虏袭掠、贸易通道控制争端以及局部生态压力导致的迁徙摩擦，而非外来帝国入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from competition among neighboring chiefdoms over resources, captive-raiding, disputes over trade routes, and migration friction caused by local ecological pressure, rather than invasion by distant empires.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于西墨西哥的村落、湖区聚落、河谷农业点和若干区域中心，整体密度不均，受地形、水源与耕地条件影响显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed among villages, lake-basin settlements, valley farming sites, and several regional centers in western Mexico, with uneven density strongly shaped by terrain, water access, and arable land.

name.zh: 埃尔·奥佩尼奥地区中心
哈利斯科高地区域中心

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括酋长与其家族、祭司和仪式主持者、战士与地方显贵、自由农民与工匠、商旅和运载者、依附者与俘虏。不同地区阶层分化程度不一。
socialClasses.en: The main social groups included chiefs and their families, priests and ritual officiants, warriors and local notables, free farmers and artisans, traders and carriers, and dependents and captives. The degree of stratification varied by region.

allies.zh: 无统一盟友体系，各酋邦通常与邻近村落联盟、婚姻伙伴和贸易伙伴保持临时性结盟；与更广泛的中美洲交换网络存在间接联系。
allies.en: There was no unified alliance system; chiefdoms typically maintained temporary alliances with neighboring village confederates, marriage partners, and trade associates, while also participating indirectly in broader Mesoamerican exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系主要限于邻近竞争酋邦、边缘袭掠群体及争夺盐源、耕地、黑曜石和通道控制权的地方势力。
enemies.en: Hostile relations were mainly with rival neighboring chiefdoms, raiding groups on the margins, and local powers competing over salt sources, farmland, obsidian, and route control.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持地方平衡、扩大婚盟和交换网络、保护资源区与减少长期破坏性冲突为主。政治影响力更多通过礼物、仪式和再分配而非领土官僚统治来实现。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining local balance, expanding marriage and exchange networks, protecting resource zones, and limiting long-term destructive conflict. Political influence was exercised more through gifts, ritual, and redistribution than through territorial bureaucracy.

era.zh: 中美洲前古典时期晚段的区域酋邦阶段
era.en: Regional chiefdom phase of Late Preclassic Mesoamerica

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟玉米农业、多作物种植、竖井墓葬建筑、精细陶器工艺、棉纺织、黑曜石工具制作、区域性礼仪中心建设与较稳定的长距交换体系。
keyInnovations.en: Mature maize agriculture, multi-crop cultivation, shaft-tomb funerary construction, fine ceramic craftsmanship, cotton weaving, obsidian tool production, regional ceremonial-center building, and relatively stable long-distance exchange systems.

strengths.zh: 社会结构灵活，地方共同体韧性较强；农业与多样生态资源相结合；手工业和葬俗传统鲜明；酋长能通过仪式与再分配整合人群；区域交换网络扩大了资源获取能力。
strengths.en: Its strengths included flexible social structures, resilient local communities, agriculture integrated with diverse ecological resources, distinctive craft and funerary traditions, chiefly integration through ritual and redistribution, and exchange networks that broadened access to resources.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权和稳定官僚体制，跨地区协调能力有限；军事组织分散；对气候波动与收成失败较为脆弱；政治合法性高度依赖个人威望与贡赋循环。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were the absence of a unified polity and stable bureaucracy, limited interregional coordination, fragmented military organization, vulnerability to climatic fluctuation and harvest failure, and political legitimacy heavily dependent on personal prestige and tribute cycles.

outlook.zh: 221 BCE时总体稳定，区域酋邦仍将延续并在礼仪、贸易与社会分层上进一步发展。未来可能出现更强的地方中心，但短期内不太可能形成类似欧亚帝国的统一国家。
outlook.en: In 221 BCE the region was broadly stable, and its chiefdoms were likely to continue developing in ritual life, trade, and social stratification. Stronger local centers might emerge in the future, but a unified state comparable to the empires of Eurasia was unlikely in the near term.

description.zh: 中部与西部墨西哥分布着多样化的村落联盟和地区酋邦。
description.en: Central and western Mexico contained diverse village alliances and regional chiefdoms.

governmentForm.zh: 以村社与部族酋长为核心、权力较为松散的地方性部族治理结构。
governmentForm.en: A localized tribal system centered on village communities and tribal chiefs with relatively loose authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村落共同体为基础，内部有长老、祭司或巫师、自由农人与附属家庭之分。亲属关系和年龄资历决定社会地位，战士群体尚未完全职业化。不同河谷与山地群体在生活方式上存在明显差异。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on village communities, with elders, ritual specialists or shamans, free cultivators, and dependent households. Kinship and seniority shaped status, and warrior groups were not yet fully professionalized. Riverine and upland communities differed markedly in their way of life.

rulingClass.zh: 统治者主要是部族酋长、村落长老和掌握祭祀权威的人物。其权力更多依赖个人威望、馈赠能力和宗教威信，而非成文官制。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum consisted mainly of tribal chiefs, village elders, and figures holding ritual authority. Their power depended more on personal prestige, gift-giving capacity, and religious standing than on a written bureaucracy.

succession.zh: 继承通常在酋长家族或主要氏族内进行，但需获得长老和重要家庭认可。若继承人能力不足，也可能由旁支或更有威望者取代。
succession.en: Succession usually remained within the chief's family or major clan, but required acceptance by elders and leading households. If an heir proved weak, a collateral relative or more prestigious candidate could replace him.

structure.zh: 政权组织以酋长居地和周边村社网络为核心，没有固定都城与复杂官僚体系。决策往往通过长老会议、祭祀活动和部族协商完成，酋长在战争、分配和对外联系上起主导作用。
structure.en: Political organization centered on the chief's settlement and its surrounding village network, without a fixed capital or complex bureaucracy. Decisions were often made through councils of elders, ritual activity, and tribal consultation, while the chief led in warfare, redistribution, and external contacts.

function.zh: 管理粮食储备、劳役分配、贡礼接收和外来使者接待。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, tribute reception, and the reception of visitors or envoys.

function.zh: 主持祖灵祭祀、农业仪式与村社纠纷调停。
function.en: Oversaw ancestor rites, agricultural rituals, and mediation of village disputes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠村长、家族长老与河谷或山地聚落首领。行政边界不固定，更多按亲族、耕地与水源使用关系划分。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on village heads, clan elders, and leaders of riverine or upland settlements. Administrative boundaries were fluid and followed kinship, cultivation, and access to water more than formal territory.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和禁忌为主，由长老与祭司共同维持。案件多通过赔偿、调解和祭仪确认解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on custom and taboo, maintained by elders and ritual specialists. Most cases were settled through compensation, mediation, and ritual confirmation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有成熟定额税制，酋长主要收取稻谷、林产、手工品与劳役形式的贡赋。重大工程和战争时会临时加派人力与物资。
taxationSystem.en: There was no mature fixed tax system; chiefs mainly received tribute in rice, forest products, handicrafts, and labor service. Major works and warfare brought temporary demands for extra manpower and supplies.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 孟-高棉人群在稻作适应、竹木建筑、鼓乐舞蹈和森林环境利用方面积累了丰富经验。其口头传说和祖灵仪式为后世东南亚大陆文化奠定了深层基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Mon-Khmer peoples accumulated rich experience in rice adaptation, bamboo-wood construction, music and dance, and the use of forest environments. Their oral traditions and ancestor rites formed a deep foundation for later mainland Southeast Asian cultures.

languageFamily.zh: 南亚语系孟-高棉语族
languageFamily.en: Austroasiatic, Mon-Khmer branch

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜和地方自然神观念为主，强调人与山林、水域和祖先之间的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: Thought was centered on animism, ancestor reverence, and local nature spirits, stressing balanced relations among people, forests, waters, and ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本土成熟文字体系，文化知识以口传、仪式和记忆传承为主。
writingSystem.en: There was not yet a mature indigenous writing system; cultural knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, ritual, and memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以刀耕火种、湿地稻作萌芽、渔猎采集和林产品交换为主。生产水平较低，但在河谷与季风环境中已形成稳定生计网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on swidden cultivation, emerging wet-rice farming, fishing, hunting-gathering, and exchange in forest products. Productivity was modest, but stable subsistence networks had formed in river valleys and monsoon environments.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作、采集、渔猎、竹木器制作、陶器、林产采办
mainIndustries.en: Rice cultivation, gathering, fishing-hunting, bamboo-wood crafts, pottery, forest product collection

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、藤竹制品、树脂、蜂蜡、象牙、兽皮、陶器、盐
tradeGoods.en: Rice, rattan and bamboo goods, resin, beeswax, ivory, hides, pottery, salt

unitName.en: sack of rice, bundle of forest goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在稻谷储藏、牲畜数量、竹木屋舍、器具与亲族劳力。较富裕家庭能在旱季储存更多粮食并拥有更广的人际交换网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in rice stores, livestock, houses of bamboo and wood, tools, and kin labor. Better-off households could store more grain through the dry season and command broader exchange networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、季节性劳作、实物交换与酋长再分配为主的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on village self-sufficiency, seasonal labor, barter, and chiefly redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

grams of silver equivalent per year

source.zh: 交换馈赠与附属聚落奉献
source.en: Exchange Gifts and Dependent Settlement Offerings

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓“国库”主要是酋长控制的粮仓、林产储备、陶器和少量贵重物，而非金属货币储存。储备多用于赈济、祭祀和巩固追随关系。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' mainly consisted of granaries, forest-product stores, pottery, and a few prestige items under chiefly control rather than metallic money. These reserves were used for relief, ritual, and the maintenance of loyalty.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政取向偏重保障生计稳定与酋长再分配权威，通常避免过度征敛，以免村社迁徙或离散。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized subsistence stability and the chief's redistributive authority, usually avoiding excessive extraction that might cause village flight or fragmentation.

description.zh: 以矛、短刀、木盾和弓箭为主，多由成年男子临时征集。
description.en: Equipped mainly with spears, short blades, wooden shields, and bows, they were mostly adult men levied when needed.

description.zh: 负责村寨巡守、粮仓看护与边缘地带预警。
description.en: They guarded villages, watched over granaries, and provided warning in frontier zones.

technology.zh: 军事技术以木竹武器、石铁混用工具、弓矛和简易栅寨防御为主，水陆机动能力有限。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wooden and bamboo weapons, mixed stone-iron tools, bows and spears, and simple palisaded defenses, with limited mobility by land and water.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族冲突、山地与河谷资源争夺，以及疾病与季节性灾害引发的人口流动。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal conflict, competition over upland and river-valley resources, and migration pressures caused by disease and seasonal hardship.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于河谷、林缘和适于稻作的小盆地，聚落规模普遍较小。不同生态区之间往来频繁，但尚未形成真正城市。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in river valleys, forest margins, and small basins suited to rice cultivation, with generally small settlements. Contacts between ecological zones were frequent, but true cities had not yet formed.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长与长老、祭司或巫师、自由农人与猎民、依附家庭、俘虏或奴仆
socialClasses.en: Chiefs and elders, priests or shamans, free cultivators and hunters, dependent households, captives or servants

allies.zh: 多依靠邻近村社、通婚部族与临时防御同盟。
allies.en: Alliances depended mainly on neighboring villages, marriage-linked tribes, and temporary defensive coalitions.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象通常是争夺土地、水源与采集区的邻近部族。
enemies.en: Hostile forces were usually neighboring tribes competing for land, water, and gathering zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以互市、通婚、祭礼往来和有限报复冲突为主，整体偏向低强度竞争与协商。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations centered on barter, marriage exchange, ritual contact, and limited retaliatory conflict, amounting overall to low-intensity competition and negotiation.

era.zh: 东南亚早期铁器时代过渡期
era.en: Transitional Early Iron Age of Southeast Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 稻作扩展、竹木建筑、基础冶铁接触、陶器烧制与季节性储粮技术。
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of rice cultivation, bamboo-wood construction, early iron use, pottery firing, and seasonal grain-storage techniques.

strengths.zh: 适应热带森林与河谷环境，村社韧性强，生活方式多样，能在生态波动中维持基本稳定。
strengths.en: They were well adapted to tropical forests and river valleys, with resilient village structures and diverse livelihoods that supported stability amid ecological fluctuation.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低、人口有限、武装薄弱，且缺乏文字与大型基础设施支撑。
weaknesses.en: They had low political integration, limited population, weak armed capacity, and lacked writing and large-scale infrastructure.

outlook.zh: 若河谷农业与交换网络继续扩大，未来可能出现更稳定的酋邦与早期国家雏形，但现阶段仍以分散部族形态为主。
outlook.en: If river-valley agriculture and exchange networks continue to expand, more stable chiefdoms and proto-states may emerge; for now, however, the region remains predominantly tribal and dispersed.

description.zh: 公元前221年前后，孟-高棉人群仍以分散村社与部族酋长组织为主，经济依赖稻作、林产与口传传统。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, Mon-Khmer peoples were still organized mainly in dispersed villages and tribal chiefdoms, with economies based on rice, forest products, and oral tradition.

governmentForm.zh: 以滨海与河口聚落为中心、由长老和酋长主导的松散部族共同体。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal community centered on coastal and estuarine settlements led by elders and chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由酋长家族、长老、船队首领、自由民和依附者构成。沿海与河口聚落在渔业、航行与交换方面较活跃，内陆群体则更多依赖采集和刀耕火种。亲属关系与婚姻联盟是维系秩序的重要纽带。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of chiefly families, elders, boat leaders, free commoners, and dependents. Coastal and estuarine settlements were more active in fishing, seafaring, and exchange, while inland groups relied more on gathering and swidden cultivation. Kinship and marriage alliances were key bonds of order.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层为部族长老、酋长及掌握航路和交换网络的头人。其权威来自年龄、祖先声望、分配能力和对船只、人力的动员能力。
rulingClass.en: The ruling layer comprised tribal elders, chiefs, and headmen who controlled routes and exchange networks. Their authority derived from age, ancestral prestige, redistributive power, and the ability to mobilize boats and manpower.

succession.zh: 继承多在显要家族中进行，但需得到长老会议与相关亲族支持。若海上能力或组织力不足，酋位可能转给更有威望的亲属。
succession.en: Succession usually took place within prominent families but required support from councils of elders and related kin groups. If a successor lacked seafaring or organizational ability, the office might pass to a more prestigious relative.

structure.zh: 政治组织以河口聚落、海湾村社和小型航海共同体为核心，没有稳定宫廷或都城。首领通过主持祭礼、分配外来物资和组织航行来维持权威，重要事务由长老协商。
structure.en: Political organization centered on estuarine settlements, bay villages, and small maritime communities, without a stable court or capital. Chiefs maintained authority by sponsoring rituals, distributing imported goods, and organizing voyages, while major matters were discussed by elders.

function.zh: 管理粮食、渔获储存、贡礼和聚落日常分配。
function.en: Managed food supplies, stored fish catches, tribute goods, and daily redistribution within the settlement.

function.zh: 组织近海航行、交换活动、侦察水路并接待外来贸易者。
function.en: Organized coastal voyages, exchange activities, route scouting, and the reception of outside traders.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理以聚落首领和家族长老为主，沿海与河口各点自治性较强。统治范围更多体现为对人群、船只和交换节点的影响，而非明确疆界。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was led by settlement chiefs and clan elders, with strong autonomy among coastal and estuarine communities. Rule was expressed more through influence over people, boats, and exchange points than through clearly bounded territory.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯与禁忌体系，涉及婚姻、渔场、水路和赔偿责任。争端通常由长老调解，并辅以誓言和祭仪确认。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted mainly of custom and taboo concerning marriage, fishing grounds, waterways, and compensation obligations. Disputes were usually mediated by elders and confirmed through oaths and ritual acts.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有固定税制，酋长主要获得渔获、稻谷、林产、贝饰和劳役形式的贡纳。出海或修船等集体活动时，会集中征调物资与人力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed tax system; chiefs mainly received tribute in fish, rice, forest goods, shell ornaments, and labor. Collective ventures such as voyages or boat-building brought concentrated requisitions of supplies and manpower.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 马来先民在独木舟与早期舟船制造、沿海导航、渔猎技术及热带材料利用方面具有重要积累。其口头航海知识和潮汐经验为后来的海洋东南亚社会发展提供了基础。
culturalAchievements.en: Pre-state Malay peoples developed important skills in dugout and early boat building, coastal navigation, fishing technology, and the use of tropical materials. Their oral maritime knowledge and understanding of tides laid foundations for later maritime Southeast Asian societies.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系马来-波利尼西亚语族
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Malayo-Polynesian branch

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜和海洋自然灵观念为核心，重视海、风、雨林与先祖庇护。
philosophy.en: Belief centered on animism, ancestor reverence, and maritime nature spirits, with strong regard for the sea, winds, rainforest, and ancestral protection.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无成熟文字体系，知识主要经由口传、航海经验与仪式歌谣传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature writing system; knowledge was transmitted mainly through oral tradition, seafaring experience, and ritual songs.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔业、采集、刀耕火种、小规模稻作和沿海交换为主。由于掌握海湾、河口和近海交通，部分聚落在区域交换中具有高于人口规模的战略价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on fishing, gathering, swidden farming, small-scale rice cultivation, and coastal exchange. Because they controlled bays, estuaries, and near-shore routes, some settlements held strategic value disproportionate to their population size.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、舟船制作、采集、刀耕火种农业、盐制取、贝饰加工
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, boat building, gathering, swidden agriculture, salt making, shell ornament production

tradeGoods.zh: 鱼干、盐、树脂、藤竹、龟甲、贝饰、香木、羽毛、稻米
tradeGoods.en: Dried fish, salt, resin, rattan, turtle shell, shell ornaments, aromatic woods, feathers, rice

unitName.en: string of shells, sack of salt

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多体现为船只、渔网、粮食、贝饰、工具与可动员的亲族劳力。拥有优良舟船和稳定交换伙伴的家庭地位更高。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was reflected in boats, nets, food stores, shell valuables, tools, and mobilizable kin labor. Families with good vessels and reliable exchange partners enjoyed higher status.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以聚落自给、海陆兼营、物物交换和酋长控制的礼物再分配为特点的海岸部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A coastal tribal economy marked by settlement self-sufficiency, combined maritime-land subsistence, barter exchange, and chiefly gift redistribution.
