category.zh: 首领家廷与馈赠再分配

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要是干鱼、盐、树脂、贝饰、船材和少量珍贵舶来品，并不形成统一金属货币金库。其作用在于救荒、修船和维持交换信誉。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves consisted mainly of dried fish, salt, resin, shell valuables, boat timber, and a few prestige imports, not a centralized metallic treasury. Their purpose was famine relief, boat repair, and maintaining exchange credibility.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持海上活动和聚落凝聚力，征纳通常较轻，以避免成员迁往其他海湾或河口。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maritime activity and settlement cohesion, and tribute demands were usually kept light to avoid people relocating to other bays or estuaries.

description.zh: 使用矛、短刀、木盾与弓箭，负责村寨防御和短程冲突。
description.en: Armed with spears, short blades, wooden shields, and bows, they defended settlements and fought in short-range clashes.

description.zh: 依托独木舟与轻型舟船执行巡逻、拦截和沿岸突袭。
description.en: Using dugouts and light craft, they carried out patrols, interception, and coastal raids.

technology.zh: 具备独木舟、缝合木板舟雏形、竹木武器、弓矛和简易栅寨等技术，善于近海机动但不擅长陆上大会战。
technology.en: They possessed dugout canoes, proto-stitched plank boats, bamboo-wood weapons, bows, spears, and simple palisades, excelling in near-shore mobility but not in large land battles.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括邻近海岸部族冲突、海盗式劫掠、季风风暴和食物短缺。内陆与沿海之间的资源竞争也可能引发摩擦。
threats.en: Major threats included conflict with neighboring coastal tribes, pirate-like raiding, monsoon storms, and food shortages. Resource competition between inland and coastal groups could also spark friction.

populationDescription.zh: 人口多分布在河口、潟湖、海湾和近海岛屿，小型聚落沿水道串联。真正密集的城市尚未出现，但部分港湾点已具区域集散功能。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in estuaries, lagoons, bays, and near-shore islands, with small settlements linked by waterways. Dense cities had not yet emerged, but some harbor points already served as regional gathering places.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族与长老、船队头人、自由渔农、工匠与采集者、依附者与俘虏
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families and elders, boat leaders, free fisher-farmers, artisans and gatherers, dependents and captives

allies.zh: 主要盟友为通婚聚落、共同航路伙伴和临时结盟的沿海部族。
allies.en: Main allies were marriage-linked settlements, partners on shared routes, and temporarily allied coastal tribes.

enemies.zh: 敌对势力多为争夺渔场、泊地和交换节点的邻近海岸群体。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring coastal groups competing for fishing grounds, anchorages, and exchange nodes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以互通有无、维护航道、通婚结盟和有限海上报复为主，务求保持海岸网络畅通。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on exchange, safeguarding routes, marriage alliances, and limited maritime retaliation, aiming to keep coastal networks open.

era.zh: 海洋东南亚早期铁器时代前国家阶段
era.en: Pre-State Early Iron Age Maritime Southeast Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟与早期舟船制造、近海导航、渔网技术、盐制取与热带林产加工。
keyInnovations.en: Dugout and early boatbuilding, coastal navigation, net fishing techniques, salt production, and tropical forest-product processing.

strengths.zh: 海岸适应力强、机动性高、具备早期航海经验，并控制若干关键河口与近海交换点。
strengths.en: They had strong coastal adaptation, high mobility, early seafaring skill, and control over several important estuarine and near-shore exchange points.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、政治分散、农业基础薄弱，难以支撑大规模长期战争或复杂国家机构。
weaknesses.en: They had a small population, fragmented politics, and a weak agricultural base, making sustained large-scale war or complex state institutions difficult.

outlook.zh: 随着海上交换扩大和航海技术积累，未来最有可能向港口型酋邦发展，但在公元前221年前后仍处于前国家阶段。
outlook.en: As maritime exchange expands and seafaring skills accumulate, they are most likely to develop into port-oriented chiefdoms, but around 221 BCE they remain firmly in a pre-state stage.

description.zh: 公元前221年前后，马来先民主要生活在河口与海湾聚落中，以渔业、航海和部族交换网络维系社会。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, pre-state Malay peoples lived mainly in estuarine and bay settlements, sustaining society through fishing, seafaring, and tribal exchange networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族酋长与自由民集会共同维系的松散部族政治体。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal polity sustained jointly by chieftains and assemblies of free people.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、氏族与地方部落为核心，自由民构成主要生产与作战力量。上层为酋长、战士首领与祭司，下层包括依附者、奴仆与战俘。各聚落之间联系存在，但尚未形成统一国家结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on households, clans, and local tribes, with free farmers forming the main productive and fighting population. The upper stratum consisted of chieftains, war leaders, and ritual specialists, while dependents, servants, and captives occupied lower positions. Connections existed among settlements, but no unified state structure had yet emerged.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是地方酋长家族与其追随武士。其权威建立在馈赠、战利品分配、宗教主持与血缘声望之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of local chieftainly families and their warrior retainers. Their authority rested on gift-giving, distribution of spoils, ritual leadership, and kin prestige.

succession.zh: 继承多由酋长家族内部推举，通常偏向成年男性亲属。若继承人威望不足，部众集会可支持其他强势支系首领。
succession.en: Succession was usually decided within the chieftain's family, often favoring adult male relatives. If an heir lacked prestige, the tribal assembly could support another strong leader from a related branch.

structure.zh: 政治组织以地方聚落和氏族为基础，酋长在战争、祭祀与争端调解中发挥核心作用。重大事务往往由自由民集会协商决定，行政分工极为有限。没有固定都城，权力随首领驻地与季节性集会地点而移动。
structure.en: Political organization was based on local settlements and clans, with the chieftain playing a central role in war, ritual, and dispute mediation. Major affairs were often decided through assemblies of free people, and administrative specialization was very limited. There was no fixed capital, and authority moved with the chief's residence and seasonal meeting places.

function.zh: 协助酋长管理贡赋、召集战士、主持宴会与传达命令。
function.en: Assisted the chieftain in managing tribute, summoning warriors, hosting feasts, and conveying orders.

name.en: Council of Elders and Ritual Specialists

function.zh: 处理祭祀、习惯法裁决、婚姻联盟与部族间协调。
function.en: Handled ritual matters, customary legal judgments, marriage alliances, and inter-tribal coordination.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由聚落首领、家族长者和地方战士头目共同管理。行政主要依赖口头命令、习惯与个人关系，而非成文机构。
localAdmin.en: Local affairs were managed jointly by settlement heads, clan elders, and local war leaders. Administration relied primarily on oral command, custom, and personal ties rather than written institutions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、赔偿原则和血亲调解为主，重视和解与赔偿而非系统性刑罚。严重冲突可能引发复仇、逐出或公开裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, compensation, and kin mediation, emphasizing settlement and restitution rather than systematic punishment. Serious disputes could lead to feud, expulsion, or public judgment.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在固定税制，酋长主要依靠贡赋、宴会回馈、战利品分配和临时征发维系权力。财富多以牲畜、皮毛、铁器与粮食形式交纳。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed tax system; chieftains relied on tribute, reciprocal feasting, distribution of war booty, and ad hoc levies. Wealth was contributed mainly in livestock, furs, iron goods, and food.

religion.zh: 北欧多神教
religion.en: Norse polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 口头史诗、英雄传说、祭仪诗歌与木工船只传统已具雏形。金属工艺、木雕与装饰性器物体现出鲜明的北方审美。
culturalAchievements.en: Oral epics, heroic legend, ritual poetry, and traditions of wooden boatbuilding were already taking shape. Metalwork, wood carving, and decorative objects displayed a distinct northern aesthetic.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系日耳曼语族前古诺斯语方言群
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Germanic branch, pre-Old Norse dialect continuum

philosophy.zh: 以北欧多神信仰、祖先崇拜、荣誉伦理与宿命观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on Norse polytheistic belief, ancestor veneration, honor ethics, and a sense of fate.

writingSystem.zh: 尚未广泛使用成熟文字，符号记忆与口述传统占主导；早期卢恩原型可能仅处于萌芽阶段。
writingSystem.en: No mature writing system was in wide use; symbolic memory and oral tradition predominated, while proto-runic forms, if present at all, remained embryonic.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农牧、渔猎、采集和地方手工业为基础，整体货币化程度很低。财富主要体现在牲畜、土地使用权、铁器、船只与储藏食物上。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence farming, herding, fishing, foraging, and local craft production, with very low monetization. Wealth was expressed mainly through livestock, use rights to land, iron tools, boats, and stored food.

mainIndustries.zh: 农牧业、渔猎、皮毛采集、铁器锻造、木工与造船
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture and herding, fishing and hunting, fur collection, iron smithing, woodworking, and boatbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、琥珀、鱼干、兽皮、铁器、骨角制品、奴仆
tradeGoods.en: Furs, amber, dried fish, hides, iron goods, bone and antler products, and slaves

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富有限，主要由牲畜、小型农具、木屋和储粮构成。酋长家庭则控制较多船只、金属器、武器和附庸劳力。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary households possessed modest wealth in livestock, simple tools, timber houses, and food stores. Chieftainly households controlled more boats, metal goods, weapons, and dependent labor.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、自给交换、酋长再分配和季节性互市组成的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining household production, subsistence exchange, chieftainly redistribution, and seasonal trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏规模很小，主要由银饰、武器、牲畜、琥珀与储粮构成，而非统一铸币储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was small and consisted mainly of silver ornaments, weapons, livestock, amber, and food stores rather than a uniform coin reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持酋长威望和战士忠诚为先，偏向再分配与应急征集。收支受战事、歉收和海上贸易波动影响很大。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice prioritized maintaining the chieftain's prestige and warrior loyalty, favoring redistribution and emergency levies. Revenues and spending were highly sensitive to warfare, poor harvests, and fluctuations in seaborne trade.

description.zh: 由自由民与酋长追随者组成，使用矛、盾、斧和少量铁剑作战。
description.en: Composed of free men and the chieftain's retainers, fighting with spears, shields, axes, and a small number of iron swords.

description.zh: 依靠轻型木船进行沿岸巡行、渔猎护卫和短距袭击。
description.en: Used light wooden boats for coastal movement, protection of fishing activity, and short-range raids.

description.zh: 负责聚落防卫、侦察和冬季机动作战。
description.en: Responsible for settlement defense, scouting, and mobile action in winter conditions.

technology.zh: 军备以木盾、长矛、骨角器和少量铁制兵器为主，具备基础造船与寒地生存能力，但缺乏攻城和大规模组织技术。
technology.en: Armament centered on wooden shields, spears, bone and antler equipment, and limited iron weapons; it possessed basic boatbuilding and cold-weather survival skills but lacked siege methods and large-scale organization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族冲突、资源短缺、冬季饥荒与沿海竞争。内部酋长间争权也可能削弱统一行动。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal conflict, resource scarcity, winter famine, and coastal competition. Rivalry among chieftains could also undermine unified action.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于峡湾、林地与沿海地带，以小型聚落和季节性营地为主。出生率与死亡率都较高，寒冷环境限制人口增长。
populationDescription.en: The population was thinly spread across fjords, forests, and coasts, mainly in small settlements and seasonal camps. Both birth and mortality rates were high, and the cold environment constrained population growth.

socialClasses.zh: 酋长家族、武士追随者、自由农民、依附者与奴仆
socialClasses.en: Chieftainly families, warrior retainers, free farmers, dependents, and servants/slaves

allies.zh: 以临时婚姻联盟、贸易伙伴与邻近友好部落为主，无持久国家级盟约。
allies.en: Allies were mainly temporary marriage partners, trade associates, and neighboring friendly tribes, with no lasting state-level treaties.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象多为争夺牧场、渔场与航道的邻近部落。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring tribes competing for pasture, fisheries, and sea routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持贸易接触、避免被包围和在必要时发动报复性袭击为主。远程外交尚不发达，更多依靠个人使者与礼物交换。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on maintaining trade contacts, avoiding encirclement, and launching retaliatory raids when necessary. Long-distance diplomacy was undeveloped and relied more on personal envoys and gift exchange.

era.zh: 北欧晚期铁器时代前段
era.en: Early Northern Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 早期铁器锻造、木船制造、寒地畜牧、渔猎工具改进与琥珀加工。
keyInnovations.en: Early ironworking, wooden boat construction, cold-climate herding, improved fishing and hunting equipment, and amber working.

strengths.zh: 适应寒冷环境，聚落机动性强，海岸航行与渔猎能力突出，社会凝聚力依托氏族与荣誉关系。
strengths.en: Well adapted to cold environments, highly mobile in settlement patterns, strong in coastal travel and fishing-hunting, and socially cohesive through clan and honor ties.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少，农业基础薄弱，政治分散，缺乏成熟行政与文字记录体系。
weaknesses.en: Population was sparse, the agricultural base was weak, politics were fragmented, and there was no mature administrative or writing system.

outlook.zh: 若沿海交流扩大并出现更稳定的酋邦，未来可能发展出更强的航海文化与区域首领权力。
outlook.en: If coastal exchange expanded and more stable chiefdoms emerged, the region could develop a stronger maritime culture and more durable regional leadership.

description.zh: 古北欧人是分布于北欧沿海与林地、以农牧渔猎和酋长集会为核心的早期铁器时代部族社会。
description.en: The Early Norsemen were an Iron Age tribal society spread across northern coasts and woodlands, centered on farming, herding, fishing, hunting, and chieftain-led assemblies.

governmentForm.zh: 由部族长老与地方首领共治的松散山地—河谷部落联盟。
governmentForm.en: A loose highland-river valley tribal confederation governed jointly by elders and local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村寨、宗族和迁徙中的部落群为基础，农业与采集并存。长老、祭师与勇士首领居于上层，普通农户与依附家庭构成主体。不同群体之间常因迁徙、婚姻与水源使用而建立联系。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on villages, kin groups, and migrating tribal clusters, combining agriculture with foraging. Elders, ritual specialists, and warrior leaders formed the upper stratum, while ordinary farming households and dependents made up the majority. Different groups were linked through migration, marriage, and access to water.

rulingClass.zh: 统治者主要是部族长老、村寨首领与掌握祭仪权威的家族。其权力来自协调灌溉、分配耕地、主持盟誓和调停争端。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of tribal elders, settlement heads, and families holding ritual authority. Their power came from coordinating irrigation, allocating fields, presiding over oaths, and mediating disputes.

succession.zh: 继承通常由首领家族内部协商决定，可由年长男性或最具威望者接任。若部族迁徙或遭遇危机，武勇与组织能力可能比单纯血缘更重要。
succession.en: Succession was usually determined within the leading family by negotiation, often favoring senior males or the most respected figure. During migration or crisis, martial ability and organizational skill could matter more than bloodline alone.

structure.zh: 政治结构分散，村寨是基本治理单位，较大的部族群在战争或迁徙时临时联合。长老议事和首领权威并行，没有常设官僚体系。无固定都城，首领驻地多依附于重要河谷或山间盆地。
structure.en: The political structure was decentralized, with the village as the basic unit of governance, while larger tribal groupings united temporarily during war or migration. Councils of elders coexisted with chiefly authority, and there was no standing bureaucracy. There was no fixed capital, and leaders' seats were usually tied to important valleys or intermontane basins.

function.zh: 处理迁徙、婚姻、土地分配、争端裁决与部族协商。
function.en: Handled migration, marriage, land allocation, dispute judgment, and tribal consultation.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、安排播种时序并协调小型灌溉设施。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, organized agricultural timing, and coordinated small-scale irrigation works.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由村寨首领、家族长者和祭师共同负责。实际管理高度依赖口头传统、亲属纽带和季节性集会。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out jointly by village heads, clan elders, and ritual specialists. Practical governance depended heavily on oral tradition, kinship ties, and seasonal gatherings.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和长老裁断为主，强调调解、赔偿和恢复群体和谐。涉及婚姻、水源和田地的争端通常通过公开协商解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary and administered by elders, emphasizing mediation, compensation, and restoration of communal harmony. Disputes over marriage, water, and fields were usually settled through open negotiation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有固定税收制度，首领主要接受谷物、劳役、猎获和手工品形式的贡纳。战争或大型祭仪时可额外征集人力与物资。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed taxation system; leaders chiefly received tribute in grain, labor, game, and handicrafts. Extra manpower and goods could be levied during war or major rituals.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了适应山地与河谷环境的稻作、竹木建筑、织造与口头叙事传统。节庆舞蹈、祖灵仪式和青铜铁器混合使用体现出多元文化过渡特征。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed rice cultivation adapted to upland and valley environments, bamboo-wood architecture, weaving, and oral narrative traditions. Festival dances, ancestor rituals, and the mixed use of bronze and iron tools reflected a transitional and diverse cultural milieu.

languageFamily.zh: 壮侗语系台语支早期方言群
languageFamily.en: Kra-Dai language family, early Tai branch dialect groups

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵观念、祖灵崇拜、土地与水灵祭祀及共同体伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, ancestor veneration, worship of land and water spirits, and communal ethics.

writingSystem.zh: 尚无本族成熟文字，知识与谱系主要依靠口述、歌谣和仪式记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no mature native writing system; knowledge and genealogy were transmitted mainly through oral recitation, song, and ritual memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以稻作、旱作、狩猎、采集和手工业为基础，村寨自给色彩浓厚。交换主要在邻近部族之间进行，贵重物资常以盐、金属器和织物体现价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on rice cultivation, dry farming, hunting, gathering, and handicrafts, with villages strongly oriented toward self-sufficiency. Exchange occurred mainly among neighboring groups, and value was often represented by salt, metal goods, and textiles.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、刀耕火种、竹木工、纺织、狩猎采集、小型冶金
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, swidden farming, bamboo and woodcraft, weaving, hunting-gathering, and small-scale metallurgy

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、盐、藤竹制品、布匹、羽毛、山货、铜铁工具
tradeGoods.en: Rice, salt, rattan and bamboo products, cloth, feathers, upland forest goods, and bronze-iron tools

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常体现为稻谷储备、家畜、农具、织物和竹木房舍。首领家庭拥有更多劳役、较好的金属器和更广泛的婚姻网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth usually took the form of stored rice, livestock, tools, textiles, and bamboo-wood houses. Leading families possessed more labor obligations, better metal goods, and wider marriage networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以村寨自给、部族互换和首领再分配为主的早期农耕部族经济。
economicSystem.en: An early agrarian tribal economy based on village self-sufficiency, inter-tribal exchange, and chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 库藏多为谷仓储备、盐、金属工具、布匹和家畜，并非铸币性财政库存。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury holdings consisted mostly of granary reserves, salt, metal tools, cloth, and livestock rather than coined fiscal stock.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政目标在于确保粮食安全、维持长老权威和支撑迁徙或防御需求。收支高度依赖收成、水利状况与部族间和平程度。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal aims were to secure food supplies, maintain elder authority, and support migration or defense when needed. Revenue and spending depended heavily on harvests, irrigation conditions, and the level of peace among tribes.

description.zh: 由农民战士组成，使用矛、刀、木盾与弓箭，适合山地和林地作战。
description.en: Made up of farmer-warriors using spears, blades, wooden shields, and bows, suited to fighting in hills and forests.

description.zh: 负责侦察、伏击、护送和熟悉地形的机动行动。
description.en: Handled scouting, ambushes, escort duties, and mobile operations in familiar terrain.

technology.zh: 军事技术较为简单，依靠竹木武器、青铜铁混合工具、弓箭与地形优势。缺乏重装部队、攻城器械和长期远征能力。
technology.en: Military technology was relatively simple, relying on bamboo-wood weapons, mixed bronze and iron tools, bows, and terrain advantage. It lacked heavy troops, siege equipment, and the capacity for sustained long-distance campaigning.

threats.zh: 威胁主要来自邻近部族争夺耕地水源、山地迁徙冲突及疫病和歉收引发的社会紧张。
threats.en: Threats came mainly from neighboring tribal competition for arable land and water, highland migration conflicts, and social strain caused by disease or poor harvests.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于山麓、河谷和盆地边缘，聚落规模较小且流动性较强。农业定居趋势已经出现，但仍保留明显的迁徙与林地利用特征。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in foothills, river valleys, and basin margins, with relatively small and mobile settlements. A trend toward agricultural settlement had emerged, but marked mobility and forest use remained important.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与首领家族、祭师、自由农户、依附家庭、战俘或奴属
socialClasses.en: Elders and leading families, ritual specialists, free cultivators, dependent households, and captives or bondspeople

allies.zh: 主要依靠婚姻结盟、共同祭仪和互助防御形成临时盟友关系。
allies.en: Alliances were formed mainly through marriage ties, shared rituals, and mutual defense arrangements.

enemies.zh: 敌对者多为争夺迁徙通道、盐源和耕地的邻近山地或河谷部族。
enemies.en: Enemies were usually neighboring highland or valley tribes competing for migration routes, salt sources, and arable land.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重视保持通道开放、避免被强邻压迫并通过馈赠稳定边缘关系。尚未形成远程国家外交，交往以部落层面为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized keeping routes open, avoiding domination by stronger neighbors, and stabilizing frontier relations through gift exchange. There was no developed long-range state diplomacy; external relations remained tribal in scale.

era.zh: 东南亚山地早期铁器时代过渡阶段
era.en: Transitional Early Iron Age in Highland Southeast Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 山地稻作、小型灌溉、竹木建筑、织造技术、青铜与早期铁器并用。
keyInnovations.en: Highland rice cultivation, small-scale irrigation, bamboo-wood architecture, weaving, and the combined use of bronze and early iron.

strengths.zh: 善于适应复杂山地环境，村寨韧性较强，农业与林地资源互补，社会以亲属网络维系稳定。
strengths.en: They were adept at adapting to complex highland environments, village communities were resilient, agriculture and forest resources complemented each other, and kinship networks supported stability.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低，军事规模小，物资积累有限，易受气候、疾病和迁徙压力影响。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was low, military capacity was small, surplus accumulation was limited, and the society was vulnerable to climate, disease, and migration pressures.

outlook.zh: 若河谷稻作扩大并形成更稳定的首领权力，未来可能演化出早期酋邦和区域性农耕政体。
outlook.en: If valley rice agriculture expanded and chiefly authority became more stable, these groups could evolve into early chiefdoms and regional agrarian polities.

description.zh: 泰族先民是分布于东南亚山地与河谷、以稻作和万物有灵信仰维系的早期部族社会。
description.en: The Early Thai Peoples were a tribal society spread across Southeast Asian highlands and river valleys, sustained by rice cultivation and animist belief.

dynasty.zh: 科伊桑狩猎采集部族群

governmentForm.zh: 以长老协商和小型营群共识为核心的分散部族组织。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order centered on elder consultation and consensus within small bands.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以小型亲属营群为单位，人口分散，流动性高。地位差异有限，但经验丰富的猎手、长老和治疗师拥有较高威望。资源分享、婚姻联结与互助网络对生存至关重要。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized into small kin-based bands with sparse population and high mobility. Status differences were limited, but skilled hunters, elders, and healers held greater prestige. Resource sharing, marriage ties, and mutual aid networks were crucial to survival.

rulingClass.zh: 严格意义上的统治阶层并不明显，长老、资深猎手和萨满式治疗者具有引导作用。其权威主要来自经验、声望与调解能力，而非强制权力。
rulingClass.en: There was no sharply defined ruling class; elders, senior hunters, and shamanic healers served as guiding figures. Their authority derived from experience, prestige, and mediation rather than coercive power.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常并非正式世袭，而是在家族声望基础上由营群自然认可。若某位长者失去信任，成员可以迁离并追随他人。
succession.en: Leadership was generally not formalized as strict heredity, but emerged from family prestige and band recognition. If an elder lost trust, members could simply move away and follow someone else.

structure.zh: 治理单位是流动营群和季节性聚会圈，长老在狩猎分配、婚姻安排和冲突调解中发挥作用。没有固定首都、官僚或常设机构。跨营群协调通常通过婚姻联系、共享水源和仪式性交往实现。
structure.en: The core units of governance were mobile bands and seasonal gathering circles, with elders guiding hunting distribution, marriage arrangements, and conflict mediation. There was no fixed capital, bureaucracy, or permanent institution. Coordination across bands was usually achieved through marriage ties, shared water sources, and ritual interaction.

function.zh: 调解争端、安排迁徙路线、维护水源与营地规则。
function.en: Mediated disputes, arranged migration routes, and maintained rules for water sources and camps.

function.zh: 主持治病仪式、巩固群体团结并传承神话与禁忌。
function.en: Led healing rites, reinforced group cohesion, and transmitted myths and taboos.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理极其简约，以营群内部共识和长者劝导为主。营地间关系多通过访亲、共享狩猎区和互通信息维系。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was extremely light, based mainly on internal band consensus and elder persuasion. Relations among camps were maintained through visiting kin, sharing hunting grounds, and exchanging information.

legalSystem.zh: 法律表现为习惯、禁忌和群体压力，重视和解与避免暴力升级。严重纠纷常通过分营、赔礼或长者调停处理。
legalSystem.en: Law took the form of custom, taboo, and communal pressure, emphasizing reconciliation and avoidance of escalating violence. Serious disputes were often handled through group fission, apology, or elder mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正式税制，群体首领仅在分配猎物、水源和交换物资时享有有限优先权。公共资源依赖共享而非征税。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders had only limited priority in the distribution of game, water, and exchanged goods. Collective resources depended on sharing rather than taxation.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 岩画传统、追踪狩猎知识、复杂的口头叙事与治疗舞仪构成其文化成就核心。对动物行为、季节变化和干旱地带生态的认识极为精细。
culturalAchievements.en: Rock art traditions, tracking knowledge, complex oral narration, and healing dances formed the core of their cultural achievements. Their understanding of animal behavior, seasonal change, and arid-zone ecology was exceptionally refined.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑诸语言群（含多种搭嘴音语言）
languageFamily.en: Khoisan language groups, including several click-consonant languages

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、自然敬畏、祖先记忆和治疗仪式中的精神力量观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, reverence for nature, ancestral memory, and beliefs in spiritual power manifested in healing rituals.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，知识通过口述神话、歌谣、舞蹈和岩画象征传承。
writingSystem.en: There was no writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral myth, song, dance, and the symbolic medium of rock art.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎采集为主，辅以小规模交换和局部牧养接触。物质剩余很少，但生态适应能力强，资源价值主要体现在食物、水源、兽皮与工具上。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on hunting and gathering, supplemented by small-scale exchange and limited contact with herding. Material surplus was low, but ecological adaptation was strong, and value lay mainly in food, water, hides, and tools.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、兽皮加工、骨石器制作、岩画与仪式工艺
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, hide processing, bone and stone toolmaking, and ritual-art production

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、鸵鸟蛋壳珠、药用植物、骨器、石料、少量肉干
tradeGoods.en: Hides, ostrich eggshell beads, medicinal plants, bone tools, stone materials, and small amounts of dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富很少以积累形式表现，更多体现在工具质量、社会互助关系和对水源、猎场知识的掌握。珠饰和兽皮具有一定身份与交换价值。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was rarely expressed as accumulation, but rather in tool quality, social support ties, and knowledge of water sources and hunting grounds. Beads and hides carried some status and exchange value.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以共享、互惠和季节性迁徙利用资源为特征的狩猎采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A hunter-gatherer economy characterized by sharing, reciprocity, and seasonal mobility in resource use.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无真正意义上的国库，所谓储备主要是干肉、皮革、珠饰和少量工具材料的公共留存。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no true state treasury; what existed as reserve consisted mainly of dried meat, leather, beads, and small stocks of tool material held in common.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政实质上是共享分配制度，重点在于保障弱者、平衡营群内部关系并应对旱季风险。缺乏稳定剩余使其难以形成集中财政。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice was essentially a system of shared distribution, aimed at supporting vulnerable members, balancing internal band relations, and coping with dry-season risk. The lack of stable surplus prevented the formation of centralized finance.

description.zh: 以弓箭、投掷武器和追踪技巧为主，擅长小规模伏击与远距离追猎。
description.en: Used bows, throwing weapons, and tracking skills, excelling in small ambushes and long-distance pursuit.

description.zh: 负责保护营地、看守水源并在突发冲突中组织撤离。
description.en: Protected camps, guarded water sources, and organized withdrawal during sudden conflict.

technology.zh: 军事技术极为轻便，依靠弓箭、毒箭、追踪、潜行和地形认知，而非集群硬战。缺乏金属铠甲、攻城技术和大规模后勤。
technology.en: Military technology was extremely light, relying on bows, poisoned arrows, tracking, stealth, and environmental knowledge rather than massed combat. It lacked metal armor, siege methods, and large-scale logistics.

threats.zh: 主要威胁是旱灾、水源竞争、邻近牧民扩张和局部营群冲突。生态压力往往比持续战争更危险。
threats.en: Main threats were drought, competition over water, expansion by neighboring herders, and local band conflict. Ecological stress was often more dangerous than sustained warfare.

populationDescription.zh: 人口高度分散在南部非洲干旱、半干旱地区，以流动营群和季节性汇聚点为主。人口密度极低，但对本地生态环境有高度适应性。
populationDescription.en: The population was highly dispersed across arid and semi-arid southern Africa, organized mainly in mobile bands and seasonal aggregation points. Population density was extremely low, but adaptation to local ecology was very high.

socialClasses.zh: 长老、资深猎手、治疗者、普通营群成员
socialClasses.en: Elders, senior hunters, healers, and ordinary band members

allies.zh: 盟友多为共享水源、通婚或互通猎场信息的邻近营群。
allies.en: Allies were usually neighboring bands that shared water, intermarried, or exchanged information on hunting grounds.

enemies.zh: 敌对对象主要是争夺水源和资源通道的邻近群体，偶尔也包括扩张中的牧民社群。
enemies.en: Enemies were mainly groups competing for water and access routes, and at times expanding pastoral communities.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持资源共享边界、避免长期仇杀和保存迁徙通道为主。外交形式以访亲、礼物交换和仪式性接触为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining boundaries of shared resources, avoiding prolonged feuds, and preserving migration routes. Diplomacy took the form of visiting kin, exchanging gifts, and ritual contact.

era.zh: 南部非洲晚期石器时代—早期牧养接触期
era.en: Later Stone Age southern Africa with early pastoral contact

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭狩猎、精细追踪术、鸵鸟蛋壳珠制作、岩画传统与旱地生存知识。
keyInnovations.en: Poison-arrow hunting, refined tracking, ostrich eggshell bead production, rock art traditions, and dryland survival knowledge.

strengths.zh: 生态知识深厚，机动性强，社会共享机制有助于在严酷环境中维持生存与弹性。
strengths.en: They possessed deep ecological knowledge, high mobility, and sharing mechanisms that supported survival and resilience in harsh environments.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、物质积累低、政治集中度弱，面对外来牧业扩张和长期资源压力时较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: They had low population, limited material accumulation, and weak political centralization, making them vulnerable to expanding pastoralism and sustained resource pressure.

outlook.zh: 在短期内仍可凭借灵活迁徙维持稳定，但若周边牧业人口增加，其活动空间可能逐步收缩。
outlook.en: In the short term they could remain stable through flexible mobility, but if surrounding pastoral populations grew, their range of movement would likely contract over time.

description.zh: 科伊桑人是南部非洲以狩猎采集、共享分配和高度生态适应为特征的分散营群社会。
description.en: The Khoisan Peoples were a dispersed band society of southern Africa characterized by hunting-gathering, shared distribution, and high ecological adaptation.

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族长老会议与战时首领并存的松散部落治理形式为主。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal system centered on clan elders' councils with temporary war leaders in times of conflict.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族群体和流动营地为核心，家庭、氏族与邻近部落构成基本组织单位。地位通常取决于年龄、狩猎能力、口才、婚姻网络与仪式声望。整体分化较低，但长老、萨满与勇士享有更高影响力。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on kin groups and mobile camps, with families, clans, and neighboring bands forming the main units of organization. Status usually depended on age, hunting skill, eloquence, marriage ties, and ritual prestige. Social stratification was limited overall, but elders, shamans, and respected warriors held greater influence.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在固定世袭贵族，实际统治者多为部落长老、知名猎手与主持祭仪者。其权威主要来自共识、声望与资源分配能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; effective leadership rested with tribal elders, renowned hunters, and ritual specialists. Their authority derived mainly from consensus, prestige, and the ability to distribute resources.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常不严格世袭，多由长老会议推举或在部族内部协商形成。战时领袖可因军事能力而临时获得指挥权。
succession.en: Leadership was usually not strictly hereditary, but emerged through elder selection or internal negotiation within the band. Wartime leaders could gain temporary command through proven martial ability.

structure.zh: 政治结构十分分散，各营地与氏族拥有高度自治。跨部落事务如迁徙路线、婚姻交换与防御合作，通常通过长老集会协商处理。
structure.en: The political structure was highly decentralized, with each camp and clan enjoying substantial autonomy. Inter-tribal matters such as migration routes, marriage exchange, and defensive cooperation were usually handled through councils of elders.

function.zh: 协调营地迁徙、争端调解、婚姻安排与对外关系。
function.en: Coordinated camp movements, mediated disputes, arranged marriages, and managed external relations.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、分配猎获并在危险时期组织武装行动。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, distributed hunting yields, and organized armed action during dangerous periods.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各流动聚落自行处理，通常以若干家庭组成的营地为单位。重大纠纷可提交给跨氏族长老调停。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled within each mobile settlement, usually organized at the camp level among several families. Major disputes could be referred to inter-clan elder mediation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为习惯法，重视补偿、和解与亲族调停。对严重冒犯行为可采取放逐、报复或祭仪性赔偿。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and kin-based mediation. Serious offenses could lead to exile, retaliation, or ritualized compensation.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正规赋税，资源主要通过共享、馈赠与猎获分配来再分配。战时或大型仪式时，各营地需提供食物、劳力与武器。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; resources were redistributed through sharing, gifting, and the allocation of hunting returns. In wartime or during major rituals, camps contributed food, labor, and weapons.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长适应草原环境的狩猎知识、皮革加工与口传叙事传统。其仪式舞蹈、身体装饰与石骨器制作体现出鲜明地域文化特征。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in grassland-adapted hunting knowledge, hide working, and oral narrative traditions. Their ritual dances, body decoration, and stone-and-bone toolmaking reflected distinctive regional cultural traits.

languageFamily.zh: 多种南美原住民语言，可能属乔南语系及邻近未定分类语言群
languageFamily.en: Multiple Indigenous South American languages, likely including Chonan and neighboring unclassified language groups

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵崇敬和人与草原生态平衡观念为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, reverence for ancestral spirits, and ideas of balance between people and the grassland environment.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，知识依靠口述传统、歌谣、仪式与图案记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No mature writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, rituals, and mnemonic designs.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集、渔猎和小规模物物交换为基础，市场化程度极低。财富主要体现为食物安全、工具、皮革制品与社会关系网络，而非可积累货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on hunting, gathering, fishing, and small-scale barter, with very low market integration. Wealth was expressed mainly in food security, tools, hides, and social relationship networks rather than accumulable money.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、渔猎、皮革加工、骨石器制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, fishing, hide processing, bone and stone tool production

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、石料、骨器、贝壳饰品、食物剩余
tradeGoods.en: Animal hides, feathers, stone materials, bone tools, shell ornaments, surplus food

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由狩猎工具、皮革、装饰物、储存食物和亲族互助能力构成。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly consisted of hunting equipment, hides, ornaments, stored food, and the strength of kin support networks.

unit.en: kg silver per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自给性采猎经济和部落间互惠交换为主的再分配体系。
economicSystem.en: A redistributive system based on subsistence foraging and reciprocal exchange between tribal groups.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Camp contributions and allocated hunting returns

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 不存在固定国库，公共储备主要以食物、皮革、武器与礼物形式保存在若干核心营地。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no fixed treasury; communal reserves were kept mainly as food, hides, weapons, and gift goods in several key camps.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政活动以维持互助、应对歉收和支持防御为主，避免过度集中积累。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining mutual aid, buffering scarcity, and supporting defense, while avoiding excessive centralized accumulation.

description.zh: 以长矛、投掷器、弓箭和木棒作战，兼具狩猎与防御功能。
description.en: Fought with spears, throwing weapons, bows, and clubs, combining hunting and defensive roles.

description.zh: 负责营地警戒、护送家属和守护储备物资。
description.en: Responsible for camp watch, escorting families, and protecting stored supplies.

technology.zh: 军事技术较低，依赖石骨木制武器、皮革护具、地形熟悉与突袭战术。
technology.en: Military technology was limited, relying on stone-, bone-, and wood-based weapons, hide protection, knowledge of terrain, and raiding tactics.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部落竞争、资源季节性短缺与局部报复冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal competition, seasonal resource shortages, and localized revenge conflicts.

populationDescription.zh: 人口较为稀疏，沿草原、河流与海岸地带分布于小型流动营地。出生率和死亡率均较高，人口增长缓慢且受气候波动影响明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse, spread across small mobile camps along grasslands, rivers, and coasts. Both birth and mortality rates were high, and population growth was slow and strongly shaped by climatic fluctuation.

socialClasses.zh: 长老、萨满与勇士具有较高声望，其余多数为普通家庭成员与青年猎采者。
socialClasses.en: Elders, shamans, and warriors held higher prestige, while most people were ordinary family members and younger hunters-foragers.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，主要与邻近友好部落维持婚姻、交换与互助关系。
allies.en: No fixed allies; relations were mainly maintained through marriage, exchange, and mutual aid with neighboring friendly tribes.

enemies.zh: 无长期国家级敌人，但与争夺猎场和水源的邻近部落常有摩擦。
enemies.en: There were no long-term state-level enemies, but frictions were common with nearby tribes competing for hunting grounds and water.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以维持迁徙安全、资源共享边界和减少报复循环为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving migration safety, managing shared resource boundaries, and reducing cycles of revenge.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代至地区性采猎文化阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age to regional foraging culture stage

keyInnovations.zh: 石骨复合工具、皮革处理、季节性迁徙知识、口述地图与生态追踪技术。
keyInnovations.en: Composite stone-bone tools, hide processing, seasonal migration knowledge, oral mapping, and ecological tracking techniques.

strengths.zh: 机动性强，熟悉草原生态，社会协作建立在亲族与互惠基础之上。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, deeply familiar with grassland ecology, and socially cohesive through kinship and reciprocity.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、组织分散、物资储备有限，难以支持长期大规模战争。
weaknesses.en: Small population, decentralized organization, and limited reserves made sustained large-scale warfare difficult.

outlook.zh: 短期内可保持稳定生存，但面对环境压力或外来高组织度势力时较为脆弱。
outlook.en: They could remain viable in the short term, but were vulnerable to environmental stress or highly organized outside powers.

description.zh: 潘帕斯地区诸采猎部落在公元前221年前后以流动营地、长老协商和草原生态适应为生存基础。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, the Pampas cultures were mobile foraging tribes sustained by elder consensus and strong adaptation to the grassland environment.

dynasty.zh: 澳洲原住民诸部族网络

governmentForm.zh: 以部族、氏族和长者权威为核心的分散式部落治理体系。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order centered on tribes, clans, and the authority of elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会组织建立在亲属关系、图腾分组、婚姻规则与仪式义务之上。各地群体规模不大，但通过贸易路径、圣地联系与仪式往来形成广泛文化网络。长者、知识持有者与仪式主持者在社会中拥有特殊地位。
socialStructure.en: Social organization rested on kinship, totemic divisions, marriage rules, and ritual obligations. Local groups were generally small, but broad cultural networks were maintained through trade routes, sacred places, and ceremonial exchange. Elders, knowledge holders, and ritual leaders occupied special positions in society.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格意义上的贵族阶层，实际权威由男性或女性长者、仪式长老和经验丰富的猎采领导者分享。不同地区权威结构有所差异，但总体上重视资历与神圣知识。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal aristocracy; authority was shared by senior men or women, ritual elders, and experienced hunting-foraging leaders. Power structures varied regionally, but seniority and sacred knowledge were broadly valued.

succession.zh: 领导权通常由年长者自然承接，或由具备仪式资格与社会认可者继任。某些职责与圣地管理权可在特定血缘或婚姻网络中传递。
succession.en: Leadership typically passed through seniority or to individuals recognized for ritual qualification and social standing. Some responsibilities and custodianship over sacred sites could pass within particular kin or marriage networks.

structure.zh: 并无统一国家结构，而是由众多地方性部族与语言群组成。决策通常在长者协商、仪式集会和亲族谈判中达成，跨区域联系依靠传统路线与盟亲关系维持。
structure.en: There was no unified state structure, but rather many local tribes and language groups. Decisions were usually reached through elder consultation, ceremonial gatherings, and kin negotiation, while interregional links were maintained by traditional routes and alliance ties.

function.zh: 处理土地使用、婚姻规则、争端调停与跨群体协商。
function.en: Handled land use, marriage rules, dispute mediation, and inter-group negotiation.

function.zh: 负责祭仪、口述法则传承、圣地维护与青年成年礼。
function.en: Oversaw ceremonies, transmission of oral law, maintenance of sacred sites, and initiation rites for youth.

localAdmin.zh: 地方层面以小型营地、氏族领地和水源地群体为单位自治。土地并非个人私有，而由特定群体按传统法则共同管理与使用。
localAdmin.en: At the local level, governance operated through small camps, clan territories, and water-source groups. Land was not treated as individual private property, but managed and used collectively by particular groups under traditional law.

legalSystem.zh: 法律为口传习惯法，与梦时传统、祖先故事和土地责任紧密相连。制裁包括公开谴责、补偿、仪式惩戒、放逐及有限暴力报复。
legalSystem.en: Law was oral customary law closely tied to Dreaming traditions, ancestral narratives, and obligations to land. Sanctions included public censure, compensation, ritual punishment, exile, and limited retaliatory violence.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共资源依靠共享食物、仪式馈赠、婚姻交换与劳务承担来维持。大型集会时，各群体会贡献猎获、植物食物和工艺品。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; communal resources were sustained through food sharing, ceremonial gifts, marriage exchange, and labor obligations. During large gatherings, groups contributed hunted food, plant foods, and crafted items.

religion.zh: 梦时代信仰
religion.en: Dreamtime

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成了复杂的歌路导航体系、丰富的岩画传统和适应多样生态区的精密知识。回旋镖、投矛器及火管理技术体现出长期环境实践积累。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed complex songline navigation systems, rich rock art traditions, and precise knowledge adapted to diverse ecological zones. Boomerangs, spear-throwers, and fire management techniques reflected long experience with the environment.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚原住民诸语言，以帕马-恩雍甘语系及多种非帕马语言为主
languageFamily.en: Australian Aboriginal languages, dominated by Pama-Nyungan and several non-Pama language groups

philosophy.zh: 梦时信仰强调祖灵创世、土地神圣性、路径记忆和人群对自然秩序的责任。
philosophy.en: Dreamtime belief emphasized ancestral creation, the sacredness of land, remembered pathways, and human responsibility toward natural order.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，知识通过歌路、神话、岩画、身体绘饰与仪式口述保存。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was preserved through songlines, myth, rock art, body painting, and ceremonial oral transmission.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济建立在狩猎、采集、渔业、火耕式景观管理和区域性交换网络上。货币不存在，但高价值物资、仪式品和远距交换路线在社会中具有重要作用。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on hunting, gathering, fishing, fire-based landscape management, and regional exchange networks. Money did not exist, but valued goods, ceremonial items, and long-distance exchange routes played important social roles.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、渔业、石器制作、木器加工、火管理
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, fishing, stone tool production, woodworking, fire management

tradeGoods.zh: 石材、赭石、贝壳、木器、回旋镖、树脂、仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Stone materials, ochre, shells, wooden goods, boomerangs, resins, ceremonial items

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富体现为工具、食物获取能力、圣地联系、婚姻纽带与交换伙伴网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed through tools, food-procurement capacity, ties to sacred places, marriage bonds, and networks of exchange partners.

unit.en: kg silver per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部族自给、互惠交换和仪式性再分配为基础的采猎经济体系。
economicSystem.en: A foraging economy based on tribal self-provisioning, reciprocal exchange, and ceremonial redistribution.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备并非集中于固定仓库，而是分散保存为赭石、石器坯料、武器、干制食物和礼仪用品。
treasuryDescription.en: Communal reserves were not stored in a fixed treasury, but dispersed as ochre, stone blanks, weapons, dried foods, and ceremonial goods.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源管理重在平衡季节波动、维系仪式秩序和保障跨群体互助。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource management emphasized balancing seasonal fluctuation, sustaining ceremonial order, and guaranteeing inter-group mutual support.

description.zh: 使用矛、投矛器、木盾、棍棒和石刃武器，承担突袭、防卫与复仇行动。
description.en: Used spears, spear-throwers, wooden shields, clubs, and stone-edged weapons for raiding, defense, and retaliatory action.

description.zh: 负责守卫妇孺、要道和水源地，在冲突时快速集结。
description.en: Protected women, children, key paths, and water sources, and could mobilize rapidly in conflict.

description.zh: 在跨部族冲突或重大报复时由多群体临时组成。
description.en: Temporarily formed from multiple groups during inter-tribal conflict or major retaliatory expeditions.

technology.zh: 军事技术以投矛器、回旋镖、木盾、火运用和地形熟悉为特色，缺乏金属武器与攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology featured spear-throwers, boomerangs, wooden shields, the use of fire, and terrain knowledge, but lacked metal weapons and siege capacity.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁为局部部族冲突、水源竞争、生态压力和沿贸易路线的纠纷。
threats.en: Main threats included local tribal conflict, competition over water, ecological stress, and disputes along exchange routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口广泛分布于海岸、河谷、沙漠边缘和森林草原地带，居住密度极低。不同生态区维持差异化生活方式，但都依赖长期环境知识和流动性。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread widely across coasts, river valleys, desert margins, and wooded grasslands, with very low density. Different ecological zones supported distinct lifeways, but all depended on long-term environmental knowledge and mobility.

name.zh: 阿纳姆地大型季节聚会地
name.en: Arnhem Land Major Seasonal Gathering Site

socialClasses.zh: 整体阶层分化有限，但长者、仪式知识持有者、熟练工匠和勇武者享有更高威望。
socialClasses.en: Overall class differentiation was limited, but elders, ritual knowledge holders, skilled artisans, and accomplished fighters enjoyed greater prestige.

allies.zh: 无统一盟国，跨部族关系主要依靠婚姻、共同圣地和交换路线维系。
allies.en: There were no unified allies; inter-tribal relations were maintained mainly through marriage, shared sacred sites, and exchange routes.

enemies.zh: 无长期统一敌国，但邻近群体间可能因土地、婚姻与复仇产生长期对立。
enemies.en: There were no permanent unified enemies, though neighboring groups could sustain long rivalries over land, marriage, and revenge.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以维持传统边界、保障通行权和在冲突后恢复仪式秩序为重点。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on maintaining traditional boundaries, securing rights of passage, and restoring ceremonial order after conflict.

era.zh: 晚期石器时代澳洲区域传统阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age Australian regional tradition stage

keyInnovations.zh: 歌路导航、投矛器、回旋镖、系统性火管理、岩画与精细石器加工。
keyInnovations.en: Songline navigation, spear-throwers, boomerangs, systematic fire management, rock art, and refined stone-working.

strengths.zh: 环境适应能力极强，文化网络广泛，口述知识体系高度发达。
strengths.en: Exceptionally adaptive to varied environments, with broad cultural networks and highly developed oral knowledge systems.

weaknesses.zh: 人口分散、政治统一度低、缺乏金属技术与大型定居农业基础。
weaknesses.en: Population was dispersed, political unity was low, and there was no metal technology or large sedentary agricultural base.

outlook.zh: 在无外来大规模压力的条件下可长期维持稳定，但难以形成集中型国家结构。
outlook.en: Under conditions without major outside pressure, these societies could remain stable for long periods, but were unlikely to develop a centralized state structure.

description.zh: 公元前221年前后的澳大利亚原住民社会由众多依托梦时传统、亲属法则和生态知识维系的地方部族网络构成。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, Australian Aboriginal society consisted of many local tribal networks sustained by Dreamtime tradition, kin law, and ecological knowledge.

dynasty.zh: 加勒比早期采猎诸群体

governmentForm.zh: 以小型岛屿部群和长者主导协商构成的松散部落体制。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal system made up of small island-based groups governed through elder-led consultation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会通常由若干亲族家庭组成的海岸或内陆聚落构成，规模较小且流动性较高。食物采集、捕鱼、贝类采集与简单手工业是日常生活核心。长者、熟练渔猎者与掌握仪式传统者具有较高影响力。
socialStructure.en: Society was usually organized into small coastal or inland settlements formed by kin-based families, with relatively high mobility. Food gathering, fishing, shellfish collection, and simple handicrafts formed the basis of daily life. Elders, skilled fishers-hunters, and ritual custodians held greater influence.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在严格统治阶层，岛上或聚落内的长者与威望人物承担领导功能。其权威主要依赖经验、亲族支持与调停能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no strict ruling class; elders and prestige figures within islands or settlements carried out leadership roles. Their authority relied mainly on experience, kin backing, and mediation skills.

succession.zh: 领导地位多通过亲族协商、年长资历或个人威望延续。若出现冲突，聚落可分离迁徙而非接受强制继承。
succession.en: Leadership usually continued through kin negotiation, seniority, or personal prestige. In case of conflict, a settlement might split and relocate rather than accept enforced succession.

structure.zh: 政治组织以岛屿或湾区聚落为核心，各群体独立性很高。跨海峡交流和婚姻往来构成有限的区域联系，但尚未形成稳定的大型酋邦。
structure.en: Political organization centered on island or bay settlements, each enjoying considerable independence. Cross-channel exchange and marriage ties created limited regional connections, but no stable large chiefdoms had yet emerged.

function.zh: 处理资源分配、婚姻安排、争端与迁徙决定。
function.en: Handled resource allocation, marriage arrangements, disputes, and relocation decisions.

function.zh: 组织捕鱼、狩猎、祭祀活动及海上往来安全。
function.en: Organized fishing, hunting, ritual activity, and the safety of seaborne travel.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理以单个聚落为单位，通常围绕海岸资源点、淡水来源和季节性营地展开。岛屿之间联系松散，缺少常设上级权力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration operated at the settlement level, usually organized around coastal resource zones, freshwater sources, and seasonal camps. Links between islands were loose, with no permanent superior authority.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和亲族调解为基础，强调赔偿、分享与关系修复。严重纠纷可能通过迁居、分群或有限报复解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom and kin mediation, emphasizing compensation, sharing, and restoration of relationships. Serious disputes might be resolved by relocation, fission of groups, or limited retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正规税收制度，公共需求依靠渔获、贝类、木材与劳力贡献。首领无法长期征收固定贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; communal needs were met through contributions of fish, shellfish, timber, and labor. Leaders could not impose lasting fixed tribute.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 善于独木舟航行、近海捕鱼、贝器制作与岛屿环境适应。其聚落布局和海洋资源利用方式为后续加勒比文化发展奠定基础。
culturalAchievements.en: They were skilled in canoe travel, nearshore fishing, shell tool production, and adaptation to island environments. Their settlement patterns and marine resource use laid foundations for later Caribbean cultural development.

languageFamily.zh: 早期加勒比原住民语言，具体分类未明，后期与阿拉瓦克相关语言群可能存在接触
languageFamily.en: Early Caribbean Indigenous languages of uncertain classification, with possible later contact toward Arawakan-related groups

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、海洋与祖灵崇拜、自然循环和航海生存经验为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animism, reverence for sea and ancestral spirits, natural cycles, and seafaring survival knowledge.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，历史与规则以口述传统、歌谣、器物样式与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; history and norms were transmitted through oral tradition, songs, artifact styles, and ritual practice.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以捕鱼、采集、小型狩猎和岛际交换为核心，农业尚不发达或仅处于初步阶段。财富主要表现为独木舟、工具、贝饰和稳定的海岸资源控制权。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on fishing, gathering, small-game hunting, and inter-island exchange, while agriculture remained limited or only incipient. Wealth was mainly expressed through canoes, tools, shell ornaments, and reliable control of coastal resource zones.

mainIndustries.zh: 捕鱼、贝类采集、狩猎、独木舟制作、贝石器加工
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, shellfish collecting, hunting, canoe building, shell and stone tool working

tradeGoods.zh: 贝壳饰品、鱼干、木材、石料、骨器、独木舟用品
tradeGoods.en: Shell ornaments, dried fish, timber, stone materials, bone tools, canoe supplies

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由舟船、渔具、居住点位置、装饰品和岛际亲缘关系组成。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth mainly consisted of boats, fishing gear, access to habitation sites, ornaments, and inter-island kin relations.

unit.en: kg silver per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以海洋采猎经济和岛际互惠交换为主的部落型再分配体系。
economicSystem.en: A tribal redistributive system based on maritime foraging and reciprocal inter-island exchange.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Fish catch and shared food contributions

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
