kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 公共储备通常表现为晒干鱼类、贝饰、木材、石料和备用独木舟，而非集中存放的金属财富。
treasuryDescription.en: Communal reserves usually took the form of dried fish, shell valuables, timber, stone materials, and spare canoes rather than centrally stored metallic wealth.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重点在保障海上交通、食物稳定和聚落间互助，不追求高度集中征收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on securing maritime mobility, food stability, and mutual support between settlements rather than highly centralized extraction.

description.zh: 以矛、木棒、石刃武器和盾牌进行近距离作战，负责聚落防御。
description.en: Fought at close range with spears, clubs, stone-edged weapons, and shields, defending settlements.

description.zh: 使用独木舟监视海岸、护送往来并实施短程突袭。
description.en: Used canoes to watch coasts, escort travel, and conduct short-range raids.

technology.zh: 军事能力以独木舟机动、近海侦察和木石骨武器为主，缺乏金属装备与复杂防御工事。
technology.en: Military capability relied on canoe mobility, nearshore scouting, and wood-stone-bone weapons, lacking metal gear and complex fortifications.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自岛际摩擦、资源竞争、风暴破坏和海上通行风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-island frictions, resource competition, storm damage, and the hazards of maritime travel.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散在多个岛屿与海岸带，聚落规模通常较小。岛屿资源承载力有限，因此人口增长缓慢且受风暴、渔获波动和淡水条件影响较大。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across multiple islands and coastal zones, with settlements usually remaining small. Island carrying capacity was limited, so population growth was slow and heavily affected by storms, fishing fluctuations, and freshwater conditions.

name.zh: 伊斯帕尼奥拉西部湾区营地

socialClasses.zh: 阶层分化较弱，长者、熟练航海者、祭仪主持者和优秀渔猎者享有较高声望。
socialClasses.en: Class differentiation was weak, though elders, skilled navigators, ritual leaders, and successful fishers-hunters held greater prestige.

allies.zh: 无固定联盟，主要通过岛际婚姻、交换和共同渔场协定建立友好关系。
allies.en: There were no fixed alliances; friendly relations were mainly built through inter-island marriage, exchange, and shared fishing arrangements.

enemies.zh: 无持久统一敌人，但邻近岛群间可能因资源点和海上路线发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no enduring unified enemies, but neighboring island groups could clash over resource sites and sea routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外策略重在维持航道安全、稳定交换关系并避免破坏关键海岸资源区。
foreignPolicy.en: External policy focused on maintaining route safety, preserving exchange ties, and avoiding disruption of key coastal resource zones.

era.zh: 加勒比前陶采猎文化阶段
era.en: Pre-ceramic Caribbean foraging culture stage

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟航行、贝器与骨器制作、沿海资源精细利用、季节性岛际迁徙知识。
keyInnovations.en: Canoe voyaging, shell and bone tool production, refined coastal resource exploitation, and seasonal inter-island mobility knowledge.

strengths.zh: 善于海洋环境适应，机动性较强，岛际联系有助于资源互补。
strengths.en: Well adapted to maritime environments, relatively mobile, and capable of resource complementarity through inter-island links.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模小、资源脆弱、政治整合度低，易受风暴和局部冲突影响。
weaknesses.en: Small population, fragile resource base, and low political integration made them vulnerable to storms and localized conflict.

outlook.zh: 短期内可在岛屿生态中维持稳定，但难以支持大规模集中化政体的发展。
outlook.en: They could remain stable within island ecologies in the short term, but were unlikely to support the development of large centralized polities.

description.zh: 公元前221年前后的加勒比采猎群体以小型岛屿聚落、海洋资源利用和长者协商治理为主要特征。
description.en: Around 221 BCE, Caribbean hunter-gatherer groups were characterized by small island settlements, reliance on marine resources, and elder-based consensual governance.

governmentForm.zh: 以若干亲族群体组成的松散部族联盟为核心，由长老会议在共识基础上处理共同事务。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal confederation of kin-based groups, governed through consensus by councils of elders for common affairs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、氏族与季节性聚落为基本单位，成员多从事渔猎、采集与有限度的原始农业。地位差异较小，但年长者、萨满与善于狩猎者享有更高威望。婚姻与互助网络维系各群体之间的联系。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around households, clans, and seasonal settlements, with livelihoods based on fishing, hunting, gathering, and limited primitive cultivation. Social inequality was low, though elders, shamans, and skilled hunters held greater prestige. Marriage ties and mutual aid networks linked communities together.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定世袭贵族，实际领导层由氏族长老、战时首领与宗教性的萨满构成。其权威主要来自资历、声望与分配能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; practical leadership rested with clan elders, war leaders, and ritual shamans. Their authority derived mainly from seniority, prestige, and the ability to allocate resources.

succession.zh: 首领多由长老推举或在家族内部协商产生，重视能力与威望而非严格长子继承。若领袖失去支持，其地位可以被替代。
succession.en: Leaders were usually chosen by elders or negotiated within leading families, valuing ability and reputation over strict primogeniture. A leader who lost support could be replaced.

structure.zh: 政务极为简化，主要围绕季节迁徙、狩猎渔场分配、纠纷调解和对外防御展开。跨部族事务通常在集会中讨论，由长老和有威望的萨满共同影响决策。
structure.en: Government was highly simple, focused on seasonal movement, allocation of hunting and fishing grounds, dispute mediation, and external defense. Inter-tribal matters were typically discussed in assemblies, where elders and influential shamans shaped decisions.

function.zh: 协调氏族关系、裁决纠纷并决定迁徙与防务事项。
function.en: Coordinated clan relations, judged disputes, and decided migration and defense matters.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、保管公共贡品与应急储藏的鱼干、兽皮和工具。
function.en: Oversaw rituals and managed communal tribute goods and emergency stores of dried fish, hides, and tools.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各氏族聚落自行处理，家长和长老在日常生活中承担管理职责。没有固定领土官僚体系。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was handled by individual clan settlements, where household heads and elders carried out routine management. There was no fixed territorial bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、口述传统和赔偿原则为主，重视调解而非严刑。严重冲突可能以逐出、血亲赔偿或和解仪式解决。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on customary norms, oral tradition, and compensation, emphasizing mediation over harsh punishment. Serious conflicts might be settled through expulsion, kin compensation, or reconciliation rituals.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正规赋税，公共事务依赖贡纳、共同劳动和战时动员。首领可在集体祭典或防务需要时征集鱼获、皮毛与劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public needs were met through tribute, communal labor, and wartime mobilization. Leaders could call for fish, furs, and labor during collective rituals or defense emergencies.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长口述史诗、祭仪歌唱、木骨角器制作与寒地生存技术。其渔猎传统和雪地、湖区适应能力体现出高度环境知识。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in oral epic tradition, ritual singing, wood-bone-antler craftsmanship, and cold-climate survival techniques. Their fishing and hunting traditions reflected deep environmental knowledge of snowy and lake-rich regions.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系诸早期芬兰-乌戈尔语支
languageFamily.en: Uralic family, early Finno-Ugric branches

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵信仰，强调人与森林、湖泊和动物世界的平衡关系。
philosophy.en: Animistic-shamanic beliefs centered on spirits and ancestors, stressing balance between humans, forests, lakes, and animal life.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字体系，知识主要通过歌谣、神话和口头记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No mature writing system; knowledge was transmitted mainly through songs, myths, and oral memory.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模很小，以自给性的渔猎采集为主，少量剩余用于交换。财富主要体现为皮毛、舟具、渔具、驯养动物和储存食物，而非金属货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was very small and based mainly on subsistence fishing, hunting, and gathering, with only limited surplus for exchange. Wealth was expressed chiefly in furs, boats, fishing gear, domesticated animals, and stored food rather than coin.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、狩猎、采集、皮毛加工、骨角木器制作
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, hunting, gathering, fur processing, and bone-antler-wood craft production

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、琥珀、鱼干、兽脂、骨角器、少量蜂蜡与毛皮制品
tradeGoods.en: Furs, amber, dried fish, animal fat, bone-antler tools, and small amounts of beeswax and hide products

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富有限，主要由渔网、舟船、武器、皮衣和越冬储粮构成。富裕家庭通常拥有更多皮毛储备与更广泛的姻亲交换网络。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was modest, consisting mainly of nets, boats, weapons, hide clothing, and winter food stores. Better-off families typically held larger fur stocks and broader kin exchange networks.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给、氏族共享和季节性互惠交换为核心的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy centered on household subsistence, clan sharing, and seasonal reciprocal exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”并非集中金库，而是分散于各聚落的皮毛、干鱼、工具和少量贵重银饰储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” was not a central vault but a dispersed stock of furs, dried fish, tools, and a small quantity of valuable silver ornaments held across settlements.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏向维持低征集和高储备，以应对严寒、歉收和部族冲突。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice favored low extraction and strong reserves to cope with severe winters, shortages, and tribal conflict.

description.zh: 以弓、矛、斧为主，熟悉森林伏击与冬季追踪作战。
description.en: Armed mainly with bows, spears, and axes, skilled in forest ambush and winter tracking.

description.zh: 利用独木舟和小型船只控制湖泊与河道交通。
description.en: Used dugout canoes and small craft to control lakes and river routes.

commanderInChief.en: War Leader Chosen in Time of Need

technology.zh: 以木、骨、角和少量铁制武器为主，擅长寒地行动、渔猎后勤和地形利用。
technology.en: Technology relied on wood, bone, antler, and limited iron weapons, with strengths in cold-weather movement, hunting-based logistics, and terrain use.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺渔场、狩猎地和迁徙通道，以及极端气候导致的资源压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for fisheries, hunting grounds, and migration routes, as well as resource stress caused by severe climate.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏，广泛分布于森林、湖区与北方湿地，聚落多为季节性。人口增长缓慢，受气候、疾病和食物波动影响较大。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse, spread across forests, lake zones, and northern wetlands in largely seasonal settlements. Growth was slow and highly vulnerable to climate, disease, and food fluctuations.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族长老、萨满、自由家庭成员、依附亲族与战俘。
socialClasses.en: Clan elders, shamans, free household members, dependent kin, and captives.

allies.zh: 与部分邻近乌拉尔语族群体保持婚姻和互市关系，但缺乏固定盟约。
allies.en: Maintained marriage ties and barter relations with some neighboring Uralic-speaking groups, though without permanent treaties.

enemies.zh: 无长期国家级敌人，局部敌对多见于资源竞争中的邻近部族。
enemies.en: Had no long-term state-level enemies; hostilities were mainly local disputes with neighboring tribes over resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以避免长期战争、维持交换网络和保护狩猎渔场为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy prioritized avoiding prolonged war, preserving exchange networks, and protecting hunting and fishing grounds.

era.zh: 铁器时代早期的北方部族阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age northern tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 独木舟与桨船、骨角复合工具、寒地皮衣制作、渔网与冰上捕鱼技术。
keyInnovations.en: Dugout canoes and paddle craft, composite bone-antler tools, cold-weather hide clothing, and net fishing with ice-fishing techniques.

strengths.zh: 熟悉寒地环境，社会韧性较强，生计方式多样，适应森林与湖区生态。
strengths.en: Well adapted to cold environments, socially resilient, economically flexible, and highly suited to forest and lake ecologies.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、组织松散、剩余有限，难以支持大型战争或复杂政体。
weaknesses.en: Small population, loose organization, and limited surplus made large-scale warfare or complex state formation difficult.

outlook.zh: 短期内可维持稳定，但未来发展高度取决于贸易接触、气候波动与周边人口迁移。
outlook.en: They could remain stable in the short term, but future development depended heavily on trade contact, climate shifts, and surrounding population movements.

description.zh: 这是一个以渔猎采集和氏族协商为基础、分布于北方森林湖区的早期芬兰-乌戈尔部族共同体。
description.en: This was an early Finno-Ugric tribal community of the northern forest-lake zone, based on fishing, hunting, gathering, and clan consensus.

governmentForm.zh: 由多个游猎氏族构成的松散联盟，通过长老与萨满的协商维持基本秩序。
governmentForm.en: A loose confederation of hunting clans that maintained order through consultation among elders and shamans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以小型亲族营地为核心，依赖驯鹿利用、狩猎、捕鱼和采集。等级分化较弱，但经验丰富的猎手、长老和通灵萨满拥有较高威望。人口流动性高，季节性迁徙是生活常态。
socialStructure.en: Society centered on small kin camps dependent on reindeer use, hunting, fishing, and gathering. Social stratification was weak, though veteran hunters, elders, and trance-performing shamans held special prestige. Mobility was high, and seasonal migration was a normal way of life.

rulingClass.zh: 无正式贵族阶层，决策精英主要是长老、优秀猎手与宗教领袖。其统治基础在于组织狩猎、分配食物和处理危机的能力。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal noble class; decision-making elites consisted mainly of elders, accomplished hunters, and ritual leaders. Their authority rested on organizing hunts, distributing food, and managing crises.

succession.zh: 营地主事者通常由最有威望的家系成员继承或被推举产生。若遭遇失败或饥荒，其地位可能迅速削弱。
succession.en: Camp leadership usually passed within prestigious families or was conferred by selection. Failure or famine could quickly erode a leader’s standing.

structure.zh: 政治组织低度集中，公共事务主要限于迁徙路线、狩猎协作、祭仪安排和对外警戒。跨营地集会不定期举行，多在资源丰富季节讨论共同问题。
structure.en: Political organization was minimally centralized, with public affairs largely limited to migration routes, cooperative hunts, ritual arrangements, and external watch. Inter-camp gatherings were irregular and usually held in seasons of relative abundance.

function.zh: 处理迁徙安排、争端调解和共同狩猎的组织。
function.en: Handled migration planning, dispute mediation, and organization of communal hunts.

function.zh: 主持萨满祭仪并保管公共雪橇、兽皮、骨器与食物储备。
function.en: Conducted shamanic rites and safeguarded communal sleds, hides, bone tools, and food reserves.

localAdmin.zh: 各营地自治，主事者通常是资深长者或猎队首领。由于聚落分散，治理更多依靠人际关系而非制度化官职。
localAdmin.en: Each camp governed itself, usually under a senior elder or hunting leader. Because settlements were dispersed, governance depended more on personal relations than formal office.

legalSystem.zh: 以口传习惯法为基础，强调赔偿、和解和群体压力。涉及禁忌、猎物分配和婚配争议时，萨满解释具有重要作用。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on oral custom, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and communal pressure. In matters involving taboos, division of game, and marriage disputes, shamanic interpretation was important.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有固定税制，公共需求通过猎获分成、贡礼和临时劳役满足。关键时期可征集雪橇、皮革和肉干。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed tax system; public needs were met through shares of game, gift tribute, and temporary labor service. In critical times, sleds, leather, and dried meat could be requisitioned.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 精于寒带迁徙知识、雪橇与皮帐制造、骨角雕刻及狩猎祭仪传统。其口述神话和动物灵观念在北亚文化中极具代表性。
culturalAchievements.en: They were highly skilled in cold-zone migration knowledge, sled and hide-tent making, bone-antler carving, and hunting ritual traditions. Their oral mythology and beliefs about animal spirits were strongly characteristic of North Asian culture.

languageFamily.zh: 古西伯利亚诸语群
languageFamily.en: Paleo-Siberian language groups

philosophy.zh: 以萨满宇宙观为核心，认为人、兽、山川与祖灵相互连通，狩猎必须遵守神圣秩序。
philosophy.en: Centered on a shamanic worldview in which humans, animals, landscapes, and ancestral spirits were interconnected, and hunting had to observe sacred order.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，记忆、仪式和传说通过口头方式保存。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; memory, ritual, and legend were preserved orally.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济完全依赖寒带环境资源，生产剩余极少。价值集中在兽皮、驯鹿相关物资、鱼干、骨角器和交通工具上。
gdpDescription.en: The economy depended entirely on subarctic resources and generated very little surplus. Value was concentrated in hides, reindeer-related goods, dried fish, bone-antler tools, and transport equipment.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、捕鱼、驯鹿利用、皮革加工、骨角器和雪橇制作
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, reindeer use, leather processing, bone-antler craft, and sled making

tradeGoods.zh: 珍贵毛皮、驯鹿皮、骨角制品、鱼干、兽脂、少量玉石或贝饰
tradeGoods.en: Valuable furs, reindeer hides, bone-antler products, dried fish, animal fat, and small amounts of jade or shell ornaments

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由皮帐、雪橇、猎具、衣物和驯鹿数量决定。财富差距有限，但在资源稀缺地区拥有运输与储藏能力的家庭更受重视。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was determined mainly by hide tents, sleds, hunting gear, clothing, and the number of reindeer. Inequality was limited, but families with stronger transport and storage capacity were more highly regarded in scarce environments.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以营地共享、家族自给和边缘性互市为特征的游猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A mobile hunting economy characterized by camp sharing, family subsistence, and marginal barter exchange.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 财储以分散储藏的皮货、肉干、雪橇部件和礼仪用珍贵物件构成，没有真正集中的金属库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of dispersed stocks of furs, dried meat, sled components, and valued ritual items, rather than a truly centralized metallic hoard.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政目标是维持最低限度的公共协调与灾荒缓冲，避免过度向小营地索取资源。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to maintain only minimal public coordination and famine buffers while avoiding excessive extraction from small camps.

description.zh: 主要使用弓箭、矛和石骨铁混合工具，在苔原与林地进行伏击。
description.en: Used bows, spears, and mixed stone-bone-iron tools for ambush in tundra and forest.

description.zh: 依靠驯鹿与雪橇执行侦察、追踪和快速转移。
description.en: Relied on reindeer and sleds for scouting, tracking, and rapid movement.

technology.zh: 军事技术简陋，但在雪地机动、耐寒装备和远距离追踪方面极具优势。
technology.en: Military technology was simple, but it had strong advantages in snow mobility, cold-weather gear, and long-range tracking.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 面临邻近游猎群体对狩猎区域的竞争、气候恶化与食物链波动所带来的生存压力。
threats.en: Threats came from competing hunting groups, climatic deterioration, and survival pressure caused by fluctuations in food resources.

populationDescription.zh: 人口极度稀疏，分布在冻原、针叶林与河谷地区，多为小型流动营地。高死亡率与恶劣环境限制了人口扩张。
populationDescription.en: Population density was extremely low across tundra, taiga, and river valleys, with most people living in small mobile camps. High mortality and harsh conditions limited demographic expansion.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与萨满、资深猎手、自由营地成员、依附者与俘获人口。
socialClasses.en: Elders and shamans, veteran hunters, free camp members, dependents, and captive persons.

allies.zh: 与相邻营地和部分通婚群体维持松散互助关系。
allies.en: Maintained loose mutual-aid ties with neighboring camps and intermarrying groups.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌对者，但常与周边部族因猎场和河道发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state enemies, but clashes with nearby tribes over hunting grounds and rivers were common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 倾向保持距离、避免消耗性战争，并在必要时通过迁徙绕开强敌。
foreignPolicy.en: They preferred distance, avoidance of attritional war, and migration around stronger opponents when necessary.

era.zh: 北亚早期铁器与骨角复合工具阶段
era.en: North Asian early iron and bone-antler composite tool stage

keyInnovations.zh: 雪橇交通、皮帐构造、寒带分层服饰、骨角鱼叉与长途追踪技术。
keyInnovations.en: Sled transport, hide-tent construction, layered cold-weather clothing, bone-antler harpoons, and long-distance tracking techniques.

strengths.zh: 机动性强，适应极端环境，猎战技巧高超，社会弹性大。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, well adapted to extreme environments, skilled in hunting warfare, and socially flexible.

weaknesses.zh: 人口太少、物资余量不足、缺乏长期集权和专业军队。
weaknesses.en: Population was too small, surplus too limited, and centralized authority or professional armies were absent.

outlook.zh: 只要生态条件尚可，部族体系能够延续，但任何气候突变都可能造成严重人口与迁徙危机。
outlook.en: The tribal system could persist so long as ecological conditions remained tolerable, but any sharp climatic change could trigger severe demographic and migration crises.

description.zh: 这是一个散布于北亚寒带的古西伯利亚游猎共同体，以营地自治、萨满信仰和高机动生计为特征。
description.en: This was a dispersed Paleo-Siberian hunting society of the North Asian cold zone, marked by camp autonomy, shamanic belief, and highly mobile subsistence.

governmentForm.zh: 由若干采集狩猎与半定居群体组成的部族社会，依靠长老协商与仪式权威维持秩序。
governmentForm.en: A tribal society of hunter-gatherer and semi-sedentary groups, maintaining order through elder consensus and ritual authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由多个血缘群体和季节性营地组成，生活依赖采集、狩猎、渔业及少量原始园圃活动。社会差异有限，但长老、祭司和善猎者拥有较高声望。亲属交换和婚姻联盟是维系较大区域联系的核心机制。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of multiple kin groups and seasonal camps, with livelihoods based on gathering, hunting, fishing, and small-scale proto-horticulture. Social differences were limited, though elders, ritual specialists, and skilled hunters enjoyed higher prestige. Kin exchange and marriage alliances were the main mechanisms connecting wider territories.

rulingClass.zh: 无固定贵族，领导层由部族长老、仪式主持者和战时头人构成。其地位建立在年龄、口才、猎获分配与宗教威望之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed nobility; leadership consisted of tribal elders, ritual officiants, and wartime headmen. Their position rested on age, eloquence, distribution of game, and religious prestige.

succession.zh: 部族领袖通常由核心家系推举，可能呈长幼有序或母系、父系并存的地方差异。继承并不绝对，若失去支持可被他人取代。
succession.en: Tribal leaders were usually selected from core lineages, with local variation ranging from seniority to mixed matrilineal and patrilineal patterns. Succession was not absolute, and leaders who lost support could be replaced.

structure.zh: 政治组织较松散，主要负责水源使用、迁徙时序、猎场协调、对外交涉和祭祀集会。跨部族事务在大型季节性聚会中讨论，决策通常依赖协商而非强制命令。
structure.en: Political organization was loose, dealing mainly with water use, migration timing, hunting-ground coordination, external relations, and ritual gatherings. Inter-tribal matters were discussed at large seasonal assemblies, and decisions relied more on consensus than coercive command.

function.zh: 调解群体纠纷、协调迁徙、水源和婚姻联盟。
function.en: Mediated group disputes and coordinated migration, water access, and marriage alliances.

function.zh: 主持仪式、管理公共储粮与分配集体狩猎所得。
function.en: Conducted rituals, managed communal food stores, and distributed the proceeds of collective hunts.

localAdmin.zh: 各营地和小聚落由家长与长老自行治理，重要决定由集会确认。不存在固定边界内的常设官僚机构。
localAdmin.en: Each camp and small settlement was governed by household heads and elders, with major decisions confirmed in assembly. There was no permanent bureaucracy operating within fixed borders.

legalSystem.zh: 依赖习惯法、口头调解和赔礼制度，强调恢复群体和谐。对偷窃、婚姻纠纷和伤害行为通常采取赔偿与公开和解。
legalSystem.en: The legal system relied on customary law, oral mediation, and compensation, emphasizing restoration of communal harmony. Theft, marriage disputes, and bodily injury were usually handled through compensation and public reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收，公共资源来自贡礼、集体狩猎分成和共同劳作。首领在旱季或冲突时期可临时征集食物和劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; public resources came from gift tribute, shares of communal hunts, and collective labor. Leaders could temporarily requisition food and labor in drought or conflict.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 在追踪动物、采集植物知识、仪式舞蹈、身体装饰和口述传统方面成就显著。其生态知识对热带草原、林地和河谷环境适应极强。
culturalAchievements.en: They achieved notable sophistication in animal tracking, plant-gathering knowledge, ritual dance, body decoration, and oral tradition. Their ecological knowledge was highly adapted to savanna, woodland, and riverine environments.

languageFamily.zh: 多样的非洲本地语系，可能包括早期尼日尔-刚果语系或科伊桑相关群体
languageFamily.en: Diverse indigenous African language families, possibly including early Niger-Congo or Khoisan-related groups

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰和祖先崇拜为主，重视土地、水源、动物与部族祖灵之间的神圣联系。
philosophy.en: Belief centered on animism and ancestor veneration, emphasizing sacred ties among land, water sources, animals, and tribal forebears.

writingSystem.zh: 无成文文字，历史与规范通过歌谣、舞蹈、口述谱系和仪式传播。
writingSystem.en: There was no formal writing system; history and norms were transmitted through song, dance, oral genealogy, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、狩猎和局部渔业为基础，部分地区辅以简单农园和家畜利用。虽然货币化程度极低，但丰富的生态资源使其人均维持水平略高于高寒狩猎社会。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on gathering, hunting, and local fishing, with some regions supplemented by simple gardens and the use of livestock. Monetization was extremely low, but rich ecological resources allowed slightly higher per-capita sustenance than in subarctic hunting societies.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、狩猎、渔业、蜂蜜采集、皮革加工、木器和石骨工具制作
mainIndustries.en: Gathering, hunting, fishing, honey collection, leather processing, and wood-stone-bone tool production

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、象牙或骨材、蜂蜜、羽饰、干肉、树脂、染料和少量贝珠
tradeGoods.en: Animal hides, ivory or bone material, honey, feather ornaments, dried meat, resins, dyes, and small amounts of shell beads

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富表现为工具、储粮、装饰品、皮革制品及在部分地区拥有的少量牲畜。社会整体平等性较强，但掌握水源和交换路线的群体更有影响力。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth appeared in tools, stored food, ornaments, leather goods, and in some areas a small number of livestock. Society remained broadly egalitarian, but groups controlling water access and exchange routes held greater influence.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以自给采集狩猎、共享分配和小规模区域互市并存的部族经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining subsistence hunting-gathering, shared distribution, and small-scale regional barter.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Shares from communal hunts and stores

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要是分散于各营地的干肉、蜂蜜、皮革、贝饰和礼仪用品储备，不是集中铸币库藏。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” consisted mainly of dispersed stores of dried meat, honey, leather, shell ornaments, and ritual goods across camps, not a centralized coin hoard.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以应对季节性短缺和维持部族联盟关系为目标，倾向轻度征集与重视互惠分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at coping with seasonal shortages and preserving tribal alliances, favoring light extraction and strong reciprocal distribution.

description.zh: 使用弓箭、投矛、短矛和木盾，擅长追踪、伏击与草原机动作战。
description.en: Used bows, throwing spears, short spears, and wooden shields, excelling in tracking, ambush, and savanna maneuver.

description.zh: 在有水网地区使用独木舟和轻便船只，负责渔场保护和近岸警戒。
description.en: In watery areas they used dugout canoes and light craft to guard fisheries and near-shore approaches.

technology.zh: 以石、骨、木和少量铁器并用，军事技术简单，但追踪、伪装和地形熟悉度极高。
technology.en: Technology combined stone, bone, wood, and limited iron; military equipment was simple, but tracking, concealment, and terrain familiarity were exceptional.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺水源、猎场和迁徙路线，以及旱灾造成的冲突升级。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribes competing for water, hunting grounds, and migration routes, as well as conflict escalation during drought.

populationDescription.zh: 人口较为分散，主要栖居于热带草原、河谷和林缘地带，以季节性营地和小型定居点为主。资源丰歉与疾病环境对人口波动影响显著。
populationDescription.en: Population was widely dispersed across savannas, river valleys, and woodland margins, living mostly in seasonal camps and small settlements. Resource variability and disease environments strongly affected demographic fluctuation.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与祭司、熟练猎手、普通家庭成员、依附亲族与战俘或外来者。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual specialists, skilled hunters, ordinary household members, dependent kin, and captives or outsiders.

allies.zh: 与通婚部族、邻近采集群体及部分早期牧耕社群保持季节性互市和互助。
allies.en: Maintained seasonal barter and mutual aid with intermarrying tribes, neighboring foraging groups, and some early herding or farming communities.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌人，但在旱季常与周边群体因水源和猎物发生冲突。
enemies.en: There were no fixed state enemies, but dry-season conflicts over water and game with nearby groups were common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 倾向通过婚姻联盟、共享仪式和交换关系缓和冲突，必要时以机动撤离避免持久战。
foreignPolicy.en: They tended to ease conflict through marriage alliances, shared ritual, and exchange relations, while using mobile withdrawal to avoid prolonged war when necessary.

era.zh: 热带非洲草原地区的晚石器至早铁器过渡阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age to Early Iron Age transition in tropical African savanna regions

keyInnovations.zh: 复合弓箭、植物采集知识、火管理、独木舟、皮革与编织容器制作。
keyInnovations.en: Composite bows and arrows, advanced plant-gathering knowledge, fire management, dugout canoes, and leather and woven-container production.

strengths.zh: 生态知识丰富，迁移灵活，群体联系广泛，能在多样环境中维持生计。
strengths.en: They possessed rich ecological knowledge, flexible mobility, broad social ties, and the ability to sustain themselves in diverse environments.

weaknesses.zh: 政治整合度低，难以积累大规模剩余，面对持续旱灾和更强邻国时较为脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Political integration was low, large-scale surplus was hard to accumulate, and they were vulnerable to prolonged drought and stronger neighboring polities.

outlook.zh: 若与周边牧耕社会接触增加，未来可能出现更稳定的交换网络和半定居化趋势，但传统采集生活仍将长期存在。
outlook.en: With increased contact with neighboring herding and farming societies, more stable exchange networks and semi-sedentary trends might emerge, though traditional foraging lifeways would likely persist for a long time.

description.zh: 这是一个分布于热带草原与河谷地带、以采集狩猎和长老协商为基础的部族共同体。
description.en: This was a tribal community of tropical savannas and river valleys, based on hunting-gathering and governance through elder consensus.

dynasty.zh: 无王朝的沙漠部落联盟

governmentForm.zh: 以长老议事会为核心、按氏族协商治理的松散部落体制。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal system centered on a council of elders and governed through clan consultation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以若干游动氏族和家族营地构成，按亲属关系、年龄与狩猎经验排列威望。成年猎手、采集者与通灵祭者各有分工，老人与族母常在资源分配中拥有重要话语权。
socialStructure.en: Society was made up of mobile clans and family camps, with prestige shaped by kinship, age, and hunting skill. Adult hunters, gatherers, and ritual specialists had distinct roles, while elders and senior matriarchs often held important influence over resource allocation.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定贵族阶层，威望最高者通常是掌握水源路线、调停纠纷并主持祭仪的长老。少数强势家族可在迁徙季节中取得更高影响力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed noble class; the most respected figures were elders who knew water routes, mediated disputes, and led rituals. A few strong families could gain greater influence during seasonal migrations.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常由氏族内部推举，重视年长、经验与祭祀能力。若原长老失去威望，议事会可改立他人。
succession.en: Leadership was usually selected within the clan by consensus, valuing age, experience, and ritual ability. If an elder lost prestige, the council could elevate another person.

structure.zh: 政治组织规模很小，通常由长老与各营地代表共同决定迁徙、婚配与争端处理。没有常设官僚机构，只有少数负责祭祀、储藏和对外交涉的人物。
structure.en: Political organization was very small in scale, with elders and camp representatives deciding migration, marriage arrangements, and dispute settlement together. There was no permanent bureaucracy, only a few individuals responsible for ritual, storage, and outside dealings.

function.zh: 决定迁徙路线、分配水源与仲裁氏族纠纷。
function.en: Determined migration routes, allocated water access, and arbitrated clan disputes.

function.zh: 主持萨满祭仪并看管皮革、干肉与交换物资。
function.en: Conducted shamanic rituals and oversaw hides, dried meat, and exchange goods.

localAdmin.zh: 各营地由家族首领自行管理，只有在季节性大会时才形成较统一的决策。远距离营地之间依靠婚姻与信使维系联系。
localAdmin.en: Each camp was managed by family heads, and more unified decisions emerged only during seasonal gatherings. Distant camps were tied together through marriage links and messengers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以赔偿、和解与祭仪净化为核心。严重冒犯如盗窃水源或破坏婚约，可能导致驱逐。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary, centered on compensation, reconciliation, and ritual purification. Serious offenses such as stealing access to water or violating marriage agreements could lead to expulsion.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税制，氏族按需要向公共储藏提供猎获物、皮毛或劳役。战时或旱季会临时提高共同供给比例。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; clans contributed game, hides, or labor to communal stores as needed. In wartime or drought, the share given to common support could be raised temporarily.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 他们擅长荒漠导航、口头史诗与动物踪迹识别。皮革加工、骨角器制作和简易岩画具有鲜明地域特征。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in desert navigation, oral epic traditions, and reading animal tracks. Leatherworking, bone-and-horn tools, and simple rock art showed distinct regional character.

languageFamily.zh: 前文字化的地方部族语言，可能属今已难考的古代沙漠语群
languageFamily.en: Pre-literate local tribal languages, possibly belonging to now-obscure ancient desert language groups

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵、祖灵护佑与对荒漠水源秩序的敬畏构成核心观念。
philosophy.en: Animism, ancestral protection, and reverence for the order of desert water sources formed the core worldview.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，信息依靠口述传统、符号刻记与结绳式记忆方式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system existed; information was preserved through oral tradition, symbolic marks, and cord-based mnemonic practices.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性采集、狩猎和少量牧养交换为主，市场化程度极低。产出受降水、猎物迁徙与邻近农耕地交换条件影响极大。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence gathering, hunting, and limited pastoral exchange, with very low marketization. Output depended heavily on rainfall, animal migration, and exchange conditions with nearby farming zones.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、皮革加工、少量驮畜饲养
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, leatherworking, and limited pack-animal herding

tradeGoods.zh: 皮毛、兽骨、盐、药草、干肉
tradeGoods.en: Furs, animal bone, salt, medicinal herbs, and dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在牲畜数量、皮革储备、工具和亲族互助网络，而非金属货币。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured mainly in livestock, stored hides, tools, and kin support networks rather than metal currency.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族共享和互惠交换为核心的生计经济，辅以边缘性跨地区贸易。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence economy centered on clan sharing and reciprocal exchange, supplemented by marginal long-distance trade.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备多以皮货、盐块、风干肉与少量贵金属碎片形式保存，并分散于多个营地。它更像共同储藏而非固定国库。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves were stored mostly as hides, salt blocks, dried meat, and small amounts of precious metal fragments, dispersed across several camps. It was more a communal store than a fixed treasury.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政原则偏向节用与灾年储备，避免对氏族征敛过重。只有在冲突和旱灾时才扩大集中征集。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored frugality and reserve-building for bad years, avoiding heavy extraction from clans. Centralized collection expanded only during conflict or drought.

description.zh: 以步行为主，擅长伏击、追踪与小规模突袭。
description.en: Primarily foot-based forces skilled in ambush, tracking, and small-scale raids.

description.zh: 负责侦察水源路线、护送营地与远距离警戒。
description.en: Responsible for scouting water routes, escorting camps, and long-range watch duties.

technology.zh: 武备以短弓、投矛、石刃或简易金属刃、皮盾为主，熟悉荒漠生存但缺乏攻城能力。
technology.en: Armament consisted mainly of short bows, javelins, stone or simple metal blades, and hide shields; they were adept at desert survival but lacked siege capability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近更强部族争夺水源、沙漠边缘农耕政权的驱逐行动以及连续旱灾。
threats.en: Main threats came from stronger neighboring tribes contesting water sources, expulsion campaigns by farming polities on the desert edge, and prolonged drought.

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏，集中于季节性水源附近，出生率与死亡率均受气候强烈影响。大多数人一生处于迁徙状态。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and concentrated near seasonal water sources, with birth and death rates strongly affected by climate. Most people spent their lives in migration.

socialClasses.zh: 长老、祭者、成年猎采者、少年与依附家庭，阶层差异较弱但威望差异明显。
socialClasses.en: Elders, ritual specialists, adult hunters-gatherers, youths, and dependent families; social stratification was weak, though prestige differences were clear.

allies.zh: 与邻近小型绿洲部族和部分边境商队维持互不侵犯与交换关系。
allies.en: Maintained non-aggression and exchange ties with nearby oasis tribes and some frontier caravans.

enemies.zh: 与争夺水源的沙漠部落和偶尔深入荒漠的边疆守军关系紧张。
enemies.en: Relations were tense with desert tribes competing for water and with frontier troops that occasionally entered the deep desert.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以避战、保路、保水源和有限贸易为核心，不追求扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on avoiding major war, protecting routes and water sources, and preserving limited trade rather than seeking expansion.

era.zh: 铁器时代边缘的荒漠部族社会
era.en: A desert tribal society on the margins of the Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 重点技术包括水源辨识、皮囊储水、季节迁徙路线记忆与骨角复合工具。
keyInnovations.en: Key techniques included locating water, storing it in skin bags, memorizing seasonal migration routes, and making composite bone-and-horn tools.

strengths.zh: 机动性高、适应荒漠环境、社会弹性强且生存知识丰富。
strengths.en: High mobility, strong adaptation to desert conditions, social flexibility, and rich survival knowledge.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、组织分散、经济余粮有限且对水源极端依赖。
weaknesses.en: Small population, dispersed organization, limited surplus, and extreme dependence on water sources.

outlook.zh: 若气候稳定并保持与边境社会的交换，可长期存续；若遭遇持续干旱或强权压迫，则易分裂外迁。
outlook.en: If climate remained stable and exchange with frontier societies continued, they could endure for a long time; under sustained drought or pressure from stronger states, they would likely fragment and migrate.

description.zh: 这是一个依赖荒漠采集、狩猎与水源路线知识维生的松散部落共同体。
description.en: This was a loose tribal community sustained by desert gathering, hunting, and expert knowledge of water routes.

governmentForm.zh: 由部族王统领、贵族氏族支持的早期部落王国。
governmentForm.en: An early tribal kingdom led by a tribal king and supported by aristocratic clans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族、氏族和战士集团为主干，山地村落中的自由民构成主要人口。贵族家族控制牧场、矿区和战时追随者，普通农牧民向其提供兵役与贡赋。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around tribes, clans, and warrior groups, with free villagers in the uplands forming the bulk of the population. Noble families controlled pastures, mining areas, and war followers, while ordinary agro-pastoralists supplied military service and tribute.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由部族王、地方酋长和武装贵族组成，他们通过婚姻、馈赠和战利品分配维持权威。部分掌握矿山与贸易通道的家族地位尤其突出。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the tribal king, local chieftains, and armed nobles, who maintained authority through marriage alliances, gift exchange, and distribution of booty. Families controlling mines and trade routes were especially prominent.

succession.zh: 王位多在强势家族内部继承，但需获得主要氏族首领承认。若继承人软弱，常引发竞争性推戴或分裂。
succession.en: The kingship usually passed within a dominant family, but required recognition by major clan chiefs. A weak heir could trigger rival acclamations or fragmentation.

structure.zh: 达尔达尼亚政权缺乏完善文官体系，王权依赖王廷、亲族与地方酋长网络。政务集中在征兵、贡赋征收、矿产控制和对外防御上。
structure.en: The Dardanian polity lacked a fully developed civil bureaucracy, and royal authority relied on the court, kinship ties, and networks of local chieftains. Administration focused on levies, tribute collection, mineral control, and external defense.

function.zh: 辅佐部族王处理外交、仲裁贵族争端和战时决策。
function.en: Assisted the tribal king in diplomacy, arbitration of noble disputes, and wartime decisions.

function.zh: 征收牲畜、粮食和矿产贡赋，并监督重要矿区。
function.en: Collected tribute in livestock, grain, and minerals, and supervised important mining zones.

function.zh: 组织部族兵役、集结山地据点守军并维护通道安全。
function.en: Organized tribal military service, assembled garrisons at hill forts, and maintained route security.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由各氏族首领和山地据点首长管理，王权通过人质、联姻和贡赋加以约束。偏远谷地的实际自治程度较高。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested with clan chiefs and leaders of hill forts, while royal power constrained them through hostages, marriages, and tribute. Remote valleys retained a high degree of practical autonomy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和王廷裁决并行，核心在血亲赔偿、土地与牲畜权属以及战利品分配。谋逆或背弃盟誓会受到严厉报复。
legalSystem.en: Law combined customary practice with royal adjudication, focusing on kin compensation, rights to land and livestock, and distribution of booty. Treason or oath-breaking invited harsh retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自贡赋而非统一税制，包括牲畜、粮食、金属和劳役。贸易通道与矿区可能另征过路费和产出分成。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from tribute rather than a standardized tax system, including livestock, grain, metals, and labor service. Trade routes and mining areas could also yield tolls and output shares.

religion.zh: 伊利里亚多神教
religion.en: Illyrian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 达尔达尼亚人以山地防御聚落、金属加工与战士装饰文化见长。口头谱系、宴会礼赠和部族盟誓是政治文化的重要组成部分。
culturalAchievements.en: The Dardanians were notable for hill-fort settlements, metalworking, and warrior ornament culture. Oral genealogies, feasting, and tribal oath-making were major elements of political culture.

languageFamily.zh: 伊利里亚语族
languageFamily.en: Illyrian language family

philosophy.zh: 宗教观念以伊利里亚多神信仰、祖先崇拜和战士荣誉伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: The worldview centered on Illyrian polytheism, ancestor veneration, and a warrior ethic of honor.

writingSystem.zh: 本地文字使用有限，正式记录多依赖邻近希腊或其他巴尔干文化的书写影响；多数事务仍为口传。
writingSystem.en: Local use of writing was limited; formal records likely depended on neighboring Greek or other Balkan literate influences, while most affairs remained oral.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以山地农牧业、采矿和区域贸易为主，较纯游牧部落更具稳定剩余。其矿产和牲畜资源使其在巴尔干内陆具有一定战略价值。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on upland farming, pastoralism, mining, and regional trade, giving it a more stable surplus than purely nomadic tribes. Its mineral and livestock resources gave it some strategic value in the Balkan interior.

mainIndustries.zh: 农牧业、采矿、冶金、林木采集
mainIndustries.en: Agro-pastoralism, mining, metallurgy, and timber extraction

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、皮革、金属矿石、铁器、木材、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, hides, metal ores, iron goods, timber, and honey

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常以牛羊头数、铁器、耕地使用权和氏族庇护关系衡量。矿区附近家庭可能拥有更高金属储备。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was commonly measured in herds, iron tools, rights to cultivated land, and access to clan protection. Families near mining areas could hold larger metal reserves.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以部族控制资源、家庭生产和贡赋交换并行的山地混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed upland economy combining tribal control of resources, household production, and tribute-based exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要由金属锭、矿石存量、牲畜和外来钱币构成，多分散于王廷据点与主要山寨。其流动性不高，但适合部族型征发。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of metal ingots, ore stocks, livestock, and foreign coinage, dispersed between the royal center and major hill forts. It was not highly liquid, but suited a tribal levy system.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政偏重控制矿区与维持贵族忠诚，征收方式灵活而不均一。王权通常避免过度压榨自由民，以防氏族离心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized control of mining zones and maintenance of noble loyalty, using flexible and uneven extraction. The kingship generally avoided overburdening free villagers so as to prevent clan defection.

description.zh: 以长矛、盾牌和短剑为主，适合山地防御和近战。
description.en: Equipped mainly with spears, shields, and short swords, suited for mountain defense and close combat.

description.zh: 由贵族追随者组成，装备较好，负责冲击与指挥核心。
description.en: Composed of noble followers, better equipped and serving as shock troops and command core.

description.zh: 熟悉峡谷与山道，负责侦察、伏击和传讯。
description.en: Familiar with passes and ravines, they handled reconnaissance, ambush, and message relay.

technology.zh: 军备处于巴尔干铁器时代水平，具备铁矛、盾牌、短剑和简式甲具，但攻城与工程能力有限。
technology.en: Military technology stood at a Balkan Iron Age level, with iron spears, shields, short swords, and simple armor, but limited siege and engineering capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马其顿势力、邻近伊利里亚竞争部族以及巴尔干内陆通道争夺。内部氏族叛离也是长期风险。
threats.en: Main threats came from Macedonian power, rival Illyrian tribes, and struggles over interior Balkan routes. Internal clan defection was also a long-term danger.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于山间谷地、牧场和设防高地聚落，农村人口占绝大多数。聚落规模普遍不大，但在矿区和交通节点较为集中。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in mountain valleys, pastures, and fortified hill settlements, with the vast majority living rurally. Settlements were generally small, though denser near mines and route junctions.

socialClasses.zh: 部族王与贵族、自由战士农牧民、工匠矿工、依附民与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Tribal king and nobles, free warrior agro-pastoralists, craftsmen and miners, dependents, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分伊利里亚部族保持临时同盟，并可能与反马其顿势力发生合作。
allies.en: Maintained temporary alliances with some Illyrian tribes and could cooperate with forces opposed to Macedonian influence.

enemies.zh: 与马其顿及争夺边境和矿区的邻近部族关系紧张。
enemies.en: Relations were tense with Macedon and with neighboring tribes contesting frontiers and mining regions.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持山地独立、控制内陆交通和在强邻之间周旋为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving upland independence, controlling inland routes, and maneuvering between stronger neighbors.

era.zh: 巴尔干铁器时代晚期部族王国
era.en: Late Iron Age tribal kingdom of the Balkans

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括山地筑寨、铁器锻造、矿石开采与以氏族动员为基础的防御体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included hill-fort construction, ironworking, ore extraction, and a defense system based on clan mobilization.

strengths.zh: 山地防御强、战士传统浓厚、矿产资源较丰富且地方社会韧性高。
strengths.en: Strong mountain defenses, a robust warrior tradition, relatively rich mineral resources, and resilient local society.

weaknesses.zh: 政权整合度有限、文字行政薄弱、贵族离心倾向明显且难以进行持久远征。
weaknesses.en: Limited political integration, weak literate administration, strong centrifugal tendencies among nobles, and difficulty sustaining long campaigns.

outlook.zh: 短期内可凭地形与部族动员维持独立，但若遭到更有组织的王国持续压迫，可能被分化或逐步纳入周边强权体系。
outlook.en: In the short term it could preserve independence through terrain and tribal mobilization, but sustained pressure from more organized kingdoms could divide it or gradually absorb it into neighboring power systems.

description.zh: 达尔达尼亚是一个依靠山地部族、矿产与战士贵族维系的巴尔干内陆部落王国。
description.en: Dardania was an inland Balkan tribal kingdom sustained by mountain clans, mineral resources, and a warrior aristocracy.

dynasty.zh: 无王朝的通古斯先民氏族联合体

governmentForm.zh: 以氏族长老和萨满影响力为核心的分散型游猎社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized hunting society centered on clan elders and the influence of shamans.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由若干氏族与季节性营地组成，依河谷、森林和狩猎带迁徙。亲属关系、狩猎能力与灵性威望决定地位，但整体阶层分化较弱。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of clans and seasonal camps moving among river valleys, forests, and hunting zones. Kinship, hunting skill, and spiritual prestige determined status, though overall stratification remained weak.

rulingClass.zh: 没有固定世袭贵族，最有权威者通常是经验丰富的氏族长老和能与灵界沟通的萨满。善于组织冬季狩猎与调解婚姻纠纷者尤受尊重。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; the most authoritative figures were experienced clan elders and shamans believed to mediate with the spirit world. Those skilled at organizing winter hunts and resolving marriage disputes were especially respected.

succession.zh: 领导地位多通过族内推举和声望积累形成，不一定严格父子相承。若某一分支失去猎场、人口或神圣威望，领导权可转移。
succession.en: Leadership usually arose through clan choice and accumulated prestige, not necessarily strict father-to-son inheritance. If a branch lost hunting grounds, followers, or sacred prestige, leadership could shift elsewhere.

structure.zh: 政治结构极为松散，氏族长老在营地会议上处理迁徙、狩猎分配和对外关系。常设管理人员极少，只有少量负责祭仪、物资与联络的角色。
structure.en: Political structure was highly loose, with clan elders handling migration, hunting distribution, and external relations at camp meetings. There were very few permanent administrators, only a small number responsible for rituals, supplies, and liaison.

function.zh: 决定季节迁徙、猎场划分与争端调解。
function.en: Determined seasonal movement, allocated hunting grounds, and settled disputes.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、保管毛皮储备并组织困难季节互助。
function.en: Led rituals, guarded fur reserves, and organized mutual aid in difficult seasons.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理即各营地自理，依河流和林区形成分散生活圈。大型共同决策多发生于渔猎旺季或冬季会盟时。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was essentially camp self-management, with dispersed life zones along rivers and forests. Large common decisions usually occurred during rich fishing-hunting seasons or winter assemblies.

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、赔偿和长老裁断为主，重视婚约、猎场边界和猎物分配。违反禁忌或破坏共同狩猎秩序可能遭到逐出营地。
legalSystem.en: Customary law, compensation, and elder judgment predominated, with emphasis on marriage agreements, hunting boundaries, and division of prey. Violating taboos or disrupting collective hunts could bring expulsion from camp.

taxationSystem.zh: 没有正式税收，氏族按季节提供毛皮、鱼干、驯养兽使用或劳力作为公共贡献。遇到饥荒和冲突时，公共征集会短期增加。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; clans contributed furs, dried fish, use of domesticated animals, or labor seasonally for common needs. During famine or conflict, communal collection increased temporarily.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 他们擅长寒地生存、皮毛缝制、桦皮器制作和河流森林路线记忆。鼓乐、祭歌与动物图腾叙事构成重要文化表达。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in cold-climate survival, fur sewing, birch-bark craftsmanship, and memorization of river and forest routes. Drumming, ritual songs, and animal totem narratives formed major cultural expressions.

languageFamily.zh: 早期通古斯语支
languageFamily.en: Early Tungusic language branch

philosophy.zh: 世界观以萨满信仰、自然灵、祖灵和人与动物之间的互惠秩序为核心。
philosophy.en: The worldview centered on shamanism, nature spirits, ancestors, and a reciprocal order between humans and animals.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，历史、谱系与地理知识依靠口传、歌谣和象征记号保存。
writingSystem.en: There was no developed writing; history, genealogy, and geographic knowledge were preserved through oral tradition, song, and symbolic markings.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以渔猎、采集、毛皮处理和少量驯养为主，几乎完全服务于生计需求。高价值毛皮虽可交换，但整体剩余有限。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on fishing, hunting, gathering, fur processing, and limited animal domestication, serving subsistence needs above all. High-value furs could be exchanged, but overall surplus remained small.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔猎、毛皮加工、采集、桦皮与木器制作
mainIndustries.en: Fishing and hunting, fur processing, gathering, and birch-bark and woodcraft production

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、鱼干、鹿角、蜂蜡、桦皮器
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried fish, antler, beeswax, and birch-bark goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为毛皮储量、渔猎工具、驯鹿或犬只数量以及可依赖的亲族合作网络。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in fur stocks, fishing and hunting tools, numbers of reindeer or dogs, and dependable kin cooperation networks.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、氏族共享和毛皮互惠交换为基础的寒地游猎经济。
economicSystem.en: A cold-region foraging economy based on household production, clan sharing, and reciprocal fur exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 储备主要以优质毛皮、鱼干、兽脂和少量金属器物形式存在，通常分散保存在多个营地。财政核心是越冬生存保障，而非积累货币。
treasuryDescription.en: Reserves existed mainly as fine furs, dried fish, animal fat, and small amounts of metal goods, usually dispersed among several camps. The fiscal core was winter survival security rather than monetary accumulation.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调低征集、重储备与困难季节互助，尽量避免破坏氏族生计平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized low extraction, strong reserves, and mutual aid in hard seasons, seeking not to disrupt clan subsistence balance.

description.zh: 使用弓矢、矛和短刃，适合林地伏击与追踪作战。
description.en: Used bows, spears, and short blades, well suited for forest ambush and tracking warfare.

description.zh: 借助小舟和河岸路线行动，负责警戒、突袭和快速联络。
description.en: Moved by small boats and riverbank routes, handling watch duties, raids, and rapid communication.

commanderInChief.zh: 氏族长老联席战时首领
commanderInChief.en: Wartime leader chosen by the council of clan elders

technology.zh: 军事技术以弓箭、骨角复合器、木矛、皮甲和寒地舟具为特色，擅长地形利用但缺乏重装与攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology featured bows, bone-and-horn composite gear, wooden spears, hide armor, and cold-region rivercraft; they excelled at terrain use but lacked heavy equipment and siege ability.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近森林草原部族竞争、严寒灾年造成的人口损失，以及更南方势力对毛皮通道的渗透。
threats.en: Main threats came from competition with neighboring forest-steppe tribes, population losses in severe winters, and southern encroachment on fur routes.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散，沿河流、湖泊和森林狩猎带季节迁移，聚居点规模很小。寒冷环境使人口增长缓慢，但氏族网络维持了较高适应力。
populationDescription.en: The population was scattered and moved seasonally along rivers, lakes, and forest hunting belts, with very small settlement sizes. The cold environment slowed population growth, but clan networks provided strong resilience.

socialClasses.zh: 氏族长老、萨满、成年猎渔者、工匠型家庭成员、依附者与孤弱者，整体阶层差异有限。
socialClasses.en: Clan elders, shamans, adult hunters and fishers, craft-oriented household members, dependents, and vulnerable persons, with limited overall class division.

allies.zh: 与部分相邻森林氏族维持婚盟和季节性互市关系。
allies.en: Maintained marriage alliances and seasonal exchange ties with some neighboring forest clans.

enemies.zh: 与争夺猎场和河道的邻近部族偶有冲突。
enemies.en: They occasionally clashed with nearby tribes over hunting grounds and river routes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以保全猎场、维护毛皮交换通道和避免大规模战争为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving hunting grounds, maintaining fur exchange routes, and avoiding large-scale war.

era.zh: 东北亚早期铁器时代边缘的森林游猎社会
era.en: A forest foraging society on the margins of early Iron Age Northeast Asia

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括寒地服装缝制、桦皮器与舟具制作、河流导航和高效毛皮处理。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included cold-weather clothing manufacture, birch-bark containers and boats, river navigation, and efficient fur processing.

strengths.zh: 适应寒地森林环境、机动性高、毛皮资源价值高且氏族协作紧密。
strengths.en: Strong adaptation to cold forest environments, high mobility, valuable fur resources, and close clan cooperation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、政治整合有限、经济剩余低且容易受气候打击。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, limited political integration, low surplus, and vulnerability to climatic shocks.

outlook.zh: 在外部压力不强时可长期维持分散生计模式；若南方贸易扩张或北方竞争加剧，可能逐步形成更稳定的部落联盟。
outlook.en: If external pressure remained light, they could maintain a dispersed subsistence pattern for a long time; if southern trade expanded or northern competition intensified, they might gradually form more stable tribal federations.

description.zh: 通古斯先民是依托森林、河流与毛皮资源生存的分散型萨满氏族社会。
description.en: The Tungus peoples were a dispersed shamanic clan society sustained by forests, rivers, and fur resources.

governmentForm.zh: 以部族联盟为基础的游牧王权，由部族王在贵族与战士首领支持下统领诸部。
governmentForm.en: A nomadic tribal kingship based on a confederation, led by a tribal king with the support of aristocrats and warrior chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以氏族、部族和从属营帐为基本单位，核心是骑射贵族与自由牧民。其下有依附部众、工匠与战俘奴隶，财富主要以马群、牛羊和战利品衡量。女性在贵族家族与宗教仪式中常具有较高地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around clans, tribes, and dependent camps, with mounted aristocrats and free pastoralists at its core. Beneath them were dependent followers, craftsmen, and war captives, while wealth was measured mainly in horses, cattle, sheep, and booty. Women could hold relatively high status within noble families and ritual life.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由王族、部族贵族和著名战士首领构成。其权威建立在血缘、战功、馈赠分配与控制草场之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the royal lineage, tribal nobles, and prominent warrior chiefs. Their authority rested on kinship, military success, gift distribution, and control of pasturelands.

succession.zh: 继承通常出自王族内部，优先考虑成年且具军事威望的男性亲属。若王权衰弱，各大部族可推举或拥立新的联盟首领。
succession.en: Succession normally remained within the royal lineage, favoring an adult male relative with military prestige. If royal authority weakened, major tribes could elevate or acclaim a new confederate leader.

structure.zh: 斯基泰政治结构较为松散，王庭随季节迁徙，并通过盟约、婚姻和战利品分配维系诸部忠诚。联盟层面的治理主要围绕军事动员、迁徙路线与对外贸易安全展开。
structure.en: Scythian political organization was relatively loose, with a mobile royal court moving seasonally and maintaining loyalty through alliances, marriages, and distribution of booty. Confederation-level governance focused mainly on military mobilization, migration routes, and the security of external trade.

function.zh: 由王族与部族首领组成，商议战争、迁徙、盟约与贡纳分配。
function.en: Composed of royal kin and tribal chiefs, it deliberated on war, migration, alliances, and tribute distribution.

function.zh: 负责协调季节牧场、牲畜统计、附庸部众贡纳与对外贸易抽成。
function.en: It coordinated seasonal pastures, livestock counts, tribute from dependent groups, and levies on external trade.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由各部族首领、氏族长老和营地主人负责。联盟对地方的控制依赖亲族关系与军事威慑，而非固定官僚机构。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled mainly by tribal chiefs, clan elders, and camp heads. Confederation control over local groups depended on kinship ties and military pressure rather than fixed bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法为主，强调血仇赔偿、牲畜所有权和对首领誓约的遵守。重大争端通常由王庭或部族会议裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based primarily on customary practice, emphasizing blood-compensation, livestock ownership, and adherence to oaths sworn to leaders. Major disputes were usually settled by the royal court or tribal assemblies.

taxationSystem.zh: 常规税负较轻，主要表现为附庸部族贡纳、战利品分成和商队通行抽贡。对游牧自由民的直接征税有限，多以出兵与献牲畜代替。
taxationSystem.en: Regular taxation was light, consisting mainly of tribute from dependent tribes, shares of booty, and levies on caravans. Direct taxation of free pastoralists was limited, with military service and livestock contributions often substituting for taxes.

religion.zh: 萨满信仰
religion.en: Shamanism

culturalAchievements.zh: 斯基泰人以动物纹金工、骑马文化和精湛复合弓技艺著称。其墓葬、饰物和武具体现出草原艺术与跨区域交流的高度融合。
culturalAchievements.en: The Scythians were renowned for animal-style goldwork, equestrian culture, and sophisticated composite bowsmanship. Their burials, ornaments, and weapons reflect a strong fusion of steppe art and long-distance cultural exchange.

languageFamily.zh: 东伊朗语支
languageFamily.en: Eastern Iranian

philosophy.zh: 萨满信仰、祖灵崇拜与尚武荣誉伦理
philosophy.en: Shamanic belief, ancestor veneration, and a martial code of honor

writingSystem.zh: 无本族广泛通用文字，重要交流多依靠口传；对外交往时可能借用希腊文或邻近定居文明文字。
writingSystem.en: No widely used native writing system; important traditions were transmitted orally, while Greek or neighboring settled scripts could be borrowed for external dealings.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以畜牧业为核心，辅以掠夺、贡纳和边境贸易，整体货币化程度较低。人均产出不高，但贵族通过控制马群、贸易节点与战利品积累显著财富。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on pastoralism, supplemented by raiding, tribute, and frontier trade, with a low degree of monetization. Per-capita output was modest, but nobles accumulated substantial wealth through herds, trade nodes, and booty.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧畜牧、马匹繁育、皮革加工、金属器制作、边境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoral herding, horse breeding, leatherworking, metalcraft, and frontier trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牲畜、皮毛、奴隶、蜂蜡、兽皮、金饰
tradeGoods.en: Horses, livestock, furs, slaves, beeswax, hides, and gold ornaments

unitName.en: head of livestock, grams of silver

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现在马匹、羊群、牛只、帐幕和武器。贵族家庭则拥有大规模牧群、金器、附庸与贸易关系。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was embodied in horses, sheep, cattle, tents, and weapons. Noble households possessed large herds, gold objects, dependents, and commercial connections.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以氏族牧场占有和部族分配为基础的游牧经济，兼具战利品再分配与有限市场交换。政治权力与牲畜控制、迁徙通道和对商路的保护密切相连。
economicSystem.en: A nomadic economy based on clan pasture use and tribal redistribution, combined with booty-sharing and limited market exchange. Political power was closely tied to herd control, migration corridors, and protection of trade routes.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
