treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非固定金库，而是由王族积存的银锭、金饰、牲畜储备和可调动贡物构成。其流动性较强，但也易受战败、内讧和牲畜疫病影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not a fixed vault but consisted of silver bullion, gold ornaments, livestock reserves, and mobilizable tribute held by the royal household. It was relatively liquid but vulnerable to defeat, internal conflict, and livestock disease.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政侧重以馈赠和分配巩固忠诚，而非建立稳定税基。近年因势力衰退，更多依赖袭掠和临时贡纳，财政波动加剧。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized loyalty through gift-giving and redistribution rather than a stable tax base. In recent years, declining power forced greater reliance on raiding and ad hoc tribute, increasing fiscal volatility.

description.zh: 以复合弓和轻甲为主，机动性极强，擅长骚扰、包抄与远程消耗。
description.en: Armed mainly with composite bows and light armor, they were highly mobile and excelled in harassment, encirclement, and ranged attrition.

description.zh: 由贵族与其扈从组成，装备长矛、剑与较厚护具，用于突击和决战。
description.en: Drawn from nobles and their retainers, they carried spears, swords, and heavier protection for shock action and decisive combat.

description.zh: 由附庸部众和随营人员组成，负责营地防卫、护送与近距离战斗。
description.en: Made up of dependent groups and camp followers, they handled camp defense, escorts, and close combat.

technology.zh: 军事技术以复合弓、马术、鳞甲或皮甲、长矛和草原机动作战经验见长。缺乏攻城器械与持久围城能力，对固定城镇作战不利。
technology.en: Military technology excelled in composite bows, horsemanship, scale or leather armor, spears, and steppe mobile warfare. It lacked siege engines and sustained siege capability, making operations against fortified towns difficult.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括东部与中亚其他草原民族的竞争、南方定居政权的边防压力，以及内部部族离散和联盟权威下降。随着萨尔马提亚等新势力扩张，传统斯基泰核心地带正被逐步蚕食。
threats.en: Major threats included competition from other steppe peoples in the east and Central Asia, border pressure from settled southern states, and internal tribal fragmentation with declining confederate authority. As rising groups such as the Sarmatians expanded, traditional Scythian core territories were being steadily eroded.

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于草原营地、冬季驻地和小型贸易据点，密度很低。整体人口因战争、迁徙压力和被新兴草原势力排挤而呈下降趋势。
populationDescription.en: The population was dispersed across steppe camps, wintering grounds, and small trade sites, with very low density. Overall numbers were declining due to warfare, migratory pressure, and displacement by emerging steppe powers.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与部族贵族、骑士战士、自由牧民、附庸部众、工匠与奴隶
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and tribal nobility, mounted warriors, free pastoralists, dependent followers, craftsmen, and slaves

allies.zh: 多为短期部族盟约，与部分黑海贸易伙伴保持互利往来。
allies.en: Allies were mostly short-term tribal partners, alongside pragmatic ties with some Black Sea trading communities.

enemies.zh: 萨尔马提亚诸部、竞争性草原联盟，以及试图控制边境贸易的周边定居政权。
enemies.en: Enemies included Sarmatian tribes, rival steppe confederations, and neighboring settled states seeking to control frontier trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持草原通道、保护贸易、结盟制衡与必要时袭掠施压为主。其外交风格灵活，但由于联盟衰弱，长期约束力有限。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining steppe corridors, protecting trade, balancing through alliances, and applying pressure by raiding when necessary. It was flexible in style but limited in long-term effectiveness because of confederate decline.

era.zh: 铁器时代晚期欧亚草原文化
era.en: Late Iron Age Eurasian Steppe culture

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟骑射战术、复合弓、马具改进、动物纹金工与高机动游牧后勤。
keyInnovations.en: Mature mounted-archery tactics, composite bows, improved horse gear, animal-style goldwork, and highly mobile nomadic logistics.

strengths.zh: 机动骑兵强大，适合远程袭扰与草原纵深作战；贵族网络能快速集结武装；对草原商路与牧场环境极为熟悉。
strengths.en: Its mobile cavalry was formidable for long-range raiding and deep steppe warfare; aristocratic networks could mobilize forces quickly; and it possessed intimate knowledge of steppe trade routes and pasture ecology.

weaknesses.zh: 政治结构松散，财政基础脆弱，难以长期维持统一行动。缺乏城市、文字官僚和攻城能力，使其在面对定居国家和新兴草原强权时处于劣势。
weaknesses.en: Its political structure was loose, its fiscal base fragile, and sustained unified action was difficult. The lack of cities, literate bureaucracy, and siege capacity placed it at a disadvantage against settled states and rising steppe powers.

outlook.zh: 至公元前221年前后，斯基泰人已进入衰退期，传统霸权地位被新草原联盟逐步取代。未来更可能分裂、迁徙或被吸纳，而非重新建立广域统治。
outlook.en: Around 221 BCE, the Scythians were already in decline, with their traditional hegemony being replaced by newer steppe confederations. Their future was more likely to involve fragmentation, migration, or absorption than the restoration of wide-ranging dominance.

description.zh: 斯基泰人是欧亚草原晚期铁器时代的骑射游牧联盟，以马背战争、动物纹艺术和松散的部族王权著称，但在前221年前后已明显走向衰落。
description.en: The Scythians were a Late Iron Age mounted nomadic confederation of the Eurasian steppe, famed for horse warfare, animal-style art, and loose tribal kingship, but by around 221 BCE they were clearly in decline.

name.zh: 伊庇鲁斯莫洛西亚王国

ruler.zh: 伊庇鲁斯国王（皮洛士后继者）

dynasty.zh: 埃阿基德王朝（后皮洛士时期）

governmentForm.zh: 希腊式君主制，国王权力受部落贵族议会制约。莫洛西亚联盟为核心。
governmentForm.en: Greek-style monarchy with royal power checked by a tribal aristocratic council. The Molossian League was the core institution.

socialStructure.zh: 王族-部落贵族-自由公民（牧民与农民）-附庸与奴隶
socialStructure.en: Royalty – tribal aristocracy – free citizens (pastoralists and farmers) – dependents and slaves

rulingClass.zh: 埃阿基德王族与莫洛西亚部落贵族共治，多多那神谕在政治中有特殊影响。
rulingClass.en: The Aeacid royal house and Molossian tribal aristocracy shared rule; the Oracle of Dodona held special political influence.

succession.zh: 王位在埃阿基德家族中世袭传承，贵族大会有权确认或罢黜国王。
succession.en: The throne was hereditary within the Aeacid family; the aristocratic assembly had the right to confirm or depose the king.

structure.zh: 以莫洛西亚联盟为核心的王国政府，国王与贵族大会共同治理。皮洛士的远征虽扩大了影响力，但也耗尽了国力。
structure.en: A royal government centered on the Molossian League, with king and aristocratic assembly co-governing. Pyrrhus's campaigns had expanded influence but also exhausted national strength.

function.zh: 辅佐国王处理外交、军事和内政决策。
function.en: Assisted the king in diplomatic, military, and domestic policy decisions.

function.zh: 管理宙斯神谕圣所，提供宗教和政治咨询。
function.en: Managed the Oracle of Zeus sanctuary, providing religious and political counsel.

function.zh: 组织军队、管理要塞和边防。
function.en: Organized the army, managed fortifications, and maintained border defenses.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由部落首领管理，主要城市设有行政官。
localAdmin.en: Local areas were managed by tribal chiefs; major cities had appointed officials.

legalSystem.zh: 希腊式法律与莫洛西亚部落习惯法并行。
legalSystem.en: Greek-style law ran parallel with Molossian tribal customary law.

taxationSystem.zh: 对农牧产品征收贡赋，贸易关税和神庙献金为重要收入来源。
taxationSystem.en: Tribute on agricultural and pastoral products, with trade customs and temple offerings as important revenue sources.

religion.zh: 希腊多神崇拜，多多那宙斯神谕为核心圣地
religion.en: Greek polytheism, with the Oracle of Zeus at Dodona as the central sanctuary

culturalAchievements.zh: 多多那宙斯神谕圣所（希腊最古老的神谕之一）、莫洛西亚猎犬培育、皮洛士军事战术遗产。
culturalAchievements.en: The Oracle of Zeus at Dodona (one of the oldest Greek oracles), Molossian hound breeding, and the military tactical legacy of Pyrrhus.

languageFamily.zh: 西北希腊语方言
languageFamily.en: Northwest Greek dialect

philosophy.zh: 希腊宗教传统与宙斯神谕信仰
philosophy.en: Greek religious tradition and faith in the Zeus Oracle

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet

gdpDescription.zh: 以畜牧业（尤其是莫洛西亚犬和牛群）和农业为基础，辅以多多那神谕带来的朝圣经济和与意大利的贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Based on pastoralism (especially Molossian hounds and cattle) and agriculture, supplemented by pilgrimage economy from the Dodona Oracle and trade with Italy.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、谷物农业、神庙经济、木材贸易
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, grain farming, temple economy, and timber trade

tradeGoods.zh: 牲畜、木材、蜂蜜、乳制品；进口希腊陶器和金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Livestock, timber, honey, and dairy; imported Greek pottery and metalware

name.zh: 伊庇鲁斯银币与希腊通货
name.en: Epirote silver coins and Greek currency

householdWealth.zh: 贵族拥有大量牧场和牲畜；自由民以小型农牧场为生计。
householdWealth.en: Nobles owned extensive pastures and livestock; freemen lived on small farming and pastoral holdings.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室和贵族主导的混合经济，神庙经济与贸易构成重要补充。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy led by the crown and aristocracy, with the temple economy and trade as important supplements.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库在皮洛士远征后大幅缩水，主要以银币和贵金属储备维持。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury shrank dramatically after Pyrrhus's campaigns, maintained mainly through silver coins and precious metal reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政在皮洛士远征后面临压力，依靠削减军事开支和增加贸易税收来维持平衡。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was under strain after Pyrrhus's campaigns, relying on military spending cuts and increased trade revenue to maintain balance.

description.zh: 受马其顿军事体系影响的重步兵，装备萨里沙长矛和圆盾。
description.en: Heavy infantry influenced by the Macedonian military system, armed with sarissa pikes and round shields.

description.zh: 以山地骑术闻名的贵族骑兵，适合希腊崎岖地形作战。
description.en: Noble cavalry famed for mountain horsemanship, suited for combat in rugged Greek terrain.

description.zh: 以山地轻步兵为主的辅助部队。
description.en: Auxiliary forces primarily of mountain light infantry.

commanderInChief.en: The King served as supreme commander

technology.zh: 继承皮洛士的先进战术遗产，包括方阵与骑兵协同、战象运用和灵活机动战术，但在其死后军事实力有所下降。
technology.en: Inherited Pyrrhus's advanced tactical legacy including phalanx-cavalry coordination, war elephant deployment, and flexible mobile tactics, but military strength declined after his death.

threats.zh: 马其顿王国的扩张压力、伊利里亚部落入侵、罗马势力在亚得里亚海的逼近、内部贵族权力斗争。
threats.en: Expansionist pressure from Macedonia, Illyrian tribal incursions, approaching Roman power in the Adriatic, and internal aristocratic power struggles.

populationDescription.zh: 约三十万人口分布于希腊西北部山区，以莫洛西亚人、忒斯普洛提人和查奥尼亚人三大部落为主。
populationDescription.en: About 300,000 people distributed across northwestern Greek mountains, mainly the three tribes of Molossians, Thesprotians, and Chaonians.

socialClasses.zh: 王族（埃阿基德）、部落贵族、祭司、自由公民（农牧民）、附庸与奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royalty (Aeacids), tribal aristocracy, priests, free citizens (farmers and pastoralists), and dependents and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分希腊城邦和意大利南部希腊殖民地保持联盟关系。
allies.en: Maintained alliances with some Greek city-states and Greek colonies in southern Italy.

enemies.zh: 马其顿王国（争夺希腊影响力）、伊利里亚部落（北部威胁）、逐渐逼近的罗马势力。
enemies.en: Kingdom of Macedonia (competing for Greek influence), Illyrian tribes (northern threat), and the gradually approaching Roman power.

foreignPolicy.zh: 在马其顿与罗马之间寻求平衡，利用多多那神谕的宗教声望增强外交影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Sought balance between Macedonia and Rome, leveraging the Dodona Oracle's religious prestige to enhance diplomatic influence.

era.zh: 希腊化时代军事王国
era.en: Hellenistic military kingdom

keyInnovations.zh: 皮洛士战术遗产（方阵-骑兵-象兵协同）、多多那青铜器制造、山地农牧技术。
keyInnovations.en: Pyrrhus's tactical legacy (phalanx-cavalry-elephant coordination), Dodona bronze craftsmanship, and mountain agricultural-pastoral techniques.

strengths.zh: 皮洛士军事遗产、多多那宗教声望、山地防御优势、与希腊世界的文化纽带。
strengths.en: Pyrrhus's military legacy, Dodona's religious prestige, mountain-defense advantage, and cultural ties to the Greek world.

weaknesses.zh: 国力在皮洛士远征后大幅衰退、人口有限、政治不稳定、夹在马其顿和罗马之间。
weaknesses.en: National strength had declined sharply after Pyrrhus's campaigns; limited population, political instability, and caught between Macedonia and Rome.

outlook.zh: 伊庇鲁斯在皮洛士死后逐渐衰落，最终在前167年被罗马征服并并入罗马省份体系。
outlook.en: Epirus gradually declined after Pyrrhus's death, ultimately conquered by Rome in 167 BCE and incorporated into the Roman provincial system.

description.zh: 公元前221年的伊庇鲁斯王国是希腊西北部的莫洛西亚王国，以多多那宙斯神谕和皮洛士的军事遗产闻名，但在皮洛士远征消耗后国力大幅衰退。
description.en: The Kingdom of Epirus in 221 BCE was the Molossian kingdom of northwestern Greece, famed for the Oracle of Zeus at Dodona and Pyrrhus's military legacy, but its national strength had greatly declined after the exhaustion of Pyrrhus's campaigns.

ruler.zh: 达契亚部落首领（前布雷比斯塔时期）

capital.zh: 阿尔杰达瓦（主要中心之一）

governmentForm.zh: 部落联盟制，各部落首领（塔拉博斯特）共商事务，祭司阶层拥有重大影响力。
governmentForm.en: Tribal confederation where chiefs (tarabostes) deliberated collectively, with the priestly class wielding significant influence.

socialStructure.zh: 祭司贵族（塔拉博斯特）-战士贵族-自由民（科莫伊）-奴隶
socialStructure.en: Priestly-noble class (tarabostes) – warrior aristocracy – freemen (comati) – slaves

rulingClass.zh: 塔拉博斯特兼具宗教权威与军事领导权。
rulingClass.en: The tarabostes combined religious authority with military leadership.

succession.zh: 首领在贵族家族中传承，受祭司认可和战功影响。
succession.en: Leadership passed within noble families, influenced by priestly approval and military achievements.

structure.zh: 以喀尔巴阡山区为核心的部落联盟，多个盖塔和达契亚部落各自治理。
structure.en: A tribal confederation centered on the Carpathian region, with multiple Getae and Dacian tribes self-governing.

function.zh: 宗教裁决、历法管理、部落间仲裁。
function.en: Religious adjudication, calendar management, and inter-tribal arbitration.

function.zh: 军事行动决策、防御协调。
function.en: Military action decisions and defense coordination.

function.zh: 达瓦（山寨城堡）的建设维护和防御部署。
function.en: Construction, maintenance, and defensive deployment of dava (hill-forts).

localAdmin.zh: 各达瓦由地方首领管理，周边村落由氏族长老治理。
localAdmin.en: Each dava was managed by a local chief, with surrounding villages governed by clan elders.

legalSystem.zh: 盖塔-达契亚习惯法，祭司参与裁决，神谕被视为法律依据。
legalSystem.en: Geto-Dacian customary law, with priestly adjudication; oracles were regarded as legal authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 以谷物、牲畜和矿产实物贡赋为主。
taxationSystem.en: In-kind tribute of grain, livestock, and minerals.

religion.zh: 以扎尔莫克西斯崇拜为核心的独特信仰体系
religion.en: A distinctive faith centered on Zalmoxis worship

culturalAchievements.zh: 银器与金饰工艺、山寨要塞建筑、曲刃短剑（西卡）。
culturalAchievements.en: Silver and gold craftsmanship, hill-fort architecture, and the curved sica sword.

languageFamily.zh: 达契亚语（色雷斯语支，印欧语系）
languageFamily.en: Dacian (Thracian branch, Indo-European family)

philosophy.zh: 扎尔莫克西斯教义：灵魂不灭、蔑视死亡
philosophy.en: Zalmoxian doctrine: immortality of the soul, contempt for death

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，少量使用希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: No native script; limited use of the Greek alphabet.

gdpDescription.zh: 以农牧业为基础，辅以喀尔巴阡山区金银铁矿开采和与希腊城邦的贸易。
gdpDescription.en: Based on agriculture and pastoralism, supplemented by Carpathian gold-silver-iron mining and trade with Greek city-states.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、畜牧业、金银铁矿开采冶炼、陶器制造
mainIndustries.en: Grain farming, pastoralism, gold-silver-iron mining and smelting, and pottery making

tradeGoods.zh: 金银矿产、蜂蜜、蜂蜡、毛皮；进口希腊陶器和葡萄酒
tradeGoods.en: Gold and silver ores, honey, beeswax, and furs; importing Greek pottery and wine

name.zh: 仿希腊银币与实物交换
name.en: Imitation Greek silver coins and barter

householdWealth.zh: 贵族积累金银制品；自由民拥有土地和牲畜。
householdWealth.en: Nobles accumulated gold and silver wares; freemen owned land and livestock.

economicSystem.zh: 以部落再分配为主的混合经济，矿产贸易带来的财富流向贵族和祭司。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy dominated by tribal redistribution; mineral-trade wealth flowed to the noble and priestly classes.

treasuryDescription.zh: 部落储备以金银矿产为优势，分散于各山寨要塞。
treasuryDescription.en: Tribal reserves benefited from gold and silver mines, dispersed across hill-forts.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以实物贡赋和矿产为财政基础，战利品按功劳分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was based on in-kind tribute and mining, with war spoils distributed by merit.

description.zh: 装备曲刃短剑（西卡）和长矛的部落战士。
description.en: Tribal warriors armed with the curved sica and spears.

description.zh: 装备标枪和长矛的骑兵。
description.en: Cavalry armed with javelins and lances.

commanderInChief.zh: 部落最高首领兼任统帅，祭司影响军事决策
commanderInChief.en: The paramount tribal chief served as commander; priests influenced military decisions

technology.zh: 铁器冶炼水平较高，曲刃短剑（西卡）是标志性武器，擅长山地防御。
technology.en: Advanced iron smelting; the sica was their signature weapon; skilled in mountain defense.

threats.zh: 凯尔特部落西进、斯基泰骑兵袭扰、马其顿北方扩张压力。
threats.en: Celtic tribal incursions, Scythian cavalry raids, and Macedonian northward expansion pressure.

populationDescription.zh: 约五十万人口分布在喀尔巴阡山区及周边平原，以定居农耕和山区畜牧为生。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000 people distributed in the Carpathian region and surrounding plains, living by settled farming and highland pastoralism.

socialClasses.zh: 祭司贵族（塔拉博斯特）、战士贵族、自由民（科莫伊）、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Priestly nobles (tarabostes), warrior aristocracy, freemen (comati), and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分色雷斯部落和黑海希腊殖民城邦保持贸易联盟。
allies.en: Maintained trade alliances with some Thracian tribes and Black Sea Greek city-states.

enemies.zh: 斯基泰游牧部落、凯尔特部落、马其顿王国。
enemies.en: Scythian nomads, Celtic tribes, and the Kingdom of Macedonia.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以山地防御为基础，通过贸易与希腊世界保持联系。
foreignPolicy.en: Based on mountain defense, maintaining ties with the Greek world through trade.

era.zh: 喀尔巴阡铁器时代部落文明
era.en: Carpathian Iron Age tribal civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 曲刃短剑锻造、金银矿冶炼、达瓦山寨建筑、仿希腊铸币。
keyInnovations.en: Curved sica forging, gold-silver smelting, dava hill-fort architecture, and imitation Greek coinage.

strengths.zh: 矿产资源丰富、山地防御优势、铁器工艺精良、宗教凝聚力强。
strengths.en: Rich mineral resources, mountain-defense advantage, fine ironwork, and strong religious cohesion.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂、城市化低、多方向外部威胁。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation, low urbanization, and multi-directional external threats.

outlook.zh: 达契亚部落在布雷比斯塔时代（约前80-44年）将实现统一，建立强大的达契亚王国。
outlook.en: The Dacian tribes would be unified under Burebista (c. 80–44 BCE), forming a powerful Dacian kingdom.

description.zh: 公元前221年的达契亚部落王国是喀尔巴阡山区的盖塔-达契亚部落联盟，以扎尔莫克西斯信仰和丰富矿产闻名，处于政治分裂但文化统一的前布雷比斯塔时期。
description.en: The Dacian Tribal Kingdoms in 221 BCE were a Geto-Dacian tribal confederation in the Carpathian region, renowned for the Zalmoxis faith and rich minerals, in a politically fragmented but culturally unified pre-Burebista era.

ruler.zh: 阿德里安海岸伊利里亚诸部首领
ruler.en: Chieftains of the Adriatic Illyrian tribes

capital.zh: 斯科德拉（主要据点之一）

governmentForm.zh: 部落联邦制，各部落有自己的王或首领，偶尔在强势领袖下形成更大联盟。
governmentForm.en: A tribal federation where each tribe had its own king or chief, occasionally forming larger unions under a strong leader.

socialStructure.zh: 部落王与军事贵族-自由战士-农民与牧民-奴隶
socialStructure.en: Tribal kings and military aristocracy – free warriors – farmers and pastoralists – slaves

rulingClass.zh: 军事贵族和部落王家构成统治精英，权力基于军事声望和财富。
rulingClass.en: Military aristocracy and tribal royal houses formed the ruling elite; power was based on military prestige and wealth.

succession.zh: 王位在王族内传承，战功和贵族支持影响继承。
succession.en: Kingship passed within royal families, with military achievements and noble support influencing succession.

structure.zh: 分散的部落政治体系，亚得里亚海沿岸的部落偶尔形成以海盗和贸易为纽带的联合体。
structure.en: Dispersed tribal political system; Adriatic coastal tribes occasionally formed unions bound by piracy and trade.

function.zh: 协调部落间军事和外交事务。
function.en: Coordinated inter-tribal military and diplomatic affairs.

function.zh: 组织亚得里亚海海盗活动和沿海贸易。
function.en: Organized Adriatic piracy and coastal trade.

function.zh: 管理山区要塞和沿海据点防御。
function.en: Managed mountain fortress and coastal stronghold defense.

localAdmin.zh: 各部落由本部王或首领治理，主要据点由军事指挥官管理。
localAdmin.en: Each tribe was governed by its own king or chief; major strongholds were managed by military commanders.

legalSystem.zh: 伊利里亚部落习惯法，受希腊法律部分影响。
legalSystem.en: Illyrian tribal customary law, partially influenced by Greek law.

taxationSystem.zh: 以实物贡赋和海盗战利品分成为主。
taxationSystem.en: Mainly in-kind tribute and shares of piracy spoils.

religion.zh: 伊利里亚本土多神信仰，部分受希腊宗教影响
religion.en: Indigenous Illyrian polytheism, partially influenced by Greek religion

culturalAchievements.zh: 精美的铜器和铁器制造、独特的伊利里亚头盔和武器风格、船舶建造（利布尔纳快船）。
culturalAchievements.en: Fine bronze and ironwork, distinctive Illyrian helmet and weapon styles, and shipbuilding (liburna warships).

languageFamily.zh: 伊利里亚语（印欧语系）
languageFamily.en: Illyrian (Indo-European family)

philosophy.zh: 伊利里亚本土宗教信仰与尚武传统
philosophy.en: Indigenous Illyrian religious beliefs and martial traditions

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，沿海地区少量借用希腊字母。
writingSystem.en: No native writing system; limited borrowing of the Greek alphabet in coastal areas.

gdpDescription.zh: 以山地农牧业和亚得里亚海沿岸贸易（含海盗活动）为双核心经济支柱。矿产开采也有一定贡献。
gdpDescription.en: Dual-centered on mountain agriculture/pastoralism and Adriatic coastal trade (including piracy). Mining also contributed.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、农业、海盗与海上贸易、银矿开采、船舶建造
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, agriculture, piracy and maritime trade, silver mining, and shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 银矿、木材、牲畜、蜂蜜、海盗掠获物；进口希腊陶器和葡萄酒
tradeGoods.en: Silver ore, timber, livestock, honey, and piracy plunder; importing Greek pottery and wine

householdWealth.zh: 沿海贵族通过贸易和海盗积累财富；内陆居民以土地和牲畜为主要资产。
householdWealth.en: Coastal nobles accumulated wealth through trade and piracy; inland residents held land and livestock as main assets.

economicSystem.zh: 以部落再分配和海盗经济为主的混合体系，沿海地区货币化程度较高。
economicSystem.en: A mixed system dominated by tribal redistribution and piracy economy, with higher monetization in coastal areas.

treasuryDescription.zh: 部落国库以银矿收入和海盗掠获物为主要来源，分散于各主要据点。
treasuryDescription.en: Tribal treasuries were mainly sourced from silver mining and piracy plunder, dispersed across major strongholds.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以海盗收入和矿产为支柱，战利品按部落和军功分配。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy relied on piracy revenue and mining, with spoils distributed by tribe and military merit.

description.zh: 装备标枪、短剑和特征性伊利里亚头盔的山地步兵。
description.en: Mountain infantry armed with javelins, short swords, and the distinctive Illyrian helmet.

description.zh: 使用利布尔纳快船的海上力量，以速度和机动性著称。
description.en: Maritime force using liburna warships, renowned for speed and maneuverability.

description.zh: 内陆地区的轻骑兵力量。
description.en: Light cavalry forces from inland regions.

commanderInChief.zh: 联盟王或最强部落首领在战时统一指挥
commanderInChief.en: The confederate king or strongest tribal chief commanded during wartime

technology.zh: 利布尔纳快船设计先进（后被罗马海军采用），陆战以山地游击战术见长，但缺乏大规模正面作战能力。
technology.en: Advanced liburna warship design (later adopted by the Roman navy); skilled in mountain guerrilla tactics on land but lacked capacity for large-scale pitched battles.

threats.zh: 罗马共和国在亚得里亚海的扩张（即将爆发第一次伊利里亚战争）、马其顿压力、内部部落纷争。
threats.en: Roman Republic's expansion in the Adriatic (the First Illyrian War was imminent), Macedonian pressure, and internal tribal feuds.

populationDescription.zh: 约五十万人口分布于亚得里亚海东岸至巴尔干内陆山区（今阿尔巴尼亚、黑山、波黑沿海），包括阿尔迪安人、达尔马提亚人、利布尔尼亚人等多个部落。
populationDescription.en: About 500,000 people distributed from the eastern Adriatic coast to the Balkan interior highlands (modern Albania, Montenegro, and coastal Bosnia), including Ardiaei, Dalmatae, Liburnians, and other tribes.

socialClasses.zh: 部落王与军事贵族、自由战士、农民与牧民、海员、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Tribal kings and military aristocracy, free warriors, farmers and pastoralists, sailors, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分希腊城邦和马其顿保持灵活的临时同盟。
allies.en: Maintained flexible temporary alliances with some Greek city-states and Macedonia.

enemies.zh: 罗马共和国（海盗冲突激化）、伊庇鲁斯、部分竞争性伊利里亚部落。
enemies.en: Roman Republic (escalating piracy conflicts), Epirus, and some rival Illyrian tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以海盗活动控制亚得里亚海航路、通过灵活联盟在罗马与马其顿间寻求平衡。
foreignPolicy.en: Controlled Adriatic shipping lanes through piracy and sought balance between Rome and Macedonia via flexible alliances.

era.zh: 铁器时代巴尔干部落文明
era.en: Iron Age Balkan tribal civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 利布尔纳快船设计（革命性轻型战船）、伊利里亚头盔锻造、山地要塞建筑。
keyInnovations.en: Liburna warship design (revolutionary light warship), Illyrian helmet forging, and mountain fortress architecture.

strengths.zh: 利布尔纳快船的海上优势、山地防御能力、亚得里亚海战略位置、矿产资源。
strengths.en: Naval superiority with liburna warships, mountain-defense capability, strategic Adriatic position, and mineral resources.

weaknesses.zh: 政治极度分裂、海盗活动引发罗马干预、缺乏文字和中央官僚体系。
weaknesses.en: Extreme political fragmentation, piracy provoking Roman intervention, and lack of writing and central bureaucracy.

outlook.zh: 伊利里亚人的海盗活动将在前229年引发罗马的第一次伊利里亚战争，开启罗马对巴尔干的征服进程。
outlook.en: Illyrian piracy would trigger the First Illyrian War with Rome in 229 BCE, beginning Rome's conquest of the Balkans.

description.zh: 公元前221年的伊利里亚部落联邦是亚得里亚海东岸至巴尔干内陆的松散部落政治体，以利布尔纳快船、海盗活动和山地战术闻名，但政治分裂使其难以应对即将到来的罗马扩张。
description.en: The Illyrian Tribal States in 221 BCE were a loose tribal polity from the eastern Adriatic coast to the Balkan interior, famed for liburna warships, piracy, and mountain tactics, but political fragmentation left them vulnerable to the imminent Roman expansion.

capital.zh: 加拉马（今利比亚费赞杰尔马）

governmentForm.zh: 绿洲城邦式君主制，国王通过控制水利（坎儿井系统）和贸易路线维持权力。
governmentForm.en: An oasis city-state monarchy where the king maintained power through control of water systems (foggara irrigation) and trade routes.

socialStructure.zh: 王族与贵族-商人阶层-农民与工匠-奴隶（大量来自撒哈拉以南）
socialStructure.en: Royalty and nobility – merchant class – farmers and artisans – slaves (many from sub-Saharan regions)

rulingClass.zh: 王族和控制贸易路线的商人贵族构成统治精英。
rulingClass.en: The royal house and merchant-nobles who controlled trade routes formed the ruling elite.

succession.zh: 王位在王族内世袭传承。
succession.en: Kingship was hereditary within the royal house.

structure.zh: 以加拉马为中心的绿洲王国，通过坎儿井灌溉系统和跨撒哈拉贸易控制维持统治。
structure.en: An oasis kingdom centered on Garama, maintaining rule through foggara irrigation and trans-Saharan trade control.

function.zh: 管理坎儿井系统、分配灌溉水源。
function.en: Managed the foggara system and allocated irrigation water.

function.zh: 组织跨撒哈拉商队、管理贸易关税。
function.en: Organized trans-Saharan caravans and managed trade customs.

function.zh: 保护绿洲和贸易路线安全。
function.en: Protected oasis and trade route security.

localAdmin.zh: 各绿洲聚落由王室任命的管理者治理。
localAdmin.en: Individual oasis settlements were governed by royally appointed administrators.

legalSystem.zh: 加拉曼特习惯法与王室法令并行。
legalSystem.en: Garamantian customary law alongside royal decrees.

taxationSystem.zh: 对贸易征收过境税，绿洲农产征收实物税。
taxationSystem.en: Transit taxes on trade and in-kind taxes on oasis agricultural produce.

religion.zh: 加拉曼特本土信仰，融合利比亚-柏柏尔宗教传统
religion.en: Indigenous Garamantian faith, blending Libyan-Berber religious traditions

culturalAchievements.zh: 坎儿井地下灌溉系统（工程奇迹）、岩画艺术、独特的撒哈拉陵墓建筑。
culturalAchievements.en: Foggara underground irrigation system (engineering marvel), rock art, and distinctive Saharan tomb architecture.

languageFamily.zh: 古利比亚-柏柏尔语（亚非语系）
languageFamily.en: Ancient Libyan-Berber (Afro-Asiatic family)

philosophy.zh: 撒哈拉本土宗教信仰与自然崇拜
philosophy.en: Saharan indigenous religious beliefs and nature worship

writingSystem.zh: 提非纳格文字（古利比亚字母）。
writingSystem.en: Tifinagh script (ancient Libyan alphabet).

gdpDescription.zh: 以坎儿井灌溉农业和跨撒哈拉贸易为经济双支柱，但干旱环境限制了整体规模。
gdpDescription.en: Dual-pillared on foggara irrigation agriculture and trans-Saharan trade, but the arid environment limited overall scale.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、跨撒哈拉贸易、畜牧业、宝石加工
mainIndustries.en: Irrigation agriculture, trans-Saharan trade, pastoralism, and gemstone processing

tradeGoods.zh: 盐、宝石（红玉髓）、椰枣、奴隶；从地中海进口陶器和金属制品
tradeGoods.en: Salt, gemstones (carnelian), dates, and slaves; imported pottery and metalware from the Mediterranean

householdWealth.zh: 贵族与商人通过贸易积累财富；农民依靠灌溉绿洲。
householdWealth.en: Nobles and merchants accumulated wealth through trade; farmers depended on irrigated oases.

economicSystem.zh: 以王室控制贸易和灌溉为核心的绿洲经济。
economicSystem.en: An oasis economy centered on royal control of trade and irrigation.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室储备以贸易商品和贵金属为主。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal reserves consisted mainly of trade goods and precious metals.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以贸易过境税和灌溉农产为支柱。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy relied on trade transit taxes and irrigated produce.

description.zh: 以四马战车和骆驼骑兵为主的机动力量，适合沙漠作战。
description.en: Mobile forces of four-horse chariots and camel cavalry, suited for desert warfare.

description.zh: 保卫绿洲和贸易路线的步兵力量。
description.en: Infantry forces protecting oases and trade routes.

description.zh: 驻守各绿洲聚落的防卫力量。
description.en: Defense forces stationed at oasis settlements.

technology.zh: 四马战车是标志性军事装备，骆驼骑兵适合沙漠长途作战，但整体兵力规模较小。
technology.en: Four-horse chariots were the signature military equipment; camel cavalry suited for long-range desert warfare, but overall troop numbers were small.

threats.zh: 迦太基和地中海势力的南侵压力、沙漠游牧民袭扰、水源争夺。
threats.en: Southward pressure from Carthage and Mediterranean powers, desert nomad raids, and water-source competition.

populationDescription.zh: 约八万人口分布于费赞绿洲带，以加拉马为中心。人口受限于沙漠环境中的水资源。
populationDescription.en: About 80,000 people distributed across the Fezzan oasis belt, centered on Garama. Population was constrained by water resources in the desert environment.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、商人、农民与工匠、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and nobles, merchants, farmers and artisans, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与撒哈拉以南部落和地中海沿岸城市保持贸易关系。
allies.en: Maintained trade relations with sub-Saharan tribes and Mediterranean coastal cities.

enemies.zh: 迦太基（潜在的对手和贸易竞争者）、竞争性沙漠部落。
enemies.en: Carthage (potential rival and trade competitor), and rival desert tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以控制跨撒哈拉贸易路线为核心利益，与地中海和撒哈拉以南双方维持贸易外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Core interest in controlling trans-Saharan trade routes, maintaining trade diplomacy with both Mediterranean and sub-Saharan sides.

era.zh: 撒哈拉绿洲灌溉文明
era.en: Saharan oasis irrigation civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 坎儿井地下灌溉系统（数百公里地下水渠）、四马战车、撒哈拉航路技术。
keyInnovations.en: Foggara underground irrigation system (hundreds of kilometers of underground channels), four-horse chariots, and Saharan navigation techniques.

strengths.zh: 坎儿井灌溉技术优势、跨撒哈拉贸易控制、沙漠战术经验。
strengths.en: Foggara irrigation advantage, trans-Saharan trade control, and desert warfare experience.

weaknesses.zh: 人口受限于沙漠环境、孤立于主要文明中心、军事力量规模小。
weaknesses.en: Population limited by desert environment, isolation from major civilization centers, and small military scale.

outlook.zh: 加拉曼特人将在随后几个世纪达到鼎盛（约公元1-200年），成为撒哈拉贸易的关键枢纽，但最终因气候变化和水资源枯竭而衰落。
outlook.en: The Garamantes would reach their zenith in coming centuries (c. 1–200 CE), becoming a key hub for Saharan trade, but ultimately declined due to climate change and water depletion.

description.zh: 公元前221年的加拉曼特人是撒哈拉费赞地区的绿洲王国，以独创的坎儿井地下灌溉系统和跨撒哈拉贸易控制闻名，正处于逐步繁荣的上升期。
description.en: The Garamantes in 221 BCE were an oasis kingdom in the Saharan Fezzan region, famed for their innovative foggara underground irrigation and trans-Saharan trade control, in a period of gradual prosperity and growth.

name.zh: 托勒密治下昔兰尼加希腊殖民地

ruler.zh: 托勒密三世任命的总督

governmentForm.zh: 托勒密王朝行省，保留部分希腊城邦自治传统，由王室任命的总督和地方议会共同管理。
governmentForm.en: A Ptolemaic province retaining some Greek polis autonomy traditions, co-managed by a royally appointed governor and local councils.

socialStructure.zh: 托勒密王室代表-希腊公民精英-利比亚-柏柏尔原住民-奴隶
socialStructure.en: Ptolemaic royal representatives – Greek citizen elite – Libyan-Berber indigenous people – slaves

rulingClass.zh: 希腊裔公民贵族和托勒密任命官员共同统治。
rulingClass.en: Greek citizen aristocracy and Ptolemaic-appointed officials jointly ruled.

succession.zh: 总督由托勒密国王任命，地方官员由城邦内部选举产生。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the Ptolemaic king; local officials were elected within the polis.

structure.zh: 托勒密行省框架下的希腊城邦联盟，以昔兰尼五城（昔兰尼、阿波罗尼亚、托勒麦斯、塔乌奇拉、贝勒尼基）为核心。
structure.en: A Greek polis league under Ptolemaic provincial framework, centered on the Pentapolis (Cyrene, Apollonia, Ptolemais, Taucheira, and Berenice).

function.zh: 代表托勒密王室管理行省事务、征税和军事。
function.en: Represented the Ptolemaic crown in provincial administration, taxation, and military affairs.

function.zh: 管理城邦内部事务、司法和公民权。
function.en: Managed internal polis affairs, justice, and citizenship.

function.zh: 管理阿波罗神庙和学术机构（昔兰尼学派）。
function.en: Managed the Temple of Apollo and academic institutions (Cyrenaic school).

localAdmin.zh: 五城各有自治议会，周边乡村由城邦官员管理。
localAdmin.en: Each of the five cities had its own autonomous council; surrounding rural areas were managed by polis officials.

legalSystem.zh: 希腊法律为基础，辅以托勒密王室法令。
legalSystem.en: Greek law as the foundation, supplemented by Ptolemaic royal decrees.

taxationSystem.zh: 向托勒密王室缴纳行省税，城邦内部征收公民税和贸易关税。
taxationSystem.en: Provincial taxes paid to the Ptolemaic crown; internal polis taxes and trade customs levied within the cities.

religion.zh: 希腊多神崇拜，以昔兰尼阿波罗神庙为核心圣地
religion.en: Greek polytheism, centered on the Temple of Apollo at Cyrene

culturalAchievements.zh: 昔兰尼学派（快乐主义哲学）、西尔菲姆植物（珍贵药草贸易）、希腊化建筑与雕塑、天文学贡献。
culturalAchievements.en: The Cyrenaic school (hedonist philosophy), silphium plant (precious herb trade), Hellenistic architecture and sculpture, and astronomical contributions.

languageFamily.zh: 希腊语（多利安方言和通用希腊语）
languageFamily.en: Greek (Doric dialect and Koine Greek)

philosophy.zh: 昔兰尼学派（阿里斯提波斯创立的快乐主义哲学）
philosophy.en: Cyrenaic school (hedonist philosophy founded by Aristippus)

writingSystem.zh: 希腊字母
writingSystem.en: Greek alphabet

gdpDescription.zh: 以西尔菲姆植物贸易、谷物农业和地中海贸易为经济支柱。昔兰尼是希腊化世界中富裕的商业城市。
gdpDescription.en: Economically supported by silphium trade, grain agriculture, and Mediterranean commerce. Cyrene was a wealthy commercial city in the Hellenistic world.

mainIndustries.zh: 西尔菲姆贸易、谷物农业、橄榄油生产、马匹培育、地中海贸易
mainIndustries.en: Silphium trade, grain farming, olive oil production, horse breeding, and Mediterranean trade

tradeGoods.zh: 西尔菲姆（极珍贵药材）、谷物、橄榄油、马匹、蜂蜜
tradeGoods.en: Silphium (highly precious medicinal herb), grain, olive oil, horses, and honey

name.zh: 昔兰尼银币与托勒密铸币
name.en: Cyrenaic silver coins and Ptolemaic coinage

householdWealth.zh: 希腊公民精英拥有农庄和商业财产；柏柏尔原住民以畜牧为生。
householdWealth.en: Greek citizen elite owned farms and commercial properties; Berber indigenous people lived by pastoralism.

economicSystem.zh: 希腊化混合经济，以城邦商业和农业为基础，在托勒密行省框架下运行。
economicSystem.en: A Hellenistic mixed economy based on polis commerce and agriculture, operating within the Ptolemaic provincial framework.

treasuryDescription.zh: 城邦国库以银币和西尔菲姆贸易收入为主要储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The polis treasury was stored mainly in silver coins and silphium trade revenue.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 在托勒密行省框架内保持城邦财政自主性，以西尔菲姆贸易垄断收入为财政支柱。
fiscalPolicy.en: Maintained polis fiscal autonomy within the Ptolemaic provincial framework, with the silphium trade monopoly as the fiscal pillar.

description.zh: 装备希腊式甲胄和长矛的公民重步兵。
description.en: Citizen heavy infantry armed with Greek armor and spears.

description.zh: 以优良马匹闻名的骑兵部队。
description.en: Cavalry units famed for their fine horses.

description.zh: 来自当地柏柏尔部落的轻步兵和标枪手。
description.en: Light infantry and javelineers recruited from local Berber tribes.

commanderInChief.zh: 托勒密任命的军事总督

technology.zh: 继承希腊军事传统，装备精良但规模有限。马匹培育水平高，骑兵是重要战力。
technology.en: Inherited Greek military traditions with well-equipped but limited-scale forces. Horse breeding was excellent, making cavalry an important asset.

threats.zh: 内部希腊公民与柏柏尔原住民之间的紧张关系、周边沙漠部落袭扰、托勒密王朝内部政治动荡对行省的影响。
threats.en: Internal tensions between Greek citizens and Berber indigenous people, raids by surrounding desert tribes, and the impact of Ptolemaic internal political turmoil on the province.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十万人口，其中希腊裔公民约占三分之一，利比亚-柏柏尔原住民占多数，另有少量犹太社区和托勒密驻军。
populationDescription.en: About 200,000 people, with Greek-origin citizens comprising roughly one-third, Libyan-Berber indigenous peoples the majority, plus a small Jewish community and Ptolemaic garrison.

socialClasses.zh: 托勒密官员、希腊公民精英、柏柏尔部落民、犹太社区、奴隶。
socialClasses.en: Ptolemaic officials, Greek citizen elite, Berber tribal people, Jewish community, and slaves.

allies.zh: 作为托勒密行省与埃及保持紧密联系，与希腊城邦世界保持文化和商业纽带。
allies.en: As a Ptolemaic province, maintained close ties with Egypt and cultural-commercial bonds with the Greek polis world.

enemies.zh: 撒哈拉沙漠部落（边境袭扰）、潜在的塞琉古势力渗透。
enemies.en: Saharan desert tribes (border raids), and potential Seleucid influence infiltration.

foreignPolicy.zh: 在托勒密框架内保持地方自治，通过西尔菲姆贸易维持经济独立性。
foreignPolicy.en: Maintained local autonomy within the Ptolemaic framework, preserving economic independence through the silphium trade.

era.zh: 希腊化时代殖民城邦文明
era.en: Hellenistic colonial polis civilization

keyInnovations.zh: 昔兰尼学派哲学、西尔菲姆栽培与贸易、希腊化建筑、天文观测。
keyInnovations.en: Cyrenaic school philosophy, silphium cultivation and trade, Hellenistic architecture, and astronomical observation.

strengths.zh: 西尔菲姆贸易垄断、希腊文化传统、托勒密保护、骑兵传统。
strengths.en: Silphium trade monopoly, Greek cultural heritage, Ptolemaic protection, and cavalry tradition.

weaknesses.zh: 对托勒密宗主的依赖、内部希腊-柏柏尔民族矛盾、人口有限。
weaknesses.en: Dependence on Ptolemaic suzerainty, internal Greek-Berber ethnic tensions, and limited population.

outlook.zh: 昔兰尼加将在托勒密衰落后经历动荡，最终于前96年被罗马继承，并成为罗马帝国的一个行省。
outlook.en: Cyrenaica would experience turmoil as the Ptolemaic dynasty declined, ultimately bequeathed to Rome in 96 BCE and becoming a Roman province.

description.zh: 公元前221年的昔兰尼加是托勒密王朝治下的希腊殖民行省，以西尔菲姆植物贸易、昔兰尼学派哲学和繁荣的五城联盟闻名，在希腊化世界中享有重要的文化和经济地位。
description.en: Cyrenaica in 221 BCE was a Greek colonial province under Ptolemaic rule, famed for the silphium trade, Cyrenaic school philosophy, and the prosperous Pentapolis league, holding an important cultural and economic position in the Hellenistic world.

governmentForm.zh: 以“大人物”制度为核心的分散部族社会，各山谷独立运作。
governmentForm.en: A dispersed tribal society centered on the 'Big Man' system, with each valley operating independently.

socialStructure.zh: 以氏族和山谷为基本单位。“大人物”通过组织盛宴、猪群交换和战争领导获得威望。社会相对平等。
socialStructure.en: Organized by clan and valley. Big Men gained prestige through organizing feasts, pig exchanges, and war leadership. Society was relatively egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: “大人物“和仪式长老。”, “en“: “Big Men and ritual elders.

succession.zh: 不世袭，须通过个人能力获得地位。
succession.en: Not hereditary; status had to be earned through personal ability.

structure.zh: 没有中央政权，各山谷独立运行。猪群交换仪式是跨部族政治协调的主要形式。
structure.en: No central authority; each valley operated independently. Pig exchange ceremonies were the main form of cross-tribal political coordination.

function.zh: 处理纠纷、婚姻安排和部族间关系。
function.en: Handled disputes, marriage arrangements, and inter-tribal relations.

function.zh: 管理园艺周期和祖先祭祀。
function.en: Managed horticultural cycles and ancestor rituals.

localAdmin.zh: 各山谷由地方”大人物“和长老自治。
localAdmin.en: Each valley was self-governed by local Big Men and elders.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法，纠纷通过赔偿或仪式性战争解决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law; disputes resolved through compensation or ritual warfare.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，以互惠义务为基础。
taxationSystem.en: No formal taxation; based on reciprocal obligations.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜与万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Ancestor worship and animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 高地园艺系统持续发展，沼泽排水和梯田技术日益成熟。文化和语言多样性极为丰富。
culturalAchievements.en: Highland horticultural systems continued to develop, with swamp drainage and terracing becoming more mature. Cultural and linguistic diversity was extremely rich.

languageFamily.zh: 跨新几内亚语系为主
languageFamily.en: Predominantly Trans-New Guinea languages

philosophy.zh: 以祖先灵力、自然精灵和互惠义务为核心的宇宙观
philosophy.en: A cosmology centered on ancestral power, nature spirits, and reciprocal obligations

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统
writingSystem.en: No writing system

gdpDescription.zh: 以沼泽排水园艺农业和猪群饲养为基础。猪群是核心财富形式。
gdpDescription.en: Based on swamp drainage horticulture and pig husbandry. Pig herds were the core form of wealth.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、猪群饲养、石器制造、盐业
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, pig husbandry, stone tool making, salt production

tradeGoods.zh: 猪、芋头、盐块、石斧、贝壳
tradeGoods.en: Pigs, taro, salt blocks, stone axes, shells

householdWealth.zh: 以猪群数量和园艺地面积为主要衡量标准。
householdWealth.en: Measured mainly by pig herd size and garden plot area.

economicSystem.zh: 以猪群交换和互惠义务为核心的自给农业经济。
economicSystem.en: A subsistence agricultural economy centered on pig exchange and reciprocal obligations.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以猪群、贝壳和盐块形式储备。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as pig herds, shells, and salt blocks.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持猪群交换网络和仪式义务为核心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining pig exchange networks and ritual obligations.

description.zh: 各氏族的勇士。
description.en: Clan warriors.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

technology.zh: 石制和木制武器，仪式性战争为主。
technology.en: Stone and wood weapons; ritual warfare predominated.

threats.zh: 山谷间的部族冲突。
threats.en: Inter-valley tribal conflicts.

populationDescription.zh: 约二十三万人分布于新几内亚高地各山谷。
populationDescription.en: About 230,000 people distributed across New Guinea highland valleys.

socialClasses.zh: “大人物”、仪式长老、普通农民。”, “en“: “Big Men, ritual elders, ordinary farmers.

allies.zh: 通过猪群交换仪式与相邻山谷保持联盟。
allies.en: Maintained alliances with neighboring valleys through pig exchange ceremonies.

enemies.zh: 与竞争山谷的部族间存在周期性仪式战争。
enemies.en: Periodic ritual warfare with competing valley tribes.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持领土完整和猪群交换网络为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining territorial integrity and pig exchange networks.

era.zh: 新几内亚高地新石器农业时代
era.en: New Guinea Highland Neolithic Agricultural Era

keyInnovations.zh: 沼泽排水技术改良、芋头栽培集约化、石斧制造。
keyInnovations.en: Improved swamp drainage, intensified taro cultivation, and stone axe manufacturing.

strengths.zh: 最古老的独立农业传统之一，高效的高地园艺，密集的社会交换网络。
strengths.en: One of the oldest independent agricultural traditions, efficient highland horticulture, and dense social exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 极度政治分散，无金属和文字。
weaknesses.en: Extremely politically fragmented, with no metals or writing.

outlook.zh: 高地社会将在隔离中持续稳定发展。
outlook.en: Highland society would continue developing stably in isolation.

description.zh: 巴布亚高地农耕社会是公元前221年新几内亚高地以沼泽排水农业和“大人物”制度为特征的部族社会。
description.en: The Papuan Highland Farmers were a tribal society in the New Guinea highlands around 221 BCE, characterized by swamp drainage agriculture and the Big Man system.

governmentForm.zh: 以汤加和萨摩亚为中心的成熟酋邦社会。世袭酋长通过玛纳和塔布体系维持权威。
governmentForm.en: A mature chiefdom society centered on Tonga and Samoa. Hereditary chiefs maintained authority through mana and tabu systems.

socialStructure.zh: 等级分明的社会。世袭酋长（阿利基）家族占据顶层，下设次级酋长、仪式专家、航海家，平民和仆从在底层。
socialStructure.en: A clearly stratified society. Hereditary chiefly (ariki) families at the top, with sub-chiefs, ritual specialists, and navigators below, and commoners and servants at the bottom.

rulingClass.zh: 世袭酋长家族。
rulingClass.en: Hereditary chiefly families.

succession.zh: 长子继承制，以玛纳血统为依据。
succession.en: Primogeniture, based on mana lineage.

structure.zh: 汤加已形成多层酋邦体系，萨摩亚的酋长会议（法诺）制度正在完善。塔布制度是法律和行政基础。
structure.en: Tonga had formed a multi-tiered chiefdom system; Samoa's chiefly council (fono) system was maturing. The tabu system was the legal and administrative foundation.

function.zh: 处理岛屿事务和对外关系。
function.en: Handled island affairs and external relations.

function.zh: 维护宗教仪式和航海传统。
function.en: Maintained religious ceremonies and navigation traditions.

localAdmin.zh: 各岛由酋长家族和次级酋长管理。
localAdmin.en: Each island managed by chiefly families and sub-chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 以塔布体系为核心的法律规范。
legalSystem.en: Legal norms centered on the tabu system.

taxationSystem.zh: 以初穂献纳和仪式劳役为基础。
taxationSystem.en: Based on first-fruit offerings and ritual labor.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神信仰
religion.en: Polynesian polytheism

culturalAchievements.zh: 口传文学体系日益成熟，航海术达到更高水平。树皮布和木雕艺术继续发展。酋邦之间的仪式性交流活跃。
culturalAchievements.en: Oral literary traditions were maturing further, and navigation reached higher levels. Bark cloth and wood carving continued to develop. Ceremonial exchanges between chiefdoms were active.

languageFamily.zh: 波利尼西亚语（南岛语系大洋洲语族）
languageFamily.en: Polynesian (Austronesian, Oceanic branch)

philosophy.zh: 以玛纳、塔布和祖先崇拜为核心的波利尼西亚宇宙观
philosophy.en: A Polynesian cosmology centered on mana, tabu, and ancestor veneration

writingSystem.zh: 无文字系统，以口传谱系和航海歌谣传递知识
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge transmitted through oral genealogies and navigation chants

gdpDescription.zh: 以园艺农业、渔业和岛屿间贸易为核心。酋长主导的再分配经济体系已经成熟。
gdpDescription.en: Centered on horticulture, fishing, and inter-island trade. The chief-directed redistribution economic system had matured.

mainIndustries.zh: 园艺农业、渔业、独木舟建造、树皮布制作、贝壳加工
mainIndustries.en: Horticultural farming, fishing, canoe building, bark cloth making, shell working

tradeGoods.zh: 芋头、鱼干、贝壳饰品、树皮布、独木舟
tradeGoods.en: Taro, dried fish, shell ornaments, bark cloth, canoes

name.zh: 实物交换与酋长再分配

householdWealth.zh: 以独木舟、猪群和园艺地为衡量标准。酋长家族控制大部分资源。
householdWealth.en: Measured by canoes, pig herds, and garden plots. Chiefly families controlled most resources.

economicSystem.zh: 以酋长再分配为核心的等级化岛屿经济。
economicSystem.en: A hierarchical island economy centered on chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: 以贝壳、树皮布和食物储备形式保存。
treasuryDescription.en: Held as shells, bark cloth, and food reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持酋长权威和航海远征为优先。
fiscalPolicy.en: Chiefly authority and navigation expeditions took priority.

description.zh: 酋长的常备武装。
description.en: The chief's standing armed force.

description.zh: 冲突时征召的壮年男子。
description.en: Able-bodied men called up during conflict.

technology.zh: 木制和石制武器，善于利用独木舟进行岛屿间战争。
technology.en: Wood and stone weapons, skilled at inter-island warfare using canoes.

threats.zh: 岛屿间的酋邦竞争。
threats.en: Inter-island chiefdom competition.

populationDescription.zh: 约四万人分布于汤加和萨摩亚群岛，人口持续增长。
populationDescription.en: About 40,000 people across Tonga and Samoa, with continued population growth.

socialClasses.zh: 世袭酋长、次级酋长、仪式专家、航海家、平民、仆从。
socialClasses.en: Hereditary chiefs, sub-chiefs, ritual specialists, navigators, commoners, servants.

allies.zh: 汤加与萨摩亚之间通过婚姻联盟保持紧密关系。
allies.en: Tonga and Samoa maintained close relations through marriage alliances.

enemies.zh: 岛屿间酋邦的领土竞争。
enemies.en: Territorial competition among island chiefdoms.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维持岛屿联盟和准备东向远航为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Centered on maintaining island alliances and preparing for eastward voyages.

era.zh: 波利尼西亚酋邦形成期
era.en: Polynesian Chiefdom Formation Period

keyInnovations.zh: 大型远洋双体独木舟、星象和洋流导航术、树皮布制造、园艺集约化。
keyInnovations.en: Large oceanic double-hulled canoes, stellar and current navigation, bark cloth manufacturing, and agricultural intensification.

strengths.zh: 卓越的航海能力，成熟的酋邦组织，丰富的海洋资源。
strengths.en: Outstanding navigation, mature chiefdom organization, and rich marine resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口规模仍然有限，岛屿资源有限。
weaknesses.en: Population still limited, with limited island resources.

outlook.zh: 西波利尼西亚酋邦即将开始向东太平洋的伟大扩张。
outlook.en: Western Polynesian chiefdoms were about to begin the great expansion into the eastern Pacific.

description.zh: 原始波利尼西亚酋邦是公元前221年汤加-萨摩亚地区以世袭酋长制和塔布体系为核心的成熟岛屿社会。
description.en: The Proto-Polynesian Chiefdoms were mature island societies in the Tonga-Samoa region around 221 BCE, centered on hereditary chieftainship and the tabu system.

governmentForm.zh: 多个聚落逐渐向特奥蒂瓦坎谷地集中，形成早期城市化政体。
governmentForm.en: Multiple settlements gradually concentrating in the Teotihuacan Valley, forming an early urbanizing polity.

socialStructure.zh: 社会分化加速，仪式精英和手工业专家阶层形成。
socialStructure.en: Social differentiation accelerating, with ritual elites and craft specialist strata forming.

rulingClass.zh: 仪式-政治精英
rulingClass.en: Ritual-political elites

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 早期城市规划和金字塔建造的萌芽，黑曜石加工技术先进。
culturalAchievements.en: Early urban planning and nascent pyramid construction; advanced obsidian working technology.

languageFamily.zh: 可能属奥托-曼格语系或纳瓦特语系前身
languageFamily.en: Possibly proto-Oto-Manguean or proto-Nahuan

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per person-year

gdpDescription.zh: 以灌溉农业和黑曜石开采加工为核心。
gdpDescription.en: Centered on irrigated agriculture and obsidian quarrying and working.

mainIndustries.zh: 灌溉农业、黑曜石加工、陶器、建筑
mainIndustries.en: Irrigated agriculture, obsidian working, pottery, construction

tradeGoods.zh: 黑曜石制品、陶器、玉米
tradeGoods.en: Obsidian products, pottery, maize

householdWealth.zh: 财富体现在土地、黑曜石矿藏和仪式建筑上。
householdWealth.en: Wealth embodied in land, obsidian deposits, and ritual architecture.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per person-year
grams of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭自给生产和部族内部再分配为主的经济体系。
economicSystem.en: An economy based on household subsistence production and intra-tribal redistribution.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year
grams of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: "国库"主要为首领控制的食物与工具储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The "treasury" consisted mainly of chiefly controlled stores of food and tools.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 邻近政体竞争。
threats.en: Competition from neighboring polities.

populationDescription.zh: 人口正在向特奥蒂瓦坎谷地集中。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrating in the Teotihuacan Valley.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 中美洲前古典晚期
era.en: Late Mesoamerican Preclassic

keyInnovations.zh: 黑曜石工艺、早期城市化、灌溉。
keyInnovations.en: Obsidian craft, early urbanization, irrigation.

strengths.zh: 黑曜石资源丰富，地理位置优越。
strengths.en: Rich obsidian resources, advantageous geographic position.

weaknesses.zh: 尚处形成初期，人口和组织有限。
weaknesses.en: Still in formative stage, limited population and organization.

outlook.zh: 将在未来几个世纪发展为美洲最大城市。
outlook.en: Would develop into the largest city in the Americas over coming centuries.

description.zh: 前特奥蒂瓦坎是墨西哥谷地正在聚集的早期城市化聚落，未来将发展为美洲第一大城市。
description.en: Proto-Teotihuacan was an early urbanizing settlement cluster in the Valley of Mexico, destined to become the largest city in the Americas.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎营地的分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: Dispersed tribal society in mobile hunting camps.

socialStructure.zh: 高度平等的扩展家庭组织。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian extended family organization.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

succession.zh: 继承主要依亲属血统与长老推举，个人威望与军事才能亦起作用。
succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 北方森林生存技术。
culturalAchievements.en: Boreal forest survival techniques.

languageFamily.zh: 阿萨巴斯卡语系与阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Athabaskan and Algonquian families

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵与祖灵崇敬构成核心世界观，强调与自然的和谐共存。
philosophy.en: Animism and ancestor reverence formed the core worldview, emphasizing harmony with nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字，知识通过口述传统、歌谣与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No developed writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and ritual.

mainIndustries.en: Hunting, fishing, fur processing

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、肉干
tradeGoods.en: Furs, dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 独木舟和猎具。
householdWealth.en: Canoes and hunting gear.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持储备和仪式再分配为目标。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice aimed to maintain reserves and support ritual redistribution.

description.zh: 由青壮年组成，使用石器、木矛和弓箭进行防御与突袭。
description.en: Composed of able-bodied men using stone tools, wooden spears, and bows for defense and raids.

technology.zh: 以石器、木骨武器和弓箭为主，无金属冶炼。
technology.en: Based on stone, wood, and bone weapons with bows; no metal smelting.

unit.en: grams of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 冬季饥荒。
threats.en: Winter famine.

populationDescription.zh: 亚寒带森林流动营地。
populationDescription.en: Mobile camps in subarctic forests.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 雪鞋、独木舟。

strengths.zh: 环境适应力强。
strengths.en: Strong environmental adaptation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口密度低。
weaknesses.en: Low population density.

outlook.zh: 将持续。
outlook.en: Would persist.

description.zh: 亚北极森林猎人以驯鹿狩猎为生的北方部族。
description.en: Subarctic Forest Hunters were northern peoples living by caribou hunting.

name.zh: 北美太平洋沿岸采集者

governmentForm.en: Complex foraging societies.

rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。

taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

culturalAchievements.en: Totem poles and potlatch.

succession.en: Succession depended on kinship, elder selection, and personal prestige or martial ability.

structure.zh: 松散的酋邦或部族联合体，由首领与长老协商决策。
structure.en: A loose chiefdom or tribal confederation in which chiefs and elders made decisions by consultation.

function.zh: 协调部族事务、纠纷调解与仪式安排。
function.en: Coordinated tribal affairs, settled disputes, and organized rituals.

function.zh: 管理食物储备、劳役调配与对外交换物资。
function.en: Managed food stores, labor allocation, and goods for external exchange.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由家族长者与营地首领执行，各聚落在日常事务上享有高度自治。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was carried out by family elders and camp leaders, with settlements enjoying broad autonomy in daily affairs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯规范、长老裁决与公开调解为基础。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on customary norms, elder adjudication, and public mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，资源通过贡礼、集体劳役和仪式再分配集中。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal tax system; resources were concentrated through tribute, collective labor, and ritual redistribution.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰
religion.en: Animism

culturalAchievements.zh: 霍普韦尔交换网络的早期萌芽，远距离贸易和土丘建造传统延续。
culturalAchievements.en: Early stirrings of the Hopewell exchange network, with long-distance trade and mound-building traditions continuing.

languageFamily.zh: 阿尔冈昆语系、易洛魁语系、苏语系
languageFamily.en: Algonquian, Iroquoian, Siouan families

threats.en: Intertribal conflict, climate fluctuations.

populationDescription.zh: 分布于北美东部森林和河谷地带。
populationDescription.en: Distributed across eastern North American forests and river valleys.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 铜冷锻、土丘建造、早期园圃农业。
keyInnovations.en: Cold-hammered copper, mound building, early garden horticulture.

strengths.zh: 远距离交换网络广泛，资源多样。
strengths.en: Extensive long-distance exchange networks, diverse resources.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散，无统一组织。
weaknesses.en: Politically fragmented, no unified organization.

outlook.zh: 霍普韦尔交换网络将在未来几个世纪达到鼎盛。
outlook.en: The Hopewell exchange network would reach its peak in coming centuries.

description.zh: 北美东部猎采者是融合狩猎采集与早期园圃农业的林地部族，以远距离交换网络闻名。
description.en: Eastern North American Hunter-Gatherers were woodland peoples combining hunting-gathering with early horticulture, known for long-distance exchange networks.

governmentForm.zh: 游猎小群体。
governmentForm.en: Small mobile hunting bands.

socialStructure.zh: 高度平等。
socialStructure.en: Highly egalitarian.

rulingClass.zh: 部族首领与长老
rulingClass.en: Tribal chiefs and elders

culturalAchievements.zh: 延续帕拉卡斯纺织传统，精美多彩陶器出现，地面线条（纳斯卡线条）的前身。
culturalAchievements.en: Continued Paracas textile traditions, fine polychrome pottery appeared, precursors of ground lines (Nazca Lines).

threats.en: Drought and El Niño.

populationDescription.zh: 秘鲁南部海岸纳斯卡河谷。
populationDescription.en: Nazca Valley of the southern Peruvian coast.

socialClasses.zh: 首领家族、长老、自由成员、依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefly families, elders, free members, dependents.

allies.zh: 无固定盟国，依赖亲缘和交换伙伴形成临时联盟。
allies.en: No fixed allies; temporary alliances formed through kin ties and exchange partners.

enemies.zh: 无持久性敌国，但邻近群体间常有资源争夺。
enemies.en: No permanent enemies, though resource competition with neighboring groups was common.

foreignPolicy.zh: 以维护领地安全与交换关系为先。
foreignPolicy.en: Policy prioritized territorial security and exchange relationships.

era.zh: 新石器至早期部族阶段
era.en: Neolithic to early tribal stage

keyInnovations.zh: 高级纺织、彩绘陶器、灌溉系统。
keyInnovations.en: Advanced textiles, polychrome pottery, irrigation.

strengths.zh: 工艺精湛，海陆资源兼备。
strengths.en: Excellent craftsmanship, combined marine and agricultural resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口有限，沿海环境脆弱。
weaknesses.en: Limited population, fragile coastal environment.

outlook.zh: 将发展为以纳斯卡线条闻名的文明。
outlook.en: Would develop into the civilization famous for the Nazca Lines.

description.zh: 早期纳斯卡文化是帕拉卡斯文化的后继者，以精美陶器和纺织品闻名。
description.en: Early Nazca Culture was the successor of Paracas, known for fine pottery and textiles.

description.zh: 秦王嬴政灭齐后完成统一，建立中国历史上第一个中央集权帝国秦朝，并自称“始皇帝”。这一事件结束战国分裂局面，深刻塑造了后世中国的政治制度。
description.en: After conquering Qi, King Zheng of Qin unified China and founded the Qin dynasty, the first centralized imperial state in Chinese history, taking the title First Emperor. The event ended the Warring States era and profoundly shaped later Chinese governance.

description.zh: 迦太基将领汉尼拔进攻与罗马结盟的伊比利亚城邦萨贡托，直接引发第二次布匿战争。此战改变了西地中海权力格局。
description.en: The Carthaginian commander Hannibal attacked Saguntum, an Iberian city allied with Rome, directly triggering the Second Punic War. The conflict reshaped the balance of power in the western Mediterranean.

title.zh: 汉尼拔翻越阿尔卑斯山

description.zh: 汉尼拔率军与战象翻越阿尔卑斯进入意大利，实施古代战争史上最著名的战略机动之一。此举将战争直接带到罗马本土。
description.en: Hannibal led his army and war elephants across the Alps into Italy in one of antiquity’s most famous strategic maneuvers. This brought the war directly onto Roman soil.

description.zh: 汉尼拔在特雷比亚河击败罗马军队，取得第二次布匿战争早期重大胜利。此战暴露了罗马在应对机动作战时的脆弱性。
description.en: Hannibal defeated a Roman army at the Trebia, securing an early major victory in the Second Punic War. The battle exposed Roman vulnerability to mobile and deceptive tactics.

title.zh: 秦始皇驾崩与沙丘之变
title.en: Death of Qin Shi Huang and the Sand Dune Succession Plot

description.zh: 秦始皇东巡途中去世，赵高与李斯伪造遗诏，扶立胡亥为秦二世。此次宫廷政变迅速削弱秦朝统治基础。
description.en: Qin Shi Huang died during an eastern tour, and Zhao Gao and Li Si forged succession orders to enthrone Huhai as the Second Emperor. This palace coup quickly weakened the foundations of Qin rule.

title.en: Chen Sheng and Wu Guang Rebellion

description.zh: 陈胜、吴广在大泽乡发动反秦起义，揭开秦末大规模叛乱序幕。它迅速激发各地反秦力量，最终导致秦朝灭亡。
description.en: Chen Sheng and Wu Guang launched an anti-Qin uprising at Dazexiang, beginning the great rebellions at the end of the Qin dynasty. It quickly inspired wider revolts that ultimately brought Qin down.

description.zh: 汉尼拔在坎尼以双重包围战术歼灭罗马大军，这是古代最著名的战术胜利之一。罗马虽遭惨败，但随后展现出惊人的国家韧性。
description.en: At Cannae, Hannibal annihilated a massive Roman army with a double envelopment, one of history’s most famous tactical victories. Despite the disaster, Rome demonstrated extraordinary state resilience afterward.

title.zh: 第一次马其顿战争开始

description.zh: 马其顿国王腓力五世与汉尼拔结盟后，罗马与马其顿爆发战争。此战标志着罗马更深度介入希腊世界事务。
description.en: After King Philip V of Macedon aligned with Hannibal, war broke out between Rome and Macedon. The conflict marked Rome’s deeper intervention in Greek affairs.

title.zh: 项羽巨鹿之战击溃秦军
title.en: Xiang Yu Defeats Qin Forces at Julu

description.zh: 项羽在巨鹿大败秦军主力，成为推翻秦朝的决定性战役之一。此战后秦帝国已无力恢复局势。
description.en: Xiang Yu crushed the main Qin forces at Julu in one of the decisive battles that overthrew the Qin dynasty. After this defeat, the Qin Empire could no longer recover.

description.zh: 刘邦进入咸阳后，秦王子婴投降，秦朝正式灭亡。这个短命帝国的崩溃为楚汉战争和汉朝建立铺平道路。
description.en: After Liu Bang entered Xianyang, Ziying surrendered and the Qin dynasty formally collapsed. The fall of this short-lived empire opened the way for the Chu-Han Contention and the rise of the Han.

description.zh: 普布利乌斯·科尔内利乌斯·西庇阿在伊比利亚夺取新迦太基，重创迦太基在西班牙的战略基地。此役为罗马最终赢得第二次布匿战争奠定基础。
description.en: Publius Cornelius Scipio captured New Carthage in Iberia, dealing a major blow to Carthage’s strategic position in Spain. The victory laid groundwork for Rome’s eventual success in the Second Punic War.

title.en: Battle of Gaixia and the Fall of Xiang Yu

description.zh: 刘邦在垓下击败项羽，结束楚汉战争，项羽随后自刎乌江。此战确定了汉王朝统一中国的道路。
description.en: Liu Bang defeated Xiang Yu at Gaixia, ending the Chu-Han Contention; Xiang Yu soon committed suicide at the Wu River. The battle secured the path for Han rule over China.

description.zh: 刘邦称帝，建立汉朝，是为汉高祖。汉朝此后成为东亚历史上最具影响力的王朝之一。
description.en: Liu Bang proclaimed himself emperor, founding the Han dynasty as Emperor Gaozu. The Han would become one of the most influential dynasties in East Asian history.

description.zh: 西庇阿在伊利帕击败迦太基军队，基本瓦解其在伊比利亚的力量。罗马由此夺得西班牙战场主动权。
description.en: Scipio defeated the Carthaginian army at Ilipa, effectively destroying Carthaginian power in Iberia. Rome thereby gained the initiative in the Spanish theater.

title.zh: 西庇阿在扎马战役击败汉尼拔
title.en: Scipio Defeats Hannibal at the Battle of Zama

description.zh: 扎马战役中，西庇阿与努米底亚盟军击败汉尼拔，结束第二次布匿战争的主战局。罗马由此确立西地中海霸权。
description.en: At Zama, Scipio and his Numidian allies defeated Hannibal, ending the main struggle of the Second Punic War. Rome thereby secured dominance in the western Mediterranean.

title.en: End of the Second Punic War

description.zh: 迦太基与罗马议和，被迫交出舰队、赔款并放弃海外战争能力。战后罗马迅速成为地中海最强共和国。
description.en: Carthage made peace with Rome, surrendering its fleet, paying indemnities, and losing the ability to wage overseas war. After the settlement, Rome rapidly emerged as the strongest republic in the Mediterranean.

title.zh: 冒顿单于建立匈奴强权

description.zh: 冒顿杀父自立后统一匈奴各部，建立强大的草原政权。匈奴的崛起成为汉初北方最大的战略威胁。
description.en: After killing his father and seizing power, Modu Chanyu unified the Xiongnu tribes into a powerful steppe state. The rise of the Xiongnu became the greatest northern strategic threat to the early Han.

description.zh: 汉高祖刘邦在白登被冒顿单于围困，几乎陷入绝境。此事迫使汉朝调整对匈奴政策，并促成后来的和亲外交。
description.en: Emperor Gaozu of Han was surrounded by Modu Chanyu at Baideng and nearly trapped fatally. The episode forced the Han to rethink its frontier strategy and helped lead to later heqin diplomacy.

description.zh: 汉朝与匈奴达成和亲政策，以宗室女子、馈赠和边境安排换取暂时和平。它成为汉初处理草原压力的重要外交模式。
description.en: The Han court established a heqin arrangement with the Xiongnu, exchanging a royal bride, gifts, and border arrangements for temporary peace. It became a key diplomatic model for the early Han in dealing with steppe pressure.

title.zh: 犬儒哲学家克律西波斯逝世

description.zh: 斯多亚学派第二代核心思想家克律西波斯于这一时期去世，其著作体系奠定了后世斯多亚哲学框架。斯多亚思想对罗马精英文化影响深远。
description.en: Chrysippus, the great systematizer of Stoicism, died around this time, leaving the intellectual structure that defined later Stoic philosophy. Stoic thought would profoundly influence Roman elite culture.

title.zh: 犬儒战争中的库诺斯刻法莱战役

description.zh: 罗马执政官弗拉米尼努斯在库诺斯刻法莱击败马其顿国王腓力五世。此战标志着罗马军团对马其顿方阵的决定性优势，并改变了希腊政治格局。
description.en: The Roman consul Flamininus defeated King Philip V of Macedon at Cynoscephalae. The battle demonstrated the decisive superiority of the Roman legion over the Macedonian phalanx and transformed Greek politics.

title.zh: 伊斯特米亚宣言：希腊自由
title.en: Isthmian Proclamation of the Freedom of the Greeks

description.zh: 罗马将领弗拉米尼努斯在伊斯特米亚运动会上宣布“希腊人自由”，象征罗马在希腊世界的政治主导地位。此举虽然以解放为名，却开启了更深的罗马干预。
description.en: At the Isthmian Games, Flamininus proclaimed the 'freedom of the Greeks,' symbolizing Rome’s political ascendancy in the Greek world. Though presented as liberation, it opened the way to deeper Roman intervention.

title.zh: 汉高祖刘邦去世，吕后摄政时代开始
title.en: Death of Emperor Gaozu and the Beginning of Empress Lü’s Regency

description.zh: 汉高祖刘邦去世后，吕后逐步掌握朝政，汉初权力结构发生重大变化。此后皇室与功臣集团关系进一步重组。
description.en: After the death of Emperor Gaozu, Empress Lü gradually took control of the Han court, transforming the early dynasty’s power structure. Relations between the imperial house and the founding nobles were subsequently reshaped.

description.zh: 塞琉古国王安条克三世应埃托利亚同盟之邀进入希腊，挑战罗马在东地中海的影响力。此举引发罗马—塞琉古战争。
description.en: King Antiochus III of the Seleucid Empire entered Greece at the invitation of the Aetolian League, challenging Roman influence in the eastern Mediterranean. This move triggered the Roman-Seleucid War.

description.zh: 罗马军队在温泉关击败安条克三世，迫使其撤离希腊。此战确认罗马在希腊的军事优势，并将战争推进到小亚细亚。
description.en: Roman forces defeated Antiochus III at Thermopylae, forcing him to withdraw from Greece. The battle confirmed Roman military superiority in Greece and shifted the war toward Asia Minor.

title.zh: 阿庇乌斯大道延伸与罗马意大利整合深化
title.en: Further Integration of Italy through Roman Road Networks

description.zh: 到前2世纪初，罗马通过阿庇乌斯大道等道路体系持续加强对意大利的军事与行政整合。道路建设虽非单一年份事件，却是罗马扩张能力的关键基础设施成果。
description.en: By the early second century BCE, Rome’s road system, including the Via Appia, had further strengthened military and administrative integration across Italy. Though not confined to a single year, this infrastructure was a crucial foundation of Roman expansion.

description.zh: 孔雀帝国皇帝阿育王于这一时期去世，他对佛教的支持推动了南亚宗教与政治文化的深远转变。其去世后孔雀帝国逐渐走向衰落。
description.en: Emperor Ashoka of the Maurya Empire died around this time; his patronage of Buddhism had transformed South Asian religious and political culture. After his death, the Mauryan Empire gradually declined.

title.zh: 塞琉古王朝重夺东方领地的安条克三世东征展开
title.en: Antiochus III Launches the Anabasis in the East

description.zh: 安条克三世开始大规模东征，试图恢复塞琉古帝国在伊朗和中亚的控制。此次远征显著重塑了西亚与中亚的政治关系。
description.en: Antiochus III began his great eastern campaign, seeking to restore Seleucid control in Iran and Central Asia. The expedition significantly reshaped political relations across western and central Asia.

title.zh: 孔雀帝国衰亡与巽伽王朝建立的前夜
title.en: Late Decline of the Maurya Empire

description.zh: 到前190年前后，孔雀帝国已显著衰弱，地方离心和中央权威下降为其不久后的覆灭埋下伏笔。这个衰退过程改变了南亚的帝国结构。
description.en: By around 190 BCE, the Maurya Empire had markedly weakened, with centrifugal regional forces and declining central authority setting the stage for its imminent fall. This decline altered the imperial structure of South Asia.
